source: “It was to you,” said the spy, “that his daughter came; and it was from your care that his daughter took him, accompanied by a neat brown monsieur; how is he called?—in a little wig—Lorry—of the bank of Tellson and Company—over to England.”
domestication: “是他女儿来找你，”间谍说道，“也是在你的照看下，他女儿带走了他，还有一位小巧的先生，穿着棕色衣服，叫什么来着？——戴着个小假发——洛里——泰勒森银行的——一起去了英格兰。”
foreignization: “是他女儿来找的你，”间谍说，“而且，也是在你的照看之下，他女儿带着他，由一位整洁的棕发先生——怎么称呼来着？——戴着一个小假发——劳瑞——来自泰勒森银行——去了英格兰。”

“It was to you,” said the spy, “that his daughter came; and it was from your care that his daughter took him, accompanied by a neat brown monsieur; how is he called?—in a little wig—Lorry—of the bank of Tellson and Company—over to England.”
“是他女儿来找你，”间谍说道，“也是在你的照看下，他女儿带走了他，还有一位小巧的先生，穿着棕色衣服，叫什么来着？——戴着个小假发——洛里——泰勒森银行的——一起去了英格兰。”

“是他女儿来找的你，”间谍说，“而且，也是在你的照看之下，他女儿带着他，由一位整洁的棕发先生——怎么称呼来着？——戴着一个小假发——劳瑞——来自泰勒森银行——去了英格兰。”

source: Jerry hoarsely professed himself at Miss Pross’s service. He had worn all his rust off long ago, but nothing would file his spiky head down.
domestication: 杰里沙哑着嗓子说愿意为普罗斯小姐效劳。他身上的锈早就磨光了，可那颗刺头怎么也锉不平。

source: “Infected with the new doctrines,” said a third, eyeing the direction through his glass in passing; “set himself in opposition to the last Marquis, abandoned the estates when he inherited them, and left them to the ruffian herd. They will recompense him now, I hope, as he deserves.”
domestication: “中了新思想的毒，”第三人边说边透过望远镜朝那个方向看了一眼，“跟上一任侯爵作对，继承了产业却弃之不顾，任由那群恶棍糟蹋。但愿他现在能自食其果，得到应有的报应。”
foreignization: “受到新教义的感染，”第三人说道，一边透过望远镜朝那个方向看了一眼；“与上一任侯爵作对，继承庄园后就放弃了它们，让那些恶棍群占据。我希望他们现在会以他应得的方式报答他。”

“Infected with the new doctrines,” said a third, eyeing the direction through his glass in passing; “set himself in opposition to the last Marquis, abandoned the estates when he inherited them, and left them to the ruffian herd. They will recompense him now, I hope, as he deserves.”
“中了新思想的毒，”第三人边说边透过望远镜朝那个方向看了一眼，“跟上一任侯爵作对，继承了产业却弃之不顾，任由那群恶棍糟蹋。但愿他现在能自食其果，得到应有的报应。”

“Infected with the new doctrines,” said a third, eyeing the direction through his glass in passing; “set himself in opposition to the last Marquis, abandoned the estates when he inherited them, and left them to the ruffian herd. They will recompense him now, I hope, as he deserves.”
“受到新教义的感染，”第三人说道，一边透过望远镜朝那个方向看了一眼；“与上一任侯爵作对，继承庄园后就放弃了它们，让那些恶棍群占据。我希望他们现在会以他应得的方式报答他。”

source: “Then I tell you again, Mr. Darnay, I am sorry for it. I am sorry to hear you putting any such extraordinary questions. Here is a fellow, who, infected by the most pestilent and blasphemous code of devilry that ever was known, abandoned his property to the vilest scum of the earth that ever did murder by wholesale, and you ask me why I am sorry that a man who instructs youth knows him? Well, but I’ll answer you. I am sorry because I believe there is contamination in such a scoundrel. That’s why.”
domestication: 那我就再跟你说一遍，达奈先生，我对此感到遗憾。听到你问这种离谱的问题，我也感到遗憾。有这么一个家伙，受了历史上最恶毒、最亵渎神明的魔鬼教条的毒害，把财产拱手让给了那些曾经大开杀戒的最下流的渣滓，你还问我为什么对一个教书的先生认识他感到遗憾？好，那我就告诉你。我之所以感到遗憾，是因为我相信这种无赖身上带着脏东西。这就是原因。
foreignization: “那么我再告诉你一次，达奈先生，我对此感到遗憾。听到你提出这样奇怪的问题我也感到遗憾。这里有一个家伙，受到有史以来最恶毒、最亵渎神明的魔鬼教义的感染，把他的财产留给了曾经进行过大屠杀的最卑鄙的地痞无赖，而你却问我为什么对你认识一个教导青年的人感到遗憾？好吧，不过我会回答你。我感到遗憾是因为我相信这样一个恶棍是有污染性的。这就是原因。”

messages_domestication.content:

Well, but I’ll answer you. I am sorry because I believe there is contamination in such a scoundrel. That’s why.”

source: Miss Pross, submitting herself to his judgment, the scheme was worked out with care. Having abundance of time for his usual methodical toilette, Mr. Lorry presented himself at the breakfast-hour in his usual white linen, and with his usual neat leg. The Doctor was summoned in the usual way, and came to breakfast.
domestication: 普罗斯小姐听从他的建议，这个计划便细心地制订了出来。洛里先生时间充裕，可以像往常一样从容不迫地打扮自己，于是早餐时分，他穿着惯常的白色亚麻衬衫，打扮得整整齐齐地出现了。医生也照例被叫来一起吃早饭。
foreignization: Miss Pross 服从他的判断，这个计划便被仔细地制定了出来。有充足的时间进行他惯常的细致打扮，洛里先生在早餐时间以他惯常的白亚麻布衣服和整洁的腿部出现在大家面前。医生像往常一样被召唤来吃早餐。

The Doctor was summoned in the usual way, and came to breakfast.

“我不太明白，”叔叔边说边啜饮着咖啡，“我可以请您解释一下吗？”

source: Through gloomy vaults where the light of day had never shone, past hideous doors of dark dens and cages, down cavernous flights of steps, and again up steep rugged ascents of stone and brick, more like dry waterfalls than staircases, Defarge, the turnkey, and Jacques Three, linked hand and arm, went with all the speed they could make. Here and there, especially at first, the inundation started on them and swept by; but when they had done descending, and were winding and climbing up a tower, they were alone. Hemmed in here by the massive thickness of walls and arches, the storm within the fortress and without was only audible to them in a dull, subdued way, as if the noise out of which they had come had almost destroyed their sense of hearing.
domestication: 穿过阴暗的拱顶，那里从未有过阳光，经过狰狞的黑牢和铁笼，沿着深不见底的阶梯往下走，再爬上陡峭崎岖的石阶和砖阶，这些台阶更像是干涸的瀑布而非楼梯，德发日和钥匙管理员雅克三世手挽着手，尽可能快地前行。一路上，特别是在刚开始的时候，人群如潮水般涌来又退去；但当他们下到最深处，又开始绕着塔楼往上爬时，周围就只剩下他们两人了。四周被厚厚的墙壁和拱门紧紧包围，堡垒内外的风暴声音变得低沉而模糊，仿佛外面的喧嚣已经把他们的听力都给毁了似的。
foreignization: 穿过从未有日光照射的阴暗拱顶，经过黑暗洞穴和笼子那可怕的门，沿着深邃的台阶往下走，再沿着石头和砖块砌成的陡峭崎岖的上坡路往上爬，这些路更像是干涸的瀑布而不是楼梯，德发日、狱卒和雅克三世手挽手，尽他们所能快速前进。这里和那里，尤其是在一开始，洪水向他们袭来又迅速掠过；但当他们下到深处，又在塔内蜿蜒而上时，他们就独自一人了。在这里，被厚重的墙壁和拱门围住，堡垒内外的风暴对他们来说只有沉闷和压抑的声音，仿佛他们刚离开的嘈杂声几乎摧毁了他们的听觉。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Through gloomy vaults where the light of day had never shone, past hideous doors of dark dens and cages, down cavernous flights of steps, and again up steep rugged ascents of stone and brick, more like dry waterfalls than staircases, Defarge, the turnkey, and Jacques Three, linked hand and arm, went with all the speed they could make. Here and there, especially at first, the inundation started on them and swept by; but when they had done descending, and were winding and climbing up a tower, they were alone. Hemmed in here by the massive thickness of walls and arches, the storm within the fortress and without was only audible to them in a dull, subdued way, as if the noise out of which they had come had almost destroyed their sense of hearing.

source: Time was, when a poet sat upon a stool in a public place, and mused in the sight of men. Mr. Cruncher, sitting on a stool in a public place, but not being a poet, mused as little as possible, and looked about him.
domestication: 从前，诗人会坐在大庭广众之下，吟诗作赋，任人观赏。可克伦彻先生虽也坐在公共场所的凳子上，但他不是诗人，自然不会吟诗，只是东张西望，尽量少动脑筋。
foreignization: 过去，当一位诗人坐在公共场合的凳子上，对着众人沉思。克伦彻先生坐在公共场合的凳子上，但他不是诗人，因此几乎不沉思，而是四处张望。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Time was, when a poet sat upon a stool in a public place, and mused in the sight of men. Mr. Cruncher, sitting on a stool in a public place, but not being a poet, mused as little as possible, and looked about him.
从前，诗人会坐在大庭广众之下，吟诗作赋，任人观赏。可克伦彻先生虽也坐在公共场所的凳子上，但他不是诗人，自然不会吟诗，只是东张西望，尽量少动脑筋。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Time was, when a poet sat upon a stool in a public place, and mused in the sight of men. Mr. Cruncher, sitting on a stool in a public place, but not being a poet, mused as little as possible, and looked about him.
过去，当一位诗人坐在公共场合的凳子上，对着众人沉思。克伦彻先生坐在公共场合的凳子上，但他不是诗人，因此几乎不沉思，而是四处张望。

source: Thus the evening wore away with the Cruncher family, until Young Jerry was ordered to bed, and his mother, laid under similar injunctions, obeyed them. Mr. Cruncher beguiled the earlier watches of the night with solitary pipes, and did not start upon his excursion until nearly one o’clock. Towards that small and ghostly hour, he rose up from his chair, took a key out of his pocket, opened a locked cupboard, and brought forth a sack, a crowbar of convenient size, a rope and chain, and other fishing tackle of that nature. Disposing these articles about him in skilful manner, he bestowed a parting defiance on Mrs. Cruncher, extinguished the light, and went out.
domestication: 就这样，克兰彻一家的夜晚慢慢消磨着，直到小杰里被吩咐上床睡觉，他妈妈也接到了同样的命令，便乖乖地遵从了。老克兰彻独自一人抽了好几袋烟，消磨掉了前半夜的时光，直到快凌晨一点钟，他才起身准备出门。这会儿天色已晚，阴森森的，他从椅子上站起来，从口袋里掏出一把钥匙，打开一个锁着的柜子，拿出一条袋子、一根方便携带的撬棍、一条绳子和铁链，还有一些类似的捕鱼工具。他熟练地把这些东西绑在自己身上，然后对克兰彻太太撂下几句狠话，吹灭了灯，便出门去了。
foreignization: 于是，克伦彻一家人的夜晚就这样过去了，直到小杰里被命令上床睡觉，而他的母亲在接到类似的指示后也遵从了。克伦彻先生在夜初时分独自抽着烟斗消磨时间，直到将近一点钟才开始他的出行。接近那个小小的、幽灵般的时刻，他从椅子上站起来，从口袋里掏出一把钥匙，打开一个锁着的橱柜，拿出一个袋子、一根方便携带的撬棍、一条绳子和链子，以及类似这样的其他捕鱼工具。以熟练的方式将这些物品安置好后，他对克伦彻太太投以最后的轻蔑，熄灭了灯光，然后出去了。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Thus the evening wore away with the Cruncher family, until Young Jerry was ordered to bed, and his mother, laid under similar injunctions, obeyed them. Mr. Cruncher beguiled the earlier watches of the night with solitary pipes, and did not start upon his excursion until nearly one o’clock. Towards that small and ghostly hour, he rose up from his chair, took a key out of his pocket, opened a locked cupboard, and brought forth a sack, a crowbar of convenient size, a rope and chain, and other fishing tackle of that nature. Disposing these articles about him in skilful manner, he bestowed a parting defiance on Mrs. Cruncher, extinguished the light, and went out.

就这样，克兰彻一家的夜晚慢慢消磨着，直到小杰里被吩咐上床睡觉，他妈妈也接到了同样的命令，便乖乖地遵从了。老克兰彻独自一人抽了好几袋烟，消磨掉了前半夜的时光，直到快凌晨一点钟，他才起身准备出门。这会儿天色已晚，阴森森的，他从椅子上站起来，从口袋里掏出一把钥匙，打开一个锁着的柜子，拿出一条袋子、一根方便携带的撬棍、一条绳子和铁链，还有一些类似的捕鱼工具。他熟练地把这些东西绑在自己身上，然后对克兰彻太太撂下几句狠话，吹灭了灯，便出门去了。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Thus the evening wore away with the Cruncher family, until Young Jerry was ordered to bed, and his mother, laid under similar injunctions, obeyed them. Mr. Cruncher beguiled the earlier watches of the night with solitary pipes, and did not start upon his excursion until nearly one o’clock. Towards that small and ghostly hour, he rose up from his chair, took a key out of his pocket, opened a locked cupboard, and brought forth a sack, a crowbar of convenient size, a rope and chain, and other fishing tackle of that nature. Disposing these articles about him in skilful manner, he bestowed a parting defiance on Mrs. Cruncher, extinguished the light, and went out.

source: Nothing more was said, and the mender of roads, being found already dozing on the topmost stair, was advised to lay himself down on the pallet-bed and take some rest. He needed no persuasion, and was soon asleep.
domestication: 再没人说什么了。修路的老头儿已经在最上面的台阶上打起了瞌睡，于是有人建议他躺到草垫床上歇一会儿。他也不推辞，很快就睡着了。
foreignization: 没有再说什么，发现修路人在最高一级楼梯上已经打瞌睡，便建议他躺在草垫床上休息一下。他不需要劝说，很快就睡着了。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Nothing more was said, and the mender of roads, being found already dozing on the topmost stair, was advised to lay himself down on the pallet-bed and take some rest. He needed no persuasion, and was soon asleep.
再没人说什么了。修路的老头儿已经在最上面的台阶上打起了瞌睡，于是有人建议他躺到草垫床上歇一会儿。他也不推辞，很快就睡着了。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Nothing more was said, and the mender of roads, being found already dozing on the topmost stair, was advised to lay himself down on the pallet-bed and take some rest. He needed no persuasion, and was soon asleep.
没有再说什么，发现修路人在最高一级楼梯上已经打瞌睡，便建议他躺在草垫床上休息一下。他不需要劝说，很快就睡着了。

source: The stillness consequent on the cessation of the rumbling and labouring of the coach, added to the stillness of the night, made it very quiet indeed. The panting of the horses communicated a tremulous motion to the coach, as if it were in a state of agitation. The hearts of the passengers beat loud enough perhaps to be heard; but at any rate, the quiet pause was audibly expressive of people out of breath, and holding the breath, and having the pulses quickened by expectation.
domestication: 马车的轰隆声和颠簸声停了下来，夜色更加静谧，四周一片安宁。马儿的喘息声让马车也跟着轻轻颤抖，好像它也在紧张。乘客们的心跳得咚咚响，几乎能听见；但无论如何，这静悄悄的一刻显然大家都屏住了呼吸，心跳加速，满心期待。
foreignization: 马车的轰隆声和颠簸声停止后随之而来的寂静，加上夜的寂静，使四周变得非常安静。马匹的喘息声使马车产生了颤抖的运动，仿佛它处于一种激动的状态。乘客们的心跳可能响亮到足以被听到；但无论如何，这安静的停顿清楚地表达了人们屏住呼吸、心跳因期待而加速的声音。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The stillness consequent on the cessation of the rumbling and labouring of the coach, added to the stillness of the night, made it very quiet indeed. The panting of the horses communicated a tremulous motion to the coach, as if it were in a state of agitation. The hearts of the passengers beat loud enough perhaps to be heard; but at any rate, the quiet pause was audibly expressive of people out of breath, and holding the breath, and having the pulses quickened by expectation.
马车的轰隆声和颠簸声停了下来，夜色更加静谧，四周一片安宁。马儿的喘息声让马车也跟着轻轻颤抖，好像它也在紧张。乘客们的心跳得咚咚响，几乎能听见；但无论如何，这静悄悄的一刻显然大家都屏住了呼吸，心跳加速，满心期待。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The stillness consequent on the cessation of the rumbling and labouring of the coach, added to the stillness of the night, made it very quiet indeed. The panting of the horses communicated a tremulous motion to the coach, as if it were in a state of agitation. The hearts of the passengers beat loud enough perhaps to be heard; but at any rate, the quiet pause was audibly expressive of people out of breath, and holding the breath, and having the pulses quickened by expectation.
马车的轰隆声和颠簸声停止后随之而来的寂静，加上夜的寂静，使四周变得非常安静。马匹的喘息声使马车产生了颤抖的运动，仿佛它处于一种激动的状态。乘客们的心跳可能响亮到足以被听到；但无论如何，这安静的停顿清楚地表达了人们屏住呼吸、心跳因期待而加速的声音。

source: “Gentlemen,” said her husband, who had kept his bright eye observantly upon her, “good day. The chamber, furnished bachelor-fashion, that you wished to see, and were inquiring for when I stepped out, is on the fifth floor. The doorway of the staircase gives on the little courtyard close to the left here,” pointing with his hand, “near to the window of my establishment. But, now that I remember, one of you has already been there, and can show the way. Gentlemen, adieu!”
domestication: 诸位，她丈夫说道，他一直用那双炯炯有神的眼睛观察着她，“诸位好。你们刚才问的那间单身汉的房间，在五楼。楼梯口就在左边不远处的小院子里，靠近我这窗户。不过我现在想起来，你们中的一位已经去过，可以带路了。诸位，告辞！”
foreignization: “先生们，”她的丈夫说，他一直用明亮的眼睛观察着她，“日安。你们想看的、并且在我出来时询问的那间单身汉布置的房间，在五楼。楼梯的门开向左边这里的那个小院子，靠近我店铺的窗户。”他用手一指，“不过，现在我想起来了，你们中的一位已经去过那里，可以带路。先生们，再见！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Gentlemen,” said her husband, who had kept his bright eye observantly upon her, “good day. The chamber, furnished bachelor-fashion, that you wished to see, and were inquiring for when I stepped out, is on the fifth floor. The doorway of the staircase gives on the little courtyard close to the left here,” pointing with his hand, “near to the window of my establishment. But, now that I remember, one of you has already been there, and can show the way. Gentlemen, adieu!”
诸位，她丈夫说道，他一直用那双炯炯有神的眼睛观察着她，“诸位好。你们刚才问的那间单身汉的房间，在五楼。楼梯口就在左边不远处的小院子里，靠近我这窗户。不过我现在想起来，你们中的一位已经去过，可以带路了。诸位，告辞！”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Gentlemen,” said her husband, who had kept his bright eye observantly upon her, “good day. The chamber, furnished bachelor-fashion, that you wished to see, and were inquiring for when I stepped out, is on the fifth floor. The doorway of the staircase gives on the little courtyard close to the left here,” pointing with his hand, “near to the window of my establishment. But, now that I remember, one of you has already been there, and can show the way. Gentlemen, adieu!”
“先生们，”她的丈夫说，他一直用明亮的眼睛观察着她，“日安。你们想看的、并且在我出来时询问的那间单身汉布置的房间，在五楼。楼梯的门开向左边这里的那个小院子，靠近我店铺的窗户。”他用手一指，“不过，现在我想起来了，你们中的一位已经去过那里，可以带路。先生们，再见！”

source: “Not dead! He feared us so much—and with reason—that he caused himself to be represented as dead, and had a grand mock-funeral. But they have found him alive, hiding in the country, and have brought him in. I have seen him but now, on his way to the Hotel de Ville, a prisoner. I have said that he had reason to fear us. Say all! Had he reason?”
domestication: 没死！他怕我们怕得要命——而且确实有理由害怕——所以假装自己死了，还搞了个隆重的假葬礼。可他们后来发现他还活着，躲在乡下，于是把他抓回来了。我刚才还看见他了，被押着去市政厅的路上。我说他有理由害怕我们，说吧！他真有理由吗？
foreignization: 没死！他如此害怕我们——而且有理由——以至于让自己被表现为已死，并举行了一场盛大的假葬礼。但他们发现了他还活着，藏在乡下，把他带了回来。我刚才还看到他，作为囚犯被送往市政厅。我说过他有理由害怕我们。说吧！他有理由吗？

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Not dead! He feared us so much—and with reason—that he caused himself to be represented as dead, and had a grand mock-funeral. But they have found him alive, hiding in the country, and have brought him in. I have seen him but now, on his way to the Hotel de Ville, a prisoner. I have said that he had reason to fear us. Say all! Had he reason?”
没死！他怕我们怕得要命——而且确实有理由害怕——所以假装自己死了，还搞了个隆重的假葬礼。可他们后来发现他还活着，躲在乡下，于是把他抓回来了。我刚才还看见他了，被押着去市政厅的路上。我说他有理由害怕我们，说吧！他真有理由吗？

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Not dead! He feared us so much—and with reason—that he caused himself to be represented as dead, and had a grand mock-funeral. But they have found him alive, hiding in the country, and have brought him in. I have seen him but now, on his way to the Hotel de Ville, a prisoner. I have said that he had reason to fear us. Say all! Had he reason?”
没死！他如此害怕我们——而且有理由——以至于让自己被表现为已死，并举行了一场盛大的假葬礼。但他们发现了他还活着，藏在乡下，把他带了回来。我刚才还看到他，作为囚犯被送往市政厅。我说过他有理由害怕我们。说吧！他有理由吗？

source: But, he had oppressed no man, he had imprisoned no man; he was so far from having harshly exacted payment of his dues, that he had relinquished them of his own will, thrown himself on a world with no favour in it, won his own private place there, and earned his own bread. Monsieur Gabelle had held the impoverished and involved estate on written instructions, to spare the people, to give them what little there was to give—such fuel as the heavy creditors would let them have in the winter, and such produce as could be saved from the same grip in the summer—and no doubt he had put the fact in plea and proof, for his own safety, so that it could not but appear now.
domestication: 但他从没压迫过谁，也没关押过谁；他不仅没有苛刻地索要债务，反而主动放弃了这些权利，自食其力，在这个毫无温情的世界里找到了自己的立足之地，靠自己的双手挣面包吃。加贝先生是按照书面指示管理那片贫困且复杂的土地的，指示上说要体恤百姓，把有限的东西分给他们——冬天给点债权人允许的柴火，夏天给点能从债权人手中省下来的粮食。他这么做无疑是为了自身的安全，现在这一切自然就明了了。
foreignization: 但是，他并没有压迫任何人，也没有囚禁任何人；他非但没有严苛地索取他的权益，反而自愿放弃了这些权益，把自己置身于一个对他毫无偏爱的世界中，赢得了自己的一席之地，并且自食其力。加贝尔先生根据书面指示管理着那个贫困且纠缠不清的地产，尽量减轻人民的负担，给予他们所能给予的微薄之物——冬天时提供债权人们允许给他们的燃料，夏天时尽可能从债权人的掌握中节省一些农产品——毫无疑问，他为了自己的安全，已经将这些事实作为辩护和证据，所以现在这些情况必然显现出来。

source: Mr. Lorry reddened as if he were conscious of having debated the point in his mind, and Mr. Carton made his way to the outside of the bar. The way out of court lay in that direction, and Jerry followed him, all eyes, ears, and spikes.
domestication: 洛里先生脸红得好像心里一直纠结着什么，卡顿先生则向外走去，穿过了酒吧。出口就在那个方向，杰瑞紧跟着他，眼睛、耳朵和头顶的发梢都竖了起来，生怕错过什么。
foreignization: 洛里先生脸红了，仿佛意识到自己曾在心里争论过这个问题，而卡顿先生则向外走去，走到了酒吧外面。出庭的方向就在那个方向，杰瑞跟着他，全神贯注地用眼睛、耳朵和尖刺观察着。

source: “Not even so. I might not have the hopefulness to do it for weeks; I might (mistaken or not mistaken) have that hopefulness to-morrow.”
domestication: 可不是这样。我也许好几个星期都提不起精神来；说不定明天（不管是对是错）我又有了这股劲头。
foreignization: “甚至不是这样。我可能没有希望去做这件事几周；我可能（错误或不错误）明天就有那种希望。”

source: Mr. Cruncher was soothed, but shook his head in a dubious and moral way. “It depends upon how you dewelop your talents. Be careful to dewelop your talents, and never to say no more than you can help to nobody, and there’s no telling at the present time what you may not come to be fit for.” As Young Jerry, thus encouraged, went on a few yards in advance, to plant the stool in the shadow of the Bar, Mr. Cruncher added to himself: “Jerry, you honest tradesman, there’s hopes wot that boy will yet be a blessing to you, and a recompense to you for his mother!”
domestication: 克伦彻先生被安抚了，但还是半信半疑地摇了摇头，一副道貌岸然的样子。“这得看你如何发挥你的才能。你可得好好培养自己的才能，少说没用的话，将来你可能会变得多有出息，现在谁也说不准。”小杰瑞受到这样的鼓励，走在前面几步远，准备在酒吧的阴影处放好小凳子。克伦彻先生自言自语道：“杰瑞啊，你这个正直的手艺人，说不定这孩子以后能成为你的福星，补偿你失去妻子的痛楚！”
foreignization: 克伦彻先生被安慰了，但还是以一种怀疑和道德的态度摇了摇头。“这取决于你如何发展你的才能。小心地发展你的才能，永远不要对任何人多说什么，目前还无法预料你将来会适合做什么。” 小杰里受到这样的鼓励后，先走了几码，在酒吧的阴影里放好凳子，克伦彻先生自言自语地加了一句：“杰里，你这个诚实的商人，那个男孩将来也许会成为你的福气，补偿你因他母亲所受的一切！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Mr. Cruncher was soothed, but shook his head in a dubious and moral way. “It depends upon how you dewelop your talents. Be careful to dewelop your talents, and never to say no more than you can help to nobody, and there’s no telling at the present time what you may not come to be fit for.” As Young Jerry, thus encouraged, went on a few yards in advance, to plant the stool in the shadow of the Bar, Mr. Cruncher added to himself: “Jerry, you honest tradesman, there’s hopes wot that boy will yet be a blessing to you, and a recompense to you for his mother!”

source: The sort of interest with which this man was stared and breathed at, was not a sort that elevated humanity. Had he stood in peril of a less horrible sentence—had there been a chance of any one of its savage details being spared—by just so much would he have lost in his fascination. The form that was to be doomed to be so shamefully mangled, was the sight; the immortal creature that was to be so butchered and torn asunder, yielded the sensation. Whatever gloss the various spectators put upon the interest, according to their several arts and powers of self-deceit, the interest was, at the root of it, Ogreish.
domestication: 这人所受的关注和注视，可不像能提升人性的东西。若他面临的不是这般可怕的刑罚，如果还有可能免除其中任何一项残忍的细节，那么他在人们心中的吸引力就会大打折扣。人们要看的是那即将遭受凌迟的身体，要感受的是那将被肢解的灵魂。无论观众们怎样用自己的手段和自欺欺人的方式解释这种兴趣，归根结底，这种兴趣是野蛮的，甚至可以说是魔鬼般的。
foreignization: 此人被注视和呼吸时的那种兴趣，并不是一种能提升人性的兴趣。如果他面临的是一种不那么可怕的刑罚——如果有可能免除其野蛮细节中的任何一项——他就不会在吸引力上减少那么多。将要遭受如此羞辱性摧残的身体，就是场景；那将要被如此残酷地宰割和撕裂的不死之物，引发了这种感觉。无论各种观众根据他们各自的心术和自我欺骗的能力对这种兴趣赋予何种光彩，这种兴趣在根子上是食人魔般的。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The sort of interest with which this man was stared and breathed at, was not a sort that elevated humanity. Had he stood in peril of a less horrible sentence—had there been a chance of any one of its savage details being spared—by just so much would he have lost in his fascination. The form that was to be doomed to be so shamefully mangled, was the sight; the immortal creature that was to be so butchered and torn asunder, yielded the sensation. Whatever gloss the various spectators put upon the interest, according to their several arts and powers of self-deceit, the interest was, at the root of it, Ogreish.
这人所受的关注和注视，可不像能提升人性的东西。若他面临的不是这般可怕的刑罚，如果还有可能免除其中任何一项残忍的细节，那么他在人们心中的吸引力就会大打折扣。人们要看的是那即将遭受凌迟的身体，要感受的是那将被肢解的灵魂。无论观众们怎样用自己的手段和自欺欺人的方式解释这种兴趣，归根结底，这种兴趣是野蛮的，甚至可以说是魔鬼般的。

source: “And now speak to your father, dearest. No other man in all this France could have done what he has done for me.”
domestication: 亲爱的，你现在去跟你父亲说说话吧。全法国再找不出第二个像他这样对我好的人了。
foreignization: “现在和你父亲说话吧，亲爱的。全法国再没有其他男人能为我做到他所做的一切。”

messages_foreignization.content:

“And now speak to your father, dearest. No other man in all this France could have done what he has done for me.”
“现在和你父亲说话吧，亲爱的。全法国再没有其他男人能为我做到他所做的一切。”

source: “Then be so kind,” urged Miss Manette, “as to leave us here. You see how composed he has become, and you cannot be afraid to leave him with me now. Why should you be? If you will lock the door to secure us from interruption, I do not doubt that you will find him, when you come back, as quiet as you leave him. In any case, I will take care of him until you return, and then we will remove him straight.”
domestication: “那就请您行个好，”曼内特小姐恳求道，“让我们留在这里吧。您看，他已经这么平静了，现在和他单独待在一起，您总不会还担心吧？有什么好担心的呢？如果您能把门锁上，免得有人打扰，我想等您回来时，他会和现在一样安静的。不管怎样，我会好好照顾他的，等您回来，我们就能立刻带他离开。”
foreignization: “那么请您好心地，”马内特小姐催促道，“让我们留在这里。您看，他已经变得多么镇定了，现在您把他留给我应该不会害怕了。您为什么该害怕呢？如果您愿意锁上门以防止被打扰，我相信当您回来时，他会和您离开时一样安静。无论如何，我会照顾他直到您回来，然后我们就会立刻将他转移。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Then be so kind,” urged Miss Manette, “as to leave us here. You see how composed he has become, and you cannot be afraid to leave him with me now. Why should you be? If you will lock the door to secure us from interruption, I do not doubt that you will find him, when you come back, as quiet as you leave him. In any case, I will take care of him until you return, and then we will remove him straight.”
“那就请您行个好，”曼内特小姐恳求道，“让我们留在这里吧。您看，他已经这么平静了，现在和他单独待在一起，您总不会还担心吧？有什么好担心的呢？如果您能把门锁上，免得有人打扰，我想等您回来时，他会和现在一样安静的。不管怎样，我会好好照顾他的，等您回来，我们就能立刻带他离开。”

source: “Do you know, Sydney,” said Mr. Stryver, looking at him with sharp eyes, and slowly drawing a hand across his florid face: “do you know, I rather thought, at the time, that you sympathised with the golden-haired doll, and were quick to see what happened to the golden-haired doll?”
domestication: 你知道吗，西德尼，”斯特莱佛先生锐利的眼神盯着他，慢慢用手抹了抹自己红润的脸，“当时我总觉得你对那个金发玩偶挺有同情心的，而且很快看出了那个金发玩偶会遇到什么事儿。”

source: “It will be much the same to your knowledge, miss, I should think,” retorted Jerry, “whether they drink your health or the Old Un’s.”
domestication: “小姐，我想对你来说也差不多，”杰里反驳道，“不管是祝你健康，还是祝那个老家伙健康。”
foreignization: “我想，小姐，对你来说，他们无论是为你的健康干杯还是为那个老家伙的健康干杯，都差不多。”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“It will be much the same to your knowledge, miss, I should think,” retorted Jerry, “whether they drink your health or the Old Un’s.”
“我想，小姐，对你来说，他们无论是为你的健康干杯还是为那个老家伙的健康干杯，都差不多。”

source: “Monseigneur, hear me! Monseigneur, hear my petition! My husband died of want; so many die of want; so many more will die of want.”
domestication: 大人，听我说！大人，求您听我一言！我的丈夫是饿死的；有多少人都是这样饿死的；将来还会有更多的人饿死。
foreignization: “大人，听我说！大人，听我的请求！我的丈夫因饥寒而死；那么多人因饥寒而死；还将有更多的人因饥寒而死。”

messages_domestication.content:

source: It was the rush and roar of rain that he typified, and it stopped him, for no voice could be heard in it. A memorable storm of thunder and lightning broke with that sweep of water, and there was not a moment’s interval in crash, and fire, and rain, until after the moon rose at midnight.
domestication: 他就像那急促而猛烈的雨声，让人停下脚步，因为任何声音都被这雨声吞没了。这是一场雷电交加、令人难忘的暴雨，水势汹涌，雷鸣、闪电和大雨交织在一起，直到午夜月亮升起，才稍稍平息。
foreignization: 那是雨的奔腾和轰鸣，他将其典型化，而这也使他停下了脚步，因为在这声音中任何话语都听不见。一场雷电交加的难忘风暴随着那股水势爆发了，在月亮午夜升起之前，雷鸣、闪电和暴雨一刻也没有停歇。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was the rush and roar of rain that he typified, and it stopped him, for no voice could be heard in it. A memorable storm of thunder and lightning broke with that sweep of water, and there was not a moment’s interval in crash, and fire, and rain, until after the moon rose at midnight.
他就像那急促而猛烈的雨声，让人停下脚步，因为任何声音都被这雨声吞没了。这是一场雷电交加、令人难忘的暴雨，水势汹涌，雷鸣、闪电和大雨交织在一起，直到午夜月亮升起，才稍稍平息。

source: The footsteps were incessant, and the hurry of them became more and more rapid. The corner echoed and re-echoed with the tread of feet; some, as it seemed, under the windows; some, as it seemed, in the room; some coming, some going, some breaking off, some stopping altogether; all in the distant streets, and not one within sight.
domestication: 脚步声连绵不绝，越来越急促。墙角回荡着脚步声，有的似乎就在窗下，有的好像就在屋里，有的来，有的去，有的中断，有的停了下来；其实都在远处的街上，却一个人都看不见。
foreignization: 脚步声不断，而且越来越急促。角落里回荡着脚步声；有些似乎是在窗户下；有些似乎是在房间里；有些在来，有些在去，有些中断了，有些完全停了下来；都在远处的街道上，没有一个在视线之内。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The corner echoed and re-echoed with the tread of feet; some, as it seemed, under the windows; some, as it seemed, in the room; some coming, some going, some breaking off, some stopping altogether; all in the distant streets, and not one within sight.

source: “Of his own necessity. As he was, when I first saw him after they found me and demanded to know if I would take him, and, at my peril be discreet—as he was then, so he is now.”
domestication: 他这样做也是出于自己的需要。就像我第一次见到他时那样，那时他们发现了我，问我是否愿意收留他，并警告我一定要保密，不然会有危险。当时他是那样的人，现在还是这样。
foreignization: “出于他自己的需要。就像我第一次见到他时那样，当时他们找到我，要求知道我是否会接受他，并且以我的危险为代价保持谨慎——他那时是怎样的，现在还是怎样。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Of his own necessity. As he was, when I first saw him after they found me and demanded to know if I would take him, and, at my peril be discreet—as he was then, so he is now.”
他这样做也是出于自己的需要。就像我第一次见到他时那样，那时他们发现了我，问我是否愿意收留他，并警告我一定要保密，不然会有危险。当时他是那样的人，现在还是这样。

source: It was the year of Our Lord one thousand seven hundred and seventy-five. Spiritual revelations were conceded to England at that favoured period, as at this. Mrs. Southcott had recently attained her five-and-twentieth blessed birthday, of whom a prophetic private in the Life Guards had heralded the sublime appearance by announcing that arrangements were made for the swallowing up of London and Westminster. Even the Cock-lane ghost had been laid only a round dozen of years, after rapping out its messages, as the spirits of this very year last past (supernaturally deficient in originality) rapped out theirs. Mere messages in the earthly order of events had lately come to the English Crown and People, from a congress of British subjects in America: which, strange to relate, have proved more important to the human race than any communications yet received through any of the chickens of the Cock-lane brood.
domestication: 公元一千七百七十五年。那是个受宠的年代，英国人得到了神启，就像今天一样。索斯科特夫人最近刚刚过了二十五岁大寿，真是个吉祥的日子。一名禁卫军中的下士预言，伦敦和威斯敏斯特将被吞没，预示着她的神圣降临。至于考克巷的鬼魂，也仅仅在十二年前才被平息，它曾通过敲击传递信息。说来奇怪，去年那些超自然界的灵魂所传递的信息，和当年考克巷的小鸡们一样，缺乏新意。然而，不久前，英王和臣民却收到了来自美洲英国殖民地代表大会的消息，这些消息对人类的意义，远比任何一只考克巷小鸡带来的启示重要得多。
foreignization: 那是公元一七七五年。在那个受宠的时期，英国得到了神灵的启示，就像现在一样。索斯科特太太最近刚刚度过了她二十五岁的幸福生日，一位生命卫队中的先知士兵曾预告了她崇高的出现，宣称已安排好吞没伦敦和威斯敏斯特。就连考克巷的鬼魂在敲出它的信息后，也只是在十二年前被平息，而今年刚过去的幽灵（在超自然方面缺乏独创性）也敲出了它们的信息。最近，英王和人民从美洲的一群英国臣民的会议中收到了一些地面上的信息：奇怪的是，这些信息对人类的重要性超过了通过考克巷那群幽灵中的任何一个所收到的任何通信。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was the year of Our Lord one thousand seven hundred and seventy-five. Spiritual revelations were conceded to England at that favoured period, as at this. Mrs. Southcott had recently attained her five-and-twentieth blessed birthday, of whom a prophetic private in the Life Guards had heralded the sublime appearance by announcing that arrangements were made for the swallowing up of London and Westminster. Even the Cock-lane ghost had been laid only a round dozen of years, after rapping out its messages, as the spirits of this very year last past (supernaturally deficient in originality) rapped out theirs. Mere messages in the earthly order of events had lately come to the English Crown and People, from a congress of British subjects in America: which, strange to relate, have proved more important to the human race than any communications yet received through any of the chickens of the Cock-lane brood.

公元一千七百七十五年。那是个受宠的年代，英国人得到了神启，就像今天一样。索斯科特夫人最近刚刚过了二十五岁大寿，真是个吉祥的日子。一名禁卫军中的下士预言，伦敦和威斯敏斯特将被吞没，预示着她的神圣降临。至于考克巷的鬼魂，也仅仅在十二年前才被平息，它曾通过敲击传递信息。说来奇怪，去年那些超自然界的灵魂所传递的信息，和当年考克巷的小鸡们一样，缺乏新意。然而，不久前，英王和臣民却收到了来自美洲英国殖民地代表大会的消息，这些消息对人类的意义，远比任何一只考克巷小鸡带来的启示重要得多。

It was the year of Our Lord one thousand seven hundred and seventy-five. Spiritual revelations were conceded to England at that favoured period, as at this. Mrs. Southcott had recently attained her five-and-twentieth blessed birthday, of whom a prophetic private in the Life Guards had heralded the sublime appearance by announcing that arrangements were made for the swallowing up of London and Westminster. Even the Cock-lane ghost had been laid only a round dozen of years, after rapping out its messages, as the spirits of this very year last past (supernaturally deficient in originality) rapped out theirs. Mere messages in the earthly order of events had lately come to the English Crown and People, from a congress of British subjects in America: which, strange to relate, have proved more important to the human race than any communications yet received through any of the chickens of the Cock-lane brood.

那是公元一七七五年。在那个受宠的时期，英国得到了神灵的启示，就像现在一样。索斯科特太太最近刚刚度过了她二十五岁的幸福生日，一位生命卫队中的先知士兵曾预告了她崇高的出现，宣称已安排好吞没伦敦和威斯敏斯特。就连考克巷的鬼魂在敲出它的信息后，也只是在十二年前被平息，而今年刚过去的幽灵（在超自然方面缺乏独创性）也敲出了它们的信息。最近，英王和人民从美洲的一群英国臣民的会议中收到了一些地面上的信息：奇怪的是，这些信息对人类的重要性超过了通过考克巷那群幽灵中的任何一个所收到的任何通信。

source: “Not at all, but I hope to know it better. I am so profoundly interested in its miserable inhabitants.”
domestication: 哪里哪里，但我希望能更多地了解它。我对那里的可怜人真是太感兴趣了。
foreignization: “一点也不，但我希望更了解它。我对它的悲惨居民非常感兴趣。”

messages_foreignization.content:

“Not at all, but I hope to know it better. I am so profoundly interested in its miserable inhabitants.”
“一点也不，但我希望更了解它。我对它的悲惨居民非常感兴趣。”

source: There were three rooms on a floor, and, the doors by which they communicated being put open that the air might pass freely through them all, Mr. Lorry, smilingly observant of that fanciful resemblance which he detected all around him, walked from one to another. The first was the best room, and in it were Lucie’s birds, and flowers, and books, and desk, and work-table, and box of water-colours; the second was the Doctor’s consulting-room, used also as the dining-room; the third, changingly speckled by the rustle of the plane-tree in the yard, was the Doctor’s bedroom, and there, in a corner, stood the disused shoemaker’s bench and tray of tools, much as it had stood on the fifth floor of the dismal house by the wine-shop, in the suburb of Saint Antoine in Paris.
domestication: 这层楼上有三间房，为了能让空气流通，门都敞开着。洛里先生边走边笑着，留意着周围这些奇妙的相似之处。第一间是最好的房间，里面放着露西的鸟儿、花、书、书桌、工作台和水彩颜料盒；第二间是医生的诊疗室，也兼作餐厅；第三间因院子里梧桐树的沙沙声而时明时暗，这是医生的卧室，角落里摆着那张废弃的鞋匠工作台和工具盘，就像当年在巴黎圣安托万郊区那间阴暗的五楼房间里一样。
foreignization: 有一层楼上有三间房间，为了使空气能够自由流通，这些房间之间的门都敞开着，洛里先生微笑着观察着在他周围发现的这种奇特的相似之处，从一间走到另一间。第一间是最好的房间，里面放着露西的鸟儿、花朵、书籍、书桌、工作台和水彩颜料盒；第二间是医生的诊疗室，也兼作餐厅；第三间因院子里梧桐树的沙沙声而时有时无地斑驳着，这是医生的卧室，在一个角落里，摆着那个废弃不用的鞋匠工作台和工具盘，就像它曾经在巴黎圣安托万郊区那座阴郁房屋的五楼所摆放的一样。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

There were three rooms on a floor, and, the doors by which they communicated being put open that the air might pass freely through them all, Mr. Lorry, smilingly observant of that fanciful resemblance which he detected all around him, walked from one to another. The first was the best room, and in it were Lucie’s birds, and flowers, and books, and desk, and work-table, and box of water-colours; the second was the Doctor’s consulting-room, used also as the dining-room; the third, changingly speckled by the rustle of the plane-tree in the yard, was the Doctor’s bedroom, and there, in a corner, stood the disused shoemaker’s bench and tray of tools, much as it had stood on the fifth floor of the dismal house by the wine-shop, in the suburb of Saint Antoine in Paris.

source: “And I hear them!” he added again, after a peal of thunder. “Here they come, fast, fierce, and furious!”
domestication: “我听见了！”他话音刚落，一阵雷声轰鸣而至。“他们来了，来得又快又猛，势不可挡！”
foreignization: “我听见他们了！”他再次补充道，在一阵雷鸣之后。“他们来了，快速、猛烈、狂怒！”

source: “And then breakfast, sir? Yes, sir. That way, sir, if you please. Show Concord! Gentleman’s valise and hot water to Concord. Pull off gentleman’s boots in Concord. (You will find a fine sea-coal fire, sir.) Fetch barber to Concord. Stir about there, now, for Concord!”
domestication: 然后是早餐，先生？好的，先生。这边请，先生。带客人去康科德！客人的手提箱和热水也送到康科德。在康科德帮客人脱靴子。（您会发现那里有旺旺的煤炭火，先生。）请理发师去康科德。快点儿，为了康科德，动起来！
foreignization: “然后是早餐，先生？是的，先生。如果您愿意，请往这边走，先生。带康科德！先生的行李箱和热水送到康科德。在康科德帮先生脱靴子。（您会发现那里有一个很好的海煤火，先生。）叫理发师到康科德。现在为康科德快点动起来！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“And then breakfast, sir? Yes, sir. That way, sir, if you please. Show Concord! Gentleman’s valise and hot water to Concord. Pull off gentleman’s boots in Concord. (You will find a fine sea-coal fire, sir.) Fetch barber to Concord. Stir about there, now, for Concord!”

source: “To the Devil with all that!” interrupted the other, rolling his eye over the landscape. “I go through no streets and past no fountains. Well?”
domestication: “这些破事儿，见鬼去吧！”那人打断道，眼睛扫了一圈四周的景色。“我可没走过什么街道，也没经过什么喷泉。怎么样？”
foreignization: “把这些都见鬼去吧！”另一个人打断道，他的眼睛扫过风景。“我可没走过什么街道，也没经过什么喷泉。好吧？”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“To the Devil with all that!” interrupted the other, rolling his eye over the landscape. “I go through no streets and past no fountains. Well?”
“这些破事儿，见鬼去吧！”那人打断道，眼睛扫了一圈四周的景色。“我可没走过什么街道，也没经过什么喷泉。怎么样？”

messages_foreignization.content:

“To the Devil with all that!” interrupted the other, rolling his eye over the landscape. “I go through no streets and past no fountains. Well?”
“把这些都见鬼去吧！”另一个人打断道，他的眼睛扫过风景。“我可没走过什么街道，也没经过什么喷泉。好吧？”

source: The mender of roads went home, with the dust going on before him according to the set of the wind, and was soon at the fountain, squeezing himself in among the lean kine brought there to drink, and appearing even to whisper to them in his whispering to all the village. When the village had taken its poor supper, it did not creep to bed, as it usually did, but came out of doors again, and remained there. A curious contagion of whispering was upon it, and also, when it gathered together at the fountain in the dark, another curious contagion of looking expectantly at the sky in one direction only. Monsieur Gabelle, chief functionary of the place, became uneasy; went out on his house-top alone, and looked in that direction too; glanced down from behind his chimneys at the darkening faces by the fountain below, and sent word to the sacristan who kept the keys of the church, that there might be need to ring the tocsin by-and-bye.
domestication: 修路的汉子回家了，尘土在他前面随风飘扬。他很快就到了村里的喷泉边，挤在那些来喝水的瘦骨嶙峋的牛群中，似乎还在对它们耳语，其实是在跟全村人悄悄说话。等村里人吃过那顿简陋的晚餐后，并没有像往常一样早早地钻进被窝，而是又出了门，在外面待着。村里弥漫着一种奇怪的低语，当大家聚到喷泉边时，还有一种奇怪的现象，就是所有人都朝着一个方向望天。镇上的头面人物加贝尔先生坐不住了，独自上了自家屋顶，也朝那个方向看了看；又从烟囱后面往下瞥了一眼喷泉旁越来越阴暗的脸庞，便派人告诉教堂的司事，说不定过会儿得敲响警钟。
foreignization: 修路的人回家了，灰尘随着风的方向在他前面飘扬，他很快就到了喷泉边，挤在被带到那里饮水的瘦牛中间，甚至似乎在对他所经过的整个村庄低语时也在对它们低语。当村庄吃完可怜的晚餐后，并没有像往常那样爬到床上去，而是再次走出门，在外面停留。一种奇怪的低语传染给了整个村庄，而且，当他们在黑暗中聚集在喷泉旁时，还有一种奇怪的传染，只朝一个方向期待地望着天空。加贝尔先生，这个地方的主要官员，变得不安；独自一人走上他的屋顶，也朝那个方向看；从烟囱后面向下瞥了一眼下面喷泉旁越来越暗的脸庞，并派人通知保管教堂钥匙的司事，可能需要稍后敲响警钟。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The mender of roads went home, with the dust going on before him according to the set of the wind, and was soon at the fountain, squeezing himself in among the lean kine brought there to drink, and appearing even to whisper to them in his whispering to all the village. When the village had taken its poor supper, it did not creep to bed, as it usually did, but came out of doors again, and remained there. A curious contagion of whispering was upon it, and also, when it gathered together at the fountain in the dark, another curious contagion of looking expectantly at the sky in one direction only. Monsieur Gabelle, chief functionary of the place, became uneasy; went out on his house-top alone, and looked in that direction too; glanced down from behind his chimneys at the darkening faces by the fountain below, and sent word to the sacristan who kept the keys of the church, that there might be need to ring the tocsin by-and-bye.

修路的汉子回家了，尘土在他前面随风飘扬。他很快就到了村里的喷泉边，挤在那些来喝水的瘦骨嶙峋的牛群中，似乎还在对它们耳语，其实是在跟全村人悄悄说话。等村里人吃过那顿简陋的晚餐后，并没有像往常一样早早地钻进被窝，而是又出了门，在外面待着。村里弥漫着一种奇怪的低语，当大家聚到喷泉边时，还有一种奇怪的现象，就是所有人都朝着一个方向望天。镇上的头面人物加贝尔先生坐不住了，独自上了自家屋顶，也朝那个方向看了看；又从烟囱后面往下瞥了一眼喷泉旁越来越阴暗的脸庞，便派人告诉教堂的司事，说不定过会儿得敲响警钟。

source: Accordingly, when Mr. Lorry called that night as late as ten o’clock, Mr. Stryver, among a quantity of books and papers littered out for the purpose, seemed to have nothing less on his mind than the subject of the morning. He even showed surprise when he saw Mr. Lorry, and was altogether in an absent and preoccupied state.
domestication: 于是，那天晚上，洛里先生十点钟才来拜访时，斯特瑞佛先生正埋头在一堆书本和文件中，似乎对上午的事半点不在意。看到洛里先生，他甚至显得有些惊讶，整个人心不在焉，一副魂不守舍的样子。
foreignization: 因此，当洛里先生那天晚上十点钟来拜访时，斯特莱弗先生在一堆为了这个目的散开的书籍和文件中，似乎根本没有把心思放在上午的话题上。他甚至看到洛里先生时显得很惊讶，并且整个人都处于心不在焉和心事重重的状态。

Accordingly, when Mr. Lorry called that night as late as ten o’clock, Mr. Stryver, among a quantity of books and papers littered out for the purpose, seemed to have nothing less on his mind than the subject of the morning. 因此，当洛里先生那天晚上十点钟来拜访时，斯特莱弗先生在一堆为了这个目的散开的书籍和文件中，似乎根本没有把心思放在上午的话题上。他甚至看到洛里先生时显得很惊讶，并且整个人都处于心不在焉和心事重重的状态。

source: “It is the law,” remarked the ancient clerk, turning his surprised spectacles upon him. “It is the law.”
domestication: “这是规矩。”老文书抬起头，眼镜后的眼睛透着惊讶，“这是规矩。”
foreignization: “这是法律的规定，”那位老职员说道，吃惊地把眼镜转向他。“这是法律的规定。”

source: The hands of the clock verging upon the hour of closing the Bank, there was a general set of the current of talkers past Mr. Lorry’s desk. He held the letter out inquiringly; and Monseigneur looked at it, in the person of this plotting and indignant refugee; and Monseigneur looked at it in the person of that plotting and indignant refugee; and This, That, and The Other, all had something disparaging to say, in French or in English, concerning the Marquis who was not to be found.
domestication: 银行快关门了，大钟的指针快指向了闭门的时间，这时众人纷纷涌向洛里先生的办公桌前，议论纷纷。洛里先生手里举着信，疑惑地望着；这位阴谋策划、愤愤不平的难民先生看了看信；那位同样阴谋策划、愤愤不平的难民先生也看了看信；这个、那个、还有那个，都对找不到的侯爵大人说三道四，有的用法语，有的用英语，总之没一句好话。
foreignization: 时钟的指针接近银行关门的时间，谈话的人流纷纷从劳里先生的办公桌旁经过。他疑惑地伸出信；这位阴谋策划且愤怒的难民扮演的老爷看了看信；而那位阴谋策划且愤怒的难民扮演的老爷也看了看信；还有这个人、那个人以及另一个人，都用法语或英语说了一些贬低找不到的侯爵的话。

source: But, though the bank was almost always with him, and though the coach (in a confused way, like the presence of pain under an opiate) was always with him, there was another current of impression that never ceased to run, all through the night. He was on his way to dig some one out of a grave.
domestication: 但是，尽管银行几乎总是站在他这边，尽管马车像鸦片下隐隐作痛的感觉一样始终伴随着他，整个夜晚还有一股思绪不断涌现。他好像要去挖出某个人的坟墓。
foreignization: 但是，尽管银行几乎总是与他同在，尽管马车（像麻醉药下的疼痛一样模糊地）也总是与他同在，还有一个印象的潮流从未停止流动，整个晚上都在持续。他正前往挖掘某人出坟墓的路上。

source: “Pretty witness,” he muttered, looking down into his glass. “I have had enough of witnesses to-day and to-night; who’s your pretty witness?”

foreignization: “漂亮的证人，”他喃喃道，低头看着他的酒杯。“我今天晚上已经受够了证人；你的漂亮证人是谁？”

source: Dress was the one unfailing talisman and charm used for keeping all things in their places. Everybody was dressed for a Fancy Ball that was never to leave off. From the Palace of the Tuileries, through Monseigneur and the whole Court, through the Chambers, the Tribunals of Justice, and all society (except the scarecrows), the Fancy Ball descended to the Common Executioner: who, in pursuance of the charm, was required to officiate “frizzled, powdered, in a gold-laced coat, pumps, and white silk stockings.” At the gallows and the wheel—the axe was a rarity—Monsieur Paris, as it was the episcopal mode among his brother Professors of the provinces, Monsieur Orleans, and the rest, to call him, presided in this dainty dress. And who among the company at Monseigneur’s reception in that seventeen hundred and eightieth year of our Lord, could possibly doubt, that a system rooted in a frizzled hangman, powdered, gold-laced, pumped, and white-silk stockinged, would see the very stars out!
domestication: 衣服是保持一切秩序不变的灵丹妙药。所有人都像是参加了一场永不散场的化装舞会。从杜伊勒里宫，到蒙塞尼奥尔和整个宫廷，再到议会、法庭和整个社会（除了那些稻草人），这场化装舞会一直延续到了刽子手那里：为了维持这股魔力，他必须“卷发、扑粉，穿着金色饰边的外套、皮鞋和白丝袜”来执行任务。在绞刑架和车裂刑具前——斧头倒是少见得很——巴黎先生，就像他在外省的同行们，如奥尔良先生等，按照主教的习惯称呼他那样，穿着这身精致的衣服主持行刑。谁又能怀疑，在我们主的1780年，蒙塞尼奥尔接待会上的宾客们，会对这样一个制度有丝毫质疑呢？这个制度以一个卷发、扑粉、穿金线衣、皮鞋和白丝袜的刽子手为基础，难道它不能与星辰共存吗！
foreignization: 服装是保持一切事物各安其位的唯一不变的符咒和魅力。每个人都打扮得像是参加一场永远不会结束的化装舞会。从杜伊勒里宫，经过公爵大人和整个宫廷，通过议会、法庭和整个社会（除了稻草人），这场化装舞会一直延续到普通的行刑者：他按照这种魅力的要求，必须以“卷发、扑粉、穿着金边制服、拖鞋和白色丝绸长袜”的形象执行职务。在绞刑架和车裂刑——斧头是罕见的——巴黎先生，正如他在各省的兄弟同行们，包括奥尔良先生和其他人，按照主教的称谓习惯称呼他那样，在这种精致的装扮下主持行刑。而在我们主公元一七八〇年的那一年，谁能在蒙塞尼耶的招待会上怀疑，一个根植于卷发、扑粉、金边装饰、穿拖鞋和白色丝绸长袜的刽子手的制度，能够见证星星的消逝呢！

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Dress was the one unfailing talisman and charm used for keeping all things in their places. Everybody was dressed for a Fancy Ball that was never to leave off. From the Palace of the Tuileries, through Monseigneur and the whole Court, through the Chambers, the Tribunals of Justice, and all society (except the scarecrows), the Fancy Ball descended to the Common Executioner: who, in pursuance of the charm, was required to officiate “frizzled, powdered, in a gold-laced coat, pumps, and white silk stockings.” At the gallows and the wheel—the axe was a rarity—Monsieur Paris, as it was the episcopal mode among his brother Professors of the provinces, Monsieur Orleans, and the rest, to call him, presided in this dainty dress. And who among the company at Monseigneur’s reception in that seventeen hundred and eightieth year of our Lord, could possibly doubt, that a system rooted in a frizzled hangman, powdered, gold-laced, pumped, and white-silk stockinged, would see the very stars out!

衣服是保持一切秩序不变的灵丹妙药。所有人都像是参加了一场永不散场的化装舞会。从杜伊勒里宫，到蒙塞尼奥尔和整个宫廷，再到议会、法庭和整个社会（除了那些稻草人），这场化装舞会一直延续到了刽子手那里：为了维持这股魔力，他必须“卷发、扑粉，穿着金色饰边的外套、皮鞋和白丝袜”来执行任务。在绞刑架和车裂刑具前——斧头倒是少见得很——巴黎先生，就像他在外省的同行们，如奥尔良先生等，按照主教的习惯称呼他那样，穿着这身精致的衣服主持行刑。谁又能怀疑，在我们主的1780年，蒙塞尼奥尔接待会上的宾客们，会对这样一个制度有丝毫质疑呢？这个制度以一个卷发、扑粉、穿金线衣、皮鞋和白丝袜的刽子手为基础，难道它不能与星辰共存吗！

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Dress was the one unfailing talisman and charm used for keeping all things in their places. Everybody was dressed for a Fancy Ball that was never to leave off. From the Palace of the Tuileries, through Monseigneur and the whole Court, through the Chambers, the Tribunals of Justice, and all society (except the scarecrows), the Fancy Ball descended to the Common Executioner: who, in pursuance of the charm, was required to officiate “frizzled, powdered, in a gold-laced coat, pumps, and white silk stockings.” At the gallows and the wheel—the axe was a rarity—Monsieur Paris, as it was the episcopal mode among his brother Professors of the provinces, Monsieur Orleans, and the rest, to call him, presided in this dainty dress. And who among the company at Monseigneur’s reception in that seventeen hundred and eightieth year of our Lord, could possibly doubt, that a system rooted in a frizzled hangman, powdered, gold-laced, pumped, and white-silk stockinged, would see the very stars out!

服装是保持一切事物各安其位的唯一不变的符咒和魅力。每个人都打扮得像是参加一场永远不会结束的化装舞会。从杜伊勒里宫，经过公爵大人和整个宫廷，通过议会、法庭和整个社会（除了稻草人），这场化装舞会一直延续到普通的行刑者：他按照这种魅力的要求，必须以“卷发、扑粉、穿着金边制服、拖鞋和白色丝绸长袜”的形象执行职务。在绞刑架和车裂刑——斧头是罕见的——巴黎先生，正如他在各省的兄弟同行们，包括奥尔良先生和其他人，按照主教的称谓习惯称呼他那样，在这种精致的装扮下主持行刑。而在我们主公元一七八〇年的那一年，谁能在蒙塞尼耶的招待会上怀疑，一个根植于卷发、扑粉、金边装饰、穿拖鞋和白色丝绸长袜的刽子手的制度，能够见证星星的消逝呢！

source: “Be comforted!” he said, “I am not worth such feeling, Miss Manette. An hour or two hence, and the low companions and low habits that I scorn but yield to, will render me less worth such tears as those, than any wretch who creeps along the streets. Be comforted! But, within myself, I shall always be, towards you, what I am now, though outwardly I shall be what you have heretofore seen me. The last supplication but one I make to you, is, that you will believe this of me.”
domestication: “别难过！”他说，“我根本不值得你这样伤心，曼内特小姐。再过一两个时辰，那些我鄙视却屈从的下流朋友和恶习，就会让我比街上任何一个爬行的可怜虫还要不值你的眼泪。别伤心了！不过，在我心里，我永远都会像现在这样对你，即使表面上我还是那个你以前见到的样子。我最后的请求，就是希望你能相信我说的话。”
foreignization: “宽慰一下吧！”他说，“我不值得你有这种感情，曼内特小姐。再过一两个小时，那些我鄙视却又屈从的卑劣同伴和恶习，会使我变得比沿街爬行的任何一个可怜虫更不值得你流这样的泪。宽慰一下吧！但是，在我的内心深处，我将永远对你保持现在这种情感，尽管表面上我会是你以前看到的那个样子。我向你提出的倒数第二个请求是，希望你能相信我对你说的话。”

source: “I speak, miss, of twenty years ago. He married—an English lady—and I was one of the trustees. His affairs, like the affairs of many other French gentlemen and French families, were entirely in Tellson’s hands. In a similar way I am, or I have been, trustee of one kind or other for scores of our customers. These are mere business relations, miss; there is no friendship in them, no particular interest, nothing like sentiment. I have passed from one to another, in the course of my business life, just as I pass from one of our customers to another in the course of my business day; in short, I have no feelings; I am a mere machine. To go on—”
domestication: 我说的是二十年前的事了。他娶了个英国太太，而我是他的信托人之一。就像很多法国绅士和家庭一样，他的所有事务都由泰勒森银行打理。我也以各种名目的信托人的身份，为我们的许多客户效劳。这些都只是生意上的关系，小姐，没有友情，没有特别的兴趣，更没有什么感情。在我的职业生涯中，我从一个客户转到另一个客户，就像我每天工作时从一个客户转到另一个客户一样；说到底，我就是个机器。接着说——
foreignization: “我说的是二十年前的事。他娶了一位英国女士——而我是其中一位受托人。他的事务，像许多其他法国绅士和法国家庭的事务一样，完全由泰尔森银行管理。以类似的方式，我是，或曾是，我们许多客户的某种类型的受托人。这些只是业务关系，小姐；其中没有友谊，没有特别的兴趣，没有什么感情色彩。在我的职业生涯中，我从一个客户转到另一个客户，就像在我的工作日中从一个客户转到另一个客户一样；简而言之，我没有感情；我只是个机器。继续说——”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

source: The garret, built to be a depository for firewood and the like, was dim and dark: for, the window of dormer shape, was in truth a door in the roof, with a little crane over it for the hoisting up of stores from the street: unglazed, and closing up the middle in two pieces, like any other door of French construction. To exclude the cold, one half of this door was fast closed, and the other was opened but a very little way. Such a scanty portion of light was admitted through these means, that it was difficult, on first coming in, to see anything; and long habit alone could have slowly formed in any one, the ability to do any work requiring nicety in such obscurity. Yet, work of that kind was being done in the garret; for, with his back towards the door, and his face towards the window where the keeper of the wine-shop stood looking at him, a white-haired man sat on a low bench, stooping forward and very busy, making shoes.
domestication: 阁楼原是堆放柴火之类东西的地方，阴暗得很。因为那扇阁楼形状的窗，其实是屋顶上的一扇门，上面还有一个小小的吊车，用来从街上吊东西上来。窗户没有玻璃，中间分成两半，像法国门那样关合。为了挡寒风，一半门紧紧关着，另一半也只是微微开了一条缝。这样一来，透进来的光就少得可怜，刚进来时几乎什么都看不见；只有长年累月的习惯，才能让人慢慢适应这种昏暗，做起精细活来。然而，在这阁楼上却有人在做这样的细活。一个白发老人背对着门，脸朝着窗户坐着，酒铺老板站在窗外看着他。老人弓着腰坐在低矮的板凳上，忙忙碌碌地做着鞋。
foreignization: 阁楼被建来存放柴火之类的东西，昏暗而阴沉：因为，这个阁楼窗其实是屋顶上的一扇门，上面有一个小起重机，可以从街上吊起物品：没有玻璃，中间分成两半，像法国其他门一样关闭。为了挡风避寒，这扇门的一半紧紧关着，另一半也只开了一点点。通过这些手段，几乎没有什么光线能透进来，刚进来的人都很难看清什么东西；只有长期的习惯才能慢慢培养出在这样昏暗的环境中进行精细工作的能力。然而，在这个阁楼里正有人在做这样的工作；因为，一个白发老人背对着门，面朝着窗户坐在一张低矮的凳子上，身体前倾，非常忙碌地做着鞋，而酒铺老板则站在窗口看着他。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

The garret, built to be a depository for firewood and the like, was dim and dark: for, the window of dormer shape, was in truth a door in the roof, with a little crane over it for the hoisting up of stores from the street: unglazed, and closing up the middle in two pieces, like any other door of French construction. To exclude the cold, one half of this door was fast closed, and the other was opened but a very little way. Such a scanty portion of light was admitted through these means, that it was difficult, on first coming in, to see anything; and long habit alone could have slowly formed in any one, the ability to do any work requiring nicety in such obscurity. Yet, work of that kind was being done in the garret; for, with his back towards the door, and his face towards the window where the keeper of the wine-shop stood looking at him, a white-haired man sat on a low bench, stooping forward and very busy, making shoes.

source: It was characteristic of this lady (as of some other people before her time and since) that whenever her original proposition was questioned, she exaggerated it.
domestication: 这女人有个特点，和她之前之后的某些人一样，每当她的原话受到质疑时，她就会夸大其词。
foreignization: 这位女士的特点是（在她之前和之后的一些人也是如此），每当她的原命题受到质疑时，她就会夸大其词。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was characteristic of this lady (as of some other people before her time and since) that whenever her original proposition was questioned, she exaggerated it.
这女人有个特点，和她之前之后的某些人一样，每当她的原话受到质疑时，她就会夸大其词。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was characteristic of this lady (as of some other people before her time and since) that whenever her original proposition was questioned, she exaggerated it.
这位女士的特点是（在她之前和之后的一些人也是如此），每当她的原命题受到质疑时，她就会夸大其词。

source: He was not long in discovering that it was worse than useless to speak to him, since, on being pressed, he became worried. He abandoned that attempt on the first day, and resolved merely to keep himself always before him, as a silent protest against the delusion into which he had fallen, or was falling. He remained, therefore, in his seat near the window, reading and writing, and expressing in as many pleasant and natural ways as he could think of, that it was a free place.
domestication: 他很快就发现，跟那人说话根本是白费力气，因为一逼问，他就显得焦虑不安。第一天，他就放弃了这个尝试，决定只是默默地待在他面前，无声地抗议他陷入的，或者正在陷入的幻觉。于是，他一直坐在窗边，看书写字，尽量用各种愉快自然的方式表现出这地方是自由的。
foreignization: 他很快发现，和他说话不仅是徒劳无功的，而且，当他被逼问时，他会变得焦虑。他在第一天就放弃了这种尝试，决定只是始终让自己出现在他面前，作为一种对他所陷入或正在陷入的幻觉的无声抗议。因此，他仍然坐在靠近窗户的位置上，阅读和写作，并尽可能地以愉快和自然的方式表达这是一个自由的地方。

source: Naturally struck by the disagreeable word, Charles Darnay requested the speaker to take notice that he was a free traveller and French citizen, in charge of an escort which the disturbed state of the country had imposed upon him, and which he had paid for.
domestication: 查尔斯·达尔内一听这话就来气了，他赶紧跟那人说道，自己是自由之身，也是法国公民，因为国内局势动荡，不得不带上了一个护卫，这钱可是他自己掏的。
foreignization: 查尔斯·达奈自然被这个不悦的字眼所触动，请求说话者注意，他是一位自由旅行者和法国公民，负责一支由于国家动荡局势而强加给他的护送队伍，并且他已经为此支付了费用。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Naturally struck by the disagreeable word, Charles Darnay requested the speaker to take notice that he was a free traveller and French citizen, in charge of an escort which the disturbed state of the country had imposed upon him, and which he had paid for.
查尔斯·达尔内一听这话就来气了，他赶紧跟那人说道，自己是自由之身，也是法国公民，因为国内局势动荡，不得不带上了一个护卫，这钱可是他自己掏的。

messages_foreignization.content:

Naturally struck by the disagreeable word, Charles Darnay requested the speaker to take notice that he was a free traveller and French citizen, in charge of an escort which the disturbed state of the country had imposed upon him, and which he had paid for.
查尔斯·达奈自然被这个不悦的字眼所触动，请求说话者注意，他是一位自由旅行者和法国公民，负责一支由于国家动荡局势而强加给他的护送队伍，并且他已经为此支付了费用。

source: “I do not show the soldiers that I recognise the tall man; he does not show the soldiers that he recognises me; we do it, and we know it, with our eyes. ‘Come on!’ says the chief of that company, pointing to the village, ‘bring him fast to his tomb!’ and they bring him faster. I follow. His arms are swelled because of being bound so tight, his wooden shoes are large and clumsy, and he is lame. Because he is lame, and consequently slow, they drive him with their guns—like this!”
domestication: 我故意不向士兵们表露我认出了那个高个子男人；他也不向士兵们表露他认出了我；我们心里都明白，只用眼神交流。带队的军官指着村子说：“走吧！快把他带到坟墓里去！”他们就加快了速度。我跟在后面。他的胳膊因为绑得太紧而肿了起来，脚上的木鞋又大又笨重，还一瘸一拐的。因为他腿脚不便，行动缓慢，士兵们就用枪驱赶着他——就像这样！
foreignization: 我不向士兵们表现出我认出了那个高个子男人；他也不向士兵们表现出他认出了我；我们用眼神交流，心照不宣。“走吧！”那队的头领说，指着村庄，“快把他带到他的坟墓里去！”他们走得更快了。我跟着。他的手臂因为被绑得太紧而肿胀，木鞋又大又笨重，他还瘸了。因为他瘸了，所以行动缓慢，他们就用枪驱赶他——就像这样！

source: A murky red and yellow sky, and a rising mist from the Seine, denoted the approach of darkness. It was almost dark when they arrived at the Bank. The stately residence of Monseigneur was altogether blighted and deserted. Above a heap of dust and ashes in the court, ran the letters: National Property. Republic One and Indivisible. Liberty, Equality, Fraternity, or Death!
domestication: 天色昏暗，红黄交织，塞纳河上雾气蒸腾，昭示着夜幕的降临。他们赶到银行时，天几乎全黑了。蒙斯居府邸昔日的辉煌已荡然无存，如今只剩下一片荒凉。庭院中尘土和灰烬堆成小山，上面赫然写着：国民财产。共和国不可分割。自由、平等、博爱，否则死亡！
foreignization: 一片昏暗的红黄色天空，塞纳河上弥漫着升起的雾气，表明夜幕即将降临。当他们到达银行时，天几乎全黑了。蒙塞涅尔先生的庄重住所完全荒废了，空无一人。在庭院中一堆尘土和灰烬之上，刻着几个字：国家财产。不可分割的共和国。自由、平等、博爱，或死亡！

Liberty, Equality, Fraternity, or Death!

source: As to the strength of his case, he had not a doubt about it, but clearly saw his way to the verdict. Argued with the jury on substantial worldly grounds—the only grounds ever worth taking into account—it was a plain case, and had not a weak spot in it. He called himself for the plaintiff, there was no getting over his evidence, the counsel for the defendant threw up his brief, and the jury did not even turn to consider. After trying it, Stryver, C. J., was satisfied that no plainer case could be.
domestication: 对于他的案子有多硬，他是一点也不怀疑，心里头明镜似的，胜券在握。拿事实说话，用人间的道理来说——这才是唯一值得考虑的依据——这案子明摆着，连个漏洞都没有。他自己出庭作原告，证据确凿，被告的律师都认输了，陪审团连商量都没商量，直接定了。斯特里弗大律师亲自审理后，更是觉得没有比这更清楚的案子了。
foreignization: 至于他的案件的力度，他毫不怀疑，但清楚地看到了通往判决的道路。以实质性的世俗理由与陪审团争论——这是唯一值得考虑的理由——这是一个明了的案件，没有弱点。他自己作为原告出庭，没有人能驳倒他的证据，被告的律师放弃了辩护，陪审团甚至没有转过身去讨论。在审理后，斯特里弗首席法官确信没有比这更明了的案件了。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

J., was satisfied that no plainer case could be.

source: At length, a person better informed on the merits of the case, tumbled against him, and from this person he learned that the funeral was the funeral of one Roger Cly.
domestication: 终于，他遇到了一个对这事儿比较了解的人，从这个人那儿，他才得知这葬礼原来是给一个叫罗杰·克利的人办的。
foreignization: 最后，一个更了解内情的人撞见了他，从这个人那里他得知葬礼是罗杰·克莱的葬礼。

source: “In her name, then, let it be done; I sanction it. But, I would not take it away while he was present. Let it be removed when he is not there; let him miss his old companion after an absence.”
domestication: 既然是她的意思，那就这么办吧，我同意。不过，他还在的时候，我可不想把东西拿走。等他不在了，再把它移走；让他回来后发现老伙伴不见了。
foreignization: 那么，以她的名义，就这样办吧；我同意。但是，当他在此时，我不会把它拿走。当他不在的时候，再把它移走；让他在离开后发现他旧日的伴侣不见了。

source: A large cask of wine had been dropped and broken, in the street. The accident had happened in getting it out of a cart; the cask had tumbled out with a run, the hoops had burst, and it lay on the stones just outside the door of the wine-shop, shattered like a walnut-shell.
domestication: 一桶大酒在街上摔了个粉碎。这事故是卸车时发生的，酒桶滚了出来，铁箍都崩开了，就像核桃壳一样，散落在酒店门口的石子上。
foreignization: 一大桶酒在街上掉下来摔破了。事故发生在从马车上卸下来的时候；酒桶滚了出来，铁箍崩裂，它就躺在酒馆门外的石头上，碎得像核桃壳一样。

source: “Courage, my dear Lucie,” said Mr. Lorry, as he raised her. “Courage, courage! So far all goes well with us—much, much better than it has of late gone with many poor souls. Cheer up, and have a thankful heart.”
domestication: “振作点，亲爱的露西，”洛里先生扶起她时说道，“振作点，振作点！到目前为止，咱们一切都好——比许多可怜人最近的境遇强多了。打起精神来，心存感激吧。”
foreignization: “勇敢点，亲爱的露西，”洛里先生一边扶起她一边说，“勇敢，勇敢！到目前为止，我们一切都很好——比许多可怜的人最近的境况好得多。振作起来，感恩在心。”

source: The chateau was left to itself to flame and burn. In the roaring and raging of the conflagration, a red-hot wind, driving straight from the infernal regions, seemed to be blowing the edifice away. With the rising and falling of the blaze, the stone faces showed as if they were in torment. When great masses of stone and timber fell, the face with the two dints in the nose became obscured: anon struggled out of the smoke again, as if it were the face of the cruel Marquis, burning at the stake and contending with the fire.
domestication: 城堡被大火吞噬，独自燃烧。火焰熊熊，仿佛一股从地狱吹来的炽热之风，要把这座建筑连根拔起。火光跳跃，石雕的脸庞似乎在痛苦中扭曲。当大块的石头和木材崩塌时，那个鼻梁有两道凹痕的脸便隐没在烟雾中；一会儿又挣扎着从烟雾中露出来，就像是那位残酷的侯爵在火刑柱上与烈焰抗争。
foreignization: 城堡被留给了自己燃烧。在大火的咆哮和肆虐中，一股似乎直从地狱吹来的炽热风似乎要把这座建筑吹走。随着火焰的起伏，石制的脸庞仿佛在痛苦中扭曲。当大块的石头和木头倒下时，那个鼻子上有两个凹陷的脸变得模糊不清：不久又从烟雾中挣扎出来，仿佛是那位残酷的侯爵在火刑柱上燃烧并与火斗争的脸。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The chateau was left to itself to flame and burn. In the roaring and raging of the conflagration, a red-hot wind, driving straight from the infernal regions, seemed to be blowing the edifice away. With the rising and falling of the blaze, the stone faces showed as if they were in torment. When great masses of stone and timber fell, the face with the two dints in the nose became obscured: anon struggled out of the smoke again, as if it were the face of the cruel Marquis, burning at the stake and contending with the fire.

With the rising and falling of the blaze, the stone faces showed as if they were in torment. When great masses of stone and timber fell, the face with the two dints in the nose became obscured: anon struggled out of the smoke again, as if it were the face of the cruel Marquis, burning at the stake and contending with the fire.
城堡被留给了自己燃烧。在大火的咆哮和肆虐中，一股似乎直从地狱吹来的炽热风似乎要把这座建筑吹走。随着火焰的起伏，石制的脸庞仿佛在痛苦中扭曲。当大块的石头和木头倒下时，那个鼻子上有两个凹陷的脸变得模糊不清：不久又从烟雾中挣扎出来，仿佛是那位残酷的侯爵在火刑柱上燃烧并与火斗争的脸。

source: His streaming white hair, his remarkable face, and the impetuous confidence of his manner, as he put the weapons aside like water, carried him in an instant to the heart of the concourse at the stone. For a few moments there was a pause, and a hurry, and a murmur, and the unintelligible sound of his voice; and then Mr. Lorry saw him, surrounded by all, and in the midst of a line of twenty men long, all linked shoulder to shoulder, and hand to shoulder, hurried out with cries of—“Live the Bastille prisoner! Help for the Bastille prisoner’s kindred in La Force! Room for the Bastille prisoner in front there! Save the prisoner Evrémonde at La Force!” and a thousand answering shouts.
domestication: 他那飘洒的白发，那张引人注目的脸庞，再加上他从容不迫地放下武器时流露出的豪迈自信，瞬间就让他成为了石头广场上众人关注的焦点。一时间，现场变得既紧张又热闹，人们纷纷议论，他的声音也变得模糊不清。接着，洛里先生看到他被人群围在中间，身后还跟着二十多个汉子，他们肩并肩、手挽手，高声呼喊着：“欢迎巴士底狱的囚犯！救救关在拉弗斯监狱的囚犯家属！给巴士底狱的囚犯让路！救救关在拉弗斯的埃弗蒙德！”一时间，四面八方都响起了回应的呼喊声。
foreignization: 他那飘扬的白发，他那引人注目的面孔，以及他像流水般放下武器时表现出的那种冲动的自信，使他在瞬间就成为石阵集会中心的人物。有那么片刻的停顿，随后是一阵忙乱，一阵低语，还有他那听不清的声音；接着，洛里先生看到他被众人围在中间，处在二十个肩并肩、手拉手的男人组成的一条长线的中央，被呼喊声簇拥着——“巴斯特尔囚犯万岁！帮助巴斯特尔囚犯的亲属在拉弗斯！给巴斯特尔囚犯前面让路！拯救拉弗斯的埃弗蒙德囚犯！”以及成千上万的回应欢呼。

source: “Five paces by four and a half, five paces by four and a half, five paces by four and a half.” The prisoner walked to and fro in his cell, counting its measurement, and the roar of the city arose like muffled drums with a wild swell of voices added to them. “He made shoes, he made shoes, he made shoes.” The prisoner counted the measurement again, and paced faster, to draw his mind with him from that latter repetition. “The ghosts that vanished when the wicket closed. There was one among them, the appearance of a lady dressed in black, who was leaning in the embrasure of a window, and she had a light shining upon her golden hair, and she looked like * * * * Let us ride on again, for God’s sake, through the illuminated villages with the people all awake! * * * * He made shoes, he made shoes, he made shoes. * * * * Five paces by four and a half.” With such scraps tossing and rolling upward from the depths of his mind, the prisoner walked faster and faster, obstinately counting and counting; and the roar of the city changed to this extent—that it still rolled in like muffled drums, but with the wail of voices that he knew, in the swell that rose above them.
domestication: “五步半，五步半，五步半。”囚犯在牢房里走来走去，量着房间的大小，而城市的喧嚣像闷鼓般回响，夹杂着此起彼伏的人声。“他做鞋，他做鞋，他做鞋。”囚犯又量了一遍，走得更快了，想让自己的思绪从这重复的话语中抽离出来。“那些鬼魂在栅门关闭时消失了。其中有一个，穿着黑衣的女子，她靠在窗台上，金色的头发在灯光下闪闪发光，她看起来像……咱们为了上帝，再骑马穿过那些灯火通明的村庄吧，那里的人们都在醒着！……他做鞋，他做鞋，他做鞋。……五步半。”随着这些片段从心底翻涌而出，囚犯越走越快，固执地数着，数着；而城市的喧嚣也变了，依旧像闷鼓般滚动，但其中夹杂着他熟悉的声音，此起彼伏，盖过了其他一切。
foreignization: “五步乘四步半，五步乘四步半，五步乘四步半。”囚犯在他的牢房里来回走动，数着它的尺寸，而城市的喧嚣声像闷鼓一样响起，夹杂着一阵阵狂野的人声。“他做鞋，他做鞋，他做鞋。”囚犯再次数着尺寸，加快了步伐，试图让自己的思绪从这后一句重复中抽离。“那些在门锁上消失的鬼魂。其中有一个穿着黑衣的女士，她靠在窗框里，有一束光照在她金色的头发上，她看起来像 * * * * 上帝保佑，让我们再次骑马穿过灯火通明的村庄，那里的人们都醒着！* * * * 他做鞋，他做鞋，他做鞋。* * * * 五步乘四步半。”带着这些碎片从他心灵深处翻腾而出，囚犯越走越快，执拗地数了又数；而城市的喧嚣声也发生了变化——它仍然像闷鼓一样滚滚而来，但在其之上，升起了他熟悉的声音的哀号。

source: “Keep near to me, Jacques Three,” cried Defarge; “and do you, Jacques One and Two, separate and put yourselves at the head of as many of these patriots as you can. Where is my wife?”
domestication: “靠拢点，三杰克。”德法热喊道；“你们俩，一杰克和二杰克，分开行动，尽可能多地带头领这些爱国者。我老婆去哪儿了？”
foreignization: “雅克三，靠近我一点，”德法日喊道；“你们，雅克一和二，分开来，尽可能多地带领这些爱国者。我的妻子在哪里？”

source: It had once been noted at the Bar, that while Mr. Stryver was a glib man, and an unscrupulous, and a ready, and a bold, he had not that faculty of extracting the essence from a heap of statements, which is among the most striking and necessary of the advocate’s accomplishments. But, a remarkable improvement came upon him as to this. The more business he got, the greater his power seemed to grow of getting at its pith and marrow; and however late at night he sat carousing with Sydney Carton, he always had his points at his fingers’ ends in the morning.
domestication: 有人曾说过，斯特莱弗先生虽然口才流利、胆大妄为、机敏狡猾，但却缺乏律师最为重要和必需的能力——从一堆陈述中提炼出精华。然而，他在这一点上有了显著的进步。他接的案子越多，似乎就越能抓住问题的核心。不管晚上和悉尼·卡顿喝到多晚，第二天早晨他总能把关键点记得清清楚楚。
foreignization: 曾经有人在法庭上指出，斯特莱弗先生虽然口齿伶俐、不择手段、反应敏捷、胆大妄为，但他缺乏从一堆陈述中提炼精华的能力，而这是律师最显著和必要的才能之一。然而，他在这一点上有了显著的进步。他承接的业务越多，似乎就越能抓住问题的核心；无论他与西德尼·卡顿夜坐狂欢到多晚，第二天早晨他总是能对案件的关键点了如指掌。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It had once been noted at the Bar, that while Mr. Stryver was a glib man, and an unscrupulous, and a ready, and a bold, he had not that faculty of extracting the essence from a heap of statements, which is among the most striking and necessary of the advocate’s accomplishments. But, a remarkable improvement came upon him as to this. The more business he got, the greater his power seemed to grow of getting at its pith and marrow; and however late at night he sat carousing with Sydney Carton, he always had his points at his fingers’ ends in the morning.

But, a remarkable improvement came upon him as to this. source: “Not at all,” retained the ancient clerk. “Speak well of the law. Take care of your chest and voice, my good friend, and leave the law to take care of itself. I give you that advice.”
domestication: “哪里哪里，”老文书官说，“法律自有公道。好朋友啊，你还是多关心关心自己的身体和嗓子吧，法律嘛，它自己会照顾好自己的。我就是这个意思。”
foreignization: “一点也不，”老职员说。“说法律的好话。照顾好你的胸部和声音，我的好朋友，让法律自己照顾自己。我给你这个建议。”

source: In such risings of fire and risings of sea—the firm earth shaken by the rushes of an angry ocean which had now no ebb, but was always on the flow, higher and higher, to the terror and wonder of the beholders on the shore—three years of tempest were consumed. Three more birthdays of little Lucie had been woven by the golden thread into the peaceful tissue of the life of her home.
domestication: 在这样的烈火升腾、海水暴涨之中——坚实的大地被愤怒的海洋一次又一次地摇撼，海浪不再退去，而是越来越高，令岸边的观者惊恐不已——三年的风暴就这样过去了。在这期间，小露西又度过了三个生日，金色的线将这三个生日编织进了她家中平静生活的布匹中。
foreignization: 在这样的火的升腾和海的升腾中——坚固的大地被愤怒的海洋的冲击所震动，这海洋现在没有退潮，而是一直在上涨，越来越高，令岸上的观者既恐惧又惊奇——三年的风暴被消耗掉了。小露西的三个更多生日已经被金色的线编织进了她家庭生活的和平织物中。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

In such risings of fire and risings of sea—the firm earth shaken by the rushes of an angry ocean which had now no ebb, but was always on the flow, higher and higher, to the terror and wonder of the beholders on the shore—three years of tempest were consumed. Three more birthdays of little Lucie had been woven by the golden thread into the peaceful tissue of the life of her home.
在这样的烈火升腾、海水暴涨之中——坚实的大地被愤怒的海洋一次又一次地摇撼，海浪不再退去，而是越来越高，令岸边的观者惊恐不已——三年的风暴就这样过去了。在这期间，小露西又度过了三个生日，金色的线将这三个生日编织进了她家中平静生活的布匹中。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

In such risings of fire and risings of sea—the firm earth shaken by the rushes of an angry ocean which had now no ebb, but was always on the flow, higher and higher, to the terror and wonder of the beholders on the shore—three years of tempest were consumed. Three more birthdays of little Lucie had been woven by the golden thread into the peaceful tissue of the life of her home.
在这样的火的升腾和海的升腾中——坚固的大地被愤怒的海洋的冲击所震动，这海洋现在没有退潮，而是一直在上涨，越来越高，令岸上的观者既恐惧又惊奇——三年的风暴被消耗掉了。小露西的三个更多生日已经被金色的线编织进了她家庭生活的和平织物中。

source: A place of business in London like Tellson’s place of business in Paris, would soon have driven the House out of its mind and into the Gazette. For, what would staid British responsibility and respectability have said to orange-trees in boxes in a Bank courtyard, and even to a Cupid over the counter? Yet such things were. Tellson’s had whitewashed the Cupid, but he was still to be seen on the ceiling, in the coolest linen, aiming (as he very often does) at money from morning to night. Bankruptcy must inevitably have come of this young Pagan, in Lombard-street, London, and also of a curtained alcove in the rear of the immortal boy, and also of a looking-glass let into the wall, and also of clerks not at all old, who danced in public on the slightest provocation. Yet, a French Tellson’s could get on with these things exceedingly well, and, as long as the times held together, no man had taken fright at them, and drawn out his money.
domestication: 伦敦的泰森银行要是像巴黎的泰森银行那样，准会把它自己整破产，上《公报》露脸。要知道，稳重的英国人讲究责任和体面，他们怎么受得了银行院子里摆着箱子里的橙子树，柜台上方还挂着个小爱神？可这种事在巴黎的泰森银行里还真有。伦敦的泰森把小爱神给粉刷了，但天花板上还能看见他穿着最凉快的亚麻衣，从早到晚瞄准钱袋（这小家伙经常干这种事）。这样的异教小青年，放在伦敦的朗巴德街上，肯定得让银行破产；还有那间带帘子的小隔间，镜子嵌在墙上的设计，以及那些年轻职员，稍微有点由头就跳起舞来，这些都得让银行关门大吉。然而，法国的泰森银行却能很好地应付这些，只要时局稳定，就没有人因为这些事儿吓得把钱提走。
foreignization: 像伦敦的泰勒森银行这样的企业在巴黎，很快就会让这家银行精神错乱并登上破产公告。因为，稳重的英国责任感和体面会怎么看待银行院子里箱子里的橙子树，甚至柜台上的丘比特呢？然而，这些事情确实存在。泰勒森银行粉刷了丘比特，但他仍然可以看到在天花板上，穿着最凉爽的亚麻布，从早到晚瞄准（正如他经常做的那样）金钱。这种年轻的异教徒在伦敦朗伯德街必定会导致破产，还有那个不朽少年后面的带帷幔的壁龛，以及嵌入墙中的镜子，以及那些根本不老的职员，一有借口就在公共场合跳舞。然而，法国的泰勒森银行能够很好地应对这些事情，只要时局稳定，没有人因这些事情而惊慌失措，取走他们的钱。

source: “She had laid her head upon my shoulder, that night when I was summoned out—she had a fear of my going, though I had none—and when I was brought to the North Tower they found these upon my sleeve. ‘You will leave me them? They can never help me to escape in the body, though they may in the spirit.’ Those were the words I said. I remember them very well.”
domestication: 那晚我被叫出去，她把头靠在我肩上——虽然我不害怕，但她却担心我会出事。等我被带到北塔时，他们从我的袖子上发现了这些。我说：“你就留着它们吧？这些虽不能帮我逃出生天，但或许能给我些精神上的慰藉。”我清楚地记得自己当时说的话。
foreignization: “那天晚上我被召出去时，她把头靠在我的肩上——尽管我不害怕，她却害怕我离开——而当我被带到北塔时，他们在我的袖子上发现了这些。‘你会把它们留给我吗？它们永远无法帮助我肉体逃脱，但也许可以在精神上帮助我。’这就是我说的话。我非常清楚地记得它们。”

source: “Can’t be helped,” said Miss Pross, shaking her head. “Touch that string, and he instantly changes for the worse. Better leave it alone. In short, must leave it alone, like or no like. Sometimes, he gets up in the dead of the night, and will be heard, by us overhead there, walking up and down, walking up and down, in his room. Ladybird has learnt to know then that his mind is walking up and down, walking up and down, in his old prison. She hurries to him, and they go on together, walking up and down, walking up and down, until he is composed. But he never says a word of the true reason of his restlessness, to her, and she finds it best not to hint at it to him. In silence they go walking up and down together, walking up and down together, till her love and company have brought him to himself.”
domestication: “没办法，”普罗斯小姐摇头说，“一碰到这个话题，他就立刻变坏了。还是别碰的好，反正都得忍着，喜欢不喜欢都得这样。有时候，他大半夜的爬起来，在楼上的房间里来回踱步，我们都能听到。小宝贝儿一听就知道，他的心又在老监狱里来回踱步了。她赶紧跑到他身边，俩人一起走来走去，直到他平静下来。但他从不跟她说起真正让他心烦的原因，她也觉得最好别提。他们就这样默默地一起走啊走，直到她的爱和陪伴让他恢复过来。”
foreignization: “没办法，”普罗斯小姐摇着头说，“碰那根弦，他立刻就会变坏。还是别碰的好。总之，不管喜不喜欢都得别碰。有时候，他在深夜起身，在我们头顶上走来走去，走来走去。小红骑学会了那时他的心正在老监狱里走来走去，走来走去。她急忙到他那里去，他们一起继续走来走去，走来走去，直到他平静下来。但他从不对她说出他焦躁不安的真正原因，她也觉得最好不要对他暗示。他们默默地一起走来走去，一起走来走去，直到她的爱和陪伴使他恢复了常态。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Can’t be helped,” said Miss Pross, shaking her head. “Touch that string, and he instantly changes for the worse. Better leave it alone. In short, must leave it alone, like or no like. Sometimes, he gets up in the dead of the night, and will be heard, by us overhead there, walking up and down, walking up and down, in his room. Ladybird has learnt to know then that his mind is walking up and down, walking up and down, in his old prison. She hurries to him, and they go on together, walking up and down, walking up and down, until he is composed. But he never says a word of the true reason of his restlessness, to her, and she finds it best not to hint at it to him. In silence they go walking up and down together, walking up and down together, till her love and company have brought him to himself.”
“没办法，”普罗斯小姐摇头说，“一碰到这个话题，他就立刻变坏了。还是别碰的好，反正都得忍着，喜欢不喜欢都得这样。有时候，他大半夜的爬起来，在楼上的房间里来回踱步，我们都能听到。小宝贝儿一听就知道，他的心又在老监狱里来回踱步了。她赶紧跑到他身边，俩人一起走来走去，直到他平静下来。但他从不跟她说起真正让他心烦的原因，她也觉得最好别提。他们就这样默默地一起走啊走，直到她的爱和陪伴让他恢复过来。”

Ladybird has learnt to know then that his mind is walking up and down, walking up and down, in his old prison. She hurries to him, and they go on together, walking up and down, walking up and down, until he is composed. But he never says a word of the true reason of his restlessness, to her, and she finds it best not to hint at it to him. In silence they go walking up and down together, walking up and down together, till her love and company have brought him to himself.”

“没办法，”普罗斯小姐摇着头说，“碰那根弦，他立刻就会变坏。还是别碰的好。总之，不管喜不喜欢都得别碰。有时候，他在深夜起身，在我们头顶上走来走去，走来走去。小红骑学会了那时他的心正在老监狱里走来走去，走来走去。她急忙到他那里去，他们一起继续走来走去，走来走去，直到他平静下来。但他从不对她说出他焦躁不安的真正原因，她也觉得最好不要对他暗示。他们默默地一起走来走去，一起走来走去，直到她的爱和陪伴使他恢复了常态。”

source: “My last supplication of all, is this; and with it, I will relieve you of a visitor with whom I well know you have nothing in unison, and between whom and you there is an impassable space. It is useless to say it, I know, but it rises out of my soul. For you, and for any dear to you, I would do anything. If my career were of that better kind that there was any opportunity or capacity of sacrifice in it, I would embrace any sacrifice for you and for those dear to you. Try to hold me in your mind, at some quiet times, as ardent and sincere in this one thing. The time will come, the time will not be long in coming, when new ties will be formed about you—ties that will bind you yet more tenderly and strongly to the home you so adorn—the dearest ties that will ever grace and gladden you. O Miss Manette, when the little picture of a happy father’s face looks up in yours, when you see your own bright beauty springing up anew at your feet, think now and then that there is a man who would give his life, to keep a life you love beside you!”
domestication: 这是我最后的请求了；说完这番话，我也就不再打扰你了，我知道我们之间没有共同之处，彼此间隔着一道无法逾越的鸿沟。其实我明知道说出来也没用，但这是发自内心的呼唤。为了你，为了你所珍爱的人，我愿意赴汤蹈火。如果我的人生有更好的前景，能够有牺牲的机会和能力，我一定毫不犹豫地为你和你所爱的人做出任何牺牲。在你平静的时刻，请偶尔想起我对这件事的真诚和热情。不久的将来，你会建立起新的纽带——这些纽带会更温柔、更牢固地将你与这个你如此光彩照人的家联系在一起——那是你一生中最珍贵、最让你高兴的纽带。曼内特小姐啊，当那张幸福的父亲的脸庞仰望着你的脸庞，当你看到自己耀眼的美丽在你脚下重新绽放时，请偶尔记起，有一个人愿意为你献出生命，来保护你所爱的人留在你身边！
foreignization: 我的最后祈求就是这个；说完它，我就会不再打扰您，一个与您毫无共鸣、与您之间有不可逾越鸿沟的访客。说这些是多余的，我知道，但它从我的灵魂深处升起。为了您，为了您所珍爱的人，我愿意做任何事。如果我的事业是那种更好的类型，有牺牲的机会和能力的话，我会欣然接受任何为您和您所珍爱的人作出的牺牲。请在一些宁静的时刻，将我视为在这唯一的一点上热情而真诚。时间会到来，不会很久就会到来，新的纽带将围绕您形成——那些将您更加温柔和牢固地与您如此装点的家庭联系在一起的纽带——那些最亲爱、最能美化和愉悦您的纽带。噢，曼内特小姐，当一幅幸福父亲面孔的小画像仰望着您，当您看到自己的美丽光辉在您脚下重新绽放时，偶尔想想，有一个男人愿意付出生命，以保持您所爱的生命在您身边！

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“My last supplication of all, is this; and with it, I will relieve you of a visitor with whom I well know you have nothing in unison, and between whom and you there is an impassable space. It is useless to say it, I know, but it rises out of my soul. For you, and for any dear to you, I would do anything. If my career were of that better kind that there was any opportunity or capacity of sacrifice in it, I would embrace any sacrifice for you and for those dear to you. Try to hold me in your mind, at some quiet times, as ardent and sincere in this one thing. The time will come, the time will not be long in coming, when new ties will be formed about you—ties that will bind you yet more tenderly and strongly to the home you so adorn—the dearest ties that will ever grace and gladden you. O Miss Manette, when the little picture of a happy father’s face looks up in yours, when you see your own bright beauty springing up anew at your feet, think now and then that there is a man who would give his life, to keep a life you love beside you!”

这是我最后的请求了；说完这番话，我也就不再打扰你了，我知道我们之间没有共同之处，彼此间隔着一道无法逾越的鸿沟。其实我明知道说出来也没用，但这是发自内心的呼唤。为了你，为了你所珍爱的人，我愿意赴汤蹈火。如果我的人生有更好的前景，能够有牺牲的机会和能力，我一定毫不犹豫地为你和你所爱的人做出任何牺牲。在你平静的时刻，请偶尔想起我对这件事的真诚和热情。不久的将来，你会建立起新的纽带——这些纽带会更温柔、更牢固地将你与这个你如此光彩照人的家联系在一起——那是你一生中最珍贵、最让你高兴的纽带。曼内特小姐啊，当那张幸福的父亲的脸庞仰望着你的脸庞，当你看到自己耀眼的美丽在你脚下重新绽放时，请偶尔记起，有一个人愿意为你献出生命，来保护你所爱的人留在你身边！

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“My last supplication of all, is this; and with it, I will relieve you of a visitor with whom I well know you have nothing in unison, and between whom and you there is an impassable space. It is useless to say it, I know, but it rises out of my soul. For you, and for any dear to you, I would do anything. If my career were of that better kind that there was any opportunity or capacity of sacrifice in it, I would embrace any sacrifice for you and for those dear to you. Try to hold me in your mind, at some quiet times, as ardent and sincere in this one thing. The time will come, the time will not be long in coming, when new ties will be formed about you—ties that will bind you yet more tenderly and strongly to the home you so adorn—the dearest ties that will ever grace and gladden you. O Miss Manette, when the little picture of a happy father’s face looks up in yours, when you see your own bright beauty springing up anew at your feet, think now and then that there is a man who would give his life, to keep a life you love beside you!”

我的最后祈求就是这个；说完它，我就会不再打扰您，一个与您毫无共鸣、与您之间有不可逾越鸿沟的访客。说这些是多余的，我知道，但它从我的灵魂深处升起。为了您，为了您所珍爱的人，我愿意做任何事。如果我的事业是那种更好的类型，有牺牲的机会和能力的话，我会欣然接受任何为您和您所珍爱的人作出的牺牲。请在一些宁静的时刻，将我视为在这唯一的一点上热情而真诚。时间会到来，不会很久就会到来，新的纽带将围绕您形成——那些将您更加温柔和牢固地与您如此装点的家庭联系在一起的纽带——那些最亲爱、最能美化和愉悦您的纽带。噢，曼内特小姐，当一幅幸福父亲面孔的小画像仰望着您，当您看到自己的美丽光辉在您脚下重新绽放时，偶尔想想，有一个男人愿意付出生命，以保持您所爱的生命在您身边！

source: “Doctor Manette,” said Mr. Lorry, touching him affectionately on the arm, “the case is the case of a particularly dear friend of mine. Pray give your mind to it, and advise me well for his sake—and above all, for his daughter’s—his daughter’s, my dear Manette.”
domestication: 洛里先生亲热地拍了拍马内特医生的胳膊，说道：“马内特老兄，这件事关系到我一个特别亲近的朋友。请你务必上心，给我出出主意，为了他的缘故——尤其是为了他女儿，他那宝贝女儿。”
foreignization: “曼内特医生，”劳瑞先生亲切地拍了拍他的手臂说，“这件事事关我一个特别亲密的朋友。请务必用心考虑，并为了他的缘故——尤其是为了他女儿——他女儿的緣故，我的亲爱的曼内特，给我一个好的建议。”

source: “But he has been—been found. He is alive. Greatly changed, it is too probable; almost a wreck, it is possible; though we will hope the best. Still, alive. Your father has been taken to the house of an old servant in Paris, and we are going there: I, to identify him if I can: you, to restore him to life, love, duty, rest, comfort.”
domestication: 但他总算是找到了，还活着。可能变化很大，甚至几乎成了废人，不过咱们还是往好的方面想。总之，他还活着。你父亲已经被带到巴黎一个老仆人家了，咱们这就去：我去认一认人，你去把他拉回生活、找回爱、尽到责任、安顿下来、好好休息。
foreignization: “但他已经被——被找到了。他还活着。变化很大，这很可能是事实；几乎成了废人，这也是可能的；不过我们会抱最好的希望。无论如何，他还活着。你的父亲已经被送到巴黎一个老仆人的家里，我们将要去那里：我去辨认他；你去让他恢复生活、爱、职责、休息和舒适。”

source: These were among the echoes to which Lucie, sometimes pensive, sometimes amused and laughing, listened in the echoing corner, until her little daughter was six years old. How near to her heart the echoes of her child’s tread came, and those of her own dear father’s, always active and self-possessed, and those of her dear husband’s, need not be told. Nor, how the lightest echo of their united home, directed by herself with such a wise and elegant thrift that it was more abundant than any waste, was music to her. Nor, how there were echoes all about her, sweet in her ears, of the many times her father had told her that he found her more devoted to him married (if that could be) than single, and of the many times her husband had said to her that no cares and duties seemed to divide her love for him or her help to him, and asked her “What is the magic secret, my darling, of your being everything to all of us, as if there were only one of us, yet never seeming to be hurried, or to have too much to do?”
domestication: 这些声音中，露西时而沉思，时而欢笑，一直听到她的小女儿六岁那年。孩子走路的声音，还有她亲爱的父亲那总是忙碌而从容的脚步声，还有她心爱的丈夫的脚步声，都离她的心如此之近，这无需多说。她自己以智慧和优雅节俭地经营着这个家，让家里的一切都丰盈而无浪费，那些细碎的声音对她来说就是音乐，这也不必多言。她周围到处都是甜蜜的声音，她父亲多次对她说，她结婚后比单身时更爱他了，她丈夫也常对她说，家务和责任并没有分散她对他的爱或对他的帮助，还问她：“亲爱的，你是怎么做到的？对我们每个人来说，你都是无所不能的，好像我们只有一个，却从不见你忙得团团转，或是觉得事情太多？”
foreignization: 这些回声中，露西有时沉思，有时愉快地笑著，在回音缭绕的角落里倾听，直到她的小女儿六岁。她孩子脚步的回声、她亲爱的父亲那总是活跃而镇定自若的脚步声、以及她亲爱的丈夫的脚步声如何贴近她的心，无需赘述。同样无需说明的是，他们共同的家庭中最轻微的回声，由于她以如此明智和优雅的方式精打细算，使它比任何浪费都更加丰富，对她来说都是音乐。此外，还有许多回声围绕着她，听起来甜美动听，那是她父亲多次告诉她，他发现她结婚后（如果可能的话）比单身时更忠心耿耿；还有她丈夫多次对她说，没有任何忧虑和职责似乎能分散她对他的爱或对他的帮助，并问她：“亲爱的，你有什么神奇的秘密，让我们每个人都觉得你是我们的全部，仿佛我们只有一个人，却从不显得匆忙，也不显得事情太多？”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

These were among the echoes to which Lucie, sometimes pensive, sometimes amused and laughing, listened in the echoing corner, until her little daughter was six years old. How near to her heart the echoes of her child’s tread came, and those of her own dear father’s, always active and self-possessed, and those of her dear husband’s, need not be told. Nor, how the lightest echo of their united home, directed by herself with such a wise and elegant thrift that it was more abundant than any waste, was music to her. Nor, how there were echoes all about her, sweet in her ears, of the many times her father had told her that he found her more devoted to him married (if that could be) than single, and of the many times her husband had said to her that no cares and duties seemed to divide her love for him or her help to him, and asked her “What is the magic secret, my darling, of your being everything to all of us, as if there were only one of us, yet never seeming to be hurried, or to have too much to do?”

这些声音中，露西时而沉思，时而欢笑，一直听到她的小女儿六岁那年。孩子走路的声音，还有她亲爱的父亲那总是忙碌而从容的脚步声，还有她心爱的丈夫的脚步声，都离她的心如此之近，这无需多说。她自己以智慧和优雅节俭地经营着这个家，让家里的一切都丰盈而无浪费，那些细碎的声音对她来说就是音乐，这也不必多言。她周围到处都是甜蜜的声音，她父亲多次对她说，她结婚后比单身时更爱他了，她丈夫也常对她说，家务和责任并没有分散她对他的爱或对他的帮助，还问她：“亲爱的，你是怎么做到的？对我们每个人来说，你都是无所不能的，好像我们只有一个，却从不见你忙得团团转，或是觉得事情太多？”

Nor, how the lightest echo of their united home, directed by herself with such a wise and elegant thrift that it was more abundant than any waste, was music to her.
