source: “You speak collectedly, and you—are collected. That’s good!” (Though his manner was less satisfied than his words.) “A matter of business. Regard it as a matter of business—business that must be done. Now if this doctor’s wife, though a lady of great courage and spirit, had suffered so intensely from this cause before her little child was born—”
domestication: 你说话镇定，人也镇定，这很好！（虽然他说话的语气并不像他说的那么满意。）这是生意上的事，就当是生意上的事——必须得办的事。要是这位医生太太，虽然胆大心细，但在孩子出生前，因为这件事受了那么多罪——
foreignization: “你说话镇定，你也——很镇定。这很好！”（尽管他的态度不如他的话那么满意。）“这是一件生意。把它当作一件生意——必须完成的生意。现在，如果这位医生的妻子，虽然是一位非常勇敢和有精神的女士，在她的小孩子出生前，因为这个原因遭受了如此剧烈的痛苦——”

You hear?”

source: The August of the year one thousand seven hundred and ninety-two was come, and Monseigneur was by this time scattered far and wide.
domestication: 一七九二年的八月到了，侯爵老爷的势力这时已经烟消云散，四处逃散了。

The August of the year one thousand seven hundred and ninety-two was come, and Monseigneur was by this time scattered far and wide.
一千七百九十二年八月已经到来，而蒙塞尼尔此时已经四散各地。

source: “I have looked at her, speculating thousands of times upon the unborn child from whom I had been rent. Whether it was alive. Whether it had been born alive, or the poor mother’s shock had killed it. Whether it was a son who would some day avenge his father. (There was a time in my imprisonment, when my desire for vengeance was unbearable.) Whether it was a son who would never know his father’s story; who might even live to weigh the possibility of his father’s having disappeared of his own will and act. Whether it was a daughter who would grow to be a woman.”
domestication: 我看她时，心里猜测了千百遍那个未曾谋面的孩子。他是否还活着？是出生时就死了，还是被母亲的悲痛夺去了生命？是不是个儿子，将来能替父亲报仇雪恨？（我在牢里时，复仇的念头曾让我难以忍受。）会不会是个儿子，永远不知道父亲的故事，甚至怀疑父亲是自愿消失的？或者是个女儿，长大后会成为女人？
foreignization: 我已经看过她，对那个尚未出生便与我分离的孩子做了无数次的揣测。它是活着的吗。它是否是活产，或是母亲的惊吓害死了它。它是否是一个有朝一日会为父亲报仇的儿子。（在被囚禁的那段日子里，我对复仇的渴望是难以忍受的。）它是否是一个永远不会知道父亲故事的儿子；甚至可能会活到考虑父亲可能是自愿消失的可能性和行动。它是否是一个会长大成为女人的女儿。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I have looked at her, speculating thousands of times upon the unborn child from whom I had been rent. Whether it was alive. Whether it had been born alive, or the poor mother’s shock had killed it. Whether it was a son who would some day avenge his father. (There was a time in my imprisonment, when my desire for vengeance was unbearable.) Whether it was a son who would never know his father’s story; who might even live to weigh the possibility of his father’s having disappeared of his own will and act. Whether it was a daughter who would grow to be a woman.”
我看她时，心里猜测了千百遍那个未曾谋面的孩子。他是否还活着？是出生时就死了，还是被母亲的悲痛夺去了生命？是不是个儿子，将来能替父亲报仇雪恨？（我在牢里时，复仇的念头曾让我难以忍受。）会不会是个儿子，永远不知道父亲的故事，甚至怀疑父亲是自愿消失的？或者是个女儿，长大后会成为女人？

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I have looked at her, speculating thousands of times upon the unborn child from whom I had been rent. Whether it was alive. Whether it had been born alive, or the poor mother’s shock had killed it. Whether it was a son who would some day avenge his father. (There was a time in my imprisonment, when my desire for vengeance was unbearable.) Whether it was a son who would never know his father’s story; who might even live to weigh the possibility of his father’s having disappeared of his own will and act. Whether it was a daughter who would grow to be a woman.”
我已经看过她，对那个尚未出生便与我分离的孩子做了无数次的揣测。它是活着的吗。它是否是活产，或是母亲的惊吓害死了它。它是否是一个有朝一日会为父亲报仇的儿子。（在被囚禁的那段日子里，我对复仇的渴望是难以忍受的。）它是否是一个永远不会知道父亲故事的儿子；甚至可能会活到考虑父亲可能是自愿消失的可能性和行动。它是否是一个会长大成为女人的女儿。

source: He lowered the window, and looked out at the rising sun. Though the earth was cold and wet, the sky was clear, and the sun rose bright, placid, and beautiful.

source: “Upon my soul, I am not sure that it was not yours. You were always driving and riving and shouldering and passing, to that restless degree that I had no chance for my life but in rust and repose. It’s a gloomy thing, however, to talk about one’s own past, with the day breaking. Turn me in some other direction before I go.”
domestication: 说真的，我都不确定那是不是你的错。你总是风风火火、横冲直撞，闹得我只能在清净和安逸中寻求一线生机。不过，天都快亮了，说这些陈年旧事真让人扫兴。走之前，咱们换个话题吧。
foreignization: 说真的，我不能确定那不是你的。你总是不停地驱赶、竞争、挤撞、超越，达到了如此不安分的程度，以至于我若想活命，只能在锈蚀和安逸中度过。然而，在天亮时谈论自己的过去是一件令人沮丧的事。在我离开之前，换个话题吧。

source: He had loved Lucie Manette from the hour of his danger. He had never heard a sound so sweet and dear as the sound of her compassionate voice; he had never seen a face so tenderly beautiful, as hers when it was confronted with his own on the edge of the grave that had been dug for him. But, he had not yet spoken to her on the subject; the assassination at the deserted chateau far away beyond the heaving water and the long, long, dusty roads—the solid stone chateau which had itself become the mere mist of a dream—had been done a year, and he had never yet, by so much as a single spoken word, disclosed to her the state of his heart.
domestication: 他从自己陷入危难的那一刻起就爱上了露西·曼内特。他从未听过比她那同情的声音更动听、更亲切的音符；他也从未见过像她在他的墓穴旁，面对着自己的那一刻那样温柔美丽的面容。但是，他还没和她说过这些心里话。那个远离喧嚣、坐落在波涛汹涌的大海和漫长尘土飞扬的道路尽头的废弃城堡里发生的刺杀事件，已经过去一年了，而他却连一个字都没向她吐露过自己的心声。
foreignization: 他从自己处于危险的那个时刻起就爱上了露西·曼内特。他从未听过比她那充满同情的声音更甜美、更亲切的声音；他从未见过如此温柔美丽的面孔，就像她在他的墓穴边缘面对着他时的那副模样——那个为他挖掘的墓穴。但是，他还没有就此与她交谈过；那座远离此地、在波涛汹涌的水面和漫长尘土飞扬的道路尽头的废弃城堡中的谋杀案——那座坚固的石堡如今已成了梦中的幻影——发生在一年前，而他至今尚未通过只言片语向她透露自己的心事。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

He had loved Lucie Manette from the hour of his danger. He had never heard a sound so sweet and dear as the sound of her compassionate voice; he had never seen a face so tenderly beautiful, as hers when it was confronted with his own on the edge of the grave that had been dug for him. But, he had not yet spoken to her on the subject; the assassination at the deserted chateau far away beyond the heaving water and the long, long, dusty roads—the solid stone chateau which had itself become the mere mist of a dream—had been done a year, and he had never yet, by so much as a single spoken word, disclosed to her the state of his heart.

source: Now, the sun was full up, and movement began in the village. Casement windows opened, crazy doors were unbarred, and people came forth shivering—chilled, as yet, by the new sweet air. Then began the rarely lightened toil of the day among the village population. Some, to the fountain; some, to the fields; men and women here, to dig and delve; men and women there, to see to the poor live stock, and lead the bony cows out, to such pasture as could be found by the roadside. In the church and at the Cross, a kneeling figure or two; attendant on the latter prayers, the led cow, trying for a breakfast among the weeds at its foot.
domestication: 现在太阳高挂，村子里开始热闹起来。窗户一扇扇打开，歪歪斜斜的门也卸下了门闩，人们打着哆嗦走出来，新鲜的空气还让他们觉得有些凉。于是，村里人开始了他们一天中难得的忙碌。有的去水井打水，有的去田里干活，男男女女在这边挖地刨土，那边则照料着那几头瘦骨嶙峋的牲口，领着它们到路边找点儿草吃。教堂里和十字架前，有几人跪着祈祷，旁边拴着的牛也不闲着，在脚下的杂草间觅食，算是它的早餐了。
foreignization: 现在，太阳已经高高升起，村子里开始有了动静。窗户打开了，古怪的门被解除了门闩，人们哆哆嗦嗦地走了出来——仍然被这新鲜的甜空气所寒冷。然后，村民们开始了他们几乎不减的日常工作。一些人去了泉边；一些人去了田里；这里有些男女在挖土和耕作；那里有些男女在照看那些可怜的牲畜，把骨瘦如柴的牛牵出去，在路边能找到的草地上放牧。在教堂和十字架前，有两三个跪着的人；在后者的祈祷旁，被牵着的牛正在它的脚下杂草中寻找早餐。

source: “I don’t know,” returned the man, clapping his hands to his mouth nevertheless, and vociferating in a surprising heat and with the greatest ardour, “Spies! Yaha! Tst, tst! Spi—ies!”

source: Doctor Manette received him kindly, and so did Lucie. But, Miss Pross suddenly became afflicted with a twitching in the head and body, and retired into the house. She was not unfrequently the victim of this disorder, and she called it, in familiar conversation, “a fit of the jerks.”

source: Monsieur the Marquis cast his eyes over the submissive faces that drooped before him, as the like of himself had drooped before Monseigneur of the Court—only the difference was, that these faces drooped merely to suffer and not to propitiate—when a grizzled mender of the roads joined the group.
domestication: 侯爵大人扫视着面前那些低垂的面孔，这些面孔低垂着只是默默忍受，不像他自己这类人在宫廷里低垂着是为了讨好老爷们。这时，一个头发花白的修路工加入了人群。
foreignization: 侯爵先生扫视着那些在他面前低垂的顺从的脸庞，就像他自己这一类人曾经在宫廷的老爷们面前低垂一样——唯一的区别是，这些脸庞只是低垂着忍受，并非为了讨好——这时，一个灰白头发的修路工人加入了这群人。

source: Few children were to be seen, and no dogs. As to the men and women, their choice on earth was stated in the prospect—Life on the lowest terms that could sustain it, down in the little village under the mill; or captivity and Death in the dominant prison on the crag.
domestication: 少见小孩，更不见狗。至于男男女女，他们的选择明摆着——要么在磨坊下的小村里，过着勉强维持生计的苦日子；要么在峭壁上的监狱里，被囚禁至死。
foreignization: 很少看到孩子，也没有狗。至于男女们，他们在世上的选择已经在展望中说明了——要么在磨坊下的小村庄里以最低的条件维持生活；要么在峭壁上的主宰监狱中被囚禁和死亡。

source: “It’s hard in the law to spile a man, I think. It’s hard enough to kill him, but it’s wery hard to spile him, sir.”

source: “No, no, no; you are too young, too blooming. It can’t be. See what the prisoner is. These are not the hands she knew, this is not the face she knew, this is not a voice she ever heard. source: If Sydney Carton ever shone anywhere, he certainly never shone in the house of Doctor Manette. He had been there often, during a whole year, and had always been the same moody and morose lounger there. When he cared to talk, he talked well; but, the cloud of caring for nothing, which overshadowed him with such a fatal darkness, was very rarely pierced by the light within him.
domestication: 如果西德尼·卡顿在哪方面有所闪光，那在他曼内特医生的家里肯定是看不出来的。他在那儿来来去去了一整年，一直都是那个情绪低落、郁郁寡欢的闲逛者。他要是愿意说话，倒也说得头头是道；但那种对什么都不在乎的阴霾，像一层致命的黑暗笼罩着他，很少有他内心那点光亮能穿透这层阴云。
foreignization: 如果西德尼·卡顿曾在什么地方发光，他当然从未在马内特医生的家里发光。他在那儿经常出现，整整一年里，一直都是那个忧郁和阴沉的闲逛者。当他愿意说话时，他谈吐很好；但是，那片笼罩着他的、致命的无所关心的阴霾，很少被他内心的光芒穿透。

source: The man moved a little further away, as soon as he could, and the mender of roads fanned himself with his blue cap: feeling it mightily close and oppressive. If he needed a King and Queen to restore him, he was fortunate in having his remedy at hand; for, soon the large-faced King and the fair-faced Queen came in their golden coach, attended by the shining Bull’s Eye of their Court, a glittering multitude of laughing ladies and fine lords; and in jewels and silks and powder and splendour and elegantly spurning figures and handsomely disdainful faces of both sexes, the mender of roads bathed himself, so much to his temporary intoxication, that he cried Long live the King, Long live the Queen, Long live everybody and everything! as if he had never heard of ubiquitous Jacques in his time. Then, there were gardens, courtyards, terraces, fountains, green banks, more King and Queen, more Bull’s Eye, more lords and ladies, more Long live they all! until he absolutely wept with sentiment. During the whole of this scene, which lasted some three hours, he had plenty of shouting and weeping and sentimental company, and throughout Defarge held him by the collar, as if to restrain him from flying at the objects of his brief devotion and tearing them to pieces.
domestication:
那人一有机会就离得远远的，修路工感到闷热难耐，便用蓝色的帽子给自己扇风。如果他需要国王和王后来恢复精神，那他算是走运了，因为不久之后，大脸的国王和美丽的王后就在他们宫廷里闪亮的“牛眼”侍卫和一大群光彩照人的笑眯眯的女士和衣着华丽的贵族陪同下，乘坐金色的马车出现了。修路工在这些珠宝、丝绸、粉黛、辉煌和高贵的人物中沐浴了一番，以至于他醉醺醺地高呼：“国王万岁！王后万岁！所有的人和事都万岁！”仿佛他从未听说过四处活动的雅克。

接着，还有花园、庭院、露台、喷泉、绿草坪，更多的国王和王后，更多的“牛眼”，更多的贵族和女士，更多的“他们万岁！”直到他感动得泪流满面。整个场面持续了大约三个小时，期间他一直有大喊大叫、流泪和感伤的伙伴们相伴，而德法日始终抓着他的衣领，好像要阻止他向他短暂崇拜的对象扑过去，将他们撕成碎片。
foreignization: 那人尽可能地向后退了一点，修路工随即用他的蓝帽子扇风：感到异常闷热和压抑。如果他需要一位国王和王后来恢复精神，他倒是幸运地找到了解决办法；因为，不久之后，大脸的国王和美丽的王后乘坐着金色的马车，在宫廷的闪耀牛眼灯、一群闪闪发光的欢笑女士和华丽的贵族的陪同下出现了；在珠宝、丝绸、粉饰、辉煌、优雅傲慢的身影以及男女们英俊高傲的脸庞中，修路工沉浸其中，以至于暂时陶醉得喊起了“国王万岁，王后万岁，所有人和所有事物万岁！”仿佛他从未听说过无处不在的雅克。然后，有花园、庭院、露台、喷泉、绿草地、更多的国王和王后、更多的牛眼灯、更多的贵族和女士、更多的“他们全都万岁！”直到他因感伤而泪流满面。在整个持续了大约三个小时的场景中，他有很多可以呐喊、哭泣和感伤的同伴，而德法日始终抓住他的衣领，仿佛要阻止他扑向这些短暂崇拜的对象并将他们撕成碎片。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The man moved a little further away, as soon as he could, and the mender of roads fanned himself with his blue cap: feeling it mightily close and oppressive. If he needed a King and Queen to restore him, he was fortunate in having his remedy at hand; for, soon the large-faced King and the fair-faced Queen came in their golden coach, attended by the shining Bull’s Eye of their Court, a glittering multitude of laughing ladies and fine lords; and in jewels and silks and powder and splendour and elegantly spurning figures and handsomely disdainful faces of both sexes, the mender of roads bathed himself, so much to his temporary intoxication, that he cried Long live the King, Long live the Queen, Long live everybody and everything! as if he had never heard of ubiquitous Jacques in his time. Then, there were gardens, courtyards, terraces, fountains, green banks, more King and Queen, more Bull’s Eye, more lords and ladies, more Long live they all! until he absolutely wept with sentiment. During the whole of this scene, which lasted some three hours, he had plenty of shouting and weeping and sentimental company, and throughout Defarge held him by the collar, as if to restrain him from flying at the objects of his brief devotion and tearing them to pieces.

那人一有机会就离得远远的，修路工感到闷热难耐，便用蓝色的帽子给自己扇风。如果他需要国王和王后来恢复精神，那他算是走运了，因为不久之后，大脸的国王和美丽的王后就在他们宫廷里闪亮的“牛眼”侍卫和一大群光彩照人的笑眯眯的女士和衣着华丽的贵族陪同下，乘坐金色的马车出现了。修路工在这些珠宝、丝绸、粉黛、辉煌和高贵的人物中沐浴了一番，以至于他醉醺醺地高呼：“国王万岁！王后万岁！所有的人和事都万岁！”仿佛他从未听说过四处活动的雅克。

接着，还有花园、庭院、露台、喷泉、绿草坪，更多的国王和王后，更多的“牛眼”，更多的贵族和女士，更多的“他们万岁！”直到他感动得泪流满面。整个场面持续了大约三个小时，期间他一直有大喊大叫、流泪和感伤的伙伴们相伴，而德法日始终抓着他的衣领，好像要阻止他向他短暂崇拜的对象扑过去，将他们撕成碎片。

source: “Dear Doctor Manette, always knowing this, always seeing her and you with this hallowed light about you, I have forborne, and forborne, as long as it was in the nature of man to do it. I have felt, and do even now feel, that to bring my love—even mine—between you, is to touch your history with something not quite so good as itself. But I love her. Heaven is my witness that I love her!”
domestication: 亲爱的马内特医生，我一直以来都知道这一点，看到你和她周围总是环绕着神圣的光芒，我一直忍着，忍着，忍到了人的极限。我曾感到，现在也依然觉得，即使是我，把我的爱放在你们之间，也会让您的过去蒙上一层不如它本身完美的阴影。但我真的爱她，老天可以作证，我真的爱她！
foreignization: 亲爱的马内特医生，我一直知道这一点，一直看到您和她身边环绕着这神圣的光芒，我已经忍耐了，已经忍耐到人类所能承受的极限。我感到，甚至现在仍然感到，将我的爱——即使是我的爱——放在你们之间，就是触及您的历史，而这种触及并非完全与之相配。但是我爱她。天哪，我爱她！

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Dear Doctor Manette, always knowing this, always seeing her and you with this hallowed light about you, I have forborne, and forborne, as long as it was in the nature of man to do it. I have felt, and do even now feel, that to bring my love—even mine—between you, is to touch your history with something not quite so good as itself. But I love her. Heaven is my witness that I love her!”
亲爱的马内特医生，我一直以来都知道这一点，看到你和她周围总是环绕着神圣的光芒，我一直忍着，忍着，忍到了人的极限。我曾感到，现在也依然觉得，即使是我，把我的爱放在你们之间，也会让您的过去蒙上一层不如它本身完美的阴影。但我真的爱她，老天可以作证，我真的爱她！

“Dear Doctor Manette, always knowing this, always seeing her and you with this hallowed light about you, I have forborne, and forborne, as long as it was in the nature of man to do it. Heaven is my witness that I love her!”
亲爱的马内特医生，我一直知道这一点，一直看到您和她身边环绕着这神圣的光芒，我已经忍耐了，已经忍耐到人类所能承受的极限。我感到，甚至现在仍然感到，将我的爱——即使是我的爱——放在你们之间，就是触及您的历史，而这种触及并非完全与之相配。但是我爱她。天哪，我爱她！

source: In the evening, at which season of all others Saint Antoine turned himself inside out, and sat on door-steps and window-ledges, and came to the corners of vile streets and courts, for a breath of air, Madame Defarge with her work in her hand was accustomed to pass from place to place and from group to group: a Missionary—there were many like her—such as the world will do well never to breed again. All the women knitted. They knitted worthless things; but, the mechanical work was a mechanical substitute for eating and drinking; the hands moved for the jaws and the digestive apparatus: if the bony fingers had been still, the stomachs would have been more famine-pinched.
domestication: 傍晚时分，圣安托万这个街区像是翻了个儿，人们纷纷坐在门口、窗台上，或是聚在肮脏的街角和庭院里，只为呼吸一口新鲜空气。德法日太太手里拿着活计，像传教士一样穿梭在各个角落和人群之间——像她这样的传教士还有很多，但愿这世上再也不要出现这样的人。女人们都在织东西，织出的东西其实没什么用，但这机械的工作成了她们吃饭喝水的替代品；手在动，仿佛也在代替嘴巴和肠胃工作：要是骨瘦如柴的手停下来，肚子恐怕会更饿得慌。
foreignization: 傍晚时分，在一年中所有的季节里，圣安托万都会把自己翻个底朝天，坐在门阶和窗台上，来到肮脏街道和院子的角落里，呼吸一口新鲜空气。德发日太太手里拿着活计，习惯于从一个地方到另一个地方，从一群到另一群人中间走动：一个传教士——像她这样的人有很多——这样的世界最好永远不要再培养出这样的人。所有的女人都在织东西。她们织的东西毫无价值；但这种机械的工作是吃饭和喝水的机械替代品；手的动作代替了下巴和消化器官：如果瘦骨嶙峋的手指不动，胃就会更加饥饿。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

In the evening, at which season of all others Saint Antoine turned himself inside out, and sat on door-steps and window-ledges, and came to the corners of vile streets and courts, for a breath of air, Madame Defarge with her work in her hand was accustomed to pass from place to place and from group to group: a Missionary—there were many like her—such as the world will do well never to breed again. All the women knitted. They knitted worthless things; but, the mechanical work was a mechanical substitute for eating and drinking; the hands moved for the jaws and the digestive apparatus: if the bony fingers had been still, the stomachs would have been more famine-pinched.

傍晚时分，圣安托万这个街区像是翻了个儿，人们纷纷坐在门口、窗台上，或是聚在肮脏的街角和庭院里，只为呼吸一口新鲜空气。德法日太太手里拿着活计，像传教士一样穿梭在各个角落和人群之间——像她这样的传教士还有很多，但愿这世上再也不要出现这样的人。女人们都在织东西，织出的东西其实没什么用，但这机械的工作成了她们吃饭喝水的替代品；手在动，仿佛也在代替嘴巴和肠胃工作：要是骨瘦如柴的手停下来，肚子恐怕会更饿得慌。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

In the evening, at which season of all others Saint Antoine turned himself inside out, and sat on door-steps and window-ledges, and came to the corners of vile streets and courts, for a breath of air, Madame Defarge with her work in her hand was accustomed to pass from place to place and from group to group: a Missionary—there were many like her—such as the world will do well never to breed again. All the women knitted. They knitted worthless things; but, the mechanical work was a mechanical substitute for eating and drinking; the hands moved for the jaws and the digestive apparatus: if the bony fingers had been still, the stomachs would have been more famine-pinched.

傍晚时分，在一年中所有的季节里，圣安托万都会把自己翻个底朝天，坐在门阶和窗台上，来到肮脏街道和院子的角落里，呼吸一口新鲜空气。德发日太太手里拿着活计，习惯于从一个地方到另一个地方，从一群到另一群人中间走动：一个传教士——像她这样的人有很多——这样的世界最好永远不要再培养出这样的人。所有的女人都在织东西。她们织的东西毫无价值；但这种机械的工作是吃饭和喝水的机械替代品；手的动作代替了下巴和消化器官：如果瘦骨嶙峋的手指不动，胃就会更加饥饿。

source: He had naturally repressed much, and some revulsion might have been expected in him when the occasion for repression was gone. But, it was the old scared lost look that troubled Mr. Lorry; and through his absent manner of clasping his head and drearily wandering away into his own room when they got up-stairs, Mr. Lorry was reminded of Defarge the wine-shop keeper, and the starlight ride.
domestication: 他原本就压抑了许多，一旦不再需要压抑，自然会有些反感。但让洛里先生担心的是他那副害怕、迷茫的样子。上楼时，他抱着头，无精打采地走回自己的房间，这情景让洛里先生想起了德法日酒馆老板和那次星光下的骑行。
foreignization: 他自然压抑了许多，当压抑的理由不再存在时，他本可能会产生一些反感。但是，让洛里先生感到不安的是那副老态的恐惧和迷失的表情；通过他那心不在焉地抱着头，以及他们上楼后凄凉地走回自己房间的样子，洛里先生想起了德法奇酒馆老板和星光下的骑行。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

He had naturally repressed much, and some revulsion might have been expected in him when the occasion for repression was gone. But, it was the old scared lost look that troubled Mr. Lorry; and through his absent manner of clasping his head and drearily wandering away into his own room when they got up-stairs, Mr. Lorry was reminded of Defarge the wine-shop keeper, and the starlight ride.
他原本就压抑了许多，一旦不再需要压抑，自然会有些反感。但让洛里先生担心的是他那副害怕、迷茫的样子。上楼时，他抱着头，无精打采地走回自己的房间，这情景让洛里先生想起了德法日酒馆老板和那次星光下的骑行。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

He had naturally repressed much, and some revulsion might have been expected in him when the occasion for repression was gone. But, it was the old scared lost look that troubled Mr. Lorry; and through his absent manner of clasping his head and drearily wandering away into his own room when they got up-stairs, Mr. Lorry was reminded of Defarge the wine-shop keeper, and the starlight ride.
他自然压抑了许多，当压抑的理由不再存在时，他本可能会产生一些反感。但是，让洛里先生感到不安的是那副老态的恐惧和迷失的表情；通过他那心不在焉地抱着头，以及他们上楼后凄凉地走回自己房间的样子，洛里先生想起了德法奇酒馆老板和星光下的骑行。

source: It was a profounder remark than Mr. Lorry had looked for. “True,” said he, “and fearful to reflect upon. Yet, a doubt lurks in my mind, Miss Pross, whether it is good for Doctor Manette to have that suppression always shut up within him. Indeed, it is this doubt and the uneasiness it sometimes causes me that has led me to our present confidence.”
domestication: 这番话比洛里先生预期的要深刻得多。“确实如此，”他说，“想想都让人感到害怕。不过，普罗斯小姐，我心里有个疑虑，那就是曼内特医生把这一切都压在心里，对他来说是否真的好呢？说实在的，正是这个疑虑和它偶尔带给我的不安，促使我今天跟你吐露心声。”
foreignization: 这比洛里先生预期的要深刻得多。“确实，”他说，“反思起来令人恐惧。然而，普罗斯小姐，我心中存有疑问，是否让曼内特医生一直压抑着这件事对他有好处。事实上，正是这种疑虑和它有时给我带来的不安，促使我与你进行了这次坦诚的交谈。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was a profounder remark than Mr. Lorry had looked for. “True,” said he, “and fearful to reflect upon. Yet, a doubt lurks in my mind, Miss Pross, whether it is good for Doctor Manette to have that suppression always shut up within him. Indeed, it is this doubt and the uneasiness it sometimes causes me that has led me to our present confidence.”
这番话比洛里先生预期的要深刻得多。“确实如此，”他说，“想想都让人感到害怕。不过，普罗斯小姐，我心里有个疑虑，那就是曼内特医生把这一切都压在心里，对他来说是否真的好呢？说实在的，正是这个疑虑和它偶尔带给我的不安，促使我今天跟你吐露心声。”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was a profounder remark than Mr. Lorry had looked for. “True,” said he, “and fearful to reflect upon. Yet, a doubt lurks in my mind, Miss Pross, whether it is good for Doctor Manette to have that suppression always shut up within him. Indeed, it is this doubt and the uneasiness it sometimes causes me that has led me to our present confidence.”
这比洛里先生预期的要深刻得多。“确实，”他说，“反思起来令人恐惧。然而，普罗斯小姐，我心中存有疑问，是否让曼内特医生一直压抑着这件事对他有好处。事实上，正是这种疑虑和它有时给我带来的不安，促使我与你进行了这次坦诚的交谈。”

source: “O my father!” for he stood before her when she lifted up the eyes she had momentarily darkened with her hand; “such a cruel, bad sight.”
domestication: “哎呀，爹爹！”她抬起手遮了下眼睛，等再睁开时，看到他站在面前，“怎么这么狠心，这么糟的样子。”
foreignization: “哦，我的父亲！”因为她用手暂时遮暗了眼睛，当她抬起眼睛时，他正站在她面前；“如此残忍，坏的景象。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“O my father!” for he stood before her when she lifted up the eyes she had momentarily darkened with her hand; “such a cruel, bad sight.”
“哎呀，爹爹！”她抬起手遮了下眼睛，等再睁开时，看到他站在面前，“怎么这么狠心，这么糟的样子。”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“O my father!” for he stood before her when she lifted up the eyes she had momentarily darkened with her hand; “such a cruel, bad sight.”
“哦，我的父亲！”因为她用手暂时遮暗了眼睛，当她抬起眼睛时，他正站在她面前；“如此残忍，坏的景象。”

source: “See!” cried madame, pointing with her knife. “See the old villain bound with ropes. That was well done to tie a bunch of grass upon his back. Ha, ha! That was well done. Let him eat it now!” Madame put her knife under her arm, and clapped her hands as at a play.
domestication: “看！”夫人喊道，用刀指着。“看那个老坏蛋被绳子绑着。在背上捆一捆草，这招真绝！哈哈！真是妙极了。让他自己吃吧！”夫人把刀夹在腋下，像看戏似的拍起手来。
foreignization: “看！”夫人喊道，用她的刀指着。“看那老恶棍被绳子绑着。在背上绑一捆草做得真好。哈，哈！做得真好。现在让他吃吧！”夫人把刀夹在腋下，像看戏一样拍着手。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“See!” cried madame, pointing with her knife. “See the old villain bound with ropes. That was well done to tie a bunch of grass upon his back. Ha, ha! That was well done. Let him eat it now!” Madame put her knife under her arm, and clapped her hands as at a play.
“看！”夫人喊道，用刀指着。“看那个老坏蛋被绳子绑着。在背上捆一捆草，这招真绝！哈哈！真是妙极了。让他自己吃吧！”夫人把刀夹在腋下，像看戏似的拍起手来。

“See!” cried madame, pointing with her knife. “See the old villain bound with ropes. That was well done to tie a bunch of grass upon his back. Ha, ha! That was well done. Let him eat it now!” Madame put her knife under her arm, and clapped her hands as at a play.
“看！”夫人喊道，用她的刀指着。“看那老恶棍被绳子绑着。在背上绑一捆草做得真好。哈，哈！做得真好。现在让他吃吧！”夫人把刀夹在腋下，像看戏一样拍着手。

source: This was the Carmagnole. As it passed, leaving Lucie frightened and bewildered in the doorway of the wood-sawyer’s house, the feathery snow fell as quietly and lay as white and soft, as if it had never been.
domestication: 这就是卡马尼奥拉舞。它从木锯匠家的门口经过，留下露西惊恐又迷茫。鹅毛大雪依旧静静地飘落，地上铺满了洁白柔软的雪，仿佛什么都没发生过一样。
foreignization: 这就是卡马尼奥尔舞。当它经过时，留下露西在木锯工家门口惊恐而困惑，那羽毛般的雪依旧静静地落下，依旧那么洁白柔软，仿佛从未被扰动过。

source: The show being over, the flutter in the air became quite a little storm, and the precious little bells went ringing downstairs. There was soon but one person left of all the crowd, and he, with his hat under his arm and his snuff-box in his hand, slowly passed among the mirrors on his way out.
domestication: 演出一结束，空气中的骚动便成了小小风暴，楼下的小铃铛也叮当作响。人群很快散去，最后只剩下一个人，他夹着帽子，捏着鼻烟盒，慢悠悠地穿过镜廊，往外走去。
foreignization: 演出结束，空气中弥漫的骚动变成了一场小风暴，珍贵的小铃铛在楼下响起。很快，人群之中只剩下一个人，他夹着帽子在手臂下，手里拿着鼻烟盒，慢慢穿过镜子走向出口。

source: “Why! Because he has lived so long, locked up, that he would be frightened—rave—tear himself to pieces—die—come to I know not what harm—if his door was left open.”
domestication: 为什么！因为他被关得太久了，要是门一开，他非得吓坏不可，说不定还会发疯，把自己撕成碎片，甚至一命呜呼，天知道还会出什么乱子。
foreignization: “为什么！因为他被关了这么久，如果门被打开，他会害怕——狂躁——撕裂自己——死——或不知会受到什么伤害。”

source: He seemed wilfully to mistake the word she had repeated, when he added, in a hurry, “Yes, customers; in the banking business we usually call our connection our customers. He was a French gentleman; a scientific gentleman; a man of great acquirements—a Doctor.”
domestication: 他好像故意曲解她重复的那句话，赶忙接着说：“对，客户；在银行业，我们通常把和我们有业务往来的人称为客户。他是个法国人，一个科学家，很有学问，还是个医生。”

source: At the instant when Darnay saw a rush in the eyes of the crowd, which another instant would have brought upon him, the postmaster turned his horse into the yard, the escort rode in close upon his horse’s flanks, and the postmaster shut and barred the crazy double gates. The farrier struck a blow upon them with his hammer, and the crowd groaned; but, no more was done.
domestication: 就在达内看到人群眼中闪烁着即将向他涌来的那一刻，邮车夫把马转向了院子，护送的骑兵紧贴着马身跟了进来，邮车夫随即关上摇摇欲坠的大门，并上了闩。铁匠用锤子敲了敲大门，人群发出一阵哀叹，但事情也就到此为止了。
foreignization: 就在达奈看到人群中出现一阵冲动，再过一瞬就会向他扑来时，邮车车夫把马转向了院子，护送队紧跟着他的马匹两侧骑了进来，而邮车车夫关上了那扇摇摇欲坠的双扇门并闩上了。铁匠用锤子在门上敲了一下，人群发出了一声呻吟；但除此之外，再没有别的动作。

source: For three heavy hours, the stone faces of the chateau, lion and human, stared blindly at the night. Dead darkness lay on all the landscape, dead darkness added its own hush to the hushing dust on all the roads. The burial-place had got to the pass that its little heaps of poor grass were undistinguishable from one another; the figure on the Cross might have come down, for anything that could be seen of it. In the village, taxers and taxed were fast asleep. Dreaming, perhaps, of banquets, as the starved usually do, and of ease and rest, as the driven slave and the yoked ox may, its lean inhabitants slept soundly, and were fed and freed.
domestication: 三个漫长的钟头里，城堡上的石雕人脸和狮脸盲目地盯着黑夜。四周一片死寂，黑暗中的静谧让路上的尘土也更显沉寂。墓地里的小草堆已经难以分辨，十字架上的身影或许早已下来，只是无人得见。村子里，征税的和纳税的人都沉睡不醒。饥饿的人们也许在梦中享用盛宴，被驱使的奴隶和拉犁的牛也或许在梦中享受安息与自由，村民们瘦弱的身体此刻安眠，得到了暂时的饱足与解放。
foreignization: 三个沉闷的小时里，城堡上的石脸，无论是狮子的还是人的，都茫然地盯着夜色。死寂的黑暗笼罩着整个景色，死寂的黑暗给所有道路上的尘土增添了寂静。墓地已经到了这样的地步：它的小草堆彼此无法区分；十字架上的身影可能已经下来了，因为根本看不见它。在村子里，征税者和被征税者都在熟睡。也许梦到了宴会，就像饥饿的人通常做的那样，以及轻松和休息，就像被迫劳作的奴隶和套着轭的牛可能会做的那样，这些瘦弱的居民睡得很香，得到了食物和自由。

The burial-place had got to the pass that its little heaps of poor grass were undistinguishable from one another; the figure on the Cross might have come down, for anything that could be seen of it. In the village, taxers and taxed were fast asleep. Dreaming, perhaps, of banquets, as the starved usually do, and of ease and rest, as the driven slave and the yoked ox may, its lean inhabitants slept soundly, and were fed and freed.

三个沉闷的小时里，城堡上的石脸，无论是狮子的还是人的，都茫然地盯着夜色。死寂的黑暗笼罩着整个景色，死寂的黑暗给所有道路上的尘土增添了寂静。墓地已经到了这样的地步：它的小草堆彼此无法区分；十字架上的身影可能已经下来了，因为根本看不见它。在村子里，征税者和被征税者都在熟睡。也许梦到了宴会，就像饥饿的人通常做的那样，以及轻松和休息，就像被迫劳作的奴隶和套着轭的牛可能会做的那样，这些瘦弱的居民睡得很香，得到了食物和自由。

source: “With your flying into the face of your own wittles and drink! I don’t know how scarce you mayn’t make the wittles and drink here, by your flopping tricks and your unfeeling conduct. Look at your boy: he is your’n, ain’t he? He’s as thin as a lath. Do you call yourself a mother, and not know that a mother’s first duty is to blow her boy out?”
domestication: 你这是自己往枪口上撞，跟自己的吃喝过不去！你这样胡闹，不体谅人，还不知道会让咱们的粮食和水短缺到什么地步呢。你看你家孩子，他可是你的亲骨肉，瘦得像根竹竿。你还是个母亲呢，难道不知道做母亲的头等大事是得让孩子吃得胖乎乎的？
foreignization: 你这样飞到自己的食物和饮料面前！我不知道你的这些翻腾把戏和无情的行为会让这里的食物和饮料变得多么稀少。看看你的孩子：他是你的，对吧？他瘦得像根棍子。你还自称为母亲，却不知道母亲的首要职责是让孩子吃得胖乎乎的？

source: “Then, I think,” said Mr. Lorry, “that I was very unhandsomely dealt with, and that I ought to have had a voice in the selection of my pattern. Enough! Now, my dear Lucie,” drawing his arm soothingly round her waist, “I hear them moving in the next room, and Miss Pross and I, as two formal folks of business, are anxious not to lose the final opportunity of saying something to you that you wish to hear. You leave your good father, my dear, in hands as earnest and as loving as your own; he shall be taken every conceivable care of; during the next fortnight, while you are in Warwickshire and thereabouts, even Tellson’s shall go to the wall (comparatively speaking) before him. And when, at the fortnight’s end, he comes to join you and your beloved husband, on your other fortnight’s trip in Wales, you shall say that we have sent him to you in the best health and in the happiest frame. Now, I hear Somebody’s step coming to the door. Let me kiss my dear girl with an old-fashioned bachelor blessing, before Somebody comes to claim his own.”
domestication: 然后，洛里先生说：“我觉得自己被耍了，选个模子也应该让我发表下意见才是。算了！亲爱的露西，”他安慰地搂住她的腰，“我听到隔壁有动静了，普罗斯小姐和我作为两个正经的生意人，不想错过最后的机会对你说些你想听的话。亲爱的，你放心，你的好爸爸交到我们手上，我们会像你一样真心实意地照顾他；接下来的两周，你去沃里克郡一带的时候，就算是杰森银行也要靠边站（当然，这是相对来说的）。等两周后，你爸爸去威尔士和你们会合，开始你们另一半的旅行时，你会发现我们把他照顾得健健康康、心情大好。现在，我听见有人朝门这边走来。让我在那人来认领他的宝贝之前，先给我的亲爱姑娘一个老单身汉的祝福吧。”
foreignization: “那么，我认为，”劳里先生说，“我受到了非常不公正的对待，而且我应该在选择我的模式时有发言权。够了！现在，亲爱的露西，”他安慰地把手臂绕过她的腰，“我听到隔壁房间里有人走动的声音，普罗斯小姐和我，作为两个正式的商务人士，担心会错过最后的机会对你说些你希望听到的话。你离开你的好父亲，亲爱的，他会交到和你一样热诚和充满爱意的人手中；我们会尽一切可以想象的照顾他；在接下来的两周内，当你在沃里克郡等地时，即使是特尔森银行也会（相对而言）为他让路。当两周结束时，他来加入你和你心爱的丈夫，在威尔士的另两周旅行中，你会说我们把他送到你这里时健康状况良好，心情愉快。现在，我听到有人的脚步声朝门走来。在有人来认领自己的东西之前，让我给我的亲爱的女孩一个老单身汉的祝福之吻。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Then, I think,” said Mr. Lorry, “that I was very unhandsomely dealt with, and that I ought to have had a voice in the selection of my pattern. Enough! Now, my dear Lucie,” drawing his arm soothingly round her waist, “I hear them moving in the next room, and Miss Pross and I, as two formal folks of business, are anxious not to lose the final opportunity of saying something to you that you wish to hear. You leave your good father, my dear, in hands as earnest and as loving as your own; he shall be taken every conceivable care of; during the next fortnight, while you are in Warwickshire and thereabouts, even Tellson’s shall go to the wall (comparatively speaking) before him. And when, at the fortnight’s end, he comes to join you and your beloved husband, on your other fortnight’s trip in Wales, you shall say that we have sent him to you in the best health and in the happiest frame. Now, I hear Somebody’s step coming to the door. Let me kiss my dear girl with an old-fashioned bachelor blessing, before Somebody comes to claim his own.”

然后，洛里先生说：“我觉得自己被耍了，选个模子也应该让我发表下意见才是。算了！亲爱的露西，”他安慰地搂住她的腰，“我听到隔壁有动静了，普罗斯小姐和我作为两个正经的生意人，不想错过最后的机会对你说些你想听的话。亲爱的，你放心，你的好爸爸交到我们手上，我们会像你一样真心实意地照顾他；接下来的两周，你去沃里克郡一带的时候，就算是杰森银行也要靠边站（当然，这是相对来说的）。等两周后，你爸爸去威尔士和你们会合，开始你们另一半的旅行时，你会发现我们把他照顾得健健康康、心情大好。现在，我听见有人朝门这边走来。让我在那人来认领他的宝贝之前，先给我的亲爱姑娘一个老单身汉的祝福吧。”

source: He found the Doctor reading in his arm-chair at a window. The energy which had at once supported him under his old sufferings and aggravated their sharpness, had been gradually restored to him. He was now a very energetic man indeed, with great firmness of purpose, strength of resolution, and vigour of action. In his recovered energy he was sometimes a little fitful and sudden, as he had at first been in the exercise of his other recovered faculties; but, this had never been frequently observable, and had grown more and more rare.
domestication: 他发现医生正坐在窗边的扶手椅上读书。当年那股既支撑他又加剧了他痛苦的能量，如今已渐渐恢复。现在的他确实是个充满活力的人，意志坚定，行动果敢。在恢复精力后，他有时会显得有些忽冷忽热、突如其来的，就像最初恢复其他能力时一样；不过这种情况并不常见，也越来越少了。
foreignization: 他发现医生正坐在窗户边的扶手椅上读书。曾经在他过去的痛苦中既支撑着他又加剧了痛苦的精力，已经逐渐恢复。现在他是一个非常有活力的人，具有极大的决心、坚强的意志和行动的力度。在他的恢复的精力中，有时他会有些忽冷忽热和突然，就像他在恢复其他能力时最初表现的那样；但是，这种情况从未频繁出现，并且变得越来越少见。

source: The unseen force was drawing him fast to itself, now, and all the tides and winds were setting straight and strong towards it. He left his two letters with a trusty porter, to be delivered half an hour before midnight, and no sooner; took horse for Dover; and began his journey. “For the love of Heaven, of justice, of generosity, of the honour of your noble name!” was the poor prisoner’s cry with which he strengthened his sinking heart, as he left all that was dear on earth behind him, and floated away for the Loadstone Rock.
domestication: 那股看不见的力量正迅速将他拉向自己，潮水和风向也一齐朝着那个方向奔涌。他把两封信交给了一位可靠的门房，叮嘱务必在午夜前半小时才送去，不能提前；随后他骑马前往多佛，开始了旅程。为了上天的仁慈，为了正义，为了慷慨，为了你们高贵名字的荣誉！这是那位可怜囚犯的呼喊，他用这呼喊来鼓舞自己逐渐下沉的心。离开人世间所有亲爱的亲人，就像漂向磁石岩一样，他独自踏上了不归路。
foreignization: 那股看不见的力量正在迅速将他吸引过去，现在所有的潮水和风都正直而强劲地朝那个方向流动。他把两封信交给了一位可靠的门房，吩咐在午夜前半小时送达，不得提前；然后骑马前往多佛；开始了他的旅程。“为了上天的爱、正义、慷慨、以及你高贵名字的荣誉！”这是那位可怜的囚犯的呼喊，他以此来鼓舞自己逐渐低落的心志，当他离开所有他在世上珍爱的一切，向着磁石岩漂去。

source: Master Cruncher (who was in his shirt) took this very ill, and, turning to his mother, strongly deprecated any praying away of his personal board.
domestication: 大啃（当时只穿着衬衫）非常生气，转头对他妈说，坚决反对谁要把他的饭碗给念没了。
foreignization: 大师克兰彻（当时穿着衬衫）对此非常不满，并转向他的母亲，强烈反对任何人祈祷他个人的伙食被取消。

source: The emphatic horse, cut short by the whip in a most decided negative, made a decided scramble for it, and the three other horses followed suit. Once more, the Dover mail struggled on, with the jack-boots of its passengers squashing along by its side. They had stopped when the coach stopped, and they kept close company with it. If any one of the three had had the hardihood to propose to another to walk on a little ahead into the mist and darkness, he would have put himself in a fair way of getting shot instantly as a highwayman.
domestication: 那匹性子急的马被马鞭一抽，立马尥蹶子尥得更欢了，其他三匹马也跟着一起撒起欢来。多佛尔邮车再次艰难前行，乘客们穿着高筒靴在旁边深一脚浅一脚地走着。马车一停，他们也就跟着停下，紧挨着马车走。这三人中要是有谁胆敢提议单独走进前面的雾里和黑暗中，恐怕立刻会被当作强盗给一枪崩了。
foreignization: 那匹强调的马被鞭子果断地阻止了，做出了一番明显的挣扎，其他三匹马也跟着效仿。多佛尔邮车再次挣扎着前进，乘客们的高筒靴在旁边嘎吱作响。当马车停下时，他们也停了下来，并且紧随其后。如果其中任何一人大胆地提议另一个人稍微走到前面的雾和黑暗中去，他就会有被当作强盗当场射杀的危险。

If any one of the three had had the hardihood to propose to another to walk on a little ahead into the mist and darkness, he would have put himself in a fair way of getting shot instantly as a highwayman.

source: Mr. Darnay had kissed her hand fervently and gratefully, and had turned to Mr. Stryver, whom he warmly thanked. Mr. Stryver, a man of little more than thirty, but looking twenty years older than he was, stout, loud, red, bluff, and free from any drawback of delicacy, had a pushing way of shouldering himself (morally and physically) into companies and conversations, that argued well for his shouldering his way up in life.
domestication: 达奈先生感激地亲吻了她的手，然后转向斯特莱弗先生，热情地感谢他。斯特莱弗先生不过三十出头，却显得比实际年龄老上二十岁，胖乎乎的，嗓门大，红光满面，一副粗犷豪爽的样子，完全不拘小节。他无论是在人群中还是在谈话中，总是喜欢硬挤进去，这种霸气的作风让他在生活中的确也闯出了一番天地。
foreignization: 达奈先生热烈而感激地吻了她的手，然后转向斯特里弗先生，热情地感谢他。斯特里弗先生，一个三十出头的人，但看起来比实际年龄老二十岁，体格健壮，声音洪亮，面色红润，粗犷豪爽，没有任何细腻的缺点，有一种（道德上和身体上）强行挤进人群和谈话的方式，这预示着他会在生活中步步高升。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Mr. Darnay had kissed her hand fervently and gratefully, and had turned to Mr. Stryver, whom he warmly thanked. Mr. Stryver, a man of little more than thirty, but looking twenty years older than he was, stout, loud, red, bluff, and free from any drawback of delicacy, had a pushing way of shouldering himself (morally and physically) into companies and conversations, that argued well for his shouldering his way up in life.

source: He had sunk in her arms, and his face dropped on her breast: a sight so touching, yet so terrible in the tremendous wrong and suffering which had gone before it, that the two beholders covered their faces.
domestication: 他倒在她的怀里，脸埋在她的胸前，这场景既让人心酸又让人揪心，想到之前所遭受的莫大冤屈和痛苦，两个旁观者都不禁掩面而泣。
foreignization: 他倒在她的怀抱里，脸贴在她的胸膛上：这一幕如此感人，却又因之前的巨大冤屈和痛苦而显得如此可怕，以至于两个旁观者都捂住了脸。

source: “Excuse me, as a persistent man of business. Assuming for a moment, that he was overworked; it would show itself in some renewal of this disorder?”
domestication: 不好意思，作为一个做生意的人，要是他真的累坏了，这毛病是不是又该犯了？
foreignization: “对不起，作为一个坚持经商的人。假设他过度劳累；这会在这种紊乱的某种重现中表现出来吗？”

不好意思，作为一个做生意的人，要是他真的累坏了，这毛病是不是又该犯了？

messages_foreignization.content:

source: Among the talkers, was Stryver, of the King’s Bench Bar, far on his way to state promotion, and, therefore, loud on the theme: broaching to Monseigneur, his devices for blowing the people up and exterminating them from the face of the earth, and doing without them: and for accomplishing many similar objects akin in their nature to the abolition of eagles by sprinkling salt on the tails of the race. Him, Darnay heard with a particular feeling of objection; and Darnay stood divided between going away that he might hear no more, and remaining to interpose his word, when the thing that was to be, went on to shape itself out.
domestication: 人群中就有斯特莱佛，他在王座法庭里混得风生水起，离高官厚禄只差一步之遥，因此说起话来嗓门特别大。他向贵族老爷们献计献策，如何把老百姓炸上天，从地球上彻底抹掉，再也看不见他们；还有如何干出许多类似的事，比如撒盐在鹰的尾巴上来消灭鹰。达奈听着这些话，心里特别反感；他站在那儿，犹豫不决，是走开不再听下去，还是留下来说几句公道话。事情的发展，最终让他做出了选择。
foreignization: 在这些人中，有斯特雷弗，他是王座法庭的律师，已经在通往高官的路上，因此对于这个话题大声发表意见：向蒙塞尼先生提出他的计划，这些计划包括炸毁人民并从地球上消灭他们，以及不需要他们而生存的方法：以及实现许多类似的目标，就像通过在鹰的尾巴上撒盐来消灭鹰一样。达奈对此特别反感；达奈在离开以免再听下去和留下来在事情发展到一定程度时插话之间犹豫不决。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Among the talkers, was Stryver, of the King’s Bench Bar, far on his way to state promotion, and, therefore, loud on the theme: broaching to Monseigneur, his devices for blowing the people up and exterminating them from the face of the earth, and doing without them: and for accomplishing many similar objects akin in their nature to the abolition of eagles by sprinkling salt on the tails of the race. Him, Darnay heard with a particular feeling of objection; and Darnay stood divided between going away that he might hear no more, and remaining to interpose his word, when the thing that was to be, went on to shape itself out.
人群中就有斯特莱佛，他在王座法庭里混得风生水起，离高官厚禄只差一步之遥，因此说起话来嗓门特别大。他向贵族老爷们献计献策，如何把老百姓炸上天，从地球上彻底抹掉，再也看不见他们；还有如何干出许多类似的事，比如撒盐在鹰的尾巴上来消灭鹰。达奈听着这些话，心里特别反感；他站在那儿，犹豫不决，是走开不再听下去，还是留下来说几句公道话。事情的发展，最终让他做出了选择。

source: With a wild rattle and clatter, and an inhuman abandonment of consideration not easy to be understood in these days, the carriage dashed through streets and swept round corners, with women screaming before it, and men clutching each other and clutching children out of its way. At last, swooping at a street corner by a fountain, one of its wheels came to a sickening little jolt, and there was a loud cry from a number of voices, and the horses reared and plunged.
domestication: 一阵狂乱的轰鸣和嘈杂，这辆马车全然不顾行人的安危，在街道上横冲直撞，拐弯时更是毫不减速，吓得妇女们尖声惊叫，男人们则手忙脚乱地拽着孩子躲避。终于，在一个街角的喷泉旁，马车的一个轮子猛然颠簸了一下，发出令人不安的响声，紧接着传来一阵震耳欲聋的喊叫声，马匹也受了惊，前蹄高高扬起，不停地挣扎。
foreignization: 随着一阵狂乱的 rattattle 和不人道的不顾他人感受的行为，在这些日子里很难理解，马车横冲直撞地穿过街道，急转弯时，妇女在前面尖叫，男人们互相抓着对方，把孩子从它的路上拉走。最后，在一个街角的喷泉旁猛冲而过时，它的一个轮子发出了一声令人恶心的小颠簸，许多声音大声喊叫起来，马儿人立起来并狂躁地跳跃。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

With a wild rattle and clatter, and an inhuman abandonment of consideration not easy to be understood in these days, the carriage dashed through streets and swept round corners, with women screaming before it, and men clutching each other and clutching children out of its way. At last, swooping at a street corner by a fountain, one of its wheels came to a sickening little jolt, and there was a loud cry from a number of voices, and the horses reared and plunged.
一阵狂乱的轰鸣和嘈杂，这辆马车全然不顾行人的安危，在街道上横冲直撞，拐弯时更是毫不减速，吓得妇女们尖声惊叫，男人们则手忙脚乱地拽着孩子躲避。终于，在一个街角的喷泉旁，马车的一个轮子猛然颠簸了一下，发出令人不安的响声，紧接着传来一阵震耳欲聋的喊叫声，马匹也受了惊，前蹄高高扬起，不停地挣扎。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

With a wild rattle and clatter, and an inhuman abandonment of consideration not easy to be understood in these days, the carriage dashed through streets and swept round corners, with women screaming before it, and men clutching each other and clutching children out of its way. At last, swooping at a street corner by a fountain, one of its wheels came to a sickening little jolt, and there was a loud cry from a number of voices, and the horses reared and plunged.
随着一阵狂乱的 rattattle 和不人道的不顾他人感受的行为，在这些日子里很难理解，马车横冲直撞地穿过街道，急转弯时，妇女在前面尖叫，男人们互相抓着对方，把孩子从它的路上拉走。最后，在一个街角的喷泉旁猛冲而过时，它的一个轮子发出了一声令人恶心的小颠簸，许多声音大声喊叫起来，马儿人立起来并狂躁地跳跃。

source: “Not exactly so. I mean to tell you, Mr. Stryver,” said Mr. Lorry, reddening, “that I will hear no disrespectful word of that young lady from any lips; and that if I knew any man—which I hope I do not—whose taste was so coarse, and whose temper was so overbearing, that he could not restrain himself from speaking disrespectfully of that young lady at this desk, not even Tellson’s should prevent my giving him a piece of my mind.”
domestication: 不完全是这样。劳瑞先生脸一红，对斯特莱佛先生说道：“我是想告诉你，我听不得任何人对那位小姐说半点不敬的话；要是真有哪个男人——但愿没有——口味这么粗俗，脾气又这么霸道，竟在这张桌子前对那位小姐出言不逊，我就是拼了跟泰勒森银行翻脸，也要好好教训他一顿。”
foreignization: “不完全是这样。我的意思是，斯特莱弗先生，”劳里先生脸红着说，“我决不允许任何人对那位小姐出言不敬；如果我知道有哪个男人——我希望没有这样的男人——品味如此粗俗，脾气如此暴躁，以至于不能克制自己，在这张桌子前对那位小姐出言不敬，即使他是泰勒森银行的顾客，我也要给他点颜色看看。”

source: There were in the cell, a chair, a table, and a straw mattress. As the gaoler made a general inspection of these objects, and of the four walls, before going out, a wandering fancy wandered through the mind of the prisoner leaning against the wall opposite to him, that this gaoler was so unwholesomely bloated, both in face and person, as to look like a man who had been drowned and filled with water. When the gaoler was gone, he thought in the same wandering way, “Now am I left, as if I were dead.” Stopping then, to look down at the mattress, he turned from it with a sick feeling, and thought, “And here in these crawling creatures is the first condition of the body after death.”
domestication: 牢房里有张椅子、一张桌子和一张稻草垫。狱卒在离开前，仔细检查了这些物件和四面墙。这时，靠在对面墙上的囚犯脑子里浮现出一个怪念头：这狱卒脸和身体都肿得不像样，活像个淹死灌满水的人。等狱卒走了，他心里还胡思乱想着：“现在我就像个死人一样被留在这儿。” 他停下来看着那张稻草垫，觉得一阵恶心，转过身去，心想：“这些爬来爬去的小虫子，不就是人死后身体的第一状态吗？”
foreignization: 牢房里有一把椅子、一张桌子和一张草垫子。当狱卒在出去之前对这些物品和四面墙壁进行一番检查时，一个漫无边际的念头在靠在对面墙上的囚犯心中游荡，觉得这个狱卒的脸和身体都肿得不健康，看起来像一个溺水后被水充满的人。狱卒走后，他以同样的漫无目的的方式想着，“现在我被留下了，好像我已经死了。”然后，他停下来俯视着草垫子，带着一种恶心的感觉转过身去，想道，“而在这里，在这些爬行的生物中，是死后身体的第一种状态。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

There were in the cell, a chair, a table, and a straw mattress. As the gaoler made a general inspection of these objects, and of the four walls, before going out, a wandering fancy wandered through the mind of the prisoner leaning against the wall opposite to him, that this gaoler was so unwholesomely bloated, both in face and person, as to look like a man who had been drowned and filled with water. When the gaoler was gone, he thought in the same wandering way, “Now am I left, as if I were dead.” Stopping then, to look down at the mattress, he turned from it with a sick feeling, and thought, “And here in these crawling creatures is the first condition of the body after death.”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

source: “A likely thing, too!” replied the strong woman. “If it was ever intended that I should go across salt water, do you suppose Providence would have cast my lot in an island?”
domestication: “这也太离谱了吧！”那强壮的女人回应道。“要是老天爷真打算让我出远门过海，怎么会让我生在这孤岛上呢？”
foreignization: “这也可能！”强壮的女人回答道。“如果命中注定我要渡过大海，你觉得上天会把我安排在一个岛上吗？”

source: Monseigneur had been out at a little supper last night, where the Comedy and the Grand Opera were charmingly represented. Monseigneur was out at a little supper most nights, with fascinating company. So polite and so impressible was Monseigneur, that the Comedy and the Grand Opera had far more influence with him in the tiresome articles of state affairs and state secrets, than the needs of all France. A happy circumstance for France, as the like always is for all countries similarly favoured!—always was for England (by way of example), in the regretted days of the merry Stuart who sold it.
domestication: 昨晚，大人外出参加了一场小型晚宴，那里的喜剧和大歌剧演得十分精彩。大人几乎每晚都会外出赴宴，与迷人的伙伴们共度良宵。大人如此有礼貌，又如此容易受人影响，以至于喜剧和大歌剧对他在枯燥的国务和机密事务上的影响，远胜过整个法国的需求。这对法国来说真是件幸事，对于所有拥有类似幸运的国家亦是如此！——就如同英格兰，在那位令人怀念的欢乐斯图亚特王统治时期，他将国家出卖给外人的时候，也是如此。
foreignization: 蒙塞居尔昨晚外出参加了一个小型晚宴，喜剧和大歌剧在那里迷人地演出。蒙塞居尔几乎每晚都外出参加这样的小型晚宴，与迷人的同伴共度时光。蒙塞居尔如此有礼貌且容易受感动，以至于喜剧和大歌剧对他在烦人的国务和国家机密方面的影响远超过整个法国的需要。这对法国来说是一件幸事，正如类似情况总是对所有同样幸运的国家一样！——总是对英格兰有益（举例而言），在那位令人怀念的快乐斯图亚特将其出卖的年代。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Monseigneur had been out at a little supper last night, where the Comedy and the Grand Opera were charmingly represented. Monseigneur was out at a little supper most nights, with fascinating company. So polite and so impressible was Monseigneur, that the Comedy and the Grand Opera had far more influence with him in the tiresome articles of state affairs and state secrets, than the needs of all France. A happy circumstance for France, as the like always is for all countries similarly favoured!—always was for England (by way of example), in the regretted days of the merry Stuart who sold it.

source: As the concentrated expression returned to his forehead, he seemed to become conscious that it was in hers too. He turned her full to the light, and looked at her.
domestication: 他额头上凝聚的神情仿佛让他意识到她也是一样。他把她转到灯光下，细细打量起来。

source: The officiating undertakers made some protest against these changes in the ceremonies; but, the river being alarmingly near, and several voices remarking on the efficacy of cold immersion in bringing refractory members of the profession to reason, the protest was faint and brief. The remodelled procession started, with a chimney-sweep driving the hearse—advised by the regular driver, who was perched beside him, under close inspection, for the purpose—and with a pieman, also attended by his cabinet minister, driving the mourning coach. A bear-leader, a popular street character of the time, was impressed as an additional ornament, before the cavalcade had gone far down the Strand; and his bear, who was black and very mangy, gave quite an Undertaking air to that part of the procession in which he walked.
domestication: 司仪的殡葬人员对这些仪式上的改动有些不满；但河水逼近，形势逼人，再加上有人提及冷水澡能让顽固的专业人士清醒过来，这抗议也就有气无力，很快收场了。改过的送葬队伍出发了，一个扫烟囱的师傅驾着灵车——真正的司机坐在他旁边监督指导——而一个卖馅饼的小贩，在他的“内阁大臣”的陪同下，驾驶着哀悼马车。送葬队伍刚沿着斯特兰德街没走多久，就拉上了一个当时很受欢迎的街头人物——驯熊师来增加装饰效果。他的熊又黑又瘦，但在队伍中走动时，倒也给这一段送葬行列增添了几分殡葬的气息。
foreignization: 主持丧礼的人对这些仪式的改变提出了一些抗议；但是，河水令人不安地接近，而且有几个人指出冷水浸泡在使顽固的职业成员恢复理智方面的效果，因此抗议微弱而短暂。经过改造的送葬队伍出发了，一名烟囱清扫工驾驶着灵车——由正式驾驶员坐在他旁边密切监督——并且一名卖馅饼的小贩，在其内阁大臣的陪同下，驾驶着殡仪马车。在送葬队伍沿着斯特兰德街没走多远之前，一名当时受欢迎的街头人物——驯熊人——被征召为额外的装饰，他的那只又黑又脏的熊为他所行进的那部分送葬队伍增添了一种丧葬的气息。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The officiating undertakers made some protest against these changes in the ceremonies; but, the river being alarmingly near, and several voices remarking on the efficacy of cold immersion in bringing refractory members of the profession to reason, the protest was faint and brief. The remodelled procession started, with a chimney-sweep driving the hearse—advised by the regular driver, who was perched beside him, under close inspection, for the purpose—and with a pieman, also attended by his cabinet minister, driving the mourning coach. A bear-leader, a popular street character of the time, was impressed as an additional ornament, before the cavalcade had gone far down the Strand; and his bear, who was black and very mangy, gave quite an Undertaking air to that part of the procession in which he walked.

The remodelled procession started, with a chimney-sweep driving the hearse—advised by the regular driver, who was perched beside him, under close inspection, for the purpose—and with a pieman, also attended by his cabinet minister, driving the mourning coach. A bear-leader, a popular street character of the time, was impressed as an additional ornament, before the cavalcade had gone far down the Strand; and his bear, who was black and very mangy, gave quite an Undertaking air to that part of the procession in which he walked.

主持丧礼的人对这些仪式的改变提出了一些抗议；但是，河水令人不安地接近，而且有几个人指出冷水浸泡在使顽固的职业成员恢复理智方面的效果，因此抗议微弱而短暂。经过改造的送葬队伍出发了，一名烟囱清扫工驾驶着灵车——由正式驾驶员坐在他旁边密切监督——并且一名卖馅饼的小贩，在其内阁大臣的陪同下，驾驶着殡仪马车。在送葬队伍沿着斯特兰德街没走多远之前，一名当时受欢迎的街头人物——驯熊人——被征召为额外的装饰，他的那只又黑又脏的熊为他所行进的那部分送葬队伍增添了一种丧葬的气息。

source: It was such a curious corner in its acoustical properties, such a peculiar Ear of a place, that as Mr. Lorry stood at the open window, looking for the father and daughter whose steps he heard, he fancied they would never approach. Not only would the echoes die away, as though the steps had gone; but, echoes of other steps that never came would be heard in their stead, and would die away for good when they seemed close at hand. However, father and daughter did at last appear, and Miss Pross was ready at the street door to receive them.
domestication: 这个角落的声学特性真是奇怪，就像是个特殊的“耳朵”。老罗瑞先生站在敞开的窗前，听着父女俩的脚步声，心里琢磨着他们可能永远都不会出现。不仅脚步声会逐渐消失，仿佛人已经走远，还会听到其他根本没来的人的脚步声，这些声音在似乎近在咫尺时也会彻底消失。不过，父女俩最终还是出现了，普罗斯小姐也在门口等着迎接他们。
foreignization: 这是一个在声学特性上非常奇特的角落，一个如此 peculiar 地方的耳朵，以至于当洛里先生站在敞开的窗户前，听着脚步声寻找父亲和女儿时，他想象着他们永远不会靠近。不仅那些脚步声会消失，仿佛人已经走远；还会听到其他从未出现的脚步声的回音，当它们似乎近在咫尺时，最终也会彻底消失。然而，父亲和女儿最后还是出现了，普罗斯小姐也已经在街门口准备好迎接他们。

However, father and daughter did at last appear, and Miss Pross was ready at the street door to receive them.

source: His daughter had one of her hands drawn through his arm, as she sat by him, and the other pressed upon it. She had drawn close to him, in her dread of the scene, and in her pity for the prisoner. Her forehead had been strikingly expressive of an engrossing terror and compassion that saw nothing but the peril of the accused. This had been so very noticeable, so very powerfully and naturally shown, that starers who had had no pity for him were touched by her; and the whisper went about, “Who are they?”
domestication: 他的女儿一只手挽着他的胳膊，另一只手按在他身上。她因为害怕现场的气氛，又同情被告，便紧挨着他坐了下来。她的前额明显流露出全神贯注的恐惧和怜悯，眼中只有被告的危险。这神情如此显眼，表现得如此自然真切，以至于那些原本对他毫无同情之心的人，也被她打动了。于是，周围开始有人低声询问：“他们是谁？”
foreignization: 他的女儿一只手挽着他的胳膊，坐在他身旁，另一只手按在他的手臂上。由于害怕这场面，也出于对被告的同情，她靠近了他。她的额头明显地流露出一种全神贯注的恐惧和怜悯，眼中只有被告的危险。这种神情非常引人注意，表现得如此强烈而自然，以至于那些对他毫无同情之心的人也被她打动了；于是人群中传开了低语：“他们是谁？”

source: Under the over-swinging lamps—swinging ever brighter in the better streets, and ever dimmer in the worse—and by lighted shops, gay crowds, illuminated coffee-houses, and theatre-doors, to one of the city gates. Soldiers with lanterns, at the guard-house there. “Your papers, travellers!” “See here then, Monsieur the Officer,” said Defarge, getting down, and taking him gravely apart, “these are the papers of monsieur inside, with the white head. They were consigned to me, with him, at the—” He dropped his voice, there was a flutter among the military lanterns, and one of them being handed into the coach by an arm in uniform, the eyes connected with the arm looked, not an every day or an every night look, at monsieur with the white head. “It is well. Forward!” from the uniform. “Adieu!” from Defarge. And so, under a short grove of feebler and feebler over-swinging lamps, out under the great grove of stars.
domestication: 头顶上挂着摇曳的灯盏——在好街巷里愈发明亮，在破败处渐趋昏暗——沿途经过灯火通明的店铺、喧闹的人群、亮堂的咖啡馆和剧院的门口，直抵城门。城门那儿，守卫房里士兵提着灯笼。“出示证件，旅客们！”“官爷您看，”德法热下了马车，郑重其事地将那军官拉到一边，“这是车里那位白发先生的证件。在——”他压低了声音，军中的灯笼一阵骚动，其中一盏被制服的手臂递进了车厢，这双眼睛打量着白发先生，那眼神可不像是平时随便看看。“行了。继续前进！”制服下的声音命令道。“再见了！”德法热挥了挥手。于是，在一排越来越微弱的摇晃灯盏下，一行人出了城门，来到了星光灿烂的天穹之下。
foreignization: 在摇摆过度的灯下——在更好的街道上越来越亮，在更差的街道上越来越暗——以及灯光通明的店铺、欢乐的人群、灯火辉煌的咖啡馆和剧院门口，来到城市的一扇大门前。那里守卫室里有提着灯笼的士兵。“你们的证件，旅客们！”“请看这里，先生，”德法日下车，严肃地把他拉到一边说，“这是车里白发先生的证件。它们连同他一起，在——”他压低了声音，军用灯笼里一阵骚动，其中一个灯笼被一只穿制服的手递进马车，与这只手相连的眼睛看着白发先生，那眼神并非平日或夜晚常有的。“很好。出发！”穿制服的人说。“再见！”德法日道。于是，在一排越来越弱的吊灯下，穿过繁星密布的天空。

Under the over-swinging lamps—swinging ever brighter in the better streets, and ever dimmer in the worse—and by lighted shops, gay crowds, illuminated coffee-houses, and theatre-doors, to one of the city gates. Soldiers with lanterns, at the guard-house there. “Your papers, travellers!” “See here then, Monsieur the Officer,” said Defarge, getting down, and taking him gravely apart, “these are the papers of monsieur inside, with the white head. They were consigned to me, with him, at the—” He dropped his voice, there was a flutter among the military lanterns, and one of them being handed into the coach by an arm in uniform, the eyes connected with the arm looked, not an every day or an every night look, at monsieur with the white head. “It is well. Forward!” from the uniform. “Adieu!” from Defarge. And so, under a short grove of feebler and feebler over-swinging lamps, out under the great grove of stars.

source: “As a wife and mother,” cried Lucie, most earnestly, “I implore you to have pity on me and not to exercise any power that you possess, against my innocent husband, but to use it in his behalf. O sister-woman, think of me. As a wife and mother!”

source: It was but the inquiry of his first confusion and astonishment, the answer being obvious. If the impression were not produced by a real corresponding and sufficient cause, how came he, Jarvis Lorry, there? How came he to have fallen asleep, in his clothes, on the sofa in Doctor Manette’s consulting-room, and to be debating these points outside the Doctor’s bedroom door in the early morning?
domestication: 这不过是他的第一个疑惑和惊讶罢了，答案显而易见。要是没有真实且充分的原因，他又怎么会在这里呢？他又怎么会穿着衣服在曼内特医生的诊疗室沙发上睡着了，大清早还在医生卧室门外琢磨这些问题呢？
foreignization: 这只是他最初困惑和惊讶时的一个疑问，答案是显而易见的。如果这种印象不是由一个真实且充分的原因产生的，那么他，贾维斯·洛里，怎么会在这里？他怎么会穿着衣服在马内特医生的会诊室的沙发上睡着了，并且在清晨站在医生卧室门外争论这些问题？

source: It was Stryver’s grand peculiarity that he always seemed too big for any place, or space. He was so much too big for Tellson’s, that old clerks in distant corners looked up with looks of remonstrance, as though he squeezed them against the wall. The House itself, magnificently reading the paper quite in the far-off perspective, lowered displeased, as if the Stryver head had been butted into its responsible waistcoat.
domestication: 斯特莱弗有个特别的地方，就是他走到哪儿都显得地方太小。他在泰勒森银行里头，那模样就好像把个小屋子撑得快要爆了。老职员们躲在角落里，抬头看他，一脸不满，仿佛被他挤得贴在墙上似的。连那银行大厦自己，远远地在那儿看报，都不高兴了，好像斯特莱弗的脑袋撞上了它的体面外套。
foreignization: 斯特莱弗的最大特点是，他总是显得比任何地方或空间都要大。他在泰勒森银行里显得如此之大，以至于角落里的老职员们都抬头投去抗议的目光，仿佛他把他们挤到了墙上。那栋大楼本身，在远处威严地读着报纸，也显得不悦，仿佛斯特莱弗的脑袋撞进了它的负责任的背心。

source: The prison of La Force was a gloomy prison, dark and filthy, and with a horrible smell of foul sleep in it. Extraordinary how soon the noisome flavour of imprisoned sleep, becomes manifest in all such places that are ill cared for!
domestication: 拉弗斯监狱阴森森的，又黑又脏，还散发着一股令人作呕的沉睡臭味。奇怪的是，这种难闻的气味在所有疏于管理的牢房里很快就会显现出来！
foreignization: 拉弗斯监狱是一座阴郁的监狱，昏暗肮脏，里面弥漫着一种难闻的恶臭。奇怪的是，这种囚禁中的污浊气息在所有这类照料不善的地方很快就会显现出来！

source: “Now, don’t let my announcement of the name make you uncomfortable, Sydney,” said Mr. Stryver, preparing him with ostentatious friendliness for the disclosure he was about to make, “because I know you don’t mean half you say; and if you meant it all, it would be of no importance. I make this little preface, because you once mentioned the young lady to me in slighting terms.”
domestication: 现在，别让我提到名字让你不自在，悉尼，”斯特瑞弗先生故作友好地准备着要透露的事，“因为我知道你说话从不算数；就算真算数，也无关紧要。我先说这些，是因为你以前曾经轻描淡写地提到过这位小姐。
foreignization: “现在，不要让我说出这个名字让你感到不自在，悉尼，”斯特莱佛先生以夸张的友好态度准备向他透露即将要说的事情，“因为我知道你所说的话只有一半是认真的；即使全都是认真的，也没有什么重要性。我做这个小小的开场白，是因为你曾经用轻蔑的口吻向我提到过这位年轻女士。”

source: The Concord bed-chamber being always assigned to a passenger by the mail, and passengers by the mail being always heavily wrapped up from head to foot, the room had the odd interest for the establishment of the Royal George, that although but one kind of man was seen to go into it, all kinds and varieties of men came out of it. Consequently, another drawer, and two porters, and several maids and the landlady, were all loitering by accident at various points of the road between the Concord and the coffee-room, when a gentleman of sixty, formally dressed in a brown suit of clothes, pretty well worn, but very well kept, with large square cuffs and large flaps to the pockets, passed along on his way to his breakfast.
domestication: 皇家乔治旅馆的康科德客房总是分配给邮车乘客，而这些乘客从头到脚都裹得严严实实的。因此，这个房间对旅馆来说有个奇怪的现象：虽然进去的都是同一种人，出来的却是各种各样的人。所以，当一位六十岁左右的先生，穿着一套棕色的衣服，虽然有些旧但保养得很好，大大的方形袖口和大大的口袋盖，正走向早餐室时，另一个行李员、两个搬运工、几个女仆还有老板娘，都“恰巧”在康科德客房到咖啡室之间的不同地方晃悠。
foreignization: 由于康科德卧车总是分配给邮车乘客，而邮车乘客总是从头到脚裹得严严实实，所以这对皇家乔治酒店来说，有一个奇怪的兴趣点，那就是虽然只有一种类型的人被看到进入这个房间，但各种各样的人都会从这个房间出来。因此，当一位六十岁的先生穿着一套棕色衣服（相当旧了，但保养得很好），有着大的方形袖口和大口袋盖，正式地走过前往早餐的路上时，另一位服务员、两名搬运工、几名女仆和女老板都恰好在康科德卧车与咖啡厅之间的各个地点闲逛。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The Concord bed-chamber being always assigned to a passenger by the mail, and passengers by the mail being always heavily wrapped up from head to foot, the room had the odd interest for the establishment of the Royal George, that although but one kind of man was seen to go into it, all kinds and varieties of men came out of it. Consequently, another drawer, and two porters, and several maids and the landlady, were all loitering by accident at various points of the road between the Concord and the coffee-room, when a gentleman of sixty, formally dressed in a brown suit of clothes, pretty well worn, but very well kept, with large square cuffs and large flaps to the pockets, passed along on his way to his breakfast.

source: Any one of these partners would have disinherited his son on the question of rebuilding Tellson’s. In this respect the House was much on a par with the Country; which did very often disinherit its sons for suggesting improvements in laws and customs that had long been highly objectionable, but were only the more respectable.
domestication: 这些合伙人中的任何一个，为了重建泰勒森银行的事，都会把儿子逐出家门。在这个问题上，这家银行和国家差不多；国家也常常因为子孙提出改革那些早已令人不满却愈发体面的法律和习俗而剥夺他们的继承权。
foreignization: 这些合伙人中的任何一个都会因为重建泰勒森银行的问题而剥夺其儿子的继承权。在这方面，这家银行与国家非常相似；国家经常因为建议改进长期被严重反对但因此更加体面的法律和习俗而剥夺其子孙的继承权。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Any one of these partners would have disinherited his son on the question of rebuilding Tellson’s. In this respect the House was much on a par with the Country; which did very often disinherit its sons for suggesting improvements in laws and customs that had long been highly objectionable, but were only the more respectable.
这些合伙人中的任何一个，为了重建泰勒森银行的事，都会把儿子逐出家门。在这个问题上，这家银行和国家差不多；国家也常常因为子孙提出改革那些早已令人不满却愈发体面的法律和习俗而剥夺他们的继承权。

source: He stooped a little, and with his tattered blue cap pointed under the carriage. All his fellows stooped to look under the carriage.
domestication: 他弯下腰，用破旧的蓝帽子指着车底。大伙儿也都跟着弯腰，一起往车底下瞧。
foreignization: 他弯下腰一点，用他破旧的蓝帽子指着马车下面。他的所有同伴都弯腰去看马车下面。

source: The gentleman from Tellson’s had nothing left for it but to empty his glass with an air of stolid desperation, settle his odd little flaxen wig at the ears, and follow the waiter to Miss Manette’s apartment. It was a large, dark room, furnished in a funereal manner with black horsehair, and loaded with heavy dark tables. These had been oiled and oiled, until the two tall candles on the table in the middle of the room were gloomily reflected on every leaf; as if they were buried, in deep graves of black mahogany, and no light to speak of could be expected from them until they were dug out.
domestication: 这位来自泰勒森银行的先生也只能硬着头皮喝干杯中的酒，理了理耳朵边那顶古怪的小亚麻假发，跟着侍者往马内特小姐的房间走去。这是一间又大又暗的房间，黑色马毛装饰得像办丧事一样，还摆满了沉重的深色桌子。这些桌子被油了一层又一层，以至于中间桌上的两根高蜡烛在每块桌面上都映出阴沉的倒影，仿佛它们被埋在了黑胡桃木的深坑里，要挖出来才能见到光明。
foreignization: 来自泰勒森的绅士除了以一种呆板绝望的神情倒空他的酒杯，整理好他那奇特的小亚麻色假发的两侧，然后跟着服务员去曼内特小姐的公寓外，别无他法。这是一个大而昏暗的房间，用黑色马海毛以葬礼的方式布置，并摆满了沉重的深色桌子。这些桌子已经被油擦了又擦，直到房间中间桌子上的两根高高的蜡烛在每一片叶子上都昏暗地反射出来；仿佛它们被埋在了深黑色胡桃木的坟墓里，除非将它们挖出，否则几乎不可能期望从中得到任何光明。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The gentleman from Tellson’s had nothing left for it but to empty his glass with an air of stolid desperation, settle his odd little flaxen wig at the ears, and follow the waiter to Miss Manette’s apartment. It was a large, dark room, furnished in a funereal manner with black horsehair, and loaded with heavy dark tables. These had been oiled and oiled, until the two tall candles on the table in the middle of the room were gloomily reflected on every leaf; as if they were buried, in deep graves of black mahogany, and no light to speak of could be expected from them until they were dug out.
这位来自泰勒森银行的先生也只能硬着头皮喝干杯中的酒，理了理耳朵边那顶古怪的小亚麻假发，跟着侍者往马内特小姐的房间走去。这是一间又大又暗的房间，黑色马毛装饰得像办丧事一样，还摆满了沉重的深色桌子。这些桌子被油了一层又一层，以至于中间桌上的两根高蜡烛在每块桌面上都映出阴沉的倒影，仿佛它们被埋在了黑胡桃木的深坑里，要挖出来才能见到光明。

It was a large, dark room, furnished in a funereal manner with black horsehair, and loaded with heavy dark tables. These had been oiled and oiled, until the two tall candles on the table in the middle of the room were gloomily reflected on every leaf; as if they were buried, in deep graves of black mahogany, and no light to speak of could be expected from them until they were dug out.
来自泰勒森的绅士除了以一种呆板绝望的神情倒空他的酒杯，整理好他那奇特的小亚麻色假发的两侧，然后跟着服务员去曼内特小姐的公寓外，别无他法。这是一个大而昏暗的房间，用黑色马海毛以葬礼的方式布置，并摆满了沉重的深色桌子。这些桌子已经被油擦了又擦，直到房间中间桌子上的两根高高的蜡烛在每一片叶子上都昏暗地反射出来；仿佛它们被埋在了深黑色胡桃木的坟墓里，除非将它们挖出，否则几乎不可能期望从中得到任何光明。

source: This answer had a happy effect upon the audience. Cries in exaltation of the well-known good physician rent the hall. So capriciously were the people moved, that tears immediately rolled down several ferocious countenances which had been glaring at the prisoner a moment before, as if with impatience to pluck him out into the streets and kill him.
domestication: 这个回答让听众十分高兴。大厅里立刻响起了对这位名医的赞美之声。人们的情绪变化莫测，刚才还恶狠狠盯着被告，好像迫不及待要将他拖出去打死的几张凶神恶煞的脸，此刻已是热泪盈眶。
foreignization: 这个回答对听众产生了愉快的影响。赞美那位著名的良医的呼喊声充满了大厅。人们的情绪如此反复无常，以至于刚才还凶神恶煞般瞪着被告、似乎迫不及待要将他拖出去杀死的几张面孔上，立刻滚下了泪水。

source: The mender of roads looked through rather than at the low ceiling, and pointed as if he saw the gallows somewhere in the sky.
domestication: 修路的人没看着低矮的天花板，而是望向了别处，还用手一指，好像在天空中看到了什么绞架似的。

source: The drunken patriot had them in his cap, and produced them. Casting his eyes over Gabelle’s letter, the same personage in authority showed some disorder and surprise, and looked at Darnay with a close attention.
domestication: 醉醺醺的爱国者从帽子里掏出了那些东西。他扫了一眼加贝尔的信，这当官的一下子显得有些慌乱和惊讶，然后紧紧盯着达奈看。
foreignization: 醉酒的爱国者从他的帽子里拿出了那些东西，并展示了出来。扫视了加贝尔的信后，这位有权势的人显得有些混乱和惊讶，并以密切的注意力看着达奈。

source: On these few steps of his dangerous way, Charles Darnay had set his foot according to Doctor Manette’s reiterated instructions. The same cautious counsel directed every step that lay before him, and had prepared every inch of his road.
domestication: 查尔斯·达奈在这危险之旅的几步上，都按照曼内特医生反复叮嘱的那样行事。这谨慎的建议指导着他接下来的每一步，也铺好了他路上的每一步。
foreignization: 在这危险旅途的几步上，查尔斯·达奈按照马内特医生一再叮嘱的指示迈出了脚步。同样的谨慎建议指导着他前面的每一步，并且为他准备好了路上的每一寸。

source: Therefore, when Sunday came, the mender of roads was not enchanted (though he said he was) to find that madame was to accompany monsieur and himself to Versailles. It was additionally disconcerting to have madame knitting all the way there, in a public conveyance; it was additionally disconcerting yet, to have madame in the crowd in the afternoon, still with her knitting in her hands as the crowd waited to see the carriage of the King and Queen.
domestication: 因此，周日一到，修路工虽然嘴上说很高兴，但其实心里并不怎么乐意得知夫人要陪先生和他一起去凡尔赛宫。更让他不安的是，夫人一路上都在公共马车上织毛衣；到了下午，人群里等国王王后马车经过时，夫人手上还一直拿着那未完的毛衣，这更是让人心烦意乱。
foreignization: 因此，当星期天到来时，修路工发现夫人将陪同先生和他自己去凡尔赛宫，并不感到高兴（虽然他说他很高兴）。更令人不安的是，在公共交通工具上，夫人一路上都在织毛衣；更令人不安的是，下午在人群中，夫人仍然手里拿着她的毛衣，等待观看国王和王后的马车。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Therefore, when Sunday came, the mender of roads was not enchanted (though he said he was) to find that madame was to accompany monsieur and himself to Versailles. It was additionally disconcerting to have madame knitting all the way there, in a public conveyance; it was additionally disconcerting yet, to have madame in the crowd in the afternoon, still with her knitting in her hands as the crowd waited to see the carriage of the King and Queen.
因此，周日一到，修路工虽然嘴上说很高兴，但其实心里并不怎么乐意得知夫人要陪先生和他一起去凡尔赛宫。更让他不安的是，夫人一路上都在公共马车上织毛衣；到了下午，人群里等国王王后马车经过时，夫人手上还一直拿着那未完的毛衣，这更是让人心烦意乱。

source: “Oh dear yes! Yes. Oh yes, you’re eligible!” said Mr. Lorry. “If you say eligible, you are eligible.”
domestication: “哎呀，没错！对，当然可以！您符合条件！”洛里先生说。“只要您说符合条件，那您就是符合条件。”

source: In a quarter of an hour Monseigneur was ready, and sat down alone to his sumptuous and choice supper. His chair was opposite to the window, and he had taken his soup, and was raising his glass of Bordeaux to his lips, when he put it down.
domestication: 一刻钟后，老爷准备妥当，独自一人坐下来享用他那丰盛又精致的晚餐。他的座位正对着窗户，汤已经喝完了，正要举杯品尝波尔多红酒，却突然停了下来。
foreignization: 一刻钟后，大人准备好了，独自坐下来享用他丰盛而精选的晚餐。他的椅子对着窗户，他已经喝了汤，正要把波尔多酒杯举到唇边，这时他又放下了。

source: “I know, my dear, I know. I have seen it many times. Don’t be frightened! Not one of them would harm you.”
domestication: 我知道，亲爱的，我知道。我见多了，别害怕！他们一个都不会伤害你的。
foreignization: 我知道，亲爱的，我知道。我见过很多次了。不要害怕！他们一个都不会伤害你。

source: “My dear Manette, I am anxious to have your opinion, in confidence, on a very curious case in which I am deeply interested; that is to say, it is very curious to me; perhaps, to your better information it may be less so.”
domestication: 亲爱的曼内特，我有个非常奇特的案子，想听听你的高见，咱们私下里说说。这案子对我来说真是稀奇古怪，不过以你见多识广，可能就不觉得那么奇怪了。
foreignization: “我亲爱的马内特，我希望能私下听听你对一个非常奇特的案件的意见；我对此案深感兴趣；对我来说，这非常奇特；也许，对你来说，凭借你更丰富的知识，它可能就不那么奇特了。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“My dear Manette, I am anxious to have your opinion, in confidence, on a very curious case in which I am deeply interested; that is to say, it is very curious to me; perhaps, to your better information it may be less so.”

“我亲爱的马内特，我希望能私下听听你对一个非常奇特的案件的意见；我对此案深感兴趣；对我来说，这非常奇特；也许，对你来说，凭借你更丰富的知识，它可能就不那么奇特了。”

source: It was the popular theme for jests; it was the best cure for headache, it infallibly prevented the hair from turning grey, it imparted a peculiar delicacy to the complexion, it was the National Razor which shaved close: who kissed La Guillotine, looked through the little window and sneezed into the sack. It was the sign of the regeneration of the human race. It superseded the Cross. Models of it were worn on breasts from which the Cross was discarded, and it was bowed down to and believed in where the Cross was denied.
domestication: 这可是大家茶余饭后的谈资；它是治头痛的灵丹妙药，能防止头发变白，还能让皮肤细嫩光滑；它就像一把国民剃刀，谁要是亲吻了断头台，从那小窗户看过去，最后只能在袋子里打个喷嚏。它象征着人类的重生，取代了十字架的位置。人们胸前佩戴它的模型，抛弃了十字架，对它顶礼膜拜，相信它的地方，就不再信奉十字架。
foreignization: 它曾是人们取笑的主题；它是治疗头痛的最好药方，它绝对能防止头发变灰，它赋予肤色一种特别的细腻，它是剃得干净的国民剃刀：谁亲吻了断头台，从那个小窗口望出去，然后打了个喷嚏到袋子里。它是人类种族再生的标志。它取代了十字架。它的模型被佩戴在抛弃了十字架的胸膛上，并且在否认十字架的地方，它被顶礼膜拜并信仰着。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was the sign of the regeneration of the human race. It superseded the Cross. Models of it were worn on breasts from which the Cross was discarded, and it was bowed down to and believed in where the Cross was denied.

source: To be confronted with such pity, and such earnest youth and beauty, was far more trying to the accused than to be confronted with all the crowd. Standing, as it were, apart with her on the edge of his grave, not all the staring curiosity that looked on, could, for the moment, nerve him to remain quite still. His hurried right hand parcelled out the herbs before him into imaginary beds of flowers in a garden; and his efforts to control and steady his breathing shook the lips from which the colour rushed to his heart. The buzz of the great flies was loud again.
domestication: 面对这样的同情，还有这般青春和美丽，对被告来说，比面对所有围观的人群都要难熬得多。仿佛站在他坟墓边缘的只有她和他，一时之间，再多的好奇目光也无法让他镇定下来。他匆匆忙忙地右手把面前的草药分成了想象中的花园花床；想要控制住呼吸的努力，却让嘴唇失去了血色，脸色苍白。大苍蝇又嗡嗡作响，声音震耳欲聋。
foreignization: 面对这样的同情，以及这样真诚的青春和美丽，对于被告来说，比面对所有的人群更加难以承受。仿佛站在他的墓穴边缘，与她隔开，此时此刻，所有的注视好奇都无法使他完全保持镇静。他急促的右手将面前的草药分成了想象中的花园里的花床；他努力控制和稳定呼吸的努力使嘴唇上的血色涌向了心脏。大苍蝇的嗡嗡声再次响亮起来。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

To be confronted with such pity, and such earnest youth and beauty, was far more trying to the accused than to be confronted with all the crowd. Standing, as it were, apart with her on the edge of his grave, not all the staring curiosity that looked on, could, for the moment, nerve him to remain quite still. His hurried right hand parcelled out the herbs before him into imaginary beds of flowers in a garden; and his efforts to control and steady his breathing shook the lips from which the colour rushed to his heart. The buzz of the great flies was loud again.
面对这样的同情，还有这般青春和美丽，对被告来说，比面对所有围观的人群都要难熬得多。仿佛站在他坟墓边缘的只有她和他，一时之间，再多的好奇目光也无法让他镇定下来。他匆匆忙忙地右手把面前的草药分成了想象中的花园花床；想要控制住呼吸的努力，却让嘴唇失去了血色，脸色苍白。大苍蝇又嗡嗡作响，声音震耳欲聋。

His hurried right hand parcelled out the herbs before him into imaginary beds of flowers in a garden; and his efforts to control and steady his breathing shook the lips from which the colour rushed to his heart. The buzz of the great flies was loud again.
面对这样的同情，以及这样真诚的青春和美丽，对于被告来说，比面对所有的人群更加难以承受。仿佛站在他的墓穴边缘，与她隔开，此时此刻，所有的注视好奇都无法使他完全保持镇静。他急促的右手将面前的草药分成了想象中的花园里的花床；他努力控制和稳定呼吸的努力使嘴唇上的血色涌向了心脏。大苍蝇的嗡嗡声再次响亮起来。

source: “All sorts of people have been proposed to me, but I will have nothing to say to any of them. I intend to take Jerry. Jerry has been my bodyguard on Sunday nights for a long time past and I am used to him. Nobody will suspect Jerry of being anything but an English bull-dog, or of having any design in his head but to fly at anybody who touches his master.”
domestication: 什么样的人都有人给我介绍过，但我一个都看不上。我打定主意要带杰瑞。杰瑞每个星期天晚上都给我当保镖，时间长了，我也习惯了。谁也不会怀疑杰瑞除了是一只英国斗牛犬外还能是什么，更不会想到他除了保护主人不受侵犯之外还有什么别的想法。

source: That might not be so long as the Marquis supposed. If a picture of the chateau as it was to be a very few years hence, and of fifty like it as they too were to be a very few years hence, could have been shown to him that night, he might have been at a loss to claim his own from the ghastly, fire-charred, plunder-wrecked rains. As for the roof he vaunted, he might have found that shutting out the sky in a new way—to wit, for ever, from the eyes of the bodies into which its lead was fired, out of the barrels of a hundred thousand muskets.
domestication: 这时间或许没有侯爵想象的那么长。如果那天晚上能给他看一幅城堡几年后的样子，还有五十座类似城堡几年后的景象，他可能会认不出哪一座是自己的，因为这些城堡都变成了惨不忍睹、被大火烧焦、遭洗劫后的废墟。至于他夸耀的那个屋顶，可能早就成了千千万万士兵眼中永远不再看见天空的东西，因为它的铅皮被装进了无数火枪的枪管里，射向了那些人的身体。
foreignization: 那可能不会像侯爵所设想的那么长久。如果能在那天晚上给他展示一幅这座城堡几年后的情景，以及五十座类似的城堡几年后的情景，他可能会难以从那些可怕的、被火烧毁、被掠夺破坏的废墟中认出自己的城堡。至于他夸耀的屋顶，他可能会发现它以一种新的方式——也就是说，永远地——从成千上万支步枪的枪管中射出的铅弹射入的身体的眼睛中遮挡住了天空。

source: “It’s plain enough, I should think, why he may be. It’s a dreadful remembrance. Besides that, his loss of himself grew out of it. Not knowing how he lost himself, or how he recovered himself, he may never feel certain of not losing himself again. That alone wouldn’t make the subject pleasant, I should think.”
domestication: 这事儿明摆着，依我看。这回忆太可怕了。再说，他失去自我的起因就在这里。他自己都不知道是怎么迷失的，又是怎么找回自己的，所以他可能永远都不会确信自己不会再迷失。单是这点，就够让人不愉快的了。
foreignization: 这足够明显了，我想，他可能的原因。这是一个可怕的回忆。除此之外，他的自我丧失也是从中产生的。不知道他是如何失去自我，或是如何恢复自我的，他可能永远无法确定自己不会再失去自我。仅这一点就足以让这个话题不那么令人愉快了，我想。

source: A moment of profound silence followed. Defarge and his wife looked steadfastly at one another. The Vengeance stooped, and the jar of a drum was heard as she moved it at her feet behind the counter.
domestication: 接着是一阵深沉的静默。德法日和他的妻子互相凝视着。复仇女神弯下腰，她在柜台后面挪动脚下的鼓时，传来了一阵咚咚的声音。
foreignization: 随后是一片深沉的寂静。德发日和他的妻子坚定地彼此对视。复仇者弯下腰，当她移动柜台后面的鼓时，听到了陶罐的声音。

随后是一片深沉的寂静。德发日和他的妻子坚定地彼此对视。复仇者弯下腰，当她移动柜台后面的鼓时，听到了陶罐的声音。

source: Thus it had come to pass, that Tellson’s was the triumphant perfection of inconvenience. After bursting open a door of idiotic obstinacy with a weak rattle in its throat, you fell into Tellson’s down two steps, and came to your senses in a miserable little shop, with two little counters, where the oldest of men made your cheque shake as if the wind rustled it, while they examined the signature by the dingiest of windows, which were always under a shower-bath of mud from Fleet-street, and which were made the dingier by their own iron bars proper, and the heavy shadow of Temple Bar. If your business necessitated your seeing “the House,” you were put into a species of Condemned Hold at the back, where you meditated on a misspent life, until the House came with its hands in its pockets, and you could hardly blink at it in the dismal twilight. Your money came out of, or went into, wormy old wooden drawers, particles of which flew up your nose and down your throat when they were opened and shut. Your bank-notes had a musty odour, as if they were fast decomposing into rags again. Your plate was stowed away among the neighbouring cesspools, and evil communications corrupted its good polish in a day or two. Your deeds got into extemporised strong-rooms made of kitchens and sculleries, and fretted all the fat out of their parchments into the banking-house air. Your lighter boxes of family papers went up-stairs into a Barmecide room, that always had a great dining-table in it and never had a dinner, and where, even in the year one thousand seven hundred and eighty, the first letters written to you by your old love, or by your little children, were but newly released from the horror of being ogled through the windows, by the heads exposed on Temple Bar with an insensate brutality and ferocity worthy of Abyssinia or Ashantee.
domestication: 就这样，泰勒森银行成了不方便的典范。推开一扇固执得让人无奈的门，门轴还会发出微弱的嘎吱声，你便跌跌撞撞地走进泰勒森银行，下了两级台阶，眼前是个凄惨的小铺子，只有两个小小的柜台。那些年长的职员拿着你的支票，像被风吹动一样颤抖，他们在最昏暗的窗户下核对签名，而这些窗户总是被舰队街上的泥水溅得到处都是，再加上自己铁栏杆的阴影和法院大门投下的重重黑影，显得更加阴暗。如果业务需要见“高层”，你会被关进一个类似死囚室的后屋，让你反思自己是否虚度了光阴，直到高层两手插在口袋里出现，你几乎要在那昏暗的光线下眯起眼睛看他们。钱从虫蛀的老木抽屉里进进出出，开合之间，木屑飞进你的鼻子和喉咙。纸币散发出一股霉味，好像随时都会腐烂成破布。你的银器被存放在附近的污水池旁，几天下来，好好的光泽就被腐蚀殆尽。你的契约文件被塞进临时改造成金库的厨房和洗衣间，那里的空气都充满了纸张的酸味。家庭文件则被搬到楼上一间空荡荡的大餐厅，里面摆着一张大餐桌，却从未有人在此用餐。即使到了一七八〇年，你年轻时恋人写给你的第一封信，或是孩子们稚嫩的笔迹，刚刚才从那种透过窗户就能看到的恐怖景象中解脱出来——那些人头被挂在法院大门上，展示着一种野蛮和残忍，不亚于阿比西尼亚或阿散蒂。
foreignization: 于是，泰勒森银行成了不方便的完美典范。在用力推开一扇顽固得像傻瓜一样的门，发出微弱的嘎吱声后，你就会跌进泰勒森银行，沿着两个台阶向下，然后在一间可怜的小商店里恢复神智，这里只有两个小柜台，最年长的老头们会像风拂过一样让你的支票颤抖，而他们则在最昏暗的窗子下检查签名，这些窗子总是受到弗利特街泥浆雨的洗礼，并因自身的铁栏杆和国会大厦投下的沉重阴影而变得更加昏暗。如果你的业务需要见“银行高层”，你会被关进后面的一种死囚室，在那里反思自己的一生，直到银行高层把手插在口袋里出现，你在那昏暗的暮光中几乎看不清他们。你的钱从发霉的旧木抽屉中取出或存入，每当抽屉开合时，木屑就会飞进你的鼻子和喉咙。你的钞票散发着霉味，仿佛它们正在迅速分解成破布。你的银器被储存在附近的粪坑中，没几天就被污染得失去了光泽。你的契据被放在用厨房和洗衣房临时改成的保险库中，结果使得契据上的羊皮纸在银行空气中变得干瘪。你较轻的家庭文件箱则被搬到楼上一间巴尔米希德式的房间，里面总是摆着一张大餐桌却从未有人在这里用餐，甚至在公元一千七百八十年，你的旧情人或小孩写给你的第一批信件才刚刚摆脱透过窗户被悬挂在国会大厦上的人头注视的恐怖，这种行为的野蛮残忍程度堪比阿比西尼亚或阿散蒂。

Thus it had come to pass, that Tellson’s was the triumphant perfection of inconvenience. After bursting open a door of idiotic obstinacy with a weak rattle in its throat, you fell into Tellson’s down two steps, and came to your senses in a miserable little shop, with two little counters, where the oldest of men made your cheque shake as if the wind rustled it, while they examined the signature by the dingiest of windows, which were always under a shower-bath of mud from Fleet-street, and which were made the dingier by their own iron bars proper, and the heavy shadow of Temple Bar. Your money came out of, or went into, wormy old wooden drawers, particles of which flew up your nose and down your throat when they were opened and shut. Your bank-notes had a musty odour, as if they were fast decomposing into rags again. Your plate was stowed away among the neighbouring cesspools, and evil communications corrupted its good polish in a day or two. Your deeds got into extemporised strong-rooms made of kitchens and sculleries, and fretted all the fat out of their parchments into the banking-house air. Your lighter boxes of family papers went up-stairs into a Barmecide room, that always had a great dining-table in it and never had a dinner, and where, even in the year one thousand seven hundred and eighty, the first letters written to you by your old love, or by your little children, were but newly released from the horror of being ogled through the windows, by the heads exposed on Temple Bar with an insensate brutality and ferocity worthy of Abyssinia or Ashantee.

Your deeds got into extemporised strong-rooms made of kitchens and sculleries, and fretted all the fat out of their parchments into the banking-house air. Your lighter boxes of family papers went up-stairs into a Barmecide room, that always had a great dining-table in it and never had a dinner, and where, even in the year one thousand seven hundred and eighty, the first letters written to you by your old love, or by your little children, were but newly released from the horror of being ogled through the windows, by the heads exposed on Temple Bar with an insensate brutality and ferocity worthy of Abyssinia or Ashantee.

于是，泰勒森银行成了不方便的完美典范。在用力推开一扇顽固得像傻瓜一样的门，发出微弱的嘎吱声后，你就会跌进泰勒森银行，沿着两个台阶向下，然后在一间可怜的小商店里恢复神智，这里只有两个小柜台，最年长的老头们会像风拂过一样让你的支票颤抖，而他们则在最昏暗的窗子下检查签名，这些窗子总是受到弗利特街泥浆雨的洗礼，并因自身的铁栏杆和国会大厦投下的沉重阴影而变得更加昏暗。如果你的业务需要见“银行高层”，你会被关进后面的一种死囚室，在那里反思自己的一生，直到银行高层把手插在口袋里出现，你在那昏暗的暮光中几乎看不清他们。你的钱从发霉的旧木抽屉中取出或存入，每当抽屉开合时，木屑就会飞进你的鼻子和喉咙。你的钞票散发着霉味，仿佛它们正在迅速分解成破布。你的银器被储存在附近的粪坑中，没几天就被污染得失去了光泽。你的契据被放在用厨房和洗衣房临时改成的保险库中，结果使得契据上的羊皮纸在银行空气中变得干瘪。你较轻的家庭文件箱则被搬到楼上一间巴尔米希德式的房间，里面总是摆着一张大餐桌却从未有人在这里用餐，甚至在公元一千七百八十年，你的旧情人或小孩写给你的第一批信件才刚刚摆脱透过窗户被悬挂在国会大厦上的人头注视的恐怖，这种行为的野蛮残忍程度堪比阿比西尼亚或阿散蒂。

source: The wine-shop keeper accordingly rolled his eyes about, until they rested upon an elderly gentleman and a young lady, who were seated in a corner. Other company were there: two playing cards, two playing dominoes, three standing by the counter lengthening out a short supply of wine. As he passed behind the counter, he took notice that the elderly gentleman said in a look to the young lady, “This is our man.”
domestication: 酒馆老板于是四下张望，目光最终落在了角落里的一位老先生和一位年轻姑娘身上。店里还有其他人：两个在打牌，两个在玩多米诺骨牌，三个站在柜台边，想用少量的酒凑合着喝。老板从柜台后面走过时，注意到那位老先生朝年轻姑娘使了个眼色，意思是“这人就是我们要找的”。
foreignization: 酒馆老板于是四处张望，直到他的目光停留在角落里的一位老年绅士和一位年轻女士身上。还有其他客人在场：两个在打牌，两个在玩多米诺骨牌，三个站在柜台旁，想用少量的酒尽量多喝一些。当他从柜台后面走过时，他注意到那位老年绅士用眼神对年轻女士说：“这就是我们要找的人。”

source: There was something in its touch that gave Lucie a check. She stopped in the act of putting the note in her bosom, and, with her hands yet at her neck, looked terrified at Madame Defarge. Madame Defarge met the lifted eyebrows and forehead with a cold, impassive stare.
domestication: 它那触碰中有什么东西让露西一惊。她正要把信笺贴身收好，手还没离开脖子，就吓得直瞪着德法日太太。德法日太太抬眼与她对视，却是一副冷冰冰、毫无表情的样子。
foreignization: 它的触摸让露西感到一阵不安。她正要把信件放入口袋，却停了下来，双手还留在颈间，惊恐地望着德法日夫人。德法日夫人用冷淡、无动于衷的目光迎上了她扬起的眉毛和前额。

source: Within half an hour from the first starting, they were beyond the winking lamps, and the more than winking watchmen, and were out upon a lonely road. Another fisherman was picked up here—and that so silently, that if Young Jerry had been superstitious, he might have supposed the second follower of the gentle craft to have, all of a sudden, split himself into two.
domestication: 出发后半小时，他们就离开了那些眨眼的灯火和比灯还爱眨眼的守夜人，走上了一条冷清的路。接着，又悄无声息地接上了一个渔民，要是小杰瑞有点迷信，他准会以为先前那个渔民忽然间分身成了两个。
foreignization: 从第一次出发后不到半小时，他们就离开了那些闪烁的灯和更加警觉的看守，来到了一条孤独的路上。又在这里接上了一位渔民——而且是如此悄无声息，以至于如果小杰里迷信的话，他可能会以为那位温和职业的第二位追随者突然之间分成了两个。

source: Sydney had been working double tides that night, and the night before, and the night before that, and a good many nights in succession, making a grand clearance among Mr. Stryver’s papers before the setting in of the long vacation. The clearance was effected at last; the Stryver arrears were handsomely fetched up; everything was got rid of until November should come with its fogs atmospheric, and fogs legal, and bring grist to the mill again.
domestication: 那几天，悉尼一直在加班加点，连续好几个晚上都是这样。他忙着清理斯特瑞弗先生的文件，为即将到来的长假做准备。终于，活儿都干完了，斯特瑞弗先生积压的工作也顺利处理完毕。所有的东西都清理得一干二净，一直能撑到十一月再来。那时候，雾气弥漫，无论是天气的雾，还是法律上的“雾”，都会再次给事务所带来生意。
foreignization: 悉尼那天晚上一直在加班，前一晚也是，再前一晚也是如此，连续许多个晚上都在工作，为斯特瑞弗先生的文件做一个大清理，以迎接长假的到来。最后，清理工作终于完成了；斯特瑞弗先生的积压工作被漂亮地赶上了；一切都被处理得干干净净，直到十一月带着它的大气雾霾和法律雾霾再次到来，为磨坊带来新的谷物。

The clearance was effected at last; the Stryver arrears were handsomely fetched up; everything was got rid of until November should come with its fogs atmospheric, and fogs legal, and bring grist to the mill again.

source: After grasping the Doctor’s hand, as he stood victorious and proud before him; after grasping the hand of Mr. Lorry, who came panting in breathless from his struggle against the waterspout of the Carmagnole; after kissing little Lucie, who was lifted up to clasp her arms round his neck; and after embracing the ever zealous and faithful Pross who lifted her; he took his wife in his arms, and carried her up to their rooms.
