source: “Look well upon that gentleman, my learned friend there,” pointing to him who had tossed the paper over, “and then look well upon the prisoner. How say you? Are they very like each other?”
domestication: 大家看看这位先生，我的这位博学的朋友，就是刚才扔过纸条的那位，再仔细看看被告。你们说说，他们俩是不是长得挺像？
foreignization: “请仔细看看那位先生，我的学识渊博的朋友，”说着指了指刚才把纸扔过来的人，“然后再仔细看看被告。你说呢？他们长得像不像？”

source: “Since the state of your mind that you describe, is, at all events, attributable to some influence of mine—this is what I mean, if I can make it plain—can I use no influence to serve you? Have I no power for good, with you, at all?”
domestication: 你心里这状态，不管怎么说，总归是受了我的影响——我这么说不知能不能让你明白——那我难道就不能对你有所帮助吗？我就对你一点好作用也没有吗？
foreignization: “既然你所描述的你的精神状态，无论如何，是由于我的某种影响——这就是我的意思，如果我能说清楚的话——我难道不能用某种影响来帮助你吗？我在你身上难道完全没有行善的力量吗？”

source: A shrill sound of laughter and of amused voices—voices of men, women, and children—resounded in the street while this wine game lasted. There was little roughness in the sport, and much playfulness. There was a special companionship in it, an observable inclination on the part of every one to join some other one, which led, especially among the luckier or lighter-hearted, to frolicsome embraces, drinking of healths, shaking of hands, and even joining of hands and dancing, a dozen together. When the wine was gone, and the places where it had been most abundant were raked into a gridiron-pattern by fingers, these demonstrations ceased, as suddenly as they had broken out. The man who had left his saw sticking in the firewood he was cutting, set it in motion again; the women who had left on a door-step the little pot of hot ashes, at which she had been trying to soften the pain in her own starved fingers and toes, or in those of her child, returned to it; men with bare arms, matted locks, and cadaverous faces, who had emerged into the winter light from cellars, moved away, to descend again; and a gloom gathered on the scene that appeared more natural to it than sunshine.
domestication: 尖叫声、欢笑声在街上此起彼伏，男人、女人、孩子的声音交织在一起，这场喝酒游戏进行得热火朝天。游戏里没有多少粗鲁，倒是很有些嬉戏的意味。大家自然而然地凑成对子，尤其是那些运气好或者心情好的人，更是搂搂抱抱，互相敬酒，握手言欢，甚至手拉手跳起舞来，一伙人挤作一团。等酒喝完了，地上那些被手指划过的酒渍也像铁栅栏一样横七竖八时，这些热闹的场面突然间就消失了。那个锯子卡在柴火里的汉子又继续锯了起来；门阶上放着小锅的女人，她原本在热灰里暖自己冻得发紫的手指和脚趾，或是给孩子取暖，现在又回到了锅边；光着膀子、头发凌乱、面如死灰的男人们，从地下室出来透透气，又一个个消失回了阴暗的角落。阳光似乎不属于这里，取而代之的是一片沉寂的阴霾。
foreignization: 一阵尖锐的笑声和愉快的声音——男人、女人和孩子的声音——在这场酒戏进行期间在街上回荡。这场游戏没有什么粗暴的地方，却充满了嬉戏。其中有一种特别的友谊，每个人都有明显的倾向与另一个人结伴，这尤其在那些更幸运或更心轻气扬的人中表现出来，导致了嬉闹的拥抱、敬酒、握手，甚至十几个人手拉手一起跳舞。当酒喝光，那些酒最多的地方被手指划成烤架图案时，这些表现突然停止，就像它们突然爆发一样。那个将锯子留在他正在砍的柴火中的男人，再次将它动了起来；那些离开门阶的女人，她们把小锅里的热灰留在那里，试图缓解自己饥饿的手指和脚趾的疼痛，或是她孩子的疼痛，又回到了那里；光着膀子、头发凌乱、面容憔悴的男人，从地窖里走出来沐浴冬日的阳光，又走开，再次下去；一种阴郁笼罩着这个场景，看起来比阳光更适合这里。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

A shrill sound of laughter and of amused voices—voices of men, women, and children—resounded in the street while this wine game lasted. There was little roughness in the sport, and much playfulness. There was a special companionship in it, an observable inclination on the part of every one to join some other one, which led, especially among the luckier or lighter-hearted, to frolicsome embraces, drinking of healths, shaking of hands, and even joining of hands and dancing, a dozen together. When the wine was gone, and the places where it had been most abundant were raked into a gridiron-pattern by fingers, these demonstrations ceased, as suddenly as they had broken out. The man who had left his saw sticking in the firewood he was cutting, set it in motion again; the women who had left on a door-step the little pot of hot ashes, at which she had been trying to soften the pain in her own starved fingers and toes, or in those of her child, returned to it; men with bare arms, matted locks, and cadaverous faces, who had emerged into the winter light from cellars, moved away, to descend again; and a gloom gathered on the scene that appeared more natural to it than sunshine.

A shrill sound of laughter and of amused voices—voices of men, women, and children—resounded in the street while this wine game lasted. There was little roughness in the sport, and much playfulness. source: Within a few minutes, Miss Pross stood whispering at his side. If he had had any particle of doubt left, her talk would of necessity have resolved it; but he was by that time clear-headed, and had none. He advised that they should let the time go by until the regular breakfast-hour, and should then meet the Doctor as if nothing unusual had occurred. If he appeared to be in his customary state of mind, Mr. Lorry would then cautiously proceed to seek direction and guidance from the opinion he had been, in his anxiety, so anxious to obtain.
domestication: 没过几分钟，普罗斯小姐就轻声站在他身旁。要是他还有一丁点儿怀疑，她的话肯定能打消他的顾虑；不过这时他已经头脑清醒，不再有任何疑虑。他建议，他们等到正常的吃早饭时间再说，到时候再像往常一样和医生碰面。如果医生看起来一切正常，劳里先生就可以小心翼翼地向他征求之前急切想得到的意见和指导。
foreignization: 几分钟后，普罗斯小姐站在他身边低语。如果他还剩下任何一丝怀疑，她的话必然会消除它；但到了那时他已经头脑清醒，没有任何怀疑了。他建议他们等到正常的早餐时间，然后像什么都没有发生过一样与医生会面。如果他表现得像往常一样，劳里先生就会谨慎地寻求他因焦虑而迫切想要得到的意见的指导和建议。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Within a few minutes, Miss Pross stood whispering at his side. If he had had any particle of doubt left, her talk would of necessity have resolved it; but he was by that time clear-headed, and had none. He advised that they should let the time go by until the regular breakfast-hour, and should then meet the Doctor as if nothing unusual had occurred. If he appeared to be in his customary state of mind, Mr. Lorry would then cautiously proceed to seek direction and guidance from the opinion he had been, in his anxiety, so anxious to obtain.
没过几分钟，普罗斯小姐就轻声站在他身旁。要是他还有一丁点儿怀疑，她的话肯定能打消他的顾虑；不过这时他已经头脑清醒，不再有任何疑虑。他建议，他们等到正常的吃早饭时间再说，到时候再像往常一样和医生碰面。如果医生看起来一切正常，劳里先生就可以小心翼翼地向他征求之前急切想得到的意见和指导。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Within a few minutes, Miss Pross stood whispering at his side. If he had had any particle of doubt left, her talk would of necessity have resolved it; but he was by that time clear-headed, and had none. He advised that they should let the time go by until the regular breakfast-hour, and should then meet the Doctor as if nothing unusual had occurred. If he appeared to be in his customary state of mind, Mr. Lorry would then cautiously proceed to seek direction and guidance from the opinion he had been, in his anxiety, so anxious to obtain.
几分钟后，普罗斯小姐站在他身边低语。如果他还剩下任何一丝怀疑，她的话必然会消除它；但到了那时他已经头脑清醒，没有任何怀疑了。他建议他们等到正常的早餐时间，然后像什么都没有发生过一样与医生会面。如果他表现得像往常一样，劳里先生就会谨慎地寻求他因焦虑而迫切想要得到的意见的指导和建议。

source: Confused by the emotion of the day, and feeling his being there with this Double of coarse deportment, to be like a dream, Charles Darnay was at a loss how to answer; finally, answered not at all.
domestication: 查尔斯·达内被当天的情绪搞得晕头转向，再加上眼前这个粗鲁的家伙让他觉得一切都像在做梦一样，一时间不知道该说些什么好；最终，他什么也没说。

source: They had said little, though they had been there a long time. When it was yet light enough to work and read, she had neither engaged herself in her usual work, nor had she read to him. She had employed herself in both ways, at his side under the tree, many and many a time; but, this time was not quite like any other, and nothing could make it so.
domestication: 他们在那里待了很久，却没怎么说话。天还亮着的时候，她既没有像往常那样工作，也没有给他读书。以前，她在树下陪他时，常常一边工作一边给他念书；但这次不一样，再怎么努力也回不到从前的样子了。
foreignization: 他们几乎没有说话，尽管他们在那里已经很长时间了。当光线还足够工作和阅读时，她既没有像往常一样工作，也没有给他读书。她曾在树下他的身边，多次以这两种方式消磨时光；但，这次并不完全像其他任何时候，没有什么能让它变得如此。

source: Tellson’s Bank by Temple Bar was an old-fashioned place, even in the year one thousand seven hundred and eighty. It was very small, very dark, very ugly, very incommodious. It was an old-fashioned place, moreover, in the moral attribute that the partners in the House were proud of its smallness, proud of its darkness, proud of its ugliness, proud of its incommodiousness. They were even boastful of its eminence in those particulars, and were fired by an express conviction that, if it were less objectionable, it would be less respectable. This was no passive belief, but an active weapon which they flashed at more convenient places of business. Tellson’s (they said) wanted no elbow-room, Tellson’s wanted no light, Tellson’s wanted no embellishment. Noakes and Co.’s might, or Snooks Brothers’ might; but Tellson’s, thank Heaven—!
domestication: 位于圣殿栅栏旁的泰勒森银行，即使是在1780年，也显得十分老派。它很小，很暗，很难看，也很不方便。更别提那些合伙人还以这些缺点为傲：他们自豪于银行的小，自豪于它的暗，自豪于它的丑，自豪于它的不便。他们甚至吹嘘银行在这些方面的优越性，坚信如果银行少些这些问题，就会少些体面。这可不是无动于衷的想法，而是他们对付那些更为便利的竞争对手的武器。他们常说，泰勒森不需要更多的空间，泰勒森不需要更多的光线，泰勒森不需要任何装饰。诺克斯公司或许需要，斯努克斯兄弟或许需要，但感谢上天，泰勒森可不需要！
foreignization: 泰晤士银行在圣殿栅栏附近是一家老式的地方，即使在公元一七八〇年也是如此。它非常小，非常暗，非常丑，非常不方便。此外，这家银行在道德属性上也是一家老式的地方，因为银行的合伙人以其渺小、黑暗、丑陋和不便而自豪。他们甚至夸耀其在这方面的突出地位，并坚信，如果它不那么令人反感，就不会那么受人尊敬。这并非一种被动的信念，而是他们用来对抗更方便的商业场所的武器。泰晤士银行（他们说）不需要宽敞的空间，不需要光线，不需要装饰。诺克斯公司可能需要，或者斯努克斯兄弟可能需要；但感谢上帝——泰晤士银行不需要！

Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was an old-fashioned place, moreover, in the moral attribute that the partners in the House were proud of its smallness, proud of its darkness, proud of its ugliness, proud of its incommodiousness. They were even boastful of its eminence in those particulars, and were fired by an express conviction that, if it were less objectionable, it would be less respectable. This was no passive belief, but an active weapon which they flashed at more convenient places of business. Tellson’s (they said) wanted no elbow-room, Tellson’s wanted no light, Tellson’s wanted no embellishment. Noakes and Co.’s might, or Snooks Brothers’ might; but Tellson’s, thank Heaven—!

source: The corner being out of the way of the idle and curious, and the preparations having been very simple and few, the Doctor, Mr. Lorry, and Miss Pross, were left quite alone. It was when they turned into the welcome shade of the cool old hall, that Mr. Lorry observed a great change to have come over the Doctor; as if the golden arm uplifted there, had struck him a poisoned blow.
domestication: 角落里清静无人打扰，又没什么复杂的准备，医生、洛里先生和普罗斯小姐三人便安安静静地待在了一起。他们一走进那凉爽的老厅堂，洛里先生就发现医生好像变了个人似的，仿佛被那里的金臂给狠狠地击中了一样。
foreignization: 角落里远离了闲人和好奇者，而准备工作又非常简单且不多，因此医生、洛里先生和普罗斯小姐被完全单独留下了。就在他们走进凉爽的老大厅的宜人阴凉处时，洛里先生注意到医生发生了很大的变化；仿佛那里高举的金色手臂给了他一记毒打。

source: “Carton,” said his friend, squaring himself at him with a bullying air, as if the fire-grate had been the furnace in which sustained endeavour was forged, and the one delicate thing to be done for the old Sydney Carton of old Shrewsbury School was to shoulder him into it, “your way is, and always was, a lame way. You summon no energy and purpose. Look at me.”
domestication: “卡顿，”他的朋友摆出一副威胁的架势对他说，好像壁炉就是锤炼不懈努力的熔炉，而对付老悉尼·卡顿这个旧什鲁斯伯里学校的旧相识，唯一精致的做法就是把他推到里面去，“你一直以来都是个拖泥带水的人。你从不振作精神，定下目标。你看我。”
foreignization: “卡顿，”他的朋友摆出一副欺凌的架势对着他说，仿佛壁炉就是锻造坚持不懈努力的熔炉，而对老悉尼·卡顿，这个老什鲁斯伯里学校的老学生，唯一精致的事情就是把他推入其中，“你的方法，一直以来都是个瘸子的方法。你召唤不出任何精力和目的。看看我。”

source: They went up slowly and softly. The staircase was short, and they were soon at the top. There, as it had an abrupt turn in it, they came all at once in sight of three men, whose heads were bent down close together at the side of a door, and who were intently looking into the room to which the door belonged, through some chinks or holes in the wall. On hearing footsteps close at hand, these three turned, and rose, and showed themselves to be the three of one name who had been drinking in the wine-shop.
domestication: 他们慢慢悠悠地往上走。楼梯很短，转眼就到了顶。一拐弯，突然看见三个男人头凑着头，紧挨着一扇门，聚精会神地透过墙上的缝隙往屋里窥探。听见有人走近的脚步声，这三个男人猛地转过身，站了起来，原来是那酒馆里喝得醉醺醺的三个同名同姓的人。
foreignization: 他们慢慢地、轻轻地走上去了。楼梯很短，他们很快就到了顶。那里，因为楼梯有一个急转弯，他们突然看见了三个男人，他们的头低低地靠在一起，在一扇门旁边，正全神贯注地通过墙上的缝隙或小孔窥视着那个房间。听到近在咫尺的脚步声，这三个人转过身来，站了起来，原来是那三个同名的人，之前曾在酒馆里喝酒。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

They went up slowly and softly. The staircase was short, and they were soon at the top. There, as it had an abrupt turn in it, they came all at once in sight of three men, whose heads were bent down close together at the side of a door, and who were intently looking into the room to which the door belonged, through some chinks or holes in the wall. On hearing footsteps close at hand, these three turned, and rose, and showed themselves to be the three of one name who had been drinking in the wine-shop.
他们慢慢悠悠地往上走。楼梯很短，转眼就到了顶。一拐弯，突然看见三个男人头凑着头，紧挨着一扇门，聚精会神地透过墙上的缝隙往屋里窥探。听见有人走近的脚步声，这三个男人猛地转过身，站了起来，原来是那酒馆里喝得醉醺醺的三个同名同姓的人。

On hearing footsteps close at hand, these three turned, and rose, and showed themselves to be the three of one name who had been drinking in the wine-shop.
他们慢慢地、轻轻地走上去了。楼梯很短，他们很快就到了顶。那里，因为楼梯有一个急转弯，他们突然看见了三个男人，他们的头低低地靠在一起，在一扇门旁边，正全神贯注地通过墙上的缝隙或小孔窥视着那个房间。听到近在咫尺的脚步声，这三个人转过身来，站了起来，原来是那三个同名的人，之前曾在酒馆里喝酒。

source: The three went on, and Young Jerry went on, until the three stopped under a bank overhanging the road. Upon the top of the bank was a low brick wall, surmounted by an iron railing. In the shadow of bank and wall the three turned out of the road, and up a blind lane, of which the wall—there, risen to some eight or ten feet high—formed one side. Crouching down in a corner, peeping up the lane, the next object that Young Jerry saw, was the form of his honoured parent, pretty well defined against a watery and clouded moon, nimbly scaling an iron gate. He was soon over, and then the second fisherman got over, and then the third. They all dropped softly on the ground within the gate, and lay there a little—listening perhaps. Then, they moved away on their hands and knees.
domestication: 三人继续往前走，小杰瑞也跟着。他们一直走到路边的一个土坡下才停了下来。土坡的顶上有一道矮砖墙，墙上还装着铁栏杆。在土坡和墙的阴影里，三个人拐进了一条死胡同，这条胡同的一边就是那堵墙，墙在这里有七八米高。小杰瑞蹲在角落里，往胡同里张望，只见他尊敬的老爹身影在水汽弥漫、云遮月的夜色中格外清晰，正灵巧地翻过铁门。不一会儿，他就翻过去了，接着第二个渔夫也翻了过去，然后是第三个。他们都轻轻地落在门内的地上，趴了一会儿，可能是在听什么动静。随后，他们就手膝并用地向前爬去。
foreignization: 三个人继续走着，小杰里也跟着走，直到三个人在一条悬挂在路上的堤岸下停了下来。堤岸顶上有一道低矮的砖墙，上面装有铁栅栏。在堤岸和墙的阴影中，三个人离开了大路，走上了一条盲巷，这条巷子的一边就是那堵墙——在那里，墙高约八到十英尺。小杰里蹲在一个角落里，窥视着巷子，他看到的下一个东西就是他尊敬的父亲的身影，身影在朦胧多云的月亮前显得相当清楚，敏捷地攀越了一扇铁门。他很快就翻了过去，接着第二个渔夫也翻了过去，然后是第三个。他们都在门内的地上轻轻地落地，躺了一会儿——也许是在听。然后，他们手脚并用地向前移动。

Crouching down in a corner, peeping up the lane, the next object that Young Jerry saw, was the form of his honoured parent, pretty well defined against a watery and clouded moon, nimbly scaling an iron gate. He was soon over, and then the second fisherman got over, and then the third. They all dropped softly on the ground within the gate, and lay there a little—listening perhaps. Then, they moved away on their hands and knees.

source: But, when his nephew, leaning an elbow on the table, covered his eyes thoughtfully and dejectedly with his hand, the fine mask looked at him sideways with a stronger concentration of keenness, closeness, and dislike, than was comportable with its wearer’s assumption of indifference.
domestication: 但是，当他的侄子肘撑着桌子，手遮住眼睛，若有所思又有些沮丧时，那张精致的面具却侧目看着他，眼神中的敏锐、亲近和厌恶比假装的冷漠要强烈得多。
foreignization: 但是，当他的侄子靠在桌上，用手肘支撑着，用手遮住眼睛，显得深思而沮丧时，那张精致的面具侧目看着他，流露出更加敏锐、亲密和厌恶的表情，这与面具佩戴者所表现出的冷漠不相称。

但是，当他的侄子靠在桌上，用手肘支撑着，用手遮住眼睛，显得深思而沮丧时，那张精致的面具侧目看着他，流露出更加敏锐、亲密和厌恶的表情，这与面具佩戴者所表现出的冷漠不相称。

source: He made no effort to say why not, and said not a word more. But, Mr. Lorry thought he saw, as he leaned forward on his bench in the dusk, with his elbows on his knees and his head in his hands, that he was in some misty way asking himself, “Why not?” The sagacity of the man of business perceived an advantage here, and determined to hold it.
domestication: 他没费劲解释个中缘由，就再也没多说一个字。不过，洛里先生见他坐在昏暗的长凳上，身子前倾，两肘支在膝盖上，双手捧着头，似乎在模模糊糊地自问：“为什么不呢？”这位生意人的精明让他嗅到了一丝机会，决定抓住不放。
foreignization: 他没有努力去解释为什么不行，也没有再多说一个字。但是，洛里先生认为他在暮色中 leans forward on his bench，elbows on his knees，head in his hands 时，以一种模糊的方式在问自己：“为什么不呢？”这个商人的精明在这里看到了一个优势，并决定要抓住它。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

He made no effort to say why not, and said not a word more. But, Mr. Lorry thought he saw, as he leaned forward on his bench in the dusk, with his elbows on his knees and his head in his hands, that he was in some misty way asking himself, “Why not?” The sagacity of the man of business perceived an advantage here, and determined to hold it.

Translate the following text to Chinese.

He made no effort to say why not, and said not a word more. But, Mr. Lorry thought he saw, as he leaned forward on his bench in the dusk, with his elbows on his knees and his head in his hands, that he was in some misty way asking himself, “Why not?” The sagacity of the man of business perceived an advantage here, and determined to hold it.
他没有努力去解释为什么不行，也没有再多说一个字。但是，洛里先生认为他在暮色中 leans forward on his bench，elbows on his knees，head in his hands 时，以一种模糊的方式在问自己：“为什么不呢？”这个商人的精明在这里看到了一个优势，并决定要抓住它。

source: It was remarkable; but, the taste of Saint Antoine seemed to be decidedly opposed to a rose on the head-dress of Madame Defarge. Two men had entered separately, and had been about to order drink, when, catching sight of that novelty, they faltered, made a pretence of looking about as if for some friend who was not there, and went away. Nor, of those who had been there when this visitor entered, was there one left. They had all dropped off. The spy had kept his eyes open, but had been able to detect no sign. They had lounged away in a poverty-stricken, purposeless, accidental manner, quite natural and unimpeachable.
domestication: 这事儿可真稀奇；不过，圣安托万的人好像对德法日太太头上的玫瑰花不怎么感冒。两个男人分开进门，正打算点酒，一看到这新鲜玩意儿，就愣住了，假装四处张望，像是在找不在场的朋友，然后就走了。那些本来就在店里的人，也一个接一个地溜了，没剩下一个。这间谍瞪大了眼睛，但啥也没看出来。他们一个个懒洋洋地走了，一副穷困潦倒、漫无目的的样子，显得再自然不过了。
foreignization: 这是值得注意的；但是，圣安托万的口味似乎明显反对德发日太太头饰上的一朵玫瑰。两个男人分别进入，正要点酒，但看到这个新奇的东西时，他们犹豫了，假装四处张望好像在找一个不在场的朋友，然后离开了。而且，在这位客人进来时就已经在那里的人中，没有一个留下。他们都悄悄地离开了。间谍睁大了眼睛，但没能发现任何迹象。他们以一种贫困、无目的、偶然的方式懒散地离开了，这完全自然且无可挑剔。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was remarkable; but, the taste of Saint Antoine seemed to be decidedly opposed to a rose on the head-dress of Madame Defarge. Two men had entered separately, and had been about to order drink, when, catching sight of that novelty, they faltered, made a pretence of looking about as if for some friend who was not there, and went away. Nor, of those who had been there when this visitor entered, was there one left. They had all dropped off. The spy had kept his eyes open, but had been able to detect no sign. They had lounged away in a poverty-stricken, purposeless, accidental manner, quite natural and unimpeachable.
这事儿可真稀奇；不过，圣安托万的人好像对德法日太太头上的玫瑰花不怎么感冒。两个男人分开进门，正打算点酒，一看到这新鲜玩意儿，就愣住了，假装四处张望，像是在找不在场的朋友，然后就走了。那些本来就在店里的人，也一个接一个地溜了，没剩下一个。这间谍瞪大了眼睛，但啥也没看出来。他们一个个懒洋洋地走了，一副穷困潦倒、漫无目的的样子，显得再自然不过了。

Two men had entered separately, and had been about to order drink, when, catching sight of that novelty, they faltered, made a pretence of looking about as if for some friend who was not there, and went away. Nor, of those who had been there when this visitor entered, was there one left. They had all dropped off. The spy had kept his eyes open, but had been able to detect no sign. They had lounged away in a poverty-stricken, purposeless, accidental manner, quite natural and unimpeachable.

source: “Well, sir!” cried Mr. Lorry, thoroughly heated by his indifference, “business is a very good thing, and a very respectable thing. And, sir, if business imposes its restraints and its silences and impediments, Mr. Darnay as a young gentleman of generosity knows how to make allowance for that circumstance. Mr. Darnay, good night, God bless you, sir! I hope you have been this day preserved for a prosperous and happy life.—Chair there!”
domestication: “哎哟，先生！”洛里先生被对方的冷漠彻底激怒了，大声喊道，“做生意可是一件非常好的事，也是非常体面的事。先生啊，如果生意让人有所顾忌，有所沉默，甚至有所阻碍，达奈先生作为一个慷慨大方的年轻人，自然懂得其中的苦衷。达奈先生，晚安，上帝保佑您！希望您今天得以保全，未来生活美满幸福。——来人，抬轿！”
foreignization: “好吧，先生！”劳瑞先生因为达奈先生的冷漠而彻底激动起来，“生意是一件非常好的事情，也是非常体面的事情。而且，先生，如果生意强加了它的约束、沉默和障碍，达奈先生作为一个慷慨的年轻绅士知道如何为此情况做出让步。达奈先生，晚安，上帝保佑你，先生！我希望你今天被保佑有一个繁荣和幸福的生活。——来人，扶我上车！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Well, sir!” cried Mr. Lorry, thoroughly heated by his indifference, “business is a very good thing, and a very respectable thing. And, sir, if business imposes its restraints and its silences and impediments, Mr. Darnay as a young gentleman of generosity knows how to make allowance for that circumstance. Mr. Darnay, good night, God bless you, sir! I hope you have been this day preserved for a prosperous and happy life.—Chair there!”

source: The dread tribunal of five Judges, Public Prosecutor, and determined Jury, sat every day. Their lists went forth every evening, and were read out by the gaolers of the various prisons to their prisoners. The standard gaoler-joke was, “Come out and listen to the Evening Paper, you inside there!”
domestication: 五位法官、公诉人和坚定的陪审团每天都要开庭。每天傍晚，他们都会公布名单，各个监狱的狱卒也会把这些名单念给囚犯们听。狱卒们最爱开的玩笑就是：“里面的人，出来听听晚报吧！”
foreignization: 由五位法官、公诉人和坚定的陪审团组成的令人恐惧的法庭每天都在开庭。他们的名单每天晚上都会公布，并由各个监狱的狱卒向囚犯们宣读。狱卒的标准玩笑是：“里面的人，出来听晚报！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The dread tribunal of five Judges, Public Prosecutor, and determined Jury, sat every day. Their lists went forth every evening, and were read out by the gaolers of the various prisons to their prisoners. The standard gaoler-joke was, “Come out and listen to the Evening Paper, you inside there!”

source: “I forgot them in the surprise of your visit,” explained Monsieur Defarge. “Leave us, good boys; we have business here.”
domestication: 德法日先生解释道：“我忙着惊喜你的到来，把它们给忘了。”他又说：“好小伙子们，你们先出去吧，我们这儿有事要谈。”
foreignization: “我在你来访的惊喜中把它们忘了，”德法日先生解释道。“走吧，好孩子们；我们这里有事要处理。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I forgot them in the surprise of your visit,” explained Monsieur Defarge. “Leave us, good boys; we have business here.”

source: He stuck the paper on a file, in an ill-humour, and Charles Darnay awaited his further pleasure for half an hour: sometimes, pacing to and fro in the strong arched room: sometimes, resting on a stone seat: in either case detained to be imprinted on the memory of the chief and his subordinates.
domestication: 他没好气地把纸贴在文件上，查尔斯·达奈只好等了半个小时，看他下一步怎么发落。这间坚固的拱形房间里，达奈一会儿来回踱步，一会儿又坐在石凳上休息，无论是走是坐，都像是被故意留下来给上司和他的手下们记个清楚。
foreignization: 他心绪不佳地把纸贴在文件上，查尔斯·达尔内则等了他半小时：有时，在那间拱形的坚固房间里来回走动；有时，坐在石凳上休息：无论哪种情况，都是为了让首席和他的下属记住他。

他心绪不佳地把纸贴在文件上，查尔斯·达尔内则等了他半小时：有时，在那间拱形的坚固房间里来回走动；有时，坐在石凳上休息：无论哪种情况，都是为了让首席和他的下属记住他。

source: Allowing for my learned friend’s appearance being careless and slovenly if not debauched, they were sufficiently like each other to surprise, not only the witness, but everybody present, when they were thus brought into comparison. My Lord being prayed to bid my learned friend lay aside his wig, and giving no very gracious consent, the likeness became much more remarkable. My Lord inquired of Mr. Stryver (the prisoner’s counsel), whether they were next to try Mr. Carton (name of my learned friend) for treason? But, Mr. Stryver replied to my Lord, no; but he would ask the witness to tell him whether what happened once, might happen twice; whether he would have been so confident if he had seen this illustration of his rashness sooner, whether he would be so confident, having seen it; and more. The upshot of which, was, to smash this witness like a crockery vessel, and shiver his part of the case to useless lumber.
domestication: 虽说我的这位学究朋友打扮得有些邋遢，甚至可以说是放荡不羁，但当他们被放在一起比较时，相似之处还是让在场的所有人都大吃一惊，不仅仅是证人。法官大人应要求让我这位学究朋友摘下假发，虽然不太情愿，但这一来，两人的相似度就更明显了。法官大人还问斯特莱弗先生（被告的辩护律师），接下来是不是要以叛国罪审判卡顿先生（我的学究朋友的名字）？不过，斯特莱弗先生回答法官大人说，不，但他想问问证人，这种情况发生一次后，是否还会发生第二次；如果证人早点看到这个证明他鲁莽的例子，当时还会那么自信吗？现在看到了，还会那么自信吗？等等。这一番问下来，就把证人说得跟个碎瓷碗似的，把他那部分的证词也打得支离破碎，毫无用处了。
foreignization: 允许我的博学的朋友的外表显得粗心大意，甚至可以说是放荡，但当他们被如此比较时，他们的相似之处足以让不仅证人，而且在场的所有人都感到惊讶。请求我的大人命令我的博学的朋友摘下假发，虽然大人并没有非常乐意地同意，但这种相似之处变得更加显著。我的大人问斯特莱弗先生（被告的律师），接下来是否要因叛国罪审判卡顿先生（我博学的朋友的名字）？但是，斯特莱弗先生回答我的大人说，不；但他会询问证人，是否曾经发生过的事情可能再次发生；如果他早些看到这个对他鲁莽行为的说明是否会如此自信；看到了之后是否会依然如此自信；以及其他问题。结果是，这使得该证人像陶器一样被粉碎，他的部分案件变成了无用的碎片。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Allowing for my learned friend’s appearance being careless and slovenly if not debauched, they were sufficiently like each other to surprise, not only the witness, but everybody present, when they were thus brought into comparison. My Lord being prayed to bid my learned friend lay aside his wig, and giving no very gracious consent, the likeness became much more remarkable. My Lord inquired of Mr. Stryver (the prisoner’s counsel), whether they were next to try Mr. Carton (name of my learned friend) for treason? But, Mr. Stryver replied to my Lord, no; but he would ask the witness to tell him whether what happened once, might happen twice; whether he would have been so confident if he had seen this illustration of his rashness sooner, whether he would be so confident, having seen it; and more. The upshot of which, was, to smash this witness like a crockery vessel, and shiver his part of the case to useless lumber.

source: Tea-time, and Miss Pross making tea, with another fit of the jerks upon her, and yet no Hundreds of people. Mr. Carton had lounged in, but he made only Two.

source: “Is it not impressive, Mr. Darnay?” asked Lucie. “Sometimes, I have sat here of an evening, until I have fancied—but even the shade of a foolish fancy makes me shudder to-night, when all is so black and solemn—”
domestication: “达奈先生，您不觉得这里很壮观吗？”露西问道，“有时候，我晚上坐在这里，会胡思乱想——但今晚这么黑，这么肃穆，连一点愚蠢的念头都会让我发抖——”
foreignization: “达奈先生，这难道不令人印象深刻吗？”露西问道。“有时候，我晚上坐在这里，直到我幻想出——但即使是一个愚蠢的幻想的影子也让我今晚感到战栗，当一切如此黑暗和庄严时——”

source: “Keep where you are,” the guard called to the voice in the mist, “because, if I should make a mistake, it could never be set right in your lifetime. Gentleman of the name of Lorry answer straight.”
domestication: “你就站在那儿别动，”守卫朝着雾中的声音喊道，“万一我开枪打错了，你这辈子也别想活了。姓洛里的先生，请直接回答。”
foreignization: “站在原地别动，”卫兵朝雾中的声音喊道，“因为，如果我犯了错，在你有生之年都无法纠正。名叫洛里的先生请直接回答。”

source: “And mind you!” said Mr. Cruncher. “No games to-morrow! If I, as a honest tradesman, succeed in providing a jinte of meat or two, none of your not touching of it, and sticking to bread. If I, as a honest tradesman, am able to provide a little beer, none of your declaring on water. When you go to Rome, do as Rome does. Rome will be a ugly customer to you, if you don’t. I’m your Rome, you know.”
domestication: “听好了！” Cruncher 先生说，“明天不许闹着玩！我这做生意的人要是弄到一两块肉，你可别不吃，只啃面包。我要是能搞点啤酒回来，你也不许喝白水。到了罗马，就得按罗马的规矩来。你不按规矩来，罗马可不会对你客气。我就是你的罗马，知道吧。”
foreignization: “你听着！”克鲁彻先生说。“明天不许玩！如果我作为一个诚实的商人，能够提供一两块肉，你不许不吃，要坚持吃面包。如果我作为一个诚实的商人，能够提供一点啤酒，你不许喝清水。你到罗马去，就要按罗马的方式行事。如果你不这么做，罗马会对你很不客气。你知道，我就是你的罗马。”

If I, as a honest tradesman, am able to provide a little beer, none of your declaring on water. When you go to Rome, do as Rome does. Rome will be a ugly customer to you, if you don’t. I’m your Rome, you know.”

source: Carton rejoining, “Nothing in life!” Darnay rang. “Do you call the whole reckoning?” said Carton. On his answering in the affirmative, “Then bring me another pint of this same wine, drawer, and come and wake me at ten.”
domestication: 卡顿插话道：“人生不过如此！”达奈接着说：“你这是要结账了？”卡顿问道。达奈点了点头，“那再给我来一品脱同样的酒，伙计，到十点的时候叫我起床。”
foreignization: 卡顿接着说，“生活中一无所有！”达奈叫道。“你叫这是全部的账单吗？”卡顿说。在他肯定回答后，“那么再给我来一品脱同样的酒，伙计，并在十点时叫醒我。”

“Are you mixing that other bowl of punch?” said Stryver the portly, with his hands in his waistband, glancing round from the sofa where he lay on his back.

source: “I was returning from France a few days afterwards, and, at Calais, the prisoner came on board the packet-ship in which I returned, and made the voyage with me.”
domestication: 几天后我从法国回来，在加莱上船时，那个囚犯也登上了我回国的渡轮，跟我一起渡海。
foreignization: 几天后我从法国回来，在加来，这名囚犯登上了我返回的渡轮，并与我一起航行。

source: “Say of you, Miss Manette, and although I know better—although in the mystery of my own wretched heart I know better—I shall never forget it!”
domestication: 小姐，曼内特，虽然我心里明镜似的知道得更多——在我这颗可怜的心里藏着的秘密，我比谁都清楚——但我永远不会忘记你的话！
foreignization: “说你，曼内特小姐，虽然我知道得更多——虽然在我自己悲惨的心灵之谜中我知道得更多——但我永远不会忘记！”

source: Loitering on the way out of court not being allowed, Jerry heard no more: but left them—so like each other in feature, so unlike each other in manner—standing side by side, both reflected in the glass above them.
domestication: 法院门口不能逗留，杰里也没再听下去：但他看见两人站在一起，模样相似，举止却大相径庭，两人的身影映在上方的玻璃上。
foreignization: 不被允许在出法院的路上逗留，杰里没再听到更多：但他留下他们——相貌如此相似，举止却如此不同——并肩而立，两人都倒映在他们上方的玻璃中。

source: A figure entering at the door threw a shadow on Madame Defarge which she felt to be a new one. She laid down her knitting, and began to pin her rose in her head-dress, before she looked at the figure.
domestication: 门口进来一个人影，投在德发日太太身上，她觉得这影子陌生。她放下手里的编织活儿，先别上头饰上的玫瑰，才抬头打量来人。
foreignization: 一个进门的身影在德发日太太身上投下了一个她觉得是新的影子。她放下编织活，开始在头饰上别玫瑰，然后才去看那个身影。

source: Making his way through the tainted crowd, dispersed up and down this hideous scene of action, with the skill of a man accustomed to make his way quietly, the messenger found out the door he sought, and handed in his letter through a trap in it. For, people then paid to see the play at the Old Bailey, just as they paid to see the play in Bedlam—only the former entertainment was much the dearer. Therefore, all the Old Bailey doors were well guarded—except, indeed, the social doors by which the criminals got there, and those were always left wide open.
domestication: 穿过这污秽的场景中零散的人群，信使熟练地找到了他要找的门，从门上的小孔递进信件。那时候，人们花钱看老贝利剧院的戏，就像他们花钱看疯人院的戏一样——只不过老贝利的戏票贵多了。因此，老贝利的所有大门都守得严严实实，唯独那些罪犯进出的门总是敞开着。
foreignization: 穿过这个污秽场面中上下分散的人群，信使凭借一个惯于悄悄穿行的人的技巧找到了他要找的门，并通过门上的一个小孔递进了他的信。因为，当时人们付费观看在老贝利上演的戏剧，就像他们付费观看在贝德兰上演的戏剧一样——只是前者的价格要贵得多。因此，老贝利的所有大门都守卫得很严——除了那些罪犯用来进入的社会大门，这些门总是敞开着的。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Making his way through the tainted crowd, dispersed up and down this hideous scene of action, with the skill of a man accustomed to make his way quietly, the messenger found out the door he sought, and handed in his letter through a trap in it. For, people then paid to see the play at the Old Bailey, just as they paid to see the play in Bedlam—only the former entertainment was much the dearer. Therefore, all the Old Bailey doors were well guarded—except, indeed, the social doors by which the criminals got there, and those were always left wide open.
穿过这污秽的场景中零散的人群，信使熟练地找到了他要找的门，从门上的小孔递进信件。那时候，人们花钱看老贝利剧院的戏，就像他们花钱看疯人院的戏一样——只不过老贝利的戏票贵多了。因此，老贝利的所有大门都守得严严实实，唯独那些罪犯进出的门总是敞开着。

source: “Your confidence in me ought to be returned with full confidence on my part. My present name, though but slightly changed from my mother’s, is not, as you will remember, my own. I wish to tell you what that is, and why I am in England.”
domestication: 你对我的信任，我自然也会对你毫不保留。如今我的名字虽然和母亲的名字相差无几，但你该记得，这并不是我的本名。我想告诉你，我真正的名字是什么，还有我为何会来到英格兰。
foreignization: 你对我的信任应该得到我完全的信任作为回报。我现在的名字虽然只是稍微改变了我母亲的名字，但正如你所记得的，它并不是我自己的。我希望告诉你那是什么，以及我为什么在英国。

source: Lucie shuddered as he threw two more billets into his basket, but it was impossible to be there while the wood-sawyer was at work, and not be in his sight. Thenceforth, to secure his good will, she always spoke to him first, and often gave him drink-money, which he readily received.
domestication: 卢西抖了一下，只见他又往篮子里扔了两根木头。在伐木工工作的时候，在这里却不能不让他看见。从那以后，为了讨好他，卢西总是先跟他打招呼，还常常给他买酒的钱，他倒也乐意收下。
foreignization: 露西在他往篮子里扔进两根木柴时颤抖了一下，但当伐木工人在工作时，在场却不在他的视线内是不可能的。从此以后，为了确保他的好感，她总是先和他说话，并且经常给他酒钱，而他也很乐意接受。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

source: So, leaving only one light burning on the large hearth, he let his thin gauze curtains fall around him, and heard the night break its silence with a long sigh as he composed himself to sleep.
domestication: 于是，他只在大壁炉上留了一盏灯，放下薄薄的纱帘，周围一片寂静，夜色似乎长长地叹了一口气，他也随之安顿下来准备入睡。
foreignization: 所以，只留下壁炉上的一盏灯亮着，他让薄纱窗帘在他周围垂下，当他准备入睡时，听到夜晚发出了一声长长的叹息，打破了寂静。

source: The accursed was already under the carriage with some half-dozen particular friends, pointing out the chain with his blue cap. Some half-dozen other particular friends promptly hauled him out, and presented him breathless to Monsieur the Marquis.
domestication: 那个该死的家伙已经钻到马车底下了，身边还有五六个狐朋狗友，他用蓝帽子指了指链条。另外五六个哥们儿眼疾手快，把他拽了出来，气喘吁吁地送到了侯爵大人面前。
foreignization: 那个诅咒的人已经在马车下面，和六七个特定的朋友一起，用他的蓝帽子指着链条。另外六七个特定的朋友立刻把他拖了出来，气喘吁吁地呈现在侯爵大人面前。

source: In the universal fear and distrust that darkened the time, all the usual harmless ways of life were changed. In the Doctor’s little household, as in very many others, the articles of daily consumption that were wanted were purchased every evening, in small quantities and at various small shops. To avoid attracting notice, and to give as little occasion as possible for talk and envy, was the general desire.
domestication: 在那人人自危、互不信任的黑暗年代，往日那些无害的生活方式都变了样。像医生这样的小家庭，还有许多其他人家，每天晚上都会去买些日常生活所需的东西，每次买得不多，而且分散在几家小店里。大家的心愿就是尽量不引起注意，少给他人留下话柄和嫉妒的机会。
foreignization: 在普遍的恐惧和不信任中，那段阴暗时期，所有日常生活的惯常无害方式都发生了变化。在医生的小家庭中，就像在许多其他家庭一样，每天所需的消费品都是每晚购买，分小批量并在不同的小商店购买。为了避免引起注意，并尽可能减少闲言碎语和嫉妒，这是大家普遍的愿望。

To avoid attracting notice, and to give as little occasion as possible for talk and envy, was the general desire.

source: His way taking him past Tellson’s, and he both banking at Tellson’s and knowing Mr. Lorry as the intimate friend of the Manettes, it entered Mr. Stryver’s mind to enter the bank, and reveal to Mr. Lorry the brightness of the Soho horizon. So, he pushed open the door with the weak rattle in its throat, stumbled down the two steps, got past the two ancient cashiers, and shouldered himself into the musty back closet where Mr. Lorry sat at great books ruled for figures, with perpendicular iron bars to his window as if that were ruled for figures too, and everything under the clouds were a sum.
domestication: 他的路正好经过特尔森银行，他自己也在那里有存款，又和洛里先生关系不错，知道他是曼内特一家的密友，斯特莱弗先生就动了心思，想进银行把苏荷区那边的好消息告诉洛里先生。于是，他推开了那扇摇摇晃晃的门，下了两级台阶，绕过两位老出纳，挤进了洛里先生所在的那间发霉的后屋。洛里先生正坐在一堆标着数字的账本前，窗户上还装着垂直的铁栅栏，好像那些也是数字一样，仿佛天底下所有的事情都是一道算术题。

So, he pushed open the door with the weak rattle in its throat, stumbled down the two steps, got past the two ancient cashiers, and shouldered himself into the musty back closet where Mr. Lorry sat at great books ruled for figures, with perpendicular iron bars to his window as if that were ruled for figures too, and everything under the clouds were a sum.

source: But, not for long. Presently, the chateau began to make itself strangely visible by some light of its own, as though it were growing luminous. Then, a flickering streak played behind the architecture of the front, picking out transparent places, and showing where balustrades, arches, and windows were. Then it soared higher, and grew broader and brighter. Soon, from a score of the great windows, flames burst forth, and the stone faces awakened, stared out of fire.
domestication: 但好景不长。很快，城堡仿佛自身发出了奇异的光芒，好似变得通体透亮。接着，一道摇曳的光束在建筑正面后方跃动，勾勒出透明的部分，显露出栏杆、拱门和窗户的位置。这光束越升越高，越来越宽，也越来越亮。没过多久，数十个大窗户里突然蹿出火焰，石像的脸庞似乎被唤醒，在火光中瞪大了眼睛。
foreignization: 但是，没过多久。不久，城堡开始以一种奇特的方式自行显现，仿佛它正在发光。然后，一道闪烁的光带在前立面的建筑后面游动，勾勒出透明的地方，显示出栏杆、拱门和窗户的位置。接着，这道光带上扬，变得更宽更亮。很快，从几十个大窗户中，火焰喷涌而出，石制的脸庞苏醒过来，从火光中凝视出来。

messages_domestication.content:

Then it soared higher, and grew broader and brighter. Soon, from a score of the great windows, flames burst forth, and the stone faces awakened, stared out of fire.

But, not for long. Presently, the chateau began to make itself strangely visible by some light of its own, as though it were growing luminous. Then, a flickering streak played behind the architecture of the front, picking out transparent places, and showing where balustrades, arches, and windows were. Then it soared higher, and grew broader and brighter. Soon, from a score of the great windows, flames burst forth, and the stone faces awakened, stared out of fire.
但是，没过多久。不久，城堡开始以一种奇特的方式自行显现，仿佛它正在发光。然后，一道闪烁的光带在前立面的建筑后面游动，勾勒出透明的地方，显示出栏杆、拱门和窗户的位置。接着，这道光带上扬，变得更宽更亮。很快，从几十个大窗户中，火焰喷涌而出，石制的脸庞苏醒过来，从火光中凝视出来。

source: Besides the glancing tears that shone among the smiles of the little group when it was done, some diamonds, very bright and sparkling, glanced on the bride’s hand, which were newly released from the dark obscurity of one of Mr. Lorry’s pockets. They returned home to breakfast, and all went well, and in due course the golden hair that had mingled with the poor shoemaker’s white locks in the Paris garret, were mingled with them again in the morning sunlight, on the threshold of the door at parting.
domestication: 除了小团体微笑着时闪现的泪光，新娘的手上也闪烁着几颗新从洛里先生口袋深处解放出来的亮晶晶的钻石。他们回家吃早饭，一切顺利。当年在巴黎阁楼上与老鞋匠白发交织的金色头发，此刻又在告别时的晨光中，在门口重逢。
foreignization: 除了当一切完毕时，在这个小团体的笑容中闪烁的泪光之外，一些非常明亮和闪耀的钻石也在新娘的手上闪闪发光，这些钻石刚从洛里先生的一个口袋里的黑暗隐匿处释放出来。他们回家吃早饭，一切都很好，到了该分别的时候，在门门槛上，在早晨的阳光下，那些曾与贫穷鞋匠的白发在巴黎阁楼中交织在一起的金色头发再次与它们交织在一起。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Besides the glancing tears that shone among the smiles of the little group when it was done, some diamonds, very bright and sparkling, glanced on the bride’s hand, which were newly released from the dark obscurity of one of Mr. Lorry’s pockets. They returned home to breakfast, and all went well, and in due course the golden hair that had mingled with the poor shoemaker’s white locks in the Paris garret, were mingled with them again in the morning sunlight, on the threshold of the door at parting.

source: “Meanwhile,” said the Marquis, “I will preserve the honour and repose of the family, if you will not. But you must be fatigued. Shall we terminate our conference for the night?”
domestication: 侯爵接着说：“你要不保全家族的名誉和安宁，那我来担着。你肯定也累了，咱们今天就谈到这儿吧？”
foreignization: “与此同时，”侯爵说，“如果你不愿维护家族的荣誉和安宁，我将会这么做。但你一定很疲倦了。我们是否可以结束今晚的会谈？”

messages_domestication.content:

侯爵接着说：“你要不保全家族的名誉和安宁，那我来担着。你肯定也累了，咱们今天就谈到这儿吧？”

“Meanwhile,” said the Marquis, “I will preserve the honour and repose of the family, if you will not. But you must be fatigued. Shall we terminate our conference for the night?”
“与此同时，”侯爵说，“如果你不愿维护家族的荣誉和安宁，我将会这么做。但你一定很疲倦了。我们是否可以结束今晚的会谈？”

source: She said it aloud, but added to herself, as she resumed her knitting: “Hah! Good day, age about forty, height about five feet nine, black hair, generally rather handsome visage, complexion dark, eyes dark, thin, long and sallow face, aquiline nose but not straight, having a peculiar inclination towards the left cheek which imparts a sinister expression! Good day, one and all!”
domestication: 她大声说完了，却在心里嘀咕着，一边继续她的编织活儿：“哼！好得很，四十上下，一米七八的个头，黑头发，长得还算俊俏，就是脸色暗沉，眼睛也是黑的，瘦高个儿，脸长长的，还有点黄，鼻子挺得像鹰勾，但不正，还往左边歪，显得有点儿邪乎！好得很，各位！”
foreignization: 她说出了声，但当她重新拿起针线时，又在心里加了一句：“哈！早上好，年纪大约四十岁，身高约五英尺九英寸，黑发，通常相貌相当英俊，肤色较暗，眼睛黑色，身材瘦长且略显蜡黄，鼻子鹰钩但不直，有向左脸颊倾斜的奇特倾向，这赋予了他一种邪恶的表情！早上好，各位！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

She said it aloud, but added to herself, as she resumed her knitting: “Hah! Good day, age about forty, height about five feet nine, black hair, generally rather handsome visage, complexion dark, eyes dark, thin, long and sallow face, aquiline nose but not straight, having a peculiar inclination towards the left cheek which imparts a sinister expression! Good day, one and all!”
她大声说完了，却在心里嘀咕着，一边继续她的编织活儿：“哼！好得很，四十上下，一米七八的个头，黑头发，长得还算俊俏，就是脸色暗沉，眼睛也是黑的，瘦高个儿，脸长长的，还有点黄，鼻子挺得像鹰勾，但不正，还往左边歪，显得有点儿邪乎！好得很，各位！”

source: “Ah, what a pity! We so much regret it! But take courage; several members of our society have been in secret, at first, and it has lasted but a short time.” Then he added, raising his voice, “I grieve to inform the society—in secret.”
domestication: 唉，真可惜！我们真是后悔不已！但你振作点，我们社团里好几个人一开始也都是秘密加入的，而且时间都不长。接着他提高声音说：“我难过地通知各位社员——这是保密的。”
foreignization: “啊，真遗憾！我们非常后悔！但要振作起来；我们社团的几位成员一开始都是秘密加入的，而且持续的时间并不长。”然后他提高声音说，“我悲痛地通知社团——这是秘密的。”

source: The door of the Doctor’s room opened, and he came out with Charles Darnay. He was so deadly pale—which had not been the case when they went in together—that no vestige of colour was to be seen in his face. But, in the composure of his manner he was unaltered, except that to the shrewd glance of Mr. Lorry it disclosed some shadowy indication that the old air of avoidance and dread had lately passed over him, like a cold wind.
domestication: 医生房间的门开了，他和查尔斯·达尔内走了出来。达尔内的脸色苍白得吓人，刚才进去的时候可不是这样的，现在脸上一点血色也没有。不过，他的举止还是很镇定，只是在老练的洛里先生眼中，似乎察觉到一丝微妙的变化：好像有一股冷风刚刚从他身上掠过，带来了以往那种回避和恐惧的气息。
foreignization: 医生的房间门开了，他和查尔斯·达奈一起走了出来。他的脸色如此苍白——而他们一起进去时并非如此——以至于脸上一点血色也没有。但是，除了在洛里先生敏锐的目光中透露出一些模糊的迹象，表明最近有一股冷风般的回避和恐惧之感掠过他，他在举止上的平静并未改变。

He was so deadly pale—which had not been the case when they went in together—that no vestige of colour was to be seen in his face. But, in the composure of his manner he was unaltered, except that to the shrewd glance of Mr. Lorry it disclosed some shadowy indication that the old air of avoidance and dread had lately passed over him, like a cold wind.

source: No white-haired man was there now; but, the three men were there who had gone out of the wine-shop singly. And between them and the white-haired man afar off, was the one small link, that they had once looked in at him through the chinks in the wall.
domestication: 现在白发老翁已不见踪影，但那三个从酒馆里一个接一个出来的汉子却在那儿。他们和远处的白发老翁之间，唯一的联系就是曾经透过墙缝偷看过他一眼。
foreignization: 现在那里没有白发老人；但是，那三个曾经独自走出酒馆的人在那儿。在他们和远处的白发老人之间，唯一的小小联系是，他们曾经透过墙缝看过他。

source: “You dogs!” said the Marquis, but smoothly, and with an unchanged front, except as to the spots on his nose: “I would ride over any of you very willingly, and exterminate you from the earth. If I knew which rascal threw at the carriage, and if that brigand were sufficiently near it, he should be crushed under the wheels.”
domestication: “你们这些狗东西！”侯爵冷冷地说，脸上除了鼻头上的红斑外，看不出任何变化，“我巴不得能踩死你们任何一个，让你们从这世界上消失。要是我知道是谁敢扔东西砸我的马车，要是那家伙够近的话，我非得让他葬身车轮之下不可。”
foreignization: “你们这些狗东西！”侯爵说，语气平滑，脸上的表情也没有变，除了鼻梁上的斑点：“我非常愿意骑马踏过你们任何一个，并将你们从地球上消灭。如果我知道是谁向马车扔东西，而且那个匪徒足够靠近马车的话，他应该会被车轮碾压。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“You dogs!” said the Marquis, but smoothly, and with an unchanged front, except as to the spots on his nose: “I would ride over any of you very willingly, and exterminate you from the earth. If I knew which rascal threw at the carriage, and if that brigand were sufficiently near it, he should be crushed under the wheels.”
“你们这些狗东西！”侯爵冷冷地说，脸上除了鼻头上的红斑外，看不出任何变化，“我巴不得能踩死你们任何一个，让你们从这世界上消失。要是我知道是谁敢扔东西砸我的马车，要是那家伙够近的话，我非得让他葬身车轮之下不可。”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“You dogs!” said the Marquis, but smoothly, and with an unchanged front, except as to the spots on his nose: “I would ride over any of you very willingly, and exterminate you from the earth. If I knew which rascal threw at the carriage, and if that brigand were sufficiently near it, he should be crushed under the wheels.”
“你们这些狗东西！”侯爵说，语气平滑，脸上的表情也没有变，除了鼻梁上的斑点：“我非常愿意骑马踏过你们任何一个，并将你们从地球上消灭。如果我知道是谁向马车扔东西，而且那个匪徒足够靠近马车的话，他应该会被车轮碾压。”

source: Such a man came upon him, like a ghost, at noon in the July weather, as he sat on his heap of stones under a bank, taking such shelter as he could get from a shower of hail.
domestication: 这人就像鬼一样，在七月的中午冒了出来。当时他正坐在一堆石头上，靠在堤岸边，尽可能躲着一场冰雹。
foreignization: 这样一个男人在七月的中午像鬼一样出现在他面前，当时他正坐在河岸下的石堆上，尽可能地躲避冰雹的袭击。

source: Doctor Manette received such patients here as his old reputation, and its revival in the floating whispers of his story, brought him. His scientific knowledge, and his vigilance and skill in conducting ingenious experiments, brought him otherwise into moderate request, and he earned as much as he wanted.
domestication: 曼内特医生在这里接诊的病人，都是冲着他过去的名声和他故事里流传的只言片语来的。他那扎实的科学知识，再加上在巧妙实验中的警觉与技巧，使他在这一行里有了适度的需求，挣的钱也足够用了。
foreignization: 马内特医生在这里接待那些因为他的旧名声以及他故事中的零星传闻的复兴而找上门来的病人。他的科学知识，以及他在进行巧妙实验时的警觉性和技巧，使他在其他方面也得到了适度的需求，他赚到了他想要的那么多。

source: When he had sat in his saddle some half-hour, taking note of these things, Darnay found himself confronted by the same man in authority, who directed the guard to open the barrier. Then he delivered to the escort, drunk and sober, a receipt for the escorted, and requested him to dismount. He did so, and the two patriots, leading his tired horse, turned and rode away without entering the city.
domestication: 他骑在马上，观察了这些情况大约半个时辰后，达奈发现自己又遇到了那个有权力的人，那人命令守卫打开关卡。接着，他给护送人员，不管是清醒的还是醉酒的，开了一张收条，并要求达奈下马。达奈照做了，那两个爱国者牵着他的疲惫的马，转身离开，没有进城。
foreignization: 当他坐在马鞍上大约半小时，注意到这些事情时，达奈发现自己面对着同一位有权势的人，他命令卫兵打开路障。然后，他向押送人员，无论是醉酒的还是清醒的，交了一份被押送者的收据，并要求他下马。他照做了，两名爱国者牵着他的疲惫的马，转身骑马离开了，没有进入城市。

messages_domestication.content:

He did so, and the two patriots, leading his tired horse, turned and rode away without entering the city.

He did so, and the two patriots, leading his tired horse, turned and rode away without entering the city.
当他坐在马鞍上大约半小时，注意到这些事情时，达奈发现自己面对着同一位有权势的人，他命令卫兵打开路障。然后，他向押送人员，无论是醉酒的还是清醒的，交了一份被押送者的收据，并要求他下马。他照做了，两名爱国者牵着他的疲惫的马，转身骑马离开了，没有进入城市。

source: The pace was suddenly checked, and, with much splashing and floundering, a man’s voice called from the mist, “Is that the Dover mail?”
domestication: 马车猛然停了下来，水花四溅，人仰马翻。迷雾中，一个男人的声音喊道：“这是去多佛的邮车吗？”
foreignization: 脚步突然停了下来，伴随着大量的水花和挣扎声，一个男人的声音从雾中喊道：“这是去多佛的邮车吗？”

The pace was suddenly checked, and, with much splashing and floundering, a man’s voice called from the mist, “Is that the Dover mail?”
马车猛然停了下来，水花四溅，人仰马翻。迷雾中，一个男人的声音喊道：“这是去多佛的邮车吗？”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The pace was suddenly checked, and, with much splashing and floundering, a man’s voice called from the mist, “Is that the Dover mail?”
脚步突然停了下来，伴随着大量的水花和挣扎声，一个男人的声音从雾中喊道：“这是去多佛的邮车吗？”

source: Madame Defarge and monsieur her husband returned amicably to the bosom of Saint Antoine, while a speck in a blue cap toiled through the darkness, and through the dust, and down the weary miles of avenue by the wayside, slowly tending towards that point of the compass where the chateau of Monsieur the Marquis, now in his grave, listened to the whispering trees. Such ample leisure had the stone faces, now, for listening to the trees and to the fountain, that the few village scarecrows who, in their quest for herbs to eat and fragments of dead stick to burn, strayed within sight of the great stone courtyard and terrace staircase, had it borne in upon their starved fancy that the expression of the faces was altered. A rumour just lived in the village—had a faint and bare existence there, as its people had—that when the knife struck home, the faces changed, from faces of pride to faces of anger and pain; also, that when that dangling figure was hauled up forty feet above the fountain, they changed again, and bore a cruel look of being avenged, which they would henceforth bear for ever. In the stone face over the great window of the bed-chamber where the murder was done, two fine dints were pointed out in the sculptured nose, which everybody recognised, and which nobody had seen of old; and on the scarce occasions when two or three ragged peasants emerged from the crowd to take a hurried peep at Monsieur the Marquis petrified, a skinny finger would not have pointed to it for a minute, before they all started away among the moss and leaves, like the more fortunate hares who could find a living there.
domestication: 德法日太太和她丈夫和和气气地回到了圣安托万的怀抱。而在黑暗中，一顶蓝帽子下的身影艰难地跋涉着，穿过尘土飞扬的道路，沿着路旁绵延的林荫大道，缓慢地朝罗盘上的一个方向走去——那里有侯爵大人如今长眠的城堡，静听着轻风吹拂树叶的声音。现在，那些石雕的脸孔有了大把的时间去倾听树木和泉水的低语。因此，当少数几个村民为了寻找吃的草药和烧的枯枝而误入那座大石院子和台阶露台的视线范围内时，他们饥饿的想象中便产生了这样的念头：这些脸孔的表情变了。村子里流传着一个模糊的传言，就像村民们一样，苟延残喘地存在着——说当刀子刺入的时候，那些面孔从骄傲变成了愤怒和痛苦；而当那个吊起的人被拉到喷泉上方四十英尺高的地方时，它们又变了，变得面目狰狞，仿佛得到了复仇，从此永远带着这种表情。在发生凶杀案的卧室大窗户上方的石雕脸上，人们指出了两个鼻梁上的小凹痕，人人都认得出，但以前却没人见过；而当两三个衣衫褴褛的农民偶尔从人群中钻出来，匆匆瞥一眼已成石像的侯爵大人时，一只瘦骨嶙峋的手指刚指向它，他们就会像那些能在那儿找到活路的幸运野兔一样，飞快地消失在苔藓和树叶之间。
foreignization: 德法日太太和她的丈夫友好地回到了圣安托万的怀抱，而一个戴蓝帽子的小黑点在黑暗中、尘土中，沿着路边的林荫大道缓缓前行，逐渐朝罗爵老爷（现已入土）的城堡所在的方向移动，那座城堡聆听着树间的低语。如今，那些石头脸孔有充裕的时间倾听树木和喷泉的声音，以至于那些为了寻找可以食用的草药和可以燃烧的枯枝而偶尔进入这座巨大的石质庭院和露台楼梯视野中的乡村贫民，都产生了饥饿的幻想，觉得这些面孔的表情已经改变了。村里流传着一个模糊而微弱的传言——就像村里的居民一样——当刀子刺入时，那些面孔从骄傲变成了愤怒和痛苦；当那个悬挂的尸体被吊起在喷泉上方四十英尺时，它们再次改变，呈现出一种残酷的复仇表情，从此将永远保持这种表情。在发生谋杀案的卧室大窗户上方的石像脸上，人们指出了两个精美的凹痕，这些凹痕人人都认识，但过去却从未有人见过；而在少数几个衣衫褴褛的农民从人群中走出来匆匆一瞥已被石化的罗爵老爷时，一根瘦骨嶙峋的手指不会指向它超过一分钟，他们就会像那些能在那里找到活路的更幸运的野兔一样，在苔藓和树叶间四散奔逃。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Madame Defarge and monsieur her husband returned amicably to the bosom of Saint Antoine, while a speck in a blue cap toiled through the darkness, and through the dust, and down the weary miles of avenue by the wayside, slowly tending towards that point of the compass where the chateau of Monsieur the Marquis, now in his grave, listened to the whispering trees. Such ample leisure had the stone faces, now, for listening to the trees and to the fountain, that the few village scarecrows who, in their quest for herbs to eat and fragments of dead stick to burn, strayed within sight of the great stone courtyard and terrace staircase, had it borne in upon their starved fancy that the expression of the faces was altered. A rumour just lived in the village—had a faint and bare existence there, as its people had—that when the knife struck home, the faces changed, from faces of pride to faces of anger and pain; also, that when that dangling figure was hauled up forty feet above the fountain, they changed again, and bore a cruel look of being avenged, which they would henceforth bear for ever. In the stone face over the great window of the bed-chamber where the murder was done, two fine dints were pointed out in the sculptured nose, which everybody recognised, and which nobody had seen of old; and on the scarce occasions when two or three ragged peasants emerged from the crowd to take a hurried peep at Monsieur the Marquis petrified, a skinny finger would not have pointed to it for a minute, before they all started away among the moss and leaves, like the more fortunate hares who could find a living there.

德法日太太和她丈夫和和气气地回到了圣安托万的怀抱。而在黑暗中，一顶蓝帽子下的身影艰难地跋涉着，穿过尘土飞扬的道路，沿着路旁绵延的林荫大道，缓慢地朝罗盘上的一个方向走去——那里有侯爵大人如今长眠的城堡，静听着轻风吹拂树叶的声音。现在，那些石雕的脸孔有了大把的时间去倾听树木和泉水的低语。因此，当少数几个村民为了寻找吃的草药和烧的枯枝而误入那座大石院子和台阶露台的视线范围内时，他们饥饿的想象中便产生了这样的念头：这些脸孔的表情变了。村子里流传着一个模糊的传言，就像村民们一样，苟延残喘地存在着——说当刀子刺入的时候，那些面孔从骄傲变成了愤怒和痛苦；而当那个吊起的人被拉到喷泉上方四十英尺高的地方时，它们又变了，变得面目狰狞，仿佛得到了复仇，从此永远带着这种表情。在发生凶杀案的卧室大窗户上方的石雕脸上，人们指出了两个鼻梁上的小凹痕，人人都认得出，但以前却没人见过；而当两三个衣衫褴褛的农民偶尔从人群中钻出来，匆匆瞥一眼已成石像的侯爵大人时，一只瘦骨嶙峋的手指刚指向它，他们就会像那些能在那儿找到活路的幸运野兔一样，飞快地消失在苔藓和树叶之间。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Such ample leisure had the stone faces, now, for listening to the trees and to the fountain, that the few village scarecrows who, in their quest for herbs to eat and fragments of dead stick to burn, strayed within sight of the great stone courtyard and terrace staircase, had it borne in upon their starved fancy that the expression of the faces was altered. A rumour just lived in the village—had a faint and bare existence there, as its people had—that when the knife struck home, the faces changed, from faces of pride to faces of anger and pain; also, that when that dangling figure was hauled up forty feet above the fountain, they changed again, and bore a cruel look of being avenged, which they would henceforth bear for ever. In the stone face over the great window of the bed-chamber where the murder was done, two fine dints were pointed out in the sculptured nose, which everybody recognised, and which nobody had seen of old; and on the scarce occasions when two or three ragged peasants emerged from the crowd to take a hurried peep at Monsieur the Marquis petrified, a skinny finger would not have pointed to it for a minute, before they all started away among the moss and leaves, like the more fortunate hares who could find a living there.

source: The Doctor grasped his hand, and murmured, “That was very kind. That was very thoughtful!” Mr. Lorry grasped his hand in return, and neither of the two spoke for a little while.
domestication: 医生握着他的手，轻声说道：“真是太过意不去了，你真是太周到了！”老黎先生也紧紧回握了他的手，两个人都没再说话，沉默了好一会儿。
foreignization: 医生握住了他的手，低声说道：“这非常善良。这非常周到！”洛里先生也握住了他的手，两人有片刻都没有说话。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The Doctor grasped his hand, and murmured, “That was very kind. That was very thoughtful!” Mr. Lorry grasped his hand in return, and neither of the two spoke for a little while.
医生握着他的手，轻声说道：“真是太过意不去了，你真是太周到了！”老黎先生也紧紧回握了他的手，两个人都没再说话，沉默了好一会儿。

messages_foreignization.content:

The Doctor grasped his hand, and murmured, “That was very kind. That was very thoughtful!” Mr. Lorry grasped his hand in return, and neither of the two spoke for a little while.
医生握住了他的手，低声说道：“这非常善良。这非常周到！”洛里先生也握住了他的手，两人有片刻都没有说话。

source: “I think not. It may be the character of his mind, to be always in singular need of occupation. That may be, in part, natural to it; in part, the result of affliction. The less it was occupied with healthy things, the more it would be in danger of turning in the unhealthy direction. He may have observed himself, and made the discovery.”
domestication: 我觉得不是这样。他这个人可能天生就闲不下来，这或许一部分是他的天性，一部分是因为受过打击。心里装的事越少，就越容易往歪路上想。他自己可能也注意到了这一点，发现了这个规律。
foreignization: “我不这样认为。这可能是他的心理特点，总是有特别需要忙碌的需求。这可能部分是天生的；部分是苦难的结果。他越是不忙于健康的事物，就越是有可能转向不健康的方向。他可能观察过自己，并得出了这个发现。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I think not. It may be the character of his mind, to be always in singular need of occupation. That may be, in part, natural to it; in part, the result of affliction. The less it was occupied with healthy things, the more it would be in danger of turning in the unhealthy direction. He may have observed himself, and made the discovery.”
我觉得不是这样。他这个人可能天生就闲不下来，这或许一部分是他的天性，一部分是因为受过打击。心里装的事越少，就越容易往歪路上想。他自己可能也注意到了这一点，发现了这个规律。

“我不这样认为。这可能是他的心理特点，总是有特别需要忙碌的需求。这可能部分是天生的；部分是苦难的结果。他越是不忙于健康的事物，就越是有可能转向不健康的方向。他可能观察过自己，并得出了这个发现。”

source: “Mr. Lorry, look again upon the prisoner. Supposing him wrapped up as those two passengers were, is there anything in his bulk and stature to render it unlikely that he was one of them?”
domestication: 老洛里，你再仔细看看被告。假如他像那两位乘客一样裹得严严实实的，他的体格和身材有什么地方会让你觉得他不可能是其中一位呢？
foreignization: 洛里先生，请再看看被告。假设他像那两个乘客那样裹着衣服，他的体形和身材有没有什么特点，使得他不太可能是其中之一？

source: An hour and a half limped heavily away in the thief-and-rascal crowded passages below, even though assisted off with mutton pies and ale. The hoarse messenger, uncomfortably seated on a form after taking that refection, had dropped into a doze, when a loud murmur and a rapid tide of people setting up the stairs that led to the court, carried him along with them.
domestication: 一个半小时拖拖拉拉地过去了，尽管贼窝里的人们吃了羊肉派，喝了啤酒。那嗓音沙哑的信使，吃完这些后坐在长凳上，不太舒服，打起了瞌睡。突然，一阵喧闹声响起，人群像潮水般涌向通往法庭的楼梯，他也被卷了进去。
foreignization: 一个半小时在下面挤满小偷和恶棍的通道里沉重地过去了，尽管有羊肉馅饼和啤酒的帮助。那个声音嘶哑的信使，在吃了那顿饭后不太舒服地坐在长凳上，已经打起了瞌睡，这时一阵嘈杂声和迅速涌向通往法庭楼梯的人潮把他一起带了上去。

source: The miserable shop of the wood-sawyer was so small, that its whole surface furnished very indifferent space for this legend. He had got somebody to scrawl it up for him, however, who had squeezed Death in with most inappropriate difficulty. On his house-top, he displayed pike and cap, as a good citizen must, and in a window he had stationed his saw inscribed as his “Little Sainte Guillotine”—for the great sharp female was by that time popularly canonised. His shop was shut and he was not there, which was a relief to Lucie, and left her quite alone.
domestication: 木锯匠的破铺子小得可怜，连块像样的招牌都挂不下。不过他还是找人胡乱写了个招牌，那字迹挤得跟蚂蚁似的，把“死亡”两字硬塞了进去。房顶上，他像个好市民似的，竖着尖头帽和长矛。窗户里摆着他那把锯子，上面刻着“小圣吉伦廷”，因为那会儿大号的利刃已被人们奉为圣物。店铺关着，木锯匠也不在，这让露西松了口气，她终于可以独自一人待着了。
foreignization: 那可怜的木锯匠的店铺非常小，以至于它的整个表面只能勉强提供这个标志的空间。不过，他还是找人用潦草的手法为他写上了这个标志，而且是以极不合适的难度挤进了“死亡”这个词。在屋顶上，他像一个好市民应该做的那样展示着pike和cap，并且在窗户里放置了他的锯子，上面写着他的“小圣吉约特姆”——因为那个大的锋利的女性当时已被大众奉为圣人。他的店铺关着，而他也不在，这使露西感到宽慰，让她完全独自一人。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The miserable shop of the wood-sawyer was so small, that its whole surface furnished very indifferent space for this legend. He had got somebody to scrawl it up for him, however, who had squeezed Death in with most inappropriate difficulty. On his house-top, he displayed pike and cap, as a good citizen must, and in a window he had stationed his saw inscribed as his “Little Sainte Guillotine”—for the great sharp female was by that time popularly canonised. His shop was shut and he was not there, which was a relief to Lucie, and left her quite alone.
木锯匠的破铺子小得可怜，连块像样的招牌都挂不下。不过他还是找人胡乱写了个招牌，那字迹挤得跟蚂蚁似的，把“死亡”两字硬塞了进去。房顶上，他像个好市民似的，竖着尖头帽和长矛。窗户里摆着他那把锯子，上面刻着“小圣吉伦廷”，因为那会儿大号的利刃已被人们奉为圣物。店铺关着，木锯匠也不在，这让露西松了口气，她终于可以独自一人待着了。

source: Although the Doctor’s daughter had known nothing of the country of her birth, she appeared to have innately derived from it that ability to make much of little means, which is one of its most useful and most agreeable characteristics. Simple as the furniture was, it was set off by so many little adornments, of no value but for their taste and fancy, that its effect was delightful. The disposition of everything in the rooms, from the largest object to the least; the arrangement of colours, the elegant variety and contrast obtained by thrift in trifles, by delicate hands, clear eyes, and good sense; were at once so pleasant in themselves, and so expressive of their originator, that, as Mr. Lorry stood looking about him, the very chairs and tables seemed to ask him, with something of that peculiar expression which he knew so well by this time, whether he approved?
domestication: 尽管医生的女儿对她出生的国度一无所知，但她似乎天生就继承了那里的一个宝贵特质——那就是能用最少的资源创造出最大的价值。家具虽简朴，却因为许多小巧精致的装饰而显得格外悦目，这些装饰本身并不值钱，但因为独具匠心，令人赏心悦目。屋内的每一样东西，从大到小，颜色的搭配，细微之处的巧妙安排，都透露出优雅和对比，这一切既让人感到舒适，又充分体现了布置者的巧思。当洛里先生站在房间里环顾四周时，那些椅子和桌子仿佛也在用他早已熟悉的眼神问他：您满意吗？
foreignization: 虽然医生的女儿对她出生的国家一无所知，但她似乎天生就继承了那个国家的一种能力，即能够用很少的手段做出很多的事情，这是该国最有用也是最令人愉悦的特点之一。家具虽然简单，但却因许多小装饰而显得生动，这些装饰本身并无价值，但因其品味和创意而显得珍贵，效果令人愉悦。房间内的一切布置，从最大的物件到最小的细节；颜色的搭配，通过在琐事上的节俭、灵巧的手、明亮的眼睛和良好的判断力所获得的优雅的多样性与对比；既本身令人愉快，又充分体现了其创造者的特质，以至于当洛里先生站在那里环顾四周时，连椅子和桌子似乎都在以他此时已非常熟悉的那种特有的表情问他是否满意？

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Although the Doctor’s daughter had known nothing of the country of her birth, she appeared to have innately derived from it that ability to make much of little means, which is one of its most useful and most agreeable characteristics. Simple as the furniture was, it was set off by so many little adornments, of no value but for their taste and fancy, that its effect was delightful. The disposition of everything in the rooms, from the largest object to the least; the arrangement of colours, the elegant variety and contrast obtained by thrift in trifles, by delicate hands, clear eyes, and good sense; were at once so pleasant in themselves, and so expressive of their originator, that, as Mr. Lorry stood looking about him, the very chairs and tables seemed to ask him, with something of that peculiar expression which he knew so well by this time, whether he approved?

尽管医生的女儿对她出生的国度一无所知，但她似乎天生就继承了那里的一个宝贵特质——那就是能用最少的资源创造出最大的价值。家具虽简朴，却因为许多小巧精致的装饰而显得格外悦目，这些装饰本身并不值钱，但因为独具匠心，令人赏心悦目。屋内的每一样东西，从大到小，颜色的搭配，细微之处的巧妙安排，都透露出优雅和对比，这一切既让人感到舒适，又充分体现了布置者的巧思。当洛里先生站在房间里环顾四周时，那些椅子和桌子仿佛也在用他早已熟悉的眼神问他：您满意吗？

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Although the Doctor’s daughter had known nothing of the country of her birth, she appeared to have innately derived from it that ability to make much of little means, which is one of its most useful and most agreeable characteristics. Simple as the furniture was, it was set off by so many little adornments, of no value but for their taste and fancy, that its effect was delightful. The disposition of everything in the rooms, from the largest object to the least; the arrangement of colours, the elegant variety and contrast obtained by thrift in trifles, by delicate hands, clear eyes, and good sense; were at once so pleasant in themselves, and so expressive of their originator, that, as Mr. Lorry stood looking about him, the very chairs and tables seemed to ask him, with something of that peculiar expression which he knew so well by this time, whether he approved?

虽然医生的女儿对她出生的国家一无所知，但她似乎天生就继承了那个国家的一种能力，即能够用很少的手段做出很多的事情，这是该国最有用也是最令人愉悦的特点之一。家具虽然简单，但却因许多小装饰而显得生动，这些装饰本身并无价值，但因其品味和创意而显得珍贵，效果令人愉悦。房间内的一切布置，从最大的物件到最小的细节；颜色的搭配，通过在琐事上的节俭、灵巧的手、明亮的眼睛和良好的判断力所获得的优雅的多样性与对比；既本身令人愉快，又充分体现了其创造者的特质，以至于当洛里先生站在那里环顾四周时，连椅子和桌子似乎都在以他此时已非常熟悉的那种特有的表情问他是否满意？

source: Deep ditch, single drawbridge, massive stone walls, eight great towers, cannon, muskets, fire and smoke. One drawbridge down! “Work, comrades all, work! Work, Jacques One, Jacques Two, Jacques One Thousand, Jacques Two Thousand, Jacques Five-and-Twenty Thousand; in the name of all the Angels or the Devils—which you prefer—work!” Thus Defarge of the wine-shop, still at his gun, which had long grown hot.
domestication: 深沟、独木桥、巨石城墙、八大塔楼、大炮、火枪、硝烟弥漫。吊桥放下！“快干，同志们，快干！快干，雅克一、雅克二、雅克一千、雅克两千、雅克两万五千；不管是天使还是魔鬼，随你们高兴，总之快干！”酒馆老板德发日还在他的枪旁，枪管早就热得烫手了。
foreignization: 深沟，单吊桥，巨大的石墙，八大塔楼，大炮，火枪，烟火。一个吊桥放下来了！“工作吧，全体同志，工作吧！工作吧，雅克一，雅克二，雅克一千，雅克两千，雅克两万五千；以所有天使或魔鬼——随你们喜欢——的名义，工作！”酒馆的德发日还在他的枪旁，那枪早已发热。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Deep ditch, single drawbridge, massive stone walls, eight great towers, cannon, muskets, fire and smoke. One drawbridge down! “Work, comrades all, work! Work, Jacques One, Jacques Two, Jacques One Thousand, Jacques Two Thousand, Jacques Five-and-Twenty Thousand; in the name of all the Angels or the Devils—which you prefer—work!” Thus Defarge of the wine-shop, still at his gun, which had long grown hot.

Work, Jacques One, Jacques Two, Jacques One Thousand, Jacques Two Thousand, Jacques Five-and-Twenty Thousand; in the name of all the Angels or the Devils—which you prefer—work!” Thus Defarge of the wine-shop, still at his gun, which had long grown hot.

source: “As to the great service,” said Carton, “I am bound to avow to you, when you speak of it in that way, that it was mere professional claptrap, I don’t know that I cared what became of you, when I rendered it.—Mind! I say when I rendered it; I am speaking of the past.”
domestication: 卡顿说道：“说到你刚才说的那件大事，我得实话跟你说，那时候不过是职业上的套话，我也不知道自己当时是否真正在乎你的死活——注意！我说的是当时，我说的是过去的事。”
foreignization: “至于那件大事，”卡顿说，“我必须坦白告诉你，当你这样说起它时，那只是职业上的套话，我不知道我在提供帮助时是否在乎你的下场。——注意！我说的是提供帮助时；我说的是过去。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“As to the great service,” said Carton, “I am bound to avow to you, when you speak of it in that way, that it was mere professional claptrap, I don’t know that I cared what became of you, when I rendered it.—Mind! I say when I rendered it; I am speaking of the past.”
卡顿说道：“说到你刚才说的那件大事，我得实话跟你说，那时候不过是职业上的套话，我也不知道自己当时是否真正在乎你的死活——注意！我说的是当时，我说的是过去的事。”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“As to the great service,” said Carton, “I am bound to avow to you, when you speak of it in that way, that it was mere professional claptrap, I don’t know that I cared what became of you, when I rendered it.—Mind! I say when I rendered it; I am speaking of the past.”
“至于那件大事，”卡顿说，“我必须坦白告诉你，当你这样说起它时，那只是职业上的套话，我不知道我在提供帮助时是否在乎你的下场。——注意！我说的是提供帮助时；我说的是过去。”

source: To the eyes of Mr. Jeremiah Cruncher, sitting on his stool in Fleet-street with his grisly urchin beside him, a vast number and variety of objects in movement were every day presented. Who could sit upon anything in Fleet-street during the busy hours of the day, and not be dazed and deafened by two immense processions, one ever tending westward with the sun, the other ever tending eastward from the sun, both ever tending to the plains beyond the range of red and purple where the sun goes down!
domestication: 对坐在弗利特街上凳子上的杰里米亚·克伦彻先生来说，每天眼前都上演着各种各样的流动景象，旁边还蹲着他那副凶神恶煞模样的小崽子。谁要是能在弗利特街的繁忙时段，坐在任何一个地方，不被这两股浩浩荡荡的人流搞得眼花缭乱、耳鸣目眩呢？一边的人群随着太阳的方向往西涌动，另一边的人群则从太阳升起的地方向东走来，两股人流最终都汇入了那片红紫交织的天际尽头，太阳在那里缓缓落下！
foreignization: 在杰里迈亚·克伦彻先生的眼里，他坐在弗利特街的凳子上，身边是他那可怕的顽童，每天都会呈现出大量各种各样的移动物体。谁能坐在弗利特街上的任何东西上，在一天中的繁忙时刻，不被两个庞大的队伍弄得眼花缭乱和耳聋目眩，其中一个队伍总是随着太阳向西行进，另一个队伍总是从太阳的方向向东行进，两者都总是向着红紫色的山脉之外的平原行进，那里是太阳落下的地方！

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

To the eyes of Mr. Jeremiah Cruncher, sitting on his stool in Fleet-street with his grisly urchin beside him, a vast number and variety of objects in movement were every day presented. Who could sit upon anything in Fleet-street during the busy hours of the day, and not be dazed and deafened by two immense processions, one ever tending westward with the sun, the other ever tending eastward from the sun, both ever tending to the plains beyond the range of red and purple where the sun goes down!

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

To the eyes of Mr. Jeremiah Cruncher, sitting on his stool in Fleet-street with his grisly urchin beside him, a vast number and variety of objects in movement were every day presented. Who could sit upon anything in Fleet-street during the busy hours of the day, and not be dazed and deafened by two immense processions, one ever tending westward with the sun, the other ever tending eastward from the sun, both ever tending to the plains beyond the range of red and purple where the sun goes down!
在杰里迈亚·克伦彻先生的眼里，他坐在弗利特街的凳子上，身边是他那可怕的顽童，每天都会呈现出大量各种各样的移动物体。谁能坐在弗利特街上的任何东西上，在一天中的繁忙时刻，不被两个庞大的队伍弄得眼花缭乱和耳聋目眩，其中一个队伍总是随着太阳向西行进，另一个队伍总是从太阳的方向向东行进，两者都总是向着红紫色的山脉之外的平原行进，那里是太阳落下的地方！

source: “There’s all manner of things wanted,” said Miss Pross, “and we shall have a precious time of it. We want wine, among the rest. Nice toasts these Redheads will be drinking, wherever we buy it.”
domestication: “缺的东西多了去了，”普罗斯小姐说，“咱们可有的忙了。酒也得买，这帮红头党到哪儿喝酒，toast的肯定不是啥好话。”
foreignization: “有各种各样的东西要准备，”普罗斯小姐说，“我们将会有一段宝贵的时间。我们还需要酒。不管在哪里买，这些红头发的家伙喝的祝酒词一定会很有趣。”

source: “Worth no more than that,” repeated Mr. Cruncher. “They ain’t worth much, then. Whether or no, I won’t be prayed agin, I tell you. I can’t afford it. I’m not a going to be made unlucky by your sneaking. If you must go flopping yourself down, flop in favour of your husband and child, and not in opposition to ’em. If I had had any but a unnat’ral wife, and this poor boy had had any but a unnat’ral mother, I might have made some money last week instead of being counter-prayed and countermined and religiously circumwented into the worst of luck. B-u-u-ust me!” said Mr. Cruncher, who all this time had been putting on his clothes, “if I ain’t, what with piety and one blowed thing and another, been choused this last week into as bad luck as ever a poor devil of a honest tradesman met with! Young Jerry, dress yourself, my boy, and while I clean my boots keep a eye upon your mother now and then, and if you see any signs of more flopping, give me a call. For, I tell you,” here he addressed his wife once more, “I won’t be gone agin, in this manner. I am as rickety as a hackney-coach, I’m as sleepy as laudanum, my lines is strained to that degree that I shouldn’t know, if it wasn’t for the pain in ’em, which was me and which somebody else, yet I’m none the better for it in pocket; and it’s my suspicion that you’ve been at it from morning to night to prevent me from being the better for it in pocket, and I won’t put up with it, Aggerawayter, and what do you say now!”
domestication: “就值这么多，”克伦彻先生重复道。“那可真不值钱。不管怎样，我不会再被祷告了，我告诉你。我可承担不起。我不能因为你的偷偷摸摸而变得不走运。如果非得趴下祷告，也该为你丈夫和孩子祈祷，而不是反对他们。要是我没有娶一个不正常的妻子，这个可怜的孩子也没有一个不正常的母亲，我上周或许能赚点钱，而不是被反祷告、反挖坑，宗教上被绕得团团转，搞得最倒霉。天哪！”克伦彻先生一边说着，一边穿衣服，“这一周，因为什么虔诚啊、破事啊的，我被折腾得跟个老实本分的商贩碰上的最坏运气一样糟糕！小杰瑞，快去穿衣服，孩子，等我擦靴子的时候，你偶尔盯着你妈，要是再有要趴下的迹象，就叫一声。我跟你说，”这时他又对妻子说，“我不能再这样被折腾了。我现在就像一辆破出租马车，困得跟吃了鸦片似的，身上的每根骨头都快绷断了，要不是疼得厉害，我都不知道自己是自己还是别人，但这对我口袋里一点好处都没有；我怀疑你从早到晚都在搞这些名堂，就是为了不让我的口袋鼓起来，我可不会容忍，阿格吉韦特，你说呢！”
foreignization: “值不了那么多，”克伦彻先生重复道。“那它们就值不了多少了。不管怎样，我不会再被人祷告反对了，我告诉你。我负担不起。我不允许你通过偷偷摸摸的行为让我变得不幸运。如果你非得倒下，那就倒向你的丈夫和孩子这边，而不是反对他们。如果我有一个正常的妻子，这个可怜的孩子有一个正常的母亲，我上周可能就会赚到一些钱，而不是被反祷告、反算计和宗教地绕过，落得最坏的运气。天哪！”克伦彻先生一边说，一边穿衣服，“如果我不是因为虔诚和该死的其他事情而被骗，在过去一周里遭遇了任何一个诚实的手艺人所能遇到的最糟糕的运气！杰瑞小子，穿好衣服，孩子，当我擦靴子的时候，偶尔留意一下你妈妈，如果你看到她有倒下的迹象，就叫我一声。因为，我告诉你，”这时他又转向了他的妻子，“我不会以这种方式再次离开。我像出租马车一样摇摇晃晃，我像鸦片一样昏昏欲睡，我的神经紧张到我如果不是感到疼痛，都不知道自己是谁，是不是别人，但我口袋里没有因此多一分钱；而且我怀疑你从早到晚都在这么做，就是为了防止我口袋里多一分钱，我不会容忍这一点，阿格威特，你说呢！”

source: “Come on at a footpace! d’ye mind me? And if you’ve got holsters to that saddle o’ yourn, don’t let me see your hand go nigh ’em. For I’m a devil at a quick mistake, and when I make one it takes the form of Lead. So now let’s look at you.”
domestication: 快点儿，给我慢慢走过来！你听见没有？还有，如果你的鞍子上有枪套，别让我看到你的手靠近它们。我这个人一紧张就容易犯错，而且一错就是开枪。所以，现在咱们好好看看你。
foreignization: 慢点过来！你听清楚了吗？如果你的马鞍上有枪套，别让我看见你的手靠近它们。因为我对快速的错误可是个魔鬼，而我一旦犯错，那可就是铅弹相待。所以现在让我们看看你。

source: The plaintive tone of her compassion merged into the less musical voice of the Judge, as he said something fiercely: “Answer the questions put to you, and make no remark upon them.”
domestication: 她的哀怜声调和法官不那么悦耳的声音交织在一起，法官怒气冲冲地说：“回答问题，别多嘴。”
foreignization: 她哀怜的语气与法官较为不悦耳的声音融合在一起，当他说了一些激烈的话：“回答所提出的问题，不要对它们发表任何评论。”

source: “Lucie, I recall these old troubles in the reason that I have to-night for loving you better than words can tell, and thanking God for my great happiness. My thoughts, when they were wildest, never rose near the happiness that I have known with you, and that we have before us.”
domestication: 露西，我想起过去的那些烦恼，是因为今晚我对你爱得更深了，这份爱已无法用言语表达，我感谢上帝赐予我如此的幸福。即使在我最狂野的梦想中，也从未想过会有你带给我的这种幸福，更没想到我们未来还有更多的幸福等着我们。
foreignization: “露西，我回忆起这些旧日的烦恼，是因为今晚我对你的好感比言语所能表达的更深，感谢上帝赐予我的巨大幸福。即使在我最狂野的想象中，也从未接近过与你共度的幸福，以及我们面前的幸福。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Lucie, I recall these old troubles in the reason that I have to-night for loving you better than words can tell, and thanking God for my great happiness. My thoughts, when they were wildest, never rose near the happiness that I have known with you, and that we have before us.”
露西，我想起过去的那些烦恼，是因为今晚我对你爱得更深了，这份爱已无法用言语表达，我感谢上帝赐予我如此的幸福。即使在我最狂野的梦想中，也从未想过会有你带给我的这种幸福，更没想到我们未来还有更多的幸福等着我们。

“露西，我回忆起这些旧日的烦恼，是因为今晚我对你的好感比言语所能表达的更深，感谢上帝赐予我的巨大幸福。即使在我最狂野的想象中，也从未接近过与你共度的幸福，以及我们面前的幸福。”

source: They hanged at Tyburn, in those days, so the street outside Newgate had not obtained one infamous notoriety that has since attached to it. But, the gaol was a vile place, in which most kinds of debauchery and villainy were practised, and where dire diseases were bred, that came into court with the prisoners, and sometimes rushed straight from the dock at my Lord Chief Justice himself, and pulled him off the bench. It had more than once happened, that the Judge in the black cap pronounced his own doom as certainly as the prisoner’s, and even died before him. For the rest, the Old Bailey was famous as a kind of deadly inn-yard, from which pale travellers set out continually, in carts and coaches, on a violent passage into the other world: traversing some two miles and a half of public street and road, and shaming few good citizens, if any. So powerful is use, and so desirable to be good use in the beginning. It was famous, too, for the pillory, a wise old institution, that inflicted a punishment of which no one could foresee the extent; also, for the whipping-post, another dear old institution, very humanising and softening to behold in action; also, for extensive transactions in blood-money, another fragment of ancestral wisdom, systematically leading to the most frightful mercenary crimes that could be committed under Heaven. Altogether, the Old Bailey, at that date, was a choice illustration of the precept, that “Whatever is is right;” an aphorism that would be as final as it is lazy, did it not include the troublesome consequence, that nothing that ever was, was wrong.
domestication: 那时候，处决犯人都在泰伯恩进行，所以新门监狱外的街道还没有像后来那样声名狼藉。不过，监狱确实是个污秽之地，里面什么荒淫无度、罪恶滔天的事都有，还滋生了可怕的疾病。这些病会随着囚犯一起进到法庭，有时甚至会直接冲向大法官大人，把他从椅子上拽下来。法官戴着黑帽子宣判时，有时自己也难逃一劫，甚至比囚犯先死。除此之外，老贝利法庭就像是个死亡旅店的院子，经常有脸色苍白的旅人坐马车或轿子出发，踏上一条通往另一个世界的不归路，途径两英里半的公共街道和道路，几乎没几个正派人会为此感到羞耻。习惯的力量就是这么强大，一开始就养成好习惯是多么重要啊。老贝利还以枷刑柱闻名，这个古老的明智制度让谁也猜不到惩罚的程度；还有鞭刑柱，这又是一个让人看了觉得人性化的可爱老传统；此外，这里还经常发生买卖血钱的交易，这是祖辈智慧的一个片段，系统性地导致了世上最可怕的见钱眼开的罪行。总之，那时的老贝利法庭，充分体现了“存在即合理”这条格言。不过，这条格言若不是包含了令人头疼的后果——即过往发生的一切都不算错——那它就会是既最终又懒惰的结论了。
foreignization: 在那些日子里，他们是在泰伯恩被绞死的，所以纽盖特外面的街道还没有获得后来附着于它的那种臭名昭著的名声。但是，监狱是一个邪恶的地方，在那里进行着各种堕落和罪恶的行为，而且那里滋生了可怕的疾病，这些疾病随着囚犯一起进入法庭，有时会直接从被告席冲向首席法官大人，把他从法官席上拽下来。曾经多次发生过，戴黑帽的法官在宣判囚犯死刑的同时也宣判了自己的死刑，甚至比囚犯先死去。除此之外，老贝利以其致命的客栈院落而闻名，苍白的旅客们不断地从这里出发，乘着马车和轿子，踏上一条暴力通往另一个世界的旅程：穿越大约两英里的公共街道和道路，几乎不使任何好市民感到羞愧，如果有任何好市民的话。习惯的力量是如此强大，一开始就养成良好的习惯是多么可取。它还因杖刑台而著名，这是一种古老的明智制度，施加了一种没有人能预见其程度的惩罚；还因鞭刑柱而闻名，这是另一种亲爱的老制度，看到它在行动时非常人性化和温和；还因广泛的血钱交易而著称，这是祖先智慧的一个片段，系统地导致了在天底下所能犯下的最可怕的雇佣罪行。总而言之，老贝利在那个时期是一个完美的例证，证明了“凡是存在的就是正确的”这一格言；如果这个格言不包括令人烦恼的后果，即“凡曾经存在的都是错的”，那么它将会像它懒惰一样最终定论。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

They hanged at Tyburn, in those days, so the street outside Newgate had not obtained one infamous notoriety that has since attached to it. But, the gaol was a vile place, in which most kinds of debauchery and villainy were practised, and where dire diseases were bred, that came into court with the prisoners, and sometimes rushed straight from the dock at my Lord Chief Justice himself, and pulled him off the bench. It had more than once happened, that the Judge in the black cap pronounced his own doom as certainly as the prisoner’s, and even died before him. For the rest, the Old Bailey was famous as a kind of deadly inn-yard, from which pale travellers set out continually, in carts and coaches, on a violent passage into the other world: traversing some two miles and a half of public street and road, and shaming few good citizens, if any. So powerful is use, and so desirable to be good use in the beginning. It was famous, too, for the pillory, a wise old institution, that inflicted a punishment of which no one could foresee the extent; also, for the whipping-post, another dear old institution, very humanising and softening to behold in action; also, for extensive transactions in blood-money, another fragment of ancestral wisdom, systematically leading to the most frightful mercenary crimes that could be committed under Heaven. Altogether, the Old Bailey, at that date, was a choice illustration of the precept, that “Whatever is is right;” an aphorism that would be as final as it is lazy, did it not include the troublesome consequence, that nothing that ever was, was wrong.

source: The passage to the Conciergerie was short and dark; the night in its vermin-haunted cells was long and cold. Next day, fifteen prisoners were put to the bar before Charles Darnay’s name was called. All the fifteen were condemned, and the trials of the whole occupied an hour and a half.
domestication: 通往关押室的路又短又黑；在那虫鼠横行的牢房里，夜显得格外漫长和寒冷。第二天，查尔斯·达内前面有十五个囚犯受审。这十五人都被判了刑，整个审判过程只用了个把小时。
foreignization: 通往拘留所的通道又短又暗；在那虫害肆虐的牢房里，夜晚漫长而寒冷。第二天，查尔斯·达奈的名字被叫到之前，有十五名囚犯被带上法庭。这十五人都被判刑，整个审判过程只用了半个小时。

source: “I choose them as real men, of my name—Jacques is my name—to whom the sight is likely to do good. Enough; you are English; that is another thing. Stay there, if you please, a little moment.”
domestication: 我挑的都是真汉子，跟我同名——我叫雅克——看了这场景对他们有好处。行了；你是英国人，那又另当别论了。你要是愿意，就在那儿待一小会儿吧。
foreignization: 我选择他们作为真正的人，和我同名——雅克是我的名字——对他们来说，这景象可能会有好处。够了；你是英国人；那是另一回事。如果你愿意，请在那里待一会儿。

source: As a whirlpool of boiling waters has a centre point, so, all this raging circled round Defarge’s wine-shop, and every human drop in the caldron had a tendency to be sucked towards the vortex where Defarge himself, already begrimed with gunpowder and sweat, issued orders, issued arms, thrust this man back, dragged this man forward, disarmed one to arm another, laboured and strove in the thickest of the uproar.
domestication: 就像沸腾的漩涡有个中心点一样，所有这股怒潮都围绕着德法热的酒馆打转，锅里的每一滴人都有被吸向漩涡中心的趋势。德法热自己早已被火药和汗水弄得污黑不堪，他在那里发号施令，分发武器，把这个人推回去，把那个人拉过来，夺下一个人的武器再武装另一个人，在这场混乱的核心中拼命地忙碌着。
foreignization: 正如旋涡中心有一个点一样，所有这些狂怒的漩涡都围绕着德发日的酒馆旋转，锅里的每一滴人都有被吸入旋涡的趋势，而德发日本人自己已经沾满了火药和汗水，在最喧闹的地方发布命令，分发武器，把这个人推回去，把那个人拉上前，解除一个人的武装以武装另一个人，在最激烈的混乱中努力挣扎。

source: After a long silence, the head was lifted for another moment, and the voice replied, “Yes—I am working.” This time, a pair of haggard eyes had looked at the questioner, before the face had dropped again.

source: “No; but before I go on,” said Stryver, shaking his head in his bullying way, “I’ll have this out with you. You’ve been at Doctor Manette’s house as much as I have, or more than I have. Why, I have been ashamed of your moroseness there! Your manners have been of that silent and sullen and hangdog kind, that, upon my life and soul, I have been ashamed of you, Sydney!”
domestication: 不行；不过在我说下去之前，”斯特莱弗摆出他那惯有的霸道样子，摇着头说道，“我得跟你把这事儿说清楚。你去曼内特医生家的次数不比我少，甚至比我还多。你的样子让我都觉得丢脸！你在那里总是闷闷不乐，一副沉默寡言、无精打采的模样，我真替你感到害臊，西德尼！
foreignization: “不；但是在我继续之前，”斯特莱弗以他那欺压的方式摇着头说，“我得和你把这件事说清楚。你去曼内特医生家的次数和我一样多，或者比我还多。为什么，我对你的阴郁感到羞愧！你的举止是那么沉默、闷闷不乐和卑微，我发誓，我对你的行为感到羞愧，西德尼！”

source: This time, Mr. Lorry feigned to go out when he could extract no answer from him, and, after remaining absent for an hour, returned. In the meanwhile, the Doctor had removed to the seat in the window, and had sat there looking down at the plane-tree; but, on Mr. Lorry’s return, he slipped away to his bench.
domestication: 这回，洛里先生见他不肯开口，就假装出去了，过了一小时才回来。趁这工夫，医生挪到了窗边的座位上，一直盯着那棵梧桐树看；但等洛里先生一回来，他又溜回了自己的长凳。

source: “You see,” said Doctor Manette, turning to him after an uneasy pause, “it is very hard to explain, consistently, the innermost workings of this poor man’s mind. He once yearned so frightfully for that occupation, and it was so welcome when it came; no doubt it relieved his pain so much, by substituting the perplexity of the fingers for the perplexity of the brain, and by substituting, as he became more practised, the ingenuity of the hands, for the ingenuity of the mental torture; that he has never been able to bear the thought of putting it quite out of his reach. Even now, when I believe he is more hopeful of himself than he has ever been, and even speaks of himself with a kind of confidence, the idea that he might need that old employment, and not find it, gives him a sudden sense of terror, like that which one may fancy strikes to the heart of a lost child.”
domestication: 你看，德发日医生在一阵不安的沉默后转过身对他说，“要是一五一十地解释这个可怜人的内心活动，实在是难上加难。他以前多么渴望那份手艺活，等它真的来了，对他来说简直就是天赐良药。毫无疑问，手指的困惑替代了脑袋的困惑，减轻了他的痛苦；随着技术的提高，手上的灵巧代替了精神上的折磨，他再也无法忍受完全失去这份手艺的想法。即使现在，我觉得他对自己的未来比以往任何时候都要乐观，甚至开始自信地谈起自己，但一想到可能需要那旧时的手艺却找不到时，他就会突然感到一阵恐惧，就像迷路的孩子突然意识到自己孤零零一人时那种害怕的心情。”
foreignization: “你明白，”马内特医生在一阵不安的沉默后转向他说道，“要一致地解释这个可怜人的内心深处的想法是非常困难的。他曾经如此渴望那份工作，当它到来时又是如此受欢迎；毫无疑问，通过用手指的困惑替代大脑的困惑，以及随着他变得更加熟练，用手的灵巧替代心理折磨的灵巧，这极大地缓解了他的痛苦，以至于他从未能够忍受完全把它置于自己无法触及的境地。即使现在，我相信他对自己的情况比以往任何时候都更加乐观，甚至以一种自信的态度谈论自己，但想到他可能需要那份旧的工作却找不到它，就会给他带来一种突然的恐惧感，就像一个迷路的孩子可能会感到的那种恐惧。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“You see,” said Doctor Manette, turning to him after an uneasy pause, “it is very hard to explain, consistently, the innermost workings of this poor man’s mind. He once yearned so frightfully for that occupation, and it was so welcome when it came; no doubt it relieved his pain so much, by substituting the perplexity of the fingers for the perplexity of the brain, and by substituting, as he became more practised, the ingenuity of the hands, for the ingenuity of the mental torture; that he has never been able to bear the thought of putting it quite out of his reach. Even now, when I believe he is more hopeful of himself than he has ever been, and even speaks of himself with a kind of confidence, the idea that he might need that old employment, and not find it, gives him a sudden sense of terror, like that which one may fancy strikes to the heart of a lost child.”

你看，德发日医生在一阵不安的沉默后转过身对他说，“要是一五一十地解释这个可怜人的内心活动，实在是难上加难。他以前多么渴望那份手艺活，等它真的来了，对他来说简直就是天赐良药。毫无疑问，手指的困惑替代了脑袋的困惑，减轻了他的痛苦；随着技术的提高，手上的灵巧代替了精神上的折磨，他再也无法忍受完全失去这份手艺的想法。即使现在，我觉得他对自己的未来比以往任何时候都要乐观，甚至开始自信地谈起自己，但一想到可能需要那旧时的手艺却找不到时，他就会突然感到一阵恐惧，就像迷路的孩子突然意识到自己孤零零一人时那种害怕的心情。”

“You see,” said Doctor Manette, turning to him after an uneasy pause, “it is very hard to explain, consistently, the innermost workings of this poor man’s mind. He once yearned so frightfully for that occupation, and it was so welcome when it came; no doubt it relieved his pain so much, by substituting the perplexity of the fingers for the perplexity of the brain, and by substituting, as he became more practised, the ingenuity of the hands, for the ingenuity of the mental torture; that he has never been able to bear the thought of putting it quite out of his reach. Even now, when I believe he is more hopeful of himself than he has ever been, and even speaks of himself with a kind of confidence, the idea that he might need that old employment, and not find it, gives him a sudden sense of terror, like that which one may fancy strikes to the heart of a lost child.”
