source: “When the prisoner came on board, he noticed that my father,” turning her eyes lovingly to him as he stood beside her, “was much fatigued and in a very weak state of health. My father was so reduced that I was afraid to take him out of the air, and I had made a bed for him on the deck near the cabin steps, and I sat on the deck at his side to take care of him. There were no other passengers that night, but we four. The prisoner was so good as to beg permission to advise me how I could shelter my father from the wind and weather, better than I had done. I had not known how to do it well, not understanding how the wind would set when we were out of the harbour. He did it for me. He expressed great gentleness and kindness for my father’s state, and I am sure he felt it. That was the manner of our beginning to speak together.”
domestication: 当囚犯上船时，他看到我父亲，她 affectionately 看了一眼站在她身旁的父亲，“疲惫不堪，身体虚弱。父亲瘦得厉害，我都不敢让他离开空气流通的地方。我在船舱门附近的甲板上给他铺了个床，自己就坐在他旁边照顾他。那天晚上船上除了我们四个，没有其他乘客。囚犯好心地请求允许他给我提些建议，教我怎么更好地让父亲避开风和天气的影响。我自己也没法做得很好，因为我不知道出了港口后风向会怎么变。他帮我解决了这个问题。他对父亲表现出极大的温柔和善意，我相信他是真心的。就这样，我们开始交谈起来。”
foreignization: “当囚犯上船时，他注意到我父亲，” 她满怀爱意地看向站在她身旁的父亲，“非常疲惫，身体状况非常虚弱。父亲的身体如此虚弱，以至于我害怕让他离开空气流通的地方，我在船舱台阶附近为他铺了一张床，坐在甲板上陪伴在他身边照顾他。那晚除了我们四人外没有其他乘客。囚犯好心请求允许他给我建议，如何更好地将我父亲遮挡风雨，比我做得更好。我不知道该如何做好这件事，不明白当我们离开港口后风向会怎样变化。他为我做了这件事。他对父亲的状况表现出极大的温和与善意，我相信他是真心的。这就是我们开始交谈的方式。”

There were no other passengers that night, but we four. The prisoner was so good as to beg permission to advise me how I could shelter my father from the wind and weather, better than I had done. I had not known how to do it well, not understanding how the wind would set when we were out of the harbour. He did it for me. He expressed great gentleness and kindness for my father’s state, and I am sure he felt it. That was the manner of our beginning to speak together.”

My father was so reduced that I was afraid to take him out of the air, and I had made a bed for him on the deck near the cabin steps, and I sat on the deck at his side to take care of him. There were no other passengers that night, but we four. The prisoner was so good as to beg permission to advise me how I could shelter my father from the wind and weather, better than I had done. I had not known how to do it well, not understanding how the wind would set when we were out of the harbour. He did it for me. He expressed great gentleness and kindness for my father’s state, and I am sure he felt it. That was the manner of our beginning to speak together.”

“当囚犯上船时，他注意到我父亲，” 她满怀爱意地看向站在她身旁的父亲，“非常疲惫，身体状况非常虚弱。父亲的身体如此虚弱，以至于我害怕让他离开空气流通的地方，我在船舱台阶附近为他铺了一张床，坐在甲板上陪伴在他身边照顾他。那晚除了我们四人外没有其他乘客。囚犯好心请求允许他给我建议，如何更好地将我父亲遮挡风雨，比我做得更好。我不知道该如何做好这件事，不明白当我们离开港口后风向会怎样变化。他为我做了这件事。他对父亲的状况表现出极大的温和与善意，我相信他是真心的。这就是我们开始交谈的方式。”

source: Defarge got upon the box, and gave the word “To the Barrier!” The postilion cracked his whip, and they clattered away under the feeble over-swinging lamps.

foreignization: 德发日上了车夫的座位，喊了声“去关卡！”驭者挥鞭，他们就在昏暗的悬灯下辚辚而去。

source: By that time, there was only one adventurous traveller left be congratulated: for the two others had been set down at their respective roadside destinations. The mildewy inside of the coach, with its damp and dirty straw, its disagreeable smell, and its obscurity, was rather like a larger dog-kennel. Mr. Lorry, the passenger, shaking himself out of it in chains of straw, a tangle of shaggy wrapper, flapping hat, and muddy legs, was rather like a larger sort of dog.
domestication: 那时，只剩下一位敢于冒险的旅客值得祝贺了：另外两位早就已经在各自的路旁目的地下车了。马车里头又霉又潮，稻草脏兮兮的，气味难闻，昏暗得就像个大狗窝。洛里先生，这位乘客，从里面挣扎着出来，身上缠着稻草链子，披着乱糟糟的大衣，帽子还晃晃悠悠地挂在头上，两腿都是泥，活像一只大号的狗。
foreignization: 到那时，只剩下一位冒险的旅行者值得祝贺了：因为另外两位已经被送到了他们各自的路边目的地。马车里面霉味弥漫，潮湿肮脏的稻草、令人不悦的气味和昏暗的光线，使它有点像一个较大的狗窝。乘客洛里先生从里面抖擞着出来，身上挂着稻草链子，披头散发的斗篷、拍打着的帽子和泥泞的双腿纠缠在一起，看起来有点像一种较大的狗。

Mr. Lorry, the passenger, shaking himself out of it in chains of straw, a tangle of shaggy wrapper, flapping hat, and muddy legs, was rather like a larger sort of dog.

Mr. Lorry, the passenger, shaking himself out of it in chains of straw, a tangle of shaggy wrapper, flapping hat, and muddy legs, was rather like a larger sort of dog.
到那时，只剩下一位冒险的旅行者值得祝贺了：因为另外两位已经被送到了他们各自的路边目的地。马车里面霉味弥漫，潮湿肮脏的稻草、令人不悦的气味和昏暗的光线，使它有点像一个较大的狗窝。乘客洛里先生从里面抖擞着出来，身上挂着稻草链子，披头散发的斗篷、拍打着的帽子和泥泞的双腿纠缠在一起，看起来有点像一种较大的狗。

source: “You were, just now; I saw you do it, and I don’t wonder at it. Such a present of plate as you have made ’em, is enough to bring tears into anybody’s eyes. There’s not a fork or a spoon in the collection,” said Miss Pross, “that I didn’t cry over, last night after the box came, till I couldn’t see it.”
domestication: 你刚才还做了呢，我亲眼看见了，一点也不奇怪。像你这么一大套银器，谁见了不掉眼泪啊。昨晚箱子一到，我就一把鼻涕一把泪地哭个没完，连叉子带勺子都没看清。
foreignization: 你刚才还那样做了；我看见你那么做了，我并不感到奇怪。像你送给他们的那些银器，足以让任何人掉眼泪。收藏里的每一件叉子或勺子，”普罗斯小姐说，“昨天箱子来了之后，我都哭得看不见了。”

source: As he walked to and fro with his resolution made, he considered that neither Lucie nor her father must know of it until he was gone. Lucie should be spared the pain of separation; and her father, always reluctant to turn his thoughts towards the dangerous ground of old, should come to the knowledge of the step, as a step taken, and not in the balance of suspense and doubt. How much of the incompleteness of his situation was referable to her father, through the painful anxiety to avoid reviving old associations of France in his mind, he did not discuss with himself. But, that circumstance too, had had its influence in his course.
domestication: 他来来回回地走着，心里已经打定了主意，决定不告诉露西和她父亲，直到他离开的那一刻。露西不必承受离别的痛苦；而她父亲，一直不愿意回想过去那些危险的事，也该在事情既成事实之后再告诉他，而不是在他犹豫不决的时候。他没有细想，自己处境的不完美有多少是由于她父亲的缘故——为了不让她父亲再想起法国那些往事，总是小心翼翼的。但，这确实也是促使他做出这个决定的一个因素。
foreignization: 当他下定决心来回走动时，他考虑到露西和她父亲在他离开之前都不能知道这件事。露西应该免受分离的痛苦；而她父亲，总是不愿意把思绪转向过去那段危险的日子，应该在事情已经决定后才得知这一步，而不是在犹豫和怀疑中权衡。他并没有与自己讨论，他的处境不完整在多大程度上是由于她父亲为了避免在他的脑海中重新唤起法国旧日的联想而感到痛苦的焦虑所致。但，这一情况也对他所采取的行动产生了影响。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

As he walked to and fro with his resolution made, he considered that neither Lucie nor her father must know of it until he was gone. Lucie should be spared the pain of separation; and her father, always reluctant to turn his thoughts towards the dangerous ground of old, should come to the knowledge of the step, as a step taken, and not in the balance of suspense and doubt. How much of the incompleteness of his situation was referable to her father, through the painful anxiety to avoid reviving old associations of France in his mind, he did not discuss with himself. But, that circumstance too, had had its influence in his course.
他来来回回地走着，心里已经打定了主意，决定不告诉露西和她父亲，直到他离开的那一刻。露西不必承受离别的痛苦；而她父亲，一直不愿意回想过去那些危险的事，也该在事情既成事实之后再告诉他，而不是在他犹豫不决的时候。他没有细想，自己处境的不完美有多少是由于她父亲的缘故——为了不让她父亲再想起法国那些往事，总是小心翼翼的。但，这确实也是促使他做出这个决定的一个因素。

source: “Her husband’s destiny,” said Madame Defarge, with her usual composure, “will take him where he is to go, and will lead him to the end that is to end him. That is all I know.”
domestication: 德法日太太平静如常地说：“她丈夫的命运，该去哪儿自然会去哪儿，该有怎样的结局也躲不掉。我只知道这些。”
foreignization: “她丈夫的命运，”德法日太太以她惯有的镇定说道，“将带他去他该去的地方，并将引导他走向终结他的终点。这就是我所知道的全部。”

source: “Well! I’ll say gallantry. My meaning is that I am a man,” said Stryver, inflating himself at his friend as he made the punch, “who cares more to be agreeable, who takes more pains to be agreeable, who knows better how to be agreeable, in a woman’s society, than you do.”
domestication: 行了！我说的是风度。我的意思是，我是个男人，”斯特莱弗一边调制着潘趣酒，一边对着朋友自我膨胀地说，“在女人的圈子里，我比你更在乎讨人喜欢，也更费心去讨人喜欢，还更知道怎么讨人喜欢。”
foreignization: “好！我就说风度吧。我的意思是说，我是一个男人，”斯特莱弗一边调制 Punch 一边向他的朋友自夸道，“在女性的社交圈中，比你更在乎取悦他人，更费心去取悦他人，也更懂得如何取悦他人。”

source: It was the first time, except at the trial, of her ever hearing him refer to the period of his suffering. It gave her a strange and new sensation while his words were in her ears; and she remembered it long afterwards.

source: The chateau burned; the nearest trees, laid hold of by the fire, scorched and shrivelled; trees at a distance, fired by the four fierce figures, begirt the blazing edifice with a new forest of smoke. Molten lead and iron boiled in the marble basin of the fountain; the water ran dry; the extinguisher tops of the towers vanished like ice before the heat, and trickled down into four rugged wells of flame. Great rents and splits branched out in the solid walls, like crystallisation; stupefied birds wheeled about and dropped into the furnace; four fierce figures trudged away, East, West, North, and South, along the night-enshrouded roads, guided by the beacon they had lighted, towards their next destination. The illuminated village had seized hold of the tocsin, and, abolishing the lawful ringer, rang for joy.
domestication: 城堡烧起来了，附近的树木被火焰吞噬，焦黑枯萎；远处的树木也被四尊凶猛的雕像点燃，围绕着燃烧的建筑形成了新的烟雾森林。铅和铁在大理石的喷泉池中熔化沸腾，水干涸了，塔顶的灭火装置像冰一样在高温下消失，滴落成四个粗糙的火焰井。坚固的墙壁上裂开了大裂缝，就像晶体一样延伸；昏昏欲睡的鸟儿在空中盘旋，坠入火海；四尊凶猛的雕像踏着夜色，分别向东、西、南、北走去，他们点燃的火光指引着他们前往下一个目的地。被照亮的村庄抓住了警钟，废除了合法的敲钟人，欢快地敲响了钟声。
foreignization: 城堡燃烧起来；最近的树木被火势蔓延，烧焦并枯萎；远处的树木被四个凶猛的人物点燃，围绕着燃烧的建筑形成了新的烟雾森林。熔化的铅和铁在喷泉的大理石盆中沸腾；水干涸了；塔顶的灭火装置像冰一样在高温下消失，并滴入四个崎岖的火焰井中。坚固的墙壁上出现了巨大的裂缝和裂纹，如同结晶一般；昏迷的鸟儿在周围盘旋并掉入火炉；四个凶猛的人物沿着夜色笼罩的道路向东、西、南、北方向走去，由他们点燃的灯塔引导，前往下一个目的地。被照亮的村庄抓住了警钟，废除了合法的敲钟人，欢乐地敲响了钟声。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The chateau burned; the nearest trees, laid hold of by the fire, scorched and shrivelled; trees at a distance, fired by the four fierce figures, begirt the blazing edifice with a new forest of smoke. Molten lead and iron boiled in the marble basin of the fountain; the water ran dry; the extinguisher tops of the towers vanished like ice before the heat, and trickled down into four rugged wells of flame. Great rents and splits branched out in the solid walls, like crystallisation; stupefied birds wheeled about and dropped into the furnace; four fierce figures trudged away, East, West, North, and South, along the night-enshrouded roads, guided by the beacon they had lighted, towards their next destination. The illuminated village had seized hold of the tocsin, and, abolishing the lawful ringer, rang for joy.

source: “I think,” he whispered to Miss Pross, after anxious consideration, “I think we had best not speak to him just now, or at all disturb him. I must look in at Tellson’s; so I will go there at once and come back presently. Then, we will take him a ride into the country, and dine there, and all will be well.”
domestication: 他思量再三，轻声对普罗斯小姐说：“我觉得咱们最好别现在跟他说话，也别打搅他。我得去泰勒森银行看看，马上过去，一会儿就回来。然后，咱们带他到乡下兜兜风，去那边吃个饭，一切都会好的。”
foreignization: “我想，”他低声对普罗斯小姐说，经过焦虑的考虑后，“我们最好现在不要和他说话，或者打扰他。我必须去泰勒森银行看看；所以我马上去那里，一会儿就回来。然后，我们将带他到乡下去兜风，在那里吃晚饭，一切都会好的。”

source: He had not returned sooner, he replied, simply because he had no means of living in France, save those he had resigned; whereas, in England, he lived by giving instruction in the French language and literature. He had returned when he did, on the pressing and written entreaty of a French citizen, who represented that his life was endangered by his absence. He had come back, to save a citizen’s life, and to bear his testimony, at whatever personal hazard, to the truth. Was that criminal in the eyes of the Republic?
domestication: 他之所以没有早点回来，是因为他在法国除了已放弃的生计之外，再也没有其他谋生手段；而在英国，他靠教法语和法国文学为生。这次他回来，是因为一位法国公民再三书面恳求他，说他的不在让这位公民的生命受到威胁。他回来是为了救一个公民的命，并且无论个人安危如何，都要为真相作证。这在共和国的眼里算是犯罪吗？
foreignization: 他回答说，他没有更早回来，仅仅是因为他在法国除了已经辞去的那些外没有任何生活来源；而他在英格兰则靠教授法语和文学为生。他在接到一位法国公民的再三书面恳求时才回来，这位公民表示，他的生命因他的缺席而受到威胁。他回来是为了拯救一个公民的生命，并且无论个人风险如何，都要为真理作证。这在共和国的眼中是犯罪吗？

He had come back, to save a citizen’s life, and to bear his testimony, at whatever personal hazard, to the truth. Was that criminal in the eyes of the Republic?

source: When he was gone, and in the course of an evening passed with Miss Pross, the Doctor, and Mr. Lorry, Charles Darnay made some mention of this conversation in general terms, and spoke of Sydney Carton as a problem of carelessness and recklessness. He spoke of him, in short, not bitterly or meaning to bear hard upon him, but as anybody might who saw him as he showed himself.
domestication: 他一离开，查尔斯·达尔内便在与普罗斯小姐、医生和洛里先生共度的夜晚中，大致提到了这次对话，说悉尼·卡顿是个懒散又鲁莽的问题人物。简言之，他对卡顿的评价并非出于恶意或有意苛责，而是像任何人见到卡顿那副样子时可能说的话一样。
foreignization: 当他离开后，在与普罗斯小姐、医生和洛里先生共度的一个晚上，查尔斯·达内大致提到了这次谈话，并将西德尼·卡顿描述为一个漫不经心和鲁莽的问题。简而言之，他提到他时，并不是出于怨恨或有意苛责，而是像任何人看到他所表现的那样。

He spoke of him, in short, not bitterly or meaning to bear hard upon him, but as anybody might who saw him as he showed himself.

source: “I well understand that, without you, I could have no hope. I well understand that, even if Miss Manette held me at this moment in her innocent heart—do not think I have the presumption to assume so much—I could retain no place in it against her love for her father.”
domestication: 我明白得很，没有你，我连一点希望都没有。我也清楚，即使曼内特小姐此刻心里有我——别误会，我可没那么自以为是——她的爱终究会偏向她的父亲，我在她心中的位置也保不住。
foreignization: 我非常明白，没有你，我就没有任何希望。我非常明白，即使马内特小姐此时此刻把她无辜的心给了我——不要以为我有这么大的妄想——我也无法对抗她对她父亲的爱，而保有我在她心中的位置。

source: “Fashion of speech again! But, Mr. Darnay, oblivion is not so easy to me, as you represent it to be to you. I have by no means forgotten it, and a light answer does not help me to forget it.”
domestication: 又来这套说辞了！不过，达奈先生，想忘掉可没那么容易。我可是一直记得清清楚楚的，你这么轻描淡写地回答，根本帮不了我忘记。
foreignization: “又是这套说辞！但是，达奈先生，对我来说，遗忘并没有你所说的那么容易。我根本就没有忘记，而轻率的回答并不能帮助我忘记。”

source: Mr. Cruncher had no particular meaning in these sulky corroborations, but made use of them, as people not unfrequently do, to express general ironical dissatisfaction.
domestication: 克鲁彻先生在这些闷闷不乐的附和中并没有什么特别的意思，但他就像不少人那样，借此来表达一种普遍的不满和讽刺。
foreignization: 克伦彻先生在这些闷闷不乐的附和中并没有什么特别的意思，但他像人们常常做的那样，利用它们来表达普遍的讽刺不满。

克鲁彻先生在这些闷闷不乐的附和中并没有什么特别的意思，但他就像不少人那样，借此来表达一种普遍的不满和讽刺。

source: Both Mr. Lorry and Defarge were rather disinclined to this course, and in favour of one of them remaining. But, as there were not only carriage and horses to be seen to, but travelling papers; and as time pressed, for the day was drawing to an end, it came at last to their hastily dividing the business that was necessary to be done, and hurrying away to do it.
domestication: 老洛里先生和德发日先生起初都不大愿意这么做，更倾向于让其中一人留下。可是，不仅得安排马车和马匹，还得准备旅行证件；时间又紧迫，天色渐渐暗了下来，他们最终只好匆匆分头行动，各忙各的去了。
foreignization: 洛里先生和德法日先生都相当不愿意采取这一步骤，而倾向于其中一人留下。但由于不仅要有马车和马匹的安排，还要有旅行证件；而且时间紧迫，因为天色已晚，最后他们不得不草草地分配了必须完成的工作，然后匆匆离去去做这些事情。

source: The corner has been mentioned as a wonderful corner for echoes; it had begun to echo so resoundingly to the tread of coming feet, that it seemed as though the very mention of that weary pacing to and fro had set it going.
domestication: 这个角落素来以回声著称，脚步声在此回荡得震耳欲聋，仿佛一提到那令人疲惫的来回踱步，这里就开始呼应起来似的。
foreignization: 这个角落曾被提到是一个回声极佳的角落；它开始对即将到来的脚步声如此响亮地回响，仿佛仅仅是提到那疲倦的来回走动就让它开始了回响。

source: But for the latter inconvenience, the carriage probably would not have stopped; carriages were often known to drive on, and leave their wounded behind, and why not? But the frightened valet had got down in a hurry, and there were twenty hands at the horses’ bridles.
domestication: 要不是后面这个麻烦，马车大概也不会停下的；马车经常一走了之，把伤员扔下，这有什么稀奇的？可吓坏了的男仆急急忙忙地跳了下来，周围立刻有二十只手拽住了马的缰绳。
foreignization: 但若不是后者带来的不便，马车大概不会停下的；马车常常继续前行，把受伤的人丢下，这又有什么关系呢？但受惊的男仆急忙下了车，有二十只手抓住了马的缰绳。

messages_domestication.content:

source: For, in these times, as the mender of roads worked, solitary, in the dust, not often troubling himself to reflect that dust he was and to dust he must return, being for the most part too much occupied in thinking how little he had for supper and how much more he would eat if he had it—in these times, as he raised his eyes from his lonely labour, and viewed the prospect, he would see some rough figure approaching on foot, the like of which was once a rarity in those parts, but was now a frequent presence. As it advanced, the mender of roads would discern without surprise, that it was a shaggy-haired man, of almost barbarian aspect, tall, in wooden shoes that were clumsy even to the eyes of a mender of roads, grim, rough, swart, steeped in the mud and dust of many highways, dank with the marshy moisture of many low grounds, sprinkled with the thorns and leaves and moss of many byways through woods.
domestication: 在这个年代，修路的工人独自在尘土中劳作，很少会去想“人终有一死，尘归尘，土归土”这类问题，因为他大部分时间都在琢磨晚饭能吃上点什么，以及如果有的话，他得多吃点。每当他从孤独的工作中抬起眼，望向远方，就会看到一个粗犷的身影慢慢走近。这种身影曾经在这里极为罕见，如今却成了常客。随着那身影越来越近，修路的工人并不觉得意外，认出那是个头发凌乱、几乎像野蛮人的大汉，穿着木鞋，连修路人见了都觉得笨重。这人满脸阴沉，皮肤粗糙黝黑，身上沾满了多条道路上的泥巴和灰尘，湿漉漉的，似乎刚从沼泽地里走出来，衣服上还挂着树林小道上的刺、叶子和青苔。
foreignization: 因为，在这些日子里，当修路人在尘土中独自工作时，很少会费心去想自己由尘土而来也将归于尘土，多半是因为他忙于思考晚餐有多么少以及如果有的话他会吃多少——在这些日子里，当他从孤独的劳作中抬起眼睛，环顾四周时，他会看到一个步行接近的粗糙身影，这样的人在这一带曾经是稀罕之物，但现在却常常出现。随着那个身影的靠近，修路人不会感到惊讶地发现，那是一个几乎带有野蛮人面貌的长发男子，身材高大，穿着对修路人来说也显得笨重的木鞋，面容严峻、粗犷、黝黑，身上沾满了多条道路上的泥浆和尘土，被许多低洼湿地的湿润气息所浸透，还洒落着穿过森林小径时沾上的荆棘、树叶和苔藓。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

For, in these times, as the mender of roads worked, solitary, in the dust, not often troubling himself to reflect that dust he was and to dust he must return, being for the most part too much occupied in thinking how little he had for supper and how much more he would eat if he had it—in these times, as he raised his eyes from his lonely labour, and viewed the prospect, he would see some rough figure approaching on foot, the like of which was once a rarity in those parts, but was now a frequent presence. As it advanced, the mender of roads would discern without surprise, that it was a shaggy-haired man, of almost barbarian aspect, tall, in wooden shoes that were clumsy even to the eyes of a mender of roads, grim, rough, swart, steeped in the mud and dust of many highways, dank with the marshy moisture of many low grounds, sprinkled with the thorns and leaves and moss of many byways through woods.

在这个年代，修路的工人独自在尘土中劳作，很少会去想“人终有一死，尘归尘，土归土”这类问题，因为他大部分时间都在琢磨晚饭能吃上点什么，以及如果有的话，他得多吃点。每当他从孤独的工作中抬起眼，望向远方，就会看到一个粗犷的身影慢慢走近。这种身影曾经在这里极为罕见，如今却成了常客。随着那身影越来越近，修路的工人并不觉得意外，认出那是个头发凌乱、几乎像野蛮人的大汉，穿着木鞋，连修路人见了都觉得笨重。这人满脸阴沉，皮肤粗糙黝黑，身上沾满了多条道路上的泥巴和灰尘，湿漉漉的，似乎刚从沼泽地里走出来，衣服上还挂着树林小道上的刺、叶子和青苔。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

For, in these times, as the mender of roads worked, solitary, in the dust, not often troubling himself to reflect that dust he was and to dust he must return, being for the most part too much occupied in thinking how little he had for supper and how much more he would eat if he had it—in these times, as he raised his eyes from his lonely labour, and viewed the prospect, he would see some rough figure approaching on foot, the like of which was once a rarity in those parts, but was now a frequent presence. As it advanced, the mender of roads would discern without surprise, that it was a shaggy-haired man, of almost barbarian aspect, tall, in wooden shoes that were clumsy even to the eyes of a mender of roads, grim, rough, swart, steeped in the mud and dust of many highways, dank with the marshy moisture of many low grounds, sprinkled with the thorns and leaves and moss of many byways through woods.

因为，在这些日子里，当修路人在尘土中独自工作时，很少会费心去想自己由尘土而来也将归于尘土，多半是因为他忙于思考晚餐有多么少以及如果有的话他会吃多少——在这些日子里，当他从孤独的劳作中抬起眼睛，环顾四周时，他会看到一个步行接近的粗糙身影，这样的人在这一带曾经是稀罕之物，但现在却常常出现。随着那个身影的靠近，修路人不会感到惊讶地发现，那是一个几乎带有野蛮人面貌的长发男子，身材高大，穿着对修路人来说也显得笨重的木鞋，面容严峻、粗犷、黝黑，身上沾满了多条道路上的泥浆和尘土，被许多低洼湿地的湿润气息所浸透，还洒落着穿过森林小径时沾上的荆棘、树叶和苔藓。

source: Mr. Carton, who had so long sat looking at the ceiling of the court, changed neither his place nor his attitude, even in this excitement. While his learned friend, Mr. Stryver, massing his papers before him, whispered with those who sat near, and from time to time glanced anxiously at the jury; while all the spectators moved more or less, and grouped themselves anew; while even my Lord himself arose from his seat, and slowly paced up and down his platform, not unattended by a suspicion in the minds of the audience that his state was feverish; this one man sat leaning back, with his torn gown half off him, his untidy wig put on just as it had happened to light on his head after its removal, his hands in his pockets, and his eyes on the ceiling as they had been all day. Something especially reckless in his demeanour, not only gave him a disreputable look, but so diminished the strong resemblance he undoubtedly bore to the prisoner (which his momentary earnestness, when they were compared together, had strengthened), that many of the lookers-on, taking note of him now, said to one another they would hardly have thought the two were so alike. Mr. Cruncher made the observation to his next neighbour, and added, “I’d hold half a guinea that he don’t get no law-work to do. Don’t look like the sort of one to get any, do he?”
domestication: 卡顿先生长久以来一直盯着法庭的天花板看，在这番激动的气氛中，他既没有换位置，也没有改变姿势。而他的同行斯特莱弗先生则在他面前整理文件，低声与身旁的人交谈，还不时焦虑地瞥一眼陪审团；观众们也纷纷移动位置，重新聚在一起；就连法官大人也从座位上站起来，慢悠悠地在台上踱步，观众心中不免猜测他是不是有些心神不宁。唯有卡顿先生一人，靠坐在椅子上，身上破旧的长袍半披着，假发歪戴着，好像刚从头上随便扣上去似的，双手插在口袋里，眼睛依旧像一整天那样盯着天花板。他那副放荡不羁的样子，不仅让他显得十分不体面，还大大削弱了他与被告之间本就明显的相似之处（当他们被一同比较时，他那片刻的专注曾让这种相似更加突出）。许多旁观者注意到这一点后，相互议论说，两人其实不像他们之前认为的那么相像。克伦彻先生对身边的邻居说道：“我敢打赌，这家伙肯定没什么法律活儿可干。他这模样，也不像是能接上活儿的主儿。”
foreignization: 卡顿先生长久以来一直凝视着法庭的天花板，在这番激动中，他既没有改变位置，也没有改变姿态。当他的博学朋友斯特莱弗先生在他面前整理文件，与身旁的人低声交谈，并不时焦虑地瞥一眼陪审团；当所有观众或多或少都移动了一下，重新组合成新的群体；甚至当法官大人自己也从座位上起身，缓缓在自己的讲台上踱步，观众心中不免产生一种怀疑，认为他的状态有些发烧；唯有这个人依旧斜靠在椅子上，身上破旧的长袍半脱着，乱七八糟的假发随意地戴在头上，手插在口袋里，眼睛依然像一整天那样盯着天花板。他举止中的某种特别放荡不羁，不仅让他看起来不可靠，还大大减弱了他与被告之间无疑存在的强烈相似之处（这种相似在他们被并排比较时因短暂的认真而得到了加强），以至于许多旁观者现在注意到他时，互相说他们几乎不会认为两人如此相像。克伦彻先生向他旁边的邻居发表了这一观察，并补充道：“我敢拿半个几尼打赌，他得不到什么法律工作。他看起来就不像是能拿到活的那种人，不是吗？”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Mr. Carton, who had so long sat looking at the ceiling of the court, changed neither his place nor his attitude, even in this excitement. While his learned friend, Mr. Stryver, massing his papers before him, whispered with those who sat near, and from time to time glanced anxiously at the jury; while all the spectators moved more or less, and grouped themselves anew; while even my Lord himself arose from his seat, and slowly paced up and down his platform, not unattended by a suspicion in the minds of the audience that his state was feverish; this one man sat leaning back, with his torn gown half off him, his untidy wig put on just as it had happened to light on his head after its removal, his hands in his pockets, and his eyes on the ceiling as they had been all day. Something especially reckless in his demeanour, not only gave him a disreputable look, but so diminished the strong resemblance he undoubtedly bore to the prisoner (which his momentary earnestness, when they were compared together, had strengthened), that many of the lookers-on, taking note of him now, said to one another they would hardly have thought the two were so alike. Mr. Cruncher made the observation to his next neighbour, and added, “I’d hold half a guinea that he don’t get no law-work to do. Don’t look like the sort of one to get any, do he?”

source: “It would be ungenerous to affect not to know that your self-denial is to be referred to your consideration for her father. Her father thanks you.”
domestication: 不说破你这份克己复礼是为了顾及她父亲的情面，她父亲也谢谢你了。
foreignization: “如果说不知道您的自我牺牲是出于对她父亲的考虑，那未免太不慷慨了。她父亲感谢您。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“It would be ungenerous to affect not to know that your self-denial is to be referred to your consideration for her father. Her father thanks you.”
不说破你这份克己复礼是为了顾及她父亲的情面，她父亲也谢谢你了。

messages_foreignization.content:

“It would be ungenerous to affect not to know that your self-denial is to be referred to your consideration for her father. Her father thanks you.”
“如果说不知道您的自我牺牲是出于对她父亲的考虑，那未免太不慷慨了。她父亲感谢您。”

source: No. They were all by that time choking the Hall of Examination where this old man, ugly and wicked, was, and overflowing into the adjacent open space and streets. source: The lady in question, whose rooted conviction that she was more than a match for any foreigner, was not to be shaken by distress and, danger, appeared with folded arms, and observed in English to The Vengeance, whom her eyes first encountered, “Well, I am sure, Boldface! I hope you are pretty well!” She also bestowed a British cough on Madame Defarge; but, neither of the two took much heed of her.
domestication: 这位女士自认为比任何外国人都厉害，这点儿困境和危险根本动摇不了她的信心。她双臂交叉，一见到勇复仇（The Vengeance），就用英语说：“嘿，真是的，大胆鬼！希望你挺好的！”她还对德发日太太咳嗽了一声，但两人谁也没怎么理她。
foreignization: 那位女士，她坚信自己远胜过任何外国人，这种信念不会因困苦和危险而动摇，她双臂交叉出现，用英语对首先看到的复仇者说：“嗯，我敢肯定，Boldface！我希望你过得还不错！”她还向德发日太太发出了一声英国式的咳嗽；但是，这两个人都没有太理会她。

那位女士，她坚信自己远胜过任何外国人，这种信念不会因困苦和危险而动摇，她双臂交叉出现，用英语对首先看到的复仇者说：“嗯，我敢肯定，Boldface！我希望你过得还不错！”她还向德发日太太发出了一声英国式的咳嗽；但是，这两个人都没有太理会她。

source: “But, do not believe,” said Darnay, upon whose ear the mournful voice struck with a reproachful sound, “that if my fortune were so cast as that, being one day so happy as to make her my wife, I must at any time put any separation between her and you, I could or would breathe a word of what I now say. Besides that I should know it to be hopeless, I should know it to be a baseness. If I had any such possibility, even at a remote distance of years, harboured in my thoughts, and hidden in my heart—if it ever had been there—if it ever could be there—I could not now touch this honoured hand.”
domestication: “可是，你别误会，”达奈说道，那哀伤的声音在他耳边响起，仿佛在责备他，“要是我的命运如此，有一天能娶她为妻，我绝不会在任何时刻说出今天的话来，也不会做出任何让她和你分离的事。除了我知道这样做是徒劳的，我还知道这是卑鄙的。如果我心中有任何这样的念头，哪怕是在遥远的将来，藏在心底深处——如果它曾在那里，如果它可能在那里——我现在也不会来握这只尊贵的手。”
foreignization: “但是，不要认为，”达内说道，那悲凉的声音在他耳边响起，带着一种责备的意味，“如果我的命运如此安排，让我有一天能够成为她的丈夫，我却会在任何时候在她和你之间造成任何分离，我会或会说出我现在说的话。除了我知道这将是徒劳的，我还知道这是卑鄙的。如果我心中有任何这样的可能性，即使是在遥远的将来，藏在我的思想中，隐藏在我心中——如果它曾经在那里——如果它能够在那里——我现在就不能触及这只尊贵的手。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“But, do not believe,” said Darnay, upon whose ear the mournful voice struck with a reproachful sound, “that if my fortune were so cast as that, being one day so happy as to make her my wife, I must at any time put any separation between her and you, I could or would breathe a word of what I now say. Besides that I should know it to be hopeless, I should know it to be a baseness. If I had any such possibility, even at a remote distance of years, harboured in my thoughts, and hidden in my heart—if it ever had been there—if it ever could be there—I could not now touch this honoured hand.”
“可是，你别误会，”达奈说道，那哀伤的声音在他耳边响起，仿佛在责备他，“要是我的命运如此，有一天能娶她为妻，我绝不会在任何时刻说出今天的话来，也不会做出任何让她和你分离的事。除了我知道这样做是徒劳的，我还知道这是卑鄙的。如果我心中有任何这样的念头，哪怕是在遥远的将来，藏在心底深处——如果它曾在那里，如果它可能在那里——我现在也不会来握这只尊贵的手。”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“But, do not believe,” said Darnay, upon whose ear the mournful voice struck with a reproachful sound, “that if my fortune were so cast as that, being one day so happy as to make her my wife, I must at any time put any separation between her and you, I could or would breathe a word of what I now say. Besides that I should know it to be hopeless, I should know it to be a baseness. If I had any such possibility, even at a remote distance of years, harboured in my thoughts, and hidden in my heart—if it ever had been there—if it ever could be there—I could not now touch this honoured hand.”
“但是，不要认为，”达内说道，那悲凉的声音在他耳边响起，带着一种责备的意味，“如果我的命运如此安排，让我有一天能够成为她的丈夫，我却会在任何时候在她和你之间造成任何分离，我会或会说出我现在说的话。除了我知道这将是徒劳的，我还知道这是卑鄙的。如果我心中有任何这样的可能性，即使是在遥远的将来，藏在我的思想中，隐藏在我心中——如果它曾经在那里——如果它能够在那里——我现在就不能触及这只尊贵的手。”

source: And now that the cloud settled on Saint Antoine, which a momentary gleam had driven from his sacred countenance, the darkness of it was heavy—cold, dirt, sickness, ignorance, and want, were the lords in waiting on the saintly presence—nobles of great power all of them; but, most especially the last. Samples of a people that had undergone a terrible grinding and regrinding in the mill, and certainly not in the fabulous mill which ground old people young, shivered at every corner, passed in and out at every doorway, looked from every window, fluttered in every vestige of a garment that the wind shook. The mill which had worked them down, was the mill that grinds young people old; the children had ancient faces and grave voices; and upon them, and upon the grown faces, and ploughed into every furrow of age and coming up afresh, was the sigh, Hunger. It was prevalent everywhere. Hunger was pushed out of the tall houses, in the wretched clothing that hung upon poles and lines; Hunger was patched into them with straw and rag and wood and paper; Hunger was repeated in every fragment of the small modicum of firewood that the man sawed off; Hunger stared down from the smokeless chimneys, and started up from the filthy street that had no offal, among its refuse, of anything to eat. Hunger was the inscription on the baker’s shelves, written in every small loaf of his scanty stock of bad bread; at the sausage-shop, in every dead-dog preparation that was offered for sale. Hunger rattled its dry bones among the roasting chestnuts in the turned cylinder; Hunger was shred into atomics in every farthing porringer of husky chips of potato, fried with some reluctant drops of oil.
domestication: 乌云再次笼罩了圣安托万，那片刻的光芒刚刚从他的圣容上消散，此刻黑暗沉重——寒冷、肮脏、疾病、愚昧和贫困，成了这片圣土上的权贵——尤其是最后一位。那些在磨坊中被无情碾压又再三重磨的人们，显然不是在那传说中的能将老人变回青年的神奇磨坊里。他们瑟瑟发抖，躲在每个角落，进出每扇门，从每个窗户向外张望，风吹动他们的衣角，如同飘摇的残破布条。这磨坊，正是让年轻人未老先衰的磨坊；孩子们脸上写满了沧桑，声音也透着成熟；无论是在孩童的稚嫩面庞上，还是在成年人的面孔上，无论是刻在岁月的沟壑中，还是新生的皱纹里，无不诉说着同一个词：饥饿。饥饿无处不在。饥饿被挂在高高的房屋外，那些破烂的衣服挂在杆子和绳索上；饥饿用稻草、破布、木头和纸片补丁般缝在这些衣物上；饥饿在男人锯下的每一小块稀少的木柴碎片中显现；饥饿从没有一丝烟雾的烟囱向下凝视，在那满是垃圾却找不到任何食物残渣的街道上突显。饥饿写在面包店的货架上，用每一个少量的劣质面包书写；在香肠店里，每一根用死狗肉制成的产品都在诉说饥饿。饥饿在旋转的烤箱中与烤栗子一起嘎吱作响；饥饿被分解成微粒，在每个铜板一份的粗糙土豆片中，在几滴勉强加入的油中煎炸。
foreignization: 而现在，当云雾降落在圣安托万时，那短暂的光芒已从他神圣的面容上驱散，这黑暗变得沉重——寒冷、肮脏、疾病、无知和贫穷，是围绕在圣洁面前的侍臣——他们都是权力极大的贵族；但尤其是最后一个。那些经历过可怕碾磨和再碾磨的人们，显然不是在那座将老年人磨年轻的老磨坊中，他们颤抖在每个角落，进出每个门口，从每扇窗户望出，在每一件被风吹动的衣物残迹中飘荡。将他们碾磨下来的磨坊，是将年轻人磨老的磨坊；孩子们有着苍老的面孔和庄重的声音；在他们身上，在成人的脸上，以及刻入每一道皱纹并不断浮现的，是饥饿的叹息。它无处不在。饥饿被推出高高的房屋，挂在杆子和绳索上的破烂衣裳；饥饿被用稻草、破布、木头和纸片修补在这些衣裳上；饥饿在那个男人锯下的每一小块少量柴火碎片中重复出现；饥饿从没有烟雾的烟囱向下凝视，并从那条没有任何可食用垃圾的污秽街道中跃起。饥饿是面包店货架上的铭文，用他少量的劣质面包中的每一个小面包写就；在香肠店里，每一包以死狗肉为原料的商品上也写着饥饿。饥饿在旋转圆筒中的烤栗子里摇晃着它的枯骨；饥饿被切碎成原子，在每一个便士的粗薯片粥中，与几滴不情愿的油一起煎炸。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

And now that the cloud settled on Saint Antoine, which a momentary gleam had driven from his sacred countenance, the darkness of it was heavy—cold, dirt, sickness, ignorance, and want, were the lords in waiting on the saintly presence—nobles of great power all of them; but, most especially the last. Samples of a people that had undergone a terrible grinding and regrinding in the mill, and certainly not in the fabulous mill which ground old people young, shivered at every corner, passed in and out at every doorway, looked from every window, fluttered in every vestige of a garment that the wind shook. The mill which had worked them down, was the mill that grinds young people old; the children had ancient faces and grave voices; and upon them, and upon the grown faces, and ploughed into every furrow of age and coming up afresh, was the sigh, Hunger. It was prevalent everywhere. Hunger was pushed out of the tall houses, in the wretched clothing that hung upon poles and lines; Hunger was patched into them with straw and rag and wood and paper; Hunger was repeated in every fragment of the small modicum of firewood that the man sawed off; Hunger stared down from the smokeless chimneys, and started up from the filthy street that had no offal, among its refuse, of anything to eat. Hunger was the inscription on the baker’s shelves, written in every small loaf of his scanty stock of bad bread; at the sausage-shop, in every dead-dog preparation that was offered for sale. Hunger rattled its dry bones among the roasting chestnuts in the turned cylinder; Hunger was shred into atomics in every farthing porringer of husky chips of potato, fried with some reluctant drops of oil.

source: She curtseyed to him (young ladies made curtseys in those days), with a pretty desire to convey to him that she felt how much older and wiser he was than she. He made her another bow.
domestication: 她向他行了个屈膝礼（那时候年轻姑娘都这么做），心里想着要让他知道，她明白他比自己年长也更有智慧。他也向她回了个鞠躬。
foreignization: 她向他行了一个屈膝礼（当时的年轻女士都会这样做），带着一种美好的愿望，想让他知道她感觉到他比她年长和明智得多。他又向她鞠了一躬。

source: What money would be drawn out of Tellson’s henceforth, and what would lie there, lost and forgotten; what plate and jewels would tarnish in Tellson’s hiding-places, while the depositors rusted in prisons, and when they should have violently perished; how many accounts with Tellson’s never to be balanced in this world, must be carried over into the next; no man could have said, that night, any more than Mr. Jarvis Lorry could, though he thought heavily of these questions. He sat by a newly-lighted wood fire (the blighted and unfruitful year was prematurely cold), and on his honest and courageous face there was a deeper shade than the pendent lamp could throw, or any object in the room distortedly reflect—a shade of horror.
domestication: 往后从泰德森银行能取出来的钱有多少，又有多少会躺在那里，被遗忘；多少金银珠宝会在泰德森的隐秘之处蒙尘，而它们的主人却在牢狱中生锈腐烂，甚至惨遭横祸；多少与泰德森未结清的账目在这个世界上无法了结，只能带到另一个世界去；那天晚上，谁也说不清，就连贾维斯·洛里先生自己也是百思不得其解，尽管他对此忧心忡忡。他坐在新燃起的木火旁（这荒芜不毛的一年早早地变得寒冷），在他诚实勇敢的脸上，笼罩着比吊灯投下的阴影更深的恐惧之色，房间里任何物件扭曲的倒影也无法比拟。
foreignization: 今后从泰勒森银行提取出的钱财会是什么，什么钱财会躺在那里，遗失和遗忘；什么银器和珠宝会在泰勒森的藏匿处失去光泽，而存款人则在监狱中生锈，当他们惨遭暴力死亡时；有多少与泰勒森未在此世结算的账户必须带到下一个世界；那晚没有人能说得比贾维斯·洛里先生更清楚，尽管他对这些问题考虑得很沉重。他坐在一炉新点燃的木火旁（这个荒芜不毛的年份早早地变得寒冷），在他诚实勇敢的脸上，有一种比悬挂的灯所能投下的阴影更深的阴影，或者房间里的任何物体都无法扭曲地反射出的——一种恐怖的阴影。

He sat by a newly-lighted wood fire (the blighted and unfruitful year was prematurely cold), and on his honest and courageous face there was a deeper shade than the pendent lamp could throw, or any object in the room distortedly reflect—a shade of horror.

source: “Don’t look!” cried Mr. Lorry. “Don’t look out! Manette, for your life, don’t touch the blind!”
domestication: “别看！”洛里先生喊道，“别往外面看！曼内特，为了你的命，千万别碰那窗帘！”

source: His collected and calm manner could not prevent her blood from running cold, as he thus tried to anatomise his old condition.
domestication: 他那镇定自若的样子，却让她心惊胆战，他试图剖析自己过去的状态，仿佛在解剖一具冰冷的尸体。
foreignization: 他那镇定自若的态度并不能阻止她的血液凝固，当他试图剖析自己过去的状态时。

source: “Yes. And if you were shown a flock of birds, unable to fly, and were set upon them to strip them of their feathers for your own advantage, you would set upon the birds of the finest feathers; would you not?”
domestication: 对。如果你看到一群鸟，飞不起来，让你去拔它们的羽毛来谋取私利，你肯定会上前挑那些羽毛最漂亮的鸟来下手，不是吗？
foreignization: “是的。如果你看到一群无法飞行的鸟，并被要求为了自己的利益去剥光它们的羽毛，你会选择那些羽毛最漂亮的鸟；不是吗？”

source: They went into a dingy room lined with books and littered with papers, where there was a blazing fire. A kettle steamed upon the hob, and in the midst of the wreck of papers a table shone, with plenty of wine upon it, and brandy, and rum, and sugar, and lemons.
domestication: 他们走进一间昏暗的屋子，四壁堆满了书，地上散落着纸张，屋里却烧着旺旺的火。炉子上坐着一把冒着热气的水壶，桌子在满地的纸张中显得格外干净，上面摆满了酒、白兰地、朗姆酒、糖和柠檬。
foreignization: 他们走进了一个摆满书籍、散落着纸张的昏暗房间，那里生着熊熊的炉火。炉子上坐着一把冒着蒸汽的水壶，而在乱糟糟的纸张中间，一张桌子闪着光，桌上摆满了葡萄酒、白兰地、朗姆酒、糖和柠檬。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

他们走进了一个摆满书籍、散落着纸张的昏暗房间，那里生着熊熊的炉火。炉子上坐着一把冒着蒸汽的水壶，而在乱糟糟的纸张中间，一张桌子闪着光，桌上摆满了葡萄酒、白兰地、朗姆酒、糖和柠檬。

source: “There is no time to lose. I am well prepared, but there are precautions to be taken, that could not be taken until he was actually summoned before the Tribunal. He has not received the notice yet, but I know that he will presently be summoned for to-morrow, and removed to the Conciergerie; I have timely information. You are not afraid?”
domestication: 一刻也不能耽搁了。我准备得相当充分，但有些防范措施必须等他真正被传唤到法庭上才能实施。他还没收到传票，但我得到消息，说他很快就会被传唤到明天的庭审，并且会被转移到看守所。你不会害怕吧？
foreignization: “没有时间可以浪费。我准备得很充分，但还有一些预防措施，必须等到他真正被传唤到法庭面前才能采取。他还没有收到通知，但我知道他很快就会被传唤到明天的法庭，并被送往 Conciergerie；我有及时的消息。你不怕吗？”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“There is no time to lose. I am well prepared, but there are precautions to be taken, that could not be taken until he was actually summoned before the Tribunal. He has not received the notice yet, but I know that he will presently be summoned for to-morrow, and removed to the Conciergerie; I have timely information. You are not afraid?”

source: Mr. Stryver then called his few witnesses, and Mr. Cruncher had next to attend while Mr. Attorney-General turned the whole suit of clothes Mr. Stryver had fitted on the jury, inside out; showing how Barsad and Cly were even a hundred times better than he had thought them, and the prisoner a hundred times worse. Lastly, came my Lord himself, turning the suit of clothes, now inside out, now outside in, but on the whole decidedly trimming and shaping them into grave-clothes for the prisoner.
domestication: 斯特里弗先生随后传唤了他的几位证人，克伦彻先生则在一旁候着，等着王室检察官把斯特里弗先生给陪审团穿上的那套衣服翻了个里外；展示出巴塞德和克莱比他想象中还要好上百倍，而被告则糟糕上百倍。最后轮到法官大人亲自上阵，这套衣服一会儿里子朝外，一会儿面子朝里，但总的来说，明显是在给被告裁剪缝制寿衣了。
foreignization: 斯特莱佛先生随后传唤了他的几位证人，克伦彻先生则在一旁等候，而这时检察长先生将斯特莱佛先生为陪审团量身定制的整套衣服翻了个里外；他展示了巴塞德和克莱比他想象中还要好上一百倍，而被告则恶劣上一百倍。最后，大法官大人亲自上阵，把这套已经翻转的衣服一会儿里面朝外，一会儿外面朝内，但总体来说，他确实在把这套衣服裁剪成被告的寿衣。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Mr. Stryver then called his few witnesses, and Mr. Cruncher had next to attend while Mr. Attorney-General turned the whole suit of clothes Mr. Stryver had fitted on the jury, inside out; showing how Barsad and Cly were even a hundred times better than he had thought them, and the prisoner a hundred times worse. Lastly, came my Lord himself, turning the suit of clothes, now inside out, now outside in, but on the whole decidedly trimming and shaping them into grave-clothes for the prisoner.

source: To this lodging he at once removed Lucie and her child, and Miss Pross: giving them what comfort he could, and much more than he had himself. He left Jerry with them, as a figure to fill a doorway that would bear considerable knocking on the head, and returned to his own occupations. A disturbed and doleful mind he brought to bear upon them, and slowly and heavily the day lagged on with him.
domestication: 他立刻把露西、她的孩子和普罗斯小姐搬到了这处住所，尽量让她们过得舒服些，尽管他自己也没多少东西可以分享。他留下杰里守着她们，这人块头大，站在门口能挡不少事，自己则回去继续手头的工作。心里乱糟糟的，像压了块大石头，这一天对他来说，过得格外漫长而沉重。
foreignization: 他立即将露西和她的孩子，以及普罗斯小姐搬迁到了这个住处，尽可能地给他们提供了舒适，甚至比他自己所拥有的还要多。他留下杰里陪在他们身边，作为一个能够承受相当程度头部敲击的门柱填充人物，然后回到了自己的工作中。他带着一颗不安和悲伤的心投入工作，而这一天对他来说，缓慢而沉重地拖着脚步前进。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

A disturbed and doleful mind he brought to bear upon them, and slowly and heavily the day lagged on with him.

source: “Well!” said that good-natured emissary, after a full half-hour of bootless attempts to bring him round to the question. “I have been to Soho.”
domestication: “唉！”那位好心的使者叹了口气，费了半天劲儿，愣是没能让他绕回到正题上来。“我可是去苏活区了。”
foreignization: “好吧！”那位好心的使者在徒劳地试图让他回到问题上整整半小时后说道。“我去过苏活区。”

source: Instantly Madame Defarge’s knife was in her girdle; the drum was beating in the streets, as if it and a drummer had flown together by magic; and The Vengeance, uttering terrific shrieks, and flinging her arms about her head like all the forty Furies at once, was tearing from house to house, rousing the women.
domestication: 说时迟那时快，德法日太太的刀子已经插在腰带上；街上鼓声震天，仿佛鼓和鼓手是凭空变出来的；复仇女神一边发出恐怖的尖叫，一边挥舞着手臂，就像四十个复仇女神同时附身一样，在各家各户间穿梭，唤醒了妇女们。

source: “That’s a fair young lady to be pitied by and wept for by! How does it feel? Is it worth being tried for one’s life, to be the object of such sympathy and compassion, Mr. Darnay?”
domestication: 这姑娘真是让人怜香惜玉，看了都心疼！你说说，这种感觉怎么样？为了博得这样的同情和怜悯，值得豁出命去吗，达内先生？
foreignization: 那是一个值得同情和哭泣的年轻女士！感觉如何？为了成为这样的同情和怜悯的对象，是否值得冒生命危险，达奈先生？

source: But when they came to the town of Beauvais—which they did at eventide, when the streets were filled with people—he could not conceal from himself that the aspect of affairs was very alarming. An ominous crowd gathered to see him dismount of the posting-yard, and many voices called out loudly, “Down with the emigrant!”
domestication: 但他们傍晚时分到达博韦镇时，大街上人潮涌动，他心里清楚，形势非常严峻。一下马车，就有一群人不怀好意地围了上来，不少人高声喊着：“打倒逃亡贵族！”
foreignization: 但是当他们来到博韦镇——那时正值黄昏，街上挤满了人——他不能不承认局势非常令人担忧。一群不祥的人群聚集起来看他从驿站下车，许多人高声喊道：“打倒逃亡者！”

messages_domestication.content:

But when they came to the town of Beauvais—which they did at eventide, when the streets were filled with people—he could not conceal from himself that the aspect of affairs was very alarming. An ominous crowd gathered to see him dismount of the posting-yard, and many voices called out loudly, “Down with the emigrant!”
但他们傍晚时分到达博韦镇时，大街上人潮涌动，他心里清楚，形势非常严峻。一下马车，就有一群人不怀好意地围了上来，不少人高声喊着：“打倒逃亡贵族！”

An ominous crowd gathered to see him dismount of the posting-yard, and many voices called out loudly, “Down with the emigrant!”
但是当他们来到博韦镇——那时正值黄昏，街上挤满了人——他不能不承认局势非常令人担忧。一群不祥的人群聚集起来看他从驿站下车，许多人高声喊道：“打倒逃亡者！”

source: “Such are not my orders. You will be visited, and can ask then. At present, you may buy your food, and nothing more.”
domestication: 这可不是我的命令。到时候自会有人来拜访你，到时你再问吧。现在嘛，你就安心买吃的，别的什么都别想了。
foreignization: “这样的命令不是给我的。你会被访问，到时你可以问。目前，你只能购买食物，不能再做其他的事情。”

source: “I think it probable,” said the Doctor, breaking silence with an effort, “that the relapse you have described, my dear friend, was not quite unforeseen by its subject.”
domestication: 医生努力打破了沉默，说：“我想啊，我亲爱的朋友，你描述的这次病情反复，病人恐怕是早有预感的。”
foreignization: “我想这是可能的，”医生努力打破沉默说道，“你所描述的这种反复，我亲爱的朋友，其主体或许并非完全未曾预见。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

source: He leaned an elbow on her table, and covered his eyes with his hand. The table trembled in the silence that followed.
domestication: 他把胳膊肘支在她的桌子上，用手遮住眼睛。接着是一片寂静，桌子都在微微颤抖。
foreignization: 他把肘靠在她的桌子上，用手遮住了眼睛。随后的寂静中，桌子颤抖了一下。

source: “Yes! But it is your weakness that you sometimes need to see your victim and your opportunity, to sustain you. Sustain yourself without that. When the time comes, let loose a tiger and a devil; but wait for the time with the tiger and the devil chained—not shown—yet always ready.”
domestication: 对！但你的弱点在于有时需要看到你的对手和机会，才能支撑自己。学会在没有这些的情况下也撑下去。时机一到，就放手让老虎和魔鬼出笼；但在那之前，要把它们拴住——不露声色——却要时刻准备着。
foreignization: “是的！但你的弱点在于有时你需要见到你的受害者和机会，以维持你自己。在没有这些的情况下维持自己。当时机来临，释放一只老虎和一个魔鬼；但要带着被锁链束缚的老虎和魔鬼等待——不展示——却始终准备着。”

source: “Both were so wrapped up, and the night was so dark, and we were all so reserved, that I cannot undertake to say even that.”
domestication: 两个人都沉浸在自己的世界里，夜深得伸手不见五指，大伙儿又都拘谨得很，所以我连这事儿也不敢打包票。
foreignization: 两人都如此投入，夜晚又是如此黑暗，我们大家都如此拘谨，以至于我甚至不能肯定地说出那件事。

source: Mr. Lorry moved in his chair, and cast a troubled look towards the hospital procession of negro cupids. As if they had any help for anybody in their absurd baskets!
domestication: 洛里先生在椅子上挪了挪，不安地朝那些黑人小爱神组成的队伍瞥了一眼。好像他们那滑稽的篮子里能有什么帮得上忙的东西似的！
foreignization: 洛里先生在椅子上动了动，向医院里的黑人小爱神游行队伍投去了不安的目光。仿佛他们那可笑的篮子里有任何东西能帮助任何人！

source: Even when there were sounds of sorrow among the rest, they were not harsh nor cruel. Even when golden hair, like her own, lay in a halo on a pillow round the worn face of a little boy, and he said, with a radiant smile, “Dear papa and mamma, I am very sorry to leave you both, and to leave my pretty sister; but I am called, and I must go!” those were not tears all of agony that wetted his young mother’s cheek, as the spirit departed from her embrace that had been entrusted to it. Suffer them and forbid them not. They see my Father’s face. O Father, blessed words!
domestication: 即使周围有悲伤的声音，也不显得刺耳或残忍。就像那枕头上，金色的头发，和她的一样，环绕着一个脸色憔悴的小男孩的脸，他带着灿烂的笑容说：“亲爱的爸爸、妈妈，我很抱歉要离开你们，也要离开我漂亮的妹妹；但我被召唤了，我必须走！”当灵魂从母亲怀中离去时，母亲年轻的脸颊上流下的泪水，不全是痛苦的泪水。让孩子们来吧，不要阻止他们。他们能看到天父的容颜。哦，天父，多么美好的话语！
foreignization: 即使在其余的声音中夹杂着悲伤的声音，它们也既不刺耳也不残忍。即使当金色的头发，像她自己的那样，环绕在一个小男孩那疲惫的脸庞周围的枕头上，他带着灿烂的笑容说：“亲爱的爸爸和妈妈，我非常遗憾要离开你们俩，也要离开我的漂亮妹妹；但我被召唤了，我必须走！”在他年轻母亲的脸颊上湿润的泪水并不全是痛苦的泪水，当那个被托付给她的灵魂从她的怀抱中离去时。容忍他们，不要禁止他们。他们看见了我天父的面容。哦，天父，多么美好的话语！

Suffer them and forbid them not. They see my Father’s face. O Father, blessed words!

source: He had no opportunity of saying, or so much as thinking, anything else, until he was clear of the Old Bailey; for, the crowd came pouring out with a vehemence that nearly took him off his legs, and a loud buzz swept into the street as if the baffled blue-flies were dispersing in search of other carrion.
domestication: 他连说话或想别的事的机会都没有，直到离开了老贝利法院；因为人群像洪水般涌出，差点把他撞倒，嘈杂声如潮水般涌向街道，仿佛那些无处可去的苍蝇正四散寻找其他腐肉。
foreignization: 他根本没有机会说，甚至想其他任何事情，直到他离开了老贝利；因为，人群猛烈地涌出，差点把他撞倒，一阵嘈杂声像受挫的苍蝇四散寻找其他腐肉一般涌上了街道。

source: A second man got up and went out. Madame Defarge set wine before the mender of roads called Jacques, who doffed his blue cap to the company, and drank. In the breast of his blouse he carried some coarse dark bread; he ate of this between whiles, and sat munching and drinking near Madame Defarge’s counter. A third man got up and went out.
domestication: 又一个人起身出去了。德法日太太给那个叫雅克的修路工倒了一杯酒，他向在场的人摘下蓝帽子致意，然后就喝了起来。他的衬衫口袋里揣着些粗黑面包，喝两口酒就啃一口面包，坐在德法日太太的柜台边自得其乐。接着，第三个人也起身离开了。
foreignization: 第二个男人站起来走出去了。德发日太太给那个被称为雅克的修路工倒了酒，他向在场的人脱下蓝帽子致意，然后喝了起来。在他的衬衫胸前放着一些粗糙的黑面包；他不时吃一口，坐在德发日太太的柜台附近啃着和喝着。第三个男人站起来走出去了。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

A second man got up and went out. Madame Defarge set wine before the mender of roads called Jacques, who doffed his blue cap to the company, and drank. In the breast of his blouse he carried some coarse dark bread; he ate of this between whiles, and sat munching and drinking near Madame Defarge’s counter. A third man got up and went out.

source: “I want,” said Defarge, who had not removed his gaze from the shoemaker, “to let in a little more light here. You can bear a little more?”
domestication: 德法日说，眼睛一直盯着鞋匠，“我想再让这儿亮堂些。你能受得了更亮一点吗？”
foreignization: “我想，”德法日说，他没有移开盯着鞋匠的目光，“让这里再多一点光。你能承受多一点吗？”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I want,” said Defarge, who had not removed his gaze from the shoemaker, “to let in a little more light here. You can bear a little more?”
德法日说，眼睛一直盯着鞋匠，“我想再让这儿亮堂些。你能受得了更亮一点吗？”

messages_foreignization.content:

source: Any strongly marked expression of face on the part of a chief actor in a scene of great interest to whom many eyes are directed, will be unconsciously imitated by the spectators. Her forehead was painfully anxious and intent as she gave this evidence, and, in the pauses when she stopped for the Judge to write it down, watched its effect upon the counsel for and against. Among the lookers-on there was the same expression in all quarters of the court; insomuch, that a great majority of the foreheads there, might have been mirrors reflecting the witness, when the Judge looked up from his notes to glare at that tremendous heresy about George Washington.
domestication: 在一出引人注目的戏里，如果主角的表情非常强烈，那么很多观众的目光都会不自觉地模仿。她作证时，额头上满是焦虑和专注，每当停顿让法官记录时，她就观察律师们的不同反应。观众席上，四面八方的人都是一样的表情，仿佛大多数人的额头都成了镜子，反射着证人的神情。当法官从笔记中抬起头来，怒视那关于乔治·华盛顿的惊人异端时，这种感觉尤为强烈。
foreignization: 任何一位在场的主要演员，在许多目光注视下表现出强烈的面部表情时，观众都会不自觉地模仿这种表情。当她作证时，额头痛苦地焦虑和专注，并在停下来让法官记下她的证词的间歇中，观察这对控辩双方的影响。旁观者中到处都有同样的表情；以至于当法官从笔记中抬起头来怒视关于乔治·华盛顿的那个惊人的异端邪说时，法庭里大多数的前额都像是镜子一样反映了证人的表情。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Her forehead was painfully anxious and intent as she gave this evidence, and, in the pauses when she stopped for the Judge to write it down, watched its effect upon the counsel for and against. Among the lookers-on there was the same expression in all quarters of the court; insomuch, that a great majority of the foreheads there, might have been mirrors reflecting the witness, when the Judge looked up from his notes to glare at that tremendous heresy about George Washington.

source: “They say, those boastful English, that it is the Refuge of many. You know a compatriot who has found a Refuge there? A Doctor?”
domestication: 都说那些爱吹牛的英国人，那里是很多人的避风港。你认识在那儿找到避风港的同胞吗？比如一位医生？
foreignization: 他们说，那些自夸的英国人，那里是许多人的避难所。你认识一个在那里找到避难所的同胞吗？一个医生？

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“They say, those boastful English, that it is the Refuge of many. You know a compatriot who has found a Refuge there? A Doctor?”
都说那些爱吹牛的英国人，那里是很多人的避风港。你认识在那儿找到避风港的同胞吗？比如一位医生？

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“They say, those boastful English, that it is the Refuge of many. You know a compatriot who has found a Refuge there? A Doctor?”
他们说，那些自夸的英国人，那里是许多人的避难所。你认识一个在那里找到避难所的同胞吗？一个医生？

source: “No, dear Doctor Manette. Like you, a voluntary exile from France; like you, driven from it by its distractions, oppressions, and miseries; like you, striving to live away from it by my own exertions, and trusting in a happier future; I look only to sharing your fortunes, sharing your life and home, and being faithful to you to the death. Not to divide with Lucie her privilege as your child, companion, and friend; but to come in aid of it, and bind her closer to you, if such a thing can be.”
domestication: 不，亲爱的曼内特医生。我和您一样，是自愿离开法国的；也和您一样，是因为那里的纷扰、压迫和苦难才不得不离开；我努力靠自己在这片土地上生存，期待着更美好的未来；我只希望与您共享命运，共度生活，共享家园，至死忠心不渝。我不是要分走露西作为您的女儿、伴侣和朋友的特权；而是要来帮助她，让她与您的关系更加紧密，如果可能的话。
foreignization: “不，亲爱的马内特医生。像您一样，我是自愿流亡法国的；像您一样，因为它的纷扰、压迫和苦难而被迫离开；像您一样，努力靠自己的努力远离它，并相信一个更美好的未来；我只希望分享您的命运，分享您的生活和家庭，并至死忠于您。不是要与露西分享她作为您的孩子的特权，伴侣和朋友；而是要来帮助她，使她更加贴近您，如果可能的话。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“No, dear Doctor Manette. Like you, a voluntary exile from France; like you, driven from it by its distractions, oppressions, and miseries; like you, striving to live away from it by my own exertions, and trusting in a happier future; I look only to sharing your fortunes, sharing your life and home, and being faithful to you to the death. Not to divide with Lucie her privilege as your child, companion, and friend; but to come in aid of it, and bind her closer to you, if such a thing can be.”

source: Her uncertainty lasted but a moment; she hurried back, and tapped at his door, and softly called to him. The noise ceased at the sound of her voice, and he presently came out to her, and they walked up and down together for a long time.
domestication: 她心里的犹豫只是片刻的事；随即她快步折回，轻敲他的门，柔声唤他。听见她的声音，屋里的声响戛然而止，不一会儿，他就出来了。两人便一起在走廊上来来回回走了好一阵子。
foreignization: 她的犹豫只持续了片刻；她急忙回去，在他的门前敲了敲，并轻声呼唤他。听到她的声音，那声音停止了，过了一会儿，他出来找她，他们一起走了很久。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Her uncertainty lasted but a moment; she hurried back, and tapped at his door, and softly called to him. The noise ceased at the sound of her voice, and he presently came out to her, and they walked up and down together for a long time.
她心里的犹豫只是片刻的事；随即她快步折回，轻敲他的门，柔声唤他。听见她的声音，屋里的声响戛然而止，不一会儿，他就出来了。两人便一起在走廊上来来回回走了好一阵子。

Her uncertainty lasted but a moment; she hurried back, and tapped at his door, and softly called to him. The noise ceased at the sound of her voice, and he presently came out to her, and they walked up and down together for a long time.
她的犹豫只持续了片刻；她急忙回去，在他的门前敲了敲，并轻声呼唤他。听到她的声音，那声音停止了，过了一会儿，他出来找她，他们一起走了很久。

source: Once more, the two spectators started, as he turned upon her with a frightful suddenness. But she sat perfectly still in his grasp, and only said, in a low voice, “I entreat you, good gentlemen, do not come near us, do not speak, do not move!”
domestication: 两人又是一惊，只见他突然间朝她猛一转身。但她在他手里却一动不动，只是低声说道：“好心的先生们，求求你们，别靠近我们，别说话，别动！”
foreignization: 又一次，两位观众吓了一跳，因为他突然转向她。但她在他手中坐得一动不动，只是低声说道：“我恳求你们，好心的先生们，不要靠近我们，不要说话，不要动！”

Once more, the two spectators started, as he turned upon her with a frightful suddenness. But she sat perfectly still in his grasp, and only said, in a low voice, “I entreat you, good gentlemen, do not come near us, do not speak, do not move!”
又一次，两位观众吓了一跳，因为他突然转向她。但她在他手中坐得一动不动，只是低声说道：“我恳求你们，好心的先生们，不要靠近我们，不要说话，不要动！”

source: Not before dark night did the men and women come back to the children, wailing and breadless. Then, the miserable bakers’ shops were beset by long files of them, patiently waiting to buy bad bread; and while they waited with stomachs faint and empty, they beguiled the time by embracing one another on the triumphs of the day, and achieving them again in gossip. Gradually, these strings of ragged people shortened and frayed away; and then poor lights began to shine in high windows, and slender fires were made in the streets, at which neighbours cooked in common, afterwards supping at their doors.
domestication: 天黑了，大人们才拖着疲惫的身子回到孩子们身边，个个愁眉苦脸，两手空空。于是，那些破败的面包店前排起了长长的队伍，人们耐心地等着买劣质的面包。他们饿着肚子，却在等待中互相拥抱，聊起白天的胜利，仿佛又经历了一遍。慢慢地，这些衣衫褴褛的人群散去了；夜幕降临，高窗里亮起了微弱的灯光，街边燃起几堆小火，邻居们围着火堆一起做饭，然后坐在门口共享晚餐。
foreignization: 直到夜幕降临，男女们才回到孩子们身边，哭哭啼啼且没有面包。然后，那些可怜的面包店被长长的队伍包围，他们耐心地等待购买劣质的面包；而当他们空着肚子等待时，他们通过拥抱彼此庆祝当天的胜利，并在闲谈中再次实现这些胜利。渐渐地，这些衣衫褴褛的人们的长队变短并散去；接着，高高的窗户里亮起了昏暗的灯光，街头上生起了微弱的火堆，邻居们在这里共同烹饪，之后在自家门前共进晚餐。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Not before dark night did the men and women come back to the children, wailing and breadless. Then, the miserable bakers’ shops were beset by long files of them, patiently waiting to buy bad bread; and while they waited with stomachs faint and empty, they beguiled the time by embracing one another on the triumphs of the day, and achieving them again in gossip. Gradually, these strings of ragged people shortened and frayed away; and then poor lights began to shine in high windows, and slender fires were made in the streets, at which neighbours cooked in common, afterwards supping at their doors.
天黑了，大人们才拖着疲惫的身子回到孩子们身边，个个愁眉苦脸，两手空空。于是，那些破败的面包店前排起了长长的队伍，人们耐心地等着买劣质的面包。他们饿着肚子，却在等待中互相拥抱，聊起白天的胜利，仿佛又经历了一遍。慢慢地，这些衣衫褴褛的人群散去了；夜幕降临，高窗里亮起了微弱的灯光，街边燃起几堆小火，邻居们围着火堆一起做饭，然后坐在门口共享晚餐。

Gradually, these strings of ragged people shortened and frayed away; and then poor lights began to shine in high windows, and slender fires were made in the streets, at which neighbours cooked in common, afterwards supping at their doors.

source: “If, when I tell you, dearest dear, that your agony is over, and that I have come here to take you from it, and that we go to England to be at peace and at rest, I cause you to think of your useful life laid waste, and of our native France so wicked to you, weep for it, weep for it! And if, when I shall tell you of my name, and of my father who is living, and of my mother who is dead, you learn that I have to kneel to my honoured father, and implore his pardon for having never for his sake striven all day and lain awake and wept all night, because the love of my poor mother hid his torture from me, weep for it, weep for it! Weep for her, then, and for me! Good gentlemen, thank God! I feel his sacred tears upon my face, and his sobs strike against my heart. O, see! Thank God for us, thank God!”
domestication: 亲爱的，当我告诉你，你的痛苦已经结束，我来接你离开这一切，我们要去英国过上平静安逸的生活时，如果你因此想到了你那有用的生命被虚度，还有我们亲爱的法国对你如此不公，就哭出来吧，哭出来吧！等我告诉你我的名字，还有我父亲还活着，母亲却已去世，如果你得知我需要跪在尊敬的父亲面前，请求他原谅我没有为了他而日夜奋斗、彻夜难眠，因为我对母亲的爱让我看不见他的痛苦，就哭出来吧，哭出来吧！为她，也为我，哭一场吧！好心的先生们，感谢上帝！我感到他的圣洁泪水滴在我的脸上，他的抽泣敲打着我的心。啊，看哪！为我们的遭遇感谢上帝吧，感谢上帝！
foreignization: 如果，当我告诉你，最亲爱的，你的痛苦已经结束，我来这里带你离开它，我们将去英国寻求平静和安息时，如果你因此想到你有价值的生活被毁坏，以及我们的祖国法国对你如此不公，为它哭泣吧，为它哭泣吧！而如果，当我告诉你我的名字，以及我那还活着的父亲和已去世的母亲时，你得知我必须跪在我尊敬的父亲面前，请求他原谅我没有为了他的缘故日夜奋斗，整夜无眠地哭泣，因为对我的可怜母亲的爱使我无法看到他的痛苦，为它哭泣吧，为它哭泣吧！为她，也为我哭泣吧！好心的先生们，感谢上帝！我感到他神圣的泪水洒在我的脸上，他的啜泣撞击着我的心。哦，看啊！感谢上帝为我们，感谢上帝！

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“If, when I tell you, dearest dear, that your agony is over, and that I have come here to take you from it, and that we go to England to be at peace and at rest, I cause you to think of your useful life laid waste, and of our native France so wicked to you, weep for it, weep for it! And if, when I shall tell you of my name, and of my father who is living, and of my mother who is dead, you learn that I have to kneel to my honoured father, and implore his pardon for having never for his sake striven all day and lain awake and wept all night, because the love of my poor mother hid his torture from me, weep for it, weep for it! Weep for her, then, and for me! Good gentlemen, thank God! I feel his sacred tears upon my face, and his sobs strike against my heart. O, see! Thank God for us, thank God!”

亲爱的，当我告诉你，你的痛苦已经结束，我来接你离开这一切，我们要去英国过上平静安逸的生活时，如果你因此想到了你那有用的生命被虚度，还有我们亲爱的法国对你如此不公，就哭出来吧，哭出来吧！等我告诉你我的名字，还有我父亲还活着，母亲却已去世，如果你得知我需要跪在尊敬的父亲面前，请求他原谅我没有为了他而日夜奋斗、彻夜难眠，因为我对母亲的爱让我看不见他的痛苦，就哭出来吧，哭出来吧！为她，也为我，哭一场吧！好心的先生们，感谢上帝！我感到他的圣洁泪水滴在我的脸上，他的抽泣敲打着我的心。啊，看哪！为我们的遭遇感谢上帝吧，感谢上帝！

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“If, when I tell you, dearest dear, that your agony is over, and that I have come here to take you from it, and that we go to England to be at peace and at rest, I cause you to think of your useful life laid waste, and of our native France so wicked to you, weep for it, weep for it! And if, when I shall tell you of my name, and of my father who is living, and of my mother who is dead, you learn that I have to kneel to my honoured father, and implore his pardon for having never for his sake striven all day and lain awake and wept all night, because the love of my poor mother hid his torture from me, weep for it, weep for it! Weep for her, then, and for me! Good gentlemen, thank God! I feel his sacred tears upon my face, and his sobs strike against my heart. O, see! Thank God for us, thank God!”

如果，当我告诉你，最亲爱的，你的痛苦已经结束，我来这里带你离开它，我们将去英国寻求平静和安息时，如果你因此想到你有价值的生活被毁坏，以及我们的祖国法国对你如此不公，为它哭泣吧，为它哭泣吧！而如果，当我告诉你我的名字，以及我那还活着的父亲和已去世的母亲时，你得知我必须跪在我尊敬的父亲面前，请求他原谅我没有为了他的缘故日夜奋斗，整夜无眠地哭泣，因为对我的可怜母亲的爱使我无法看到他的痛苦，为它哭泣吧，为它哭泣吧！为她，也为我哭泣吧！好心的先生们，感谢上帝！我感到他神圣的泪水洒在我的脸上，他的啜泣撞击着我的心。哦，看啊！感谢上帝为我们，感谢上帝！

source: Such a staircase, with its accessories, in the older and more crowded parts of Paris, would be bad enough now; but, at that time, it was vile indeed to unaccustomed and unhardened senses. Every little habitation within the great foul nest of one high building—that is to say, the room or rooms within every door that opened on the general staircase—left its own heap of refuse on its own landing, besides flinging other refuse from its own windows. The uncontrollable and hopeless mass of decomposition so engendered, would have polluted the air, even if poverty and deprivation had not loaded it with their intangible impurities; the two bad sources combined made it almost insupportable. Through such an atmosphere, by a steep dark shaft of dirt and poison, the way lay. Yielding to his own disturbance of mind, and to his young companion’s agitation, which became greater every instant, Mr. Jarvis Lorry twice stopped to rest. Each of these stoppages was made at a doleful grating, by which any languishing good airs that were left uncorrupted, seemed to escape, and all spoilt and sickly vapours seemed to crawl in. Through the rusted bars, tastes, rather than glimpses, were caught of the jumbled neighbourhood; and nothing within range, nearer or lower than the summits of the two great towers of Notre-Dame, had any promise on it of healthy life or wholesome aspirations.
domestication: 这样的楼梯，连同它的附属物，在巴黎那些老旧而拥挤的街区，现在也够糟糕了；但在当时，对于不习惯和未被磨砺过的感官来说，真是糟透了。每一户人家，住在这一栋高楼这座大污秽巢穴里的每一户人家——也就是说，每扇开向公共楼梯的门后面的一间或几间房——不仅在自家门口的平台上留下一堆垃圾，还从自家窗户往外扔其他垃圾。由此产生的无法控制、无望清理的腐败物质，即使没有贫困和匮乏带来的无形污染，也会污染空气；这两种恶劣源头结合在一起，简直让人难以忍受。在这股由污秽和毒气组成的黑暗陡峭的通道中，道路就在其中。贾维斯·洛里先生心神不宁，再加上他的年轻同伴越来越激动，他两次停下来休息。每次停歇都在一个凄惨的铁栅栏旁，那里似乎让仅存的几缕未被玷污的好空气也逃走了，而所有腐坏和病态的蒸汽则爬了进来。透过生锈的栏杆，只能尝到而不是看到周围杂乱无章的环境；视线所及之处，无论是近处还是低处，除了圣母院那两座高塔的顶端，没有任何地方显示出健康生活或积极向上的希望。
foreignization: 这样一个楼梯，连同其附属设施，在巴黎较老和更拥挤的部分，现在也够糟糕的了；但在那个时代，对于不习惯和未被磨炼过的感官来说，它确实是非常恶劣的。每一座高大的肮脏建筑群中的每一个小住所——也就是说，每扇开向公共楼梯的门内的房间或几间房——除了从自己的窗户扔出其他垃圾外，还在自己的平台上留下了自己的垃圾堆。由此产生的无法控制和毫无希望的腐烂物质，即使贫困和剥夺没有给空气增添无形的污秽，也会污染空气；这两种恶劣源头结合在一起，几乎使人难以忍受。穿过这样一种氛围，沿着一条陡峭、黑暗的垃圾和毒气通道，路途就在其中。由于受到自己心烦意乱的影响，以及他的年轻同伴越来越大的激动情绪，贾维斯·洛里先生两次停下来休息。每次停歇都是在一个凄凉的铁栅栏处，似乎任何残存的清新的空气都在这里逃逸，而所有腐败和病态的蒸汽则似乎在这里爬入。透过生锈的栏杆，捕捉到的更多的是这个混乱邻里的味道，而不是景象；在视线所及范围内，低于或靠近圣母院两大塔尖顶的一切，都没有任何健康生活或良好愿望的迹象。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Such a staircase, with its accessories, in the older and more crowded parts of Paris, would be bad enough now; but, at that time, it was vile indeed to unaccustomed and unhardened senses. Every little habitation within the great foul nest of one high building—that is to say, the room or rooms within every door that opened on the general staircase—left its own heap of refuse on its own landing, besides flinging other refuse from its own windows. The uncontrollable and hopeless mass of decomposition so engendered, would have polluted the air, even if poverty and deprivation had not loaded it with their intangible impurities; the two bad sources combined made it almost insupportable. Through such an atmosphere, by a steep dark shaft of dirt and poison, the way lay. Each of these stoppages was made at a doleful grating, by which any languishing good airs that were left uncorrupted, seemed to escape, and all spoilt and sickly vapours seemed to crawl in. Through the rusted bars, tastes, rather than glimpses, were caught of the jumbled neighbourhood; and nothing within range, nearer or lower than the summits of the two great towers of Notre-Dame, had any promise on it of healthy life or wholesome aspirations.

foreignization: “我用那些话并没有打算声称它归我所有。如果它明天从你那里传给了我——”

“I had no intention, in the words I used, to claim it yet. If it passed to me from you, to-morrow—”
我这么说可不是想现在就占为己有。如果明天它从你那儿传到我这儿——

“I had no intention, in the words I used, to claim it yet. If it passed to me from you, to-morrow—”
“我用那些话并没有打算声称它归我所有。如果它明天从你那里传给了我——”

source: Nevertheless, not a moment was lost; not a moment! This Foulon was at the Hotel de Ville, and might be loosed. Never, if Saint Antoine knew his own sufferings, insults, and wrongs! Armed men and women flocked out of the Quarter so fast, and drew even these last dregs after them with such a force of suction, that within a quarter of an hour there was not a human creature in Saint Antoine’s bosom but a few old crones and the wailing children.
domestication: 说时迟，那时快；一刻也没耽搁！这富隆在市政厅，说不定就要被放了。圣安托万的人要是知道自己的苦处、受的侮辱和不公，决不能让这事儿发生！男女老少拿起武器，从街区里蜂拥而出，连最后几个躲在角落里的也给卷了进去，不到一刻钟的工夫，圣安托万就只剩几个老太婆和嚎啕大哭的孩子了。
foreignization: 然而，没有一刻耽搁；没有一刻！这个富隆在市政厅，可能会被释放。绝不，如果圣安托万知道自己的苦难、侮辱和冤屈！武装的男女从街区迅速涌出，并以如此强大的吸引力甚至把这些最后的渣滓也带走了，结果在一个小时内，圣安托万的心脏地带除了几个老太婆和哭泣的孩子外，没有一个人类生物。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Nevertheless, not a moment was lost; not a moment! This Foulon was at the Hotel de Ville, and might be loosed. Never, if Saint Antoine knew his own sufferings, insults, and wrongs! Armed men and women flocked out of the Quarter so fast, and drew even these last dregs after them with such a force of suction, that within a quarter of an hour there was not a human creature in Saint Antoine’s bosom but a few old crones and the wailing children.
说时迟，那时快；一刻也没耽搁！这富隆在市政厅，说不定就要被放了。圣安托万的人要是知道自己的苦处、受的侮辱和不公，决不能让这事儿发生！男女老少拿起武器，从街区里蜂拥而出，连最后几个躲在角落里的也给卷了进去，不到一刻钟的工夫，圣安托万就只剩几个老太婆和嚎啕大哭的孩子了。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Nevertheless, not a moment was lost; not a moment! This Foulon was at the Hotel de Ville, and might be loosed. Never, if Saint Antoine knew his own sufferings, insults, and wrongs! Armed men and women flocked out of the Quarter so fast, and drew even these last dregs after them with such a force of suction, that within a quarter of an hour there was not a human creature in Saint Antoine’s bosom but a few old crones and the wailing children.
然而，没有一刻耽搁；没有一刻！这个富隆在市政厅，可能会被释放。绝不，如果圣安托万知道自己的苦难、侮辱和冤屈！武装的男女从街区迅速涌出，并以如此强大的吸引力甚至把这些最后的渣滓也带走了，结果在一个小时内，圣安托万的心脏地带除了几个老太婆和哭泣的孩子外，没有一个人类生物。

source: “Tut! Nonsense, sir!—And, my dear Charles,” said Mr. Lorry, glancing at the House again, “you are to remember, that getting things out of Paris at this present time, no matter what things, is next to an impossibility. Papers and precious matters were this very day brought to us here (I speak in strict confidence; it is not business-like to whisper it, even to you), by the strangest bearers you can imagine, every one of whom had his head hanging on by a single hair as he passed the Barriers. At another time, our parcels would come and go, as easily as in business-like Old England; but now, everything is stopped.”
domestication: 哎呀！胡说八道，先生！——亲爱的查尔斯，”洛瑞先生又看了一眼那座房子，说道，“你得记住，如今要把东西从巴黎运出来，不管是什么东西，几乎都是不可能的。今天就有人把一些文件和贵重物品送到我们这里（我说的是绝对保密的事；即使是对你，也不能随便透露），那些送信的人个个都是命悬一线，才好不容易通过了关卡。要是换作别的时候，包裹进出就像在做生意讲规矩的老英格兰一样容易；可现在，一切都停滞不前了。
foreignization: “哎！胡说八道，先生！——还有，我亲爱的查尔斯，”洛里先生又看了一眼那所房子说，“你要记住，在目前这个时期，要把东西从巴黎带出来，不管是什么东西，几乎是不可能的。今天就有文件和贵重物品被送到了我们这里（我严格保密地说；即使是对你，这样低声细语也不像做生意的样子），每一个携带者都是以命相搏，头颅悬于一线，才通过了关卡。在另一个时候，我们的包裹进出会像在做生意的老英格兰一样容易；但现在，一切都停顿了。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Tut! Nonsense, sir!—And, my dear Charles,” said Mr. Lorry, glancing at the House again, “you are to remember, that getting things out of Paris at this present time, no matter what things, is next to an impossibility. Papers and precious matters were this very day brought to us here (I speak in strict confidence; it is not business-like to whisper it, even to you), by the strangest bearers you can imagine, every one of whom had his head hanging on by a single hair as he passed the Barriers. At another time, our parcels would come and go, as easily as in business-like Old England; but now, everything is stopped.”
哎呀！胡说八道，先生！——亲爱的查尔斯，”洛瑞先生又看了一眼那座房子，说道，“你得记住，如今要把东西从巴黎运出来，不管是什么东西，几乎都是不可能的。今天就有人把一些文件和贵重物品送到我们这里（我说的是绝对保密的事；即使是对你，也不能随便透露），那些送信的人个个都是命悬一线，才好不容易通过了关卡。要是换作别的时候，包裹进出就像在做生意讲规矩的老英格兰一样容易；可现在，一切都停滞不前了。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Tut! Nonsense, sir!—And, my dear Charles,” said Mr. Lorry, glancing at the House again, “you are to remember, that getting things out of Paris at this present time, no matter what things, is next to an impossibility. Papers and precious matters were this very day brought to us here (I speak in strict confidence; it is not business-like to whisper it, even to you), by the strangest bearers you can imagine, every one of whom had his head hanging on by a single hair as he passed the Barriers. At another time, our parcels would come and go, as easily as in business-like Old England; but now, everything is stopped.”

“哎！胡说八道，先生！——还有，我亲爱的查尔斯，”洛里先生又看了一眼那所房子说，“你要记住，在目前这个时期，要把东西从巴黎带出来，不管是什么东西，几乎是不可能的。今天就有文件和贵重物品被送到了我们这里（我严格保密地说；即使是对你，这样低声细语也不像做生意的样子），每一个携带者都是以命相搏，头颅悬于一线，才通过了关卡。在另一个时候，我们的包裹进出会像在做生意的老英格兰一样容易；但现在，一切都停顿了。”

source: He threw out a gold coin for the valet to pick up, and all the heads craned forward that all the eyes might look down at it as it fell. The tall man called out again with a most unearthly cry, “Dead!”
domestication: 他随手一扔，一枚金币划出一道弧线，落在地上，周围的人纷纷伸长了脖子，瞪大眼睛盯着那枚掉落的金币。那高个子男人又发出一声鬼哭狼嚎般的喊叫：“死了！”

source: The entrance of the Judge, and a consequent great stir and settling down in the court, stopped the dialogue. Presently, the dock became the central point of interest. Two gaolers, who had been standing there, went out, and the prisoner was brought in, and put to the bar.

source: Jacques Three, with his usual craving on him, and evidently disappointed by the dialogue taking a turn that did not seem to promise bloodshed, held by Defarge’s arm as he held by the turnkey’s. Their three heads had been close together during this brief discourse, and it had been as much as they could do to hear one another, even then: so tremendous was the noise of the living ocean, in its irruption into the Fortress, and its inundation of the courts and passages and staircases. All around outside, too, it beat the walls with a deep, hoarse roar, from which, occasionally, some partial shouts of tumult broke and leaped into the air like spray.
domestication: 雅克三世像往常一样，心里痒痒的，显然对这番对话没有朝流血的方向发展感到失望，他抓着德发日的胳膊，而德发日也抓着狱卒的胳膊。三个人的脑袋在这短短的对话中靠得很近，即便如此，他们也费了好大劲才听清彼此的话：活生生的海洋冲进堡垒，淹没庭院、走廊和楼梯，发出的巨响震耳欲聋。外面四下里，这股洪流还不断地撞击着墙壁，发出低沉的吼声，偶尔有几声喧闹的喊叫像浪花一样从这吼声中迸发而出，直冲云霄。
foreignization: 雅克三世带着他惯有的渴望，显然因为对话转向了一个似乎不会引发流血的方向而失望，拉着德法日的胳膊，就像德法日拉着狱卒的胳膊一样。在这段简短的对话期间，他们的三个头靠得很近，即便如此，他们也只能勉强听到彼此说话：因为活生生的海洋冲入堡垒，并淹没庭院、通道和楼梯时发出的噪音是如此之大。在外面，它也以一种深沉的吼声拍打着墙壁，偶尔从这吼声中，一些局部的喧哗声会突破并像浪花一样跃入空中。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Jacques Three, with his usual craving on him, and evidently disappointed by the dialogue taking a turn that did not seem to promise bloodshed, held by Defarge’s arm as he held by the turnkey’s. Their three heads had been close together during this brief discourse, and it had been as much as they could do to hear one another, even then: so tremendous was the noise of the living ocean, in its irruption into the Fortress, and its inundation of the courts and passages and staircases. All around outside, too, it beat the walls with a deep, hoarse roar, from which, occasionally, some partial shouts of tumult broke and leaped into the air like spray.

source: “Yes, dearest Charles,” with her hands on his breast, and the inquiring and attentive expression fixed upon him; “we are rather thoughtful to-night, for we have something on our mind to-night.”

source: He had no idea that this could dwell in the thoughts of his fair young wife; but, when he afterwards joined her in their own rooms, he found her waiting for him with the old pretty lifting of the forehead strongly marked.

source: Never did the sun go down with a brighter glory on the quiet corner in Soho, than one memorable evening when the Doctor and his daughter sat under the plane-tree together. Never did the moon rise with a milder radiance over great London, than on that night when it found them still seated under the tree, and shone upon their faces through its leaves.
domestication: 夕阳西下，阳光洒在索霍区的静谧角落，比往常任何时候都要灿烂夺目。那是个值得铭记的傍晚，医生和他的女儿并肩坐在梧桐树下。月亮升起，柔和的光辉笼罩着整个伦敦城，没有哪个夜晚比得上这一晚。月光透过树叶，洒在他们的脸上，他们依旧坐在树下，享受着这难得的宁静时光。
foreignization: 太阳从未以更加辉煌的光芒在苏活区的这个宁静角落落下，正如一个难忘的傍晚，当医生和他的女儿一起坐在梧桐树下。月亮从未以更加柔和的光辉照耀过伦敦，正如那个夜晚，当它发现他们仍然坐在树下，并透过树叶照耀在他们的脸上。

source: One of the first considerations which arose in the business mind of Mr. Lorry when business hours came round, was this:—that he had no right to imperil Tellson’s by sheltering the wife of an emigrant prisoner under the Bank roof. His own possessions, safety, life, he would have hazarded for Lucie and her child, without a moment’s demur; but the great trust he held was not his own, and as to that business charge he was a strict man of business.
domestication: 一到上班时间，老洛里先生脑子里最先想到的就是：他不能为了庇护一个逃犯的妻子而危及泰勒森银行的安全。自己的身家性命，他可以毫不犹豫地为露西和她的孩子去冒险；但那庞大的信托责任不是他个人的，说到这事儿，他可是个严格办业务的人。
foreignization: 当洛里先生的商业头脑开始考虑业务时，最先出现的一个问题是：他无权在银行屋顶下庇护一个逃亡囚犯的妻子，从而使泰尔森银行冒险。他自己的财产、安全、生命，他会毫不犹豫地为露西和她的孩子冒险；但他所承担的重大信任并不是属于他自己的，至于那个商业职责，他是一个严格的商人。

source: “I understand equally well, that a word from her father in any suitor’s favour, would outweigh herself and all the world. For which reason, Doctor Manette,” said Darnay, modestly but firmly, “I would not ask that word, to save my life.”
domestication: 我明白，她父亲的一句话，就能让任何求婚者胜过她自己和全世界。所以，曼内特医生，我达奈虽然这么说，但我是既谦逊又坚定，绝不会为了保命而求您说这句话。
foreignization: 我同样明白，她父亲对任何一个求婚者说一句话，就会比她自己和全世界都更有分量。因此，曼内特医生，”达奈谦逊但坚定地说，“我不会为了挽救自己的生命而请求那句话。

source: “We will set him above their mercies very soon. I left him climbing to the window, and I came to tell you. There is no one here to see. You may kiss your hand towards that highest shelving roof.”
domestication: 咱们很快就能让他摆脱那些人的折磨了。我留他在那儿往上爬窗子，就赶紧来告诉你了。这里没人，你可以朝着那最高的屋檐挥挥手。
foreignization: “我们很快就会让他受到他们的怜悯。我留下他正在往窗户爬，然后来告诉你。这里没有人看到。你可以向那最高的斜屋顶吻手。”

source: “Pray,” said Mr. Lorry, in a soothing tone, bringing his left hand from the back of the chair to lay it on the supplicatory fingers that clasped him in so violent a tremble: “pray control your agitation—a matter of business. As I was saying—”
domestication: “求您了，”洛里先生语气温和地说着，左手从椅子背后移到了紧抓着他的那双手上，那手抖得厉害，“先冷静下来，这可是正经事。刚才说到哪儿了——”
foreignization: “请,” 洛里先生用安慰的语气说，从椅子背后移开他的左手，放在那些因剧烈颤抖而紧握着他的祈求的手指上：“请控制你的激动——这是一件商务事宜。正如我刚才所说——”

source: A wonderful fact to reflect upon, that every human creature is constituted to be that profound secret and mystery to every other. A solemn consideration, when I enter a great city by night, that every one of those darkly clustered houses encloses its own secret; that every room in every one of them encloses its own secret; that every beating heart in the hundreds of thousands of breasts there, is, in some of its imaginings, a secret to the heart nearest it! Something of the awfulness, even of Death itself, is referable to this. No more can I turn the leaves of this dear book that I loved, and vainly hope in time to read it all. No more can I look into the depths of this unfathomable water, wherein, as momentary lights glanced into it, I have had glimpses of buried treasure and other things submerged. It was appointed that the book should shut with a spring, for ever and for ever, when I had read but a page. It was appointed that the water should be locked in an eternal frost, when the light was playing on its surface, and I stood in ignorance on the shore. My friend is dead, my neighbour is dead, my love, the darling of my soul, is dead; it is the inexorable consolidation and perpetuation of the secret that was always in that individuality, and which I shall carry in mine to my life’s end. In any of the burial-places of this city through which I pass, is there a sleeper more inscrutable than its busy inhabitants are, in their innermost personality, to me, or than I am to them?
domestication: 值得深思的是，每一个人都对其他人而言是一个深藏的秘密和谜团。夜幕降临，我踏入一座大城市时，心中不免涌起一股庄重之感：每一座暗沉的房子里都隐藏着自己的秘密；每个房间里的每颗跳动的心脏，在它那些飘忽不定的幻想中，对最近的心灵来说，都是一个秘密！这甚至让人联想到死亡本身所带来的那种令人敬畏的感觉。我再也不能翻阅这本我心爱的书了，再也不能奢望有朝一日能读完它；我也不能再去探寻那深不可测的水底世界，曾几何时，一缕光线短暂地照入水中，让我瞥见了埋藏的宝藏和其他沉没的物事。命运注定，当我才读了一页，这本书就永远地合上了。也注定了，当阳光在水面嬉戏时，这片水域会永冻封存，而我却只能站在岸边，茫然无知。我的朋友死了，我的邻居死了，我心中的挚爱，灵魂的宝贝，也离我而去；这些秘密原本就存在于他们每一个人身上，现在我将带着这些秘密，一直带到生命尽头。在这座城市中的任何一个墓地中，是否有一个长眠者比我所认识的任何忙碌居民，在他们最深处的人格上，对我更加难以捉摸，或我对于他们而言也同样如此？
foreignization: 一个值得反思的美妙事实是，每一个人都对其他人构成了一个深邃的秘密和神秘。当我夜晚进入一个大城市时，这是一个庄严的思考：那里的每一座聚集的黑暗房屋都包含着它自己的秘密；其中的每一个房间都包含着它自己的秘密；那里的成千上万胸膛中每颗跳动的心，在某些想象中，都是离它最近的心灵中的一个秘密！甚至死亡本身的一些可怕之处也与此有关。我再也不能翻开这本我所爱的书的页码，并徒劳地希望最终能读完它。我再也不能窥探这片深不可测的水域，当光线短暂地照入其中时，我曾瞥见过沉没的宝藏和其他沉入水底的事物。当我只读了一页时，这本书就被注定永远地合上了。当光在水面嬉戏，而我对岸上的知识一无所知时，这片水域被注定要永远冻结。我的朋友死了，我的邻居死了，我心爱的人，我灵魂的挚爱，死了；这是那个个体中始终存在的秘密的无情的凝结和延续，这个秘密我将带入我的生命尽头。在我穿过的这座城市中的任何一个墓地中，是否有一个沉睡者比我对其内在个性的忙碌居民更为难以理解，或者他们对我也是如此？

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

A wonderful fact to reflect upon, that every human creature is constituted to be that profound secret and mystery to every other. A solemn consideration, when I enter a great city by night, that every one of those darkly clustered houses encloses its own secret; that every room in every one of them encloses its own secret; that every beating heart in the hundreds of thousands of breasts there, is, in some of its imaginings, a secret to the heart nearest it! Something of the awfulness, even of Death itself, is referable to this. No more can I turn the leaves of this dear book that I loved, and vainly hope in time to read it all. No more can I look into the depths of this unfathomable water, wherein, as momentary lights glanced into it, I have had glimpses of buried treasure and other things submerged. It was appointed that the book should shut with a spring, for ever and for ever, when I had read but a page. It was appointed that the water should be locked in an eternal frost, when the light was playing on its surface, and I stood in ignorance on the shore. My friend is dead, my neighbour is dead, my love, the darling of my soul, is dead; it is the inexorable consolidation and perpetuation of the secret that was always in that individuality, and which I shall carry in mine to my life’s end. In any of the burial-places of this city through which I pass, is there a sleeper more inscrutable than its busy inhabitants are, in their innermost personality, to me, or than I am to them?

值得深思的是，每一个人都对其他人而言是一个深藏的秘密和谜团。夜幕降临，我踏入一座大城市时，心中不免涌起一股庄重之感：每一座暗沉的房子里都隐藏着自己的秘密；每个房间里的每颗跳动的心脏，在它那些飘忽不定的幻想中，对最近的心灵来说，都是一个秘密！这甚至让人联想到死亡本身所带来的那种令人敬畏的感觉。我再也不能翻阅这本我心爱的书了，再也不能奢望有朝一日能读完它；我也不能再去探寻那深不可测的水底世界，曾几何时，一缕光线短暂地照入水中，让我瞥见了埋藏的宝藏和其他沉没的物事。命运注定，当我才读了一页，这本书就永远地合上了。也注定了，当阳光在水面嬉戏时，这片水域会永冻封存，而我却只能站在岸边，茫然无知。我的朋友死了，我的邻居死了，我心中的挚爱，灵魂的宝贝，也离我而去；这些秘密原本就存在于他们每一个人身上，现在我将带着这些秘密，一直带到生命尽头。在这座城市中的任何一个墓地中，是否有一个长眠者比我所认识的任何忙碌居民，在他们最深处的人格上，对我更加难以捉摸，或我对于他们而言也同样如此？

source: “Come!” said Defarge. “Here is monsieur, who knows a well-made shoe when he sees one. Show him that shoe you are working at. Take it, monsieur.”
domestication: “来！”德法日说，“这位先生可识货，一看就知道鞋子做得好不好。把您手上的那双给他看看。先生，您拿着。”
foreignization: “来！”德发日说。“这位先生一看就知道做工精良的鞋子。给他看看你正在做的那双鞋。先生，请拿好。”
