source: Up the broad flight of shallow steps, Monsieur the Marquis, flambeau preceded, went from his carriage, sufficiently disturbing the darkness to elicit loud remonstrance from an owl in the roof of the great pile of stable building away among the trees. All else was so quiet, that the flambeau carried up the steps, and the other flambeau held at the great door, burnt as if they were in a close room of state, instead of being in the open night-air. Other sound than the owl’s voice there was none, save the falling of a fountain into its stone basin; for, it was one of those dark nights that hold their breath by the hour together, and then heave a long low sigh, and hold their breath again.
domestication: 马车停稳，只见侯爵大人迈着从容的步子，踏上了那宽阔而平缓的台阶。前面有仆人举着火把开路，这一行人足够扰动了夜色，惹得远处树丛中的大堆马厩屋顶上，一只猫头鹰大声抱怨起来。四周一片寂静，静得仿佛火把是在密闭的宫殿里燃烧，而不是在露天地里。除了那只猫头鹰的叫声，就只有喷泉落入石盆的声音，水声潺潺。这夜黑得连空气都似乎凝固了，一连几小时都纹丝不动，偶尔长长地叹一口气，又再次屏住呼吸。
foreignization: 沿着宽阔的浅台阶，马车里的侯爵先生在火把的引导下走了出来，足以扰动黑暗，引得树丛中大堆马厩屋顶上的一只猫头鹰大声抗议。其他一切都如此安静，以至于台阶上的火把和大门处手持的另一支火把仿佛是在密闭的厅堂里燃烧，而不是在露天的夜空中。除了猫头鹰的声音外，没有其他声响，只有喷泉落入石盆中的流水声；因为，这是一个漆黑的夜晚，它一连几小时屏住呼吸，然后发出一声悠长低沉的叹息，再次屏住呼吸。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Up the broad flight of shallow steps, Monsieur the Marquis, flambeau preceded, went from his carriage, sufficiently disturbing the darkness to elicit loud remonstrance from an owl in the roof of the great pile of stable building away among the trees. All else was so quiet, that the flambeau carried up the steps, and the other flambeau held at the great door, burnt as if they were in a close room of state, instead of being in the open night-air. Other sound than the owl’s voice there was none, save the falling of a fountain into its stone basin; for, it was one of those dark nights that hold their breath by the hour together, and then heave a long low sigh, and hold their breath again.
马车停稳，只见侯爵大人迈着从容的步子，踏上了那宽阔而平缓的台阶。前面有仆人举着火把开路，这一行人足够扰动了夜色，惹得远处树丛中的大堆马厩屋顶上，一只猫头鹰大声抱怨起来。四周一片寂静，静得仿佛火把是在密闭的宫殿里燃烧，而不是在露天地里。除了那只猫头鹰的叫声，就只有喷泉落入石盆的声音，水声潺潺。这夜黑得连空气都似乎凝固了，一连几小时都纹丝不动，偶尔长长地叹一口气，又再次屏住呼吸。

source: But not for long. Releasing his arm, she laid her hand upon his shoulder. After looking doubtfully at it, two or three times, as if to be sure that it was really there, he laid down his work, put his hand to his neck, and took off a blackened string with a scrap of folded rag attached to it. He opened this, carefully, on his knee, and it contained a very little quantity of hair: not more than one or two long golden hairs, which he had, in some old day, wound off upon his finger.
domestication: 但这没持续多久。她松开他的胳膊，把手搭在他肩上。他疑疑惑惑地看了好几回，像是要确认那手真的在那儿似的，然后放下手里的活，摸了摸脖子，取下一条系着一块破布的黑绳子。他小心翼翼地在膝盖上打开那块破布，里面只有几根头发，一两根长金色的头发，是他在很久以前缠在手指上的。
foreignization: 但时间不长。她松开他的手臂，把手放在他的肩膀上。在怀疑地看了它两三遍后，似乎是为了确保它真的在那里，他放下手里的活，把手放到脖子上，取下一条变黑的绳子，上面系着一块折叠的破布。他小心翼翼地在膝盖上打开它，里面只有很少的头发：不超过一两根长的金色头发，他在很久以前，曾把它们绕在手指上。

He opened this, carefully, on his knee, and it contained a very little quantity of hair: not more than one or two long golden hairs, which he had, in some old day, wound off upon his finger.

domestication: 念叨什么呢！你这大好人！你这是什么意思，趴在地上对着我祷告？

source: Jerry had just enough forehead to knuckle, and he knuckled it in acknowledgment of this communication and a shilling. Mr. Carton came up at the moment, and touched Mr. Lorry on the arm.

source: His message perplexed his mind to that degree that he was fain, several times, to take off his hat to scratch his head. Except on the crown, which was raggedly bald, he had stiff, black hair, standing jaggedly all over it, and growing down hill almost to his broad, blunt nose. It was so like Smith’s work, so much more like the top of a strongly spiked wall than a head of hair, that the best of players at leap-frog might have declined him, as the most dangerous man in the world to go over.
domestication: 他的消息让他的脑袋乱成一团糟，几次三番地想摘下帽子挠头。除了头顶上那块稀稀拉拉的秃斑外，他满头都是硬邦邦的黑发，竖得乱七八糟，几乎长到了他那宽大的鼻子根部。这头发简直像是史密斯的手艺，更像是个布满尖刺的墙顶，而不是人的头发。就算是最厉害的跳山羊选手，看到他也会打退堂鼓，觉得他是世界上最危险的人，根本不敢从他身上跳过去。
foreignization: 他的信息使他困惑到这种程度，以至于他几次不得不摘下帽子来挠头。除了头顶部分，那里稀疏地秃着，他有一头硬直的黑发，凌乱地竖立着，几乎延伸到他那宽阔、钝平的鼻子上。这头发看起来就像是史密斯的作品，更像是一个尖刺林立的墙顶，而不是一头头发，以至于即使是最好的跳山羊游戏选手也可能拒绝他，认为他是世界上最危险的人去跨越。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

His message perplexed his mind to that degree that he was fain, several times, to take off his hat to scratch his head. Except on the crown, which was raggedly bald, he had stiff, black hair, standing jaggedly all over it, and growing down hill almost to his broad, blunt nose. It was so like Smith’s work, so much more like the top of a strongly spiked wall than a head of hair, that the best of players at leap-frog might have declined him, as the most dangerous man in the world to go over.

source: “This is something new to me, Mr. Lorry. You deliberately advise me not to go up to Soho and offer myself—myself, Stryver of the King’s Bench bar?”
domestication: 老洛里先生，这可是新鲜事了。您居然有意劝我别往苏活区跑，主动献身——献身的可是王座法庭的大律师斯特莱弗我啊？
foreignization: “这是我第一次听说，洛里先生。您故意劝我不要去苏豪区自荐——自荐我自己，王座法庭的斯特莱弗？”

messages_domestication.content:

source: There appearing to be no other door on that floor, and the keeper of the wine-shop going straight to this one when they were left alone, Mr. Lorry asked him in a whisper, with a little anger:
domestication: 那层楼似乎没有其他门了，酒馆老板径直走向这扇门时，洛里先生见四下无人，便带着几分怒气低声问道：
foreignization: 由于那层楼似乎没有其他的门，而酒馆老板在他们被单独留下后径直走向这扇门时，洛里先生带着些许愤怒低声问他：

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

There appearing to be no other door on that floor, and the keeper of the wine-shop going straight to this one when they were left alone, Mr. Lorry asked him in a whisper, with a little anger:
那层楼似乎没有其他门了，酒馆老板径直走向这扇门时，洛里先生见四下无人，便带着几分怒气低声问道：

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

There appearing to be no other door on that floor, and the keeper of the wine-shop going straight to this one when they were left alone, Mr. Lorry asked him in a whisper, with a little anger:
由于那层楼似乎没有其他的门，而酒馆老板在他们被单独留下后径直走向这扇门时，洛里先生带着些许愤怒低声问他：

source: Growling, in addition, such phrases as “Ah! yes! You’re religious, too. You wouldn’t put yourself in opposition to the interests of your husband and child, would you? Not you!” and throwing off other sarcastic sparks from the whirling grindstone of his indignation, Mr. Cruncher betook himself to his boot-cleaning and his general preparation for business. In the meantime, his son, whose head was garnished with tenderer spikes, and whose young eyes stood close by one another, as his father’s did, kept the required watch upon his mother. He greatly disturbed that poor woman at intervals, by darting out of his sleeping closet, where he made his toilet, with a suppressed cry of “You are going to flop, mother. —Halloa, father!” and, after raising this fictitious alarm, darting in again with an undutiful grin.
domestication: 哼哼，杰勒先生还说道：“啊！对了！你也是个有信仰的人。你总不会跟你的丈夫和孩子对着干吧？你可不会这么做！”说着，他愤愤地抛出一句又一句讽刺的话，就像磨盘上飞溅的火星，然后便开始擦靴子，准备做他的生意去了。与此同时，他那头上的尖刺还没那么硬的儿子，两只眼睛跟父亲一样靠得很近，负责监视着母亲。这小子不时从睡觉的小屋子里冲出来，压低声音大喊：“妈妈，你要倒下了——哎呀，爸爸！”然后制造出这么一个假警报，笑嘻嘻地又跑回屋里去，让那个可怜的女人一阵阵地心惊胆战。
foreignization: 咆哮着，此外还说些诸如“啊！是的！你也信教。你不会反对你丈夫和孩子的利益吧，是不是？你可不会！”之类的话，并从他愤怒的旋转磨石上抛出其他讽刺的火花，克伦彻先生着手清理他的靴子并为生意做一般准备。与此同时，他的儿子，头上的刺更柔软，年轻的眼睛像他父亲那样靠得很近，一直照看着他母亲。他不时地从他睡觉的小房间里冲出来，压低声音喊道：“你要倒下了，妈妈。——喂，爸爸！”然后在引起这场虚假警报后，又带着不孝的笑容冲回去。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Growling, in addition, such phrases as “Ah! yes! You’re religious, too. You wouldn’t put yourself in opposition to the interests of your husband and child, would you? Not you!” and throwing off other sarcastic sparks from the whirling grindstone of his indignation, Mr. Cruncher betook himself to his boot-cleaning and his general preparation for business. In the meantime, his son, whose head was garnished with tenderer spikes, and whose young eyes stood close by one another, as his father’s did, kept the required watch upon his mother. He greatly disturbed that poor woman at intervals, by darting out of his sleeping closet, where he made his toilet, with a suppressed cry of “You are going to flop, mother. —Halloa, father!” and, after raising this fictitious alarm, darting in again with an undutiful grin.

source: It would have been of as much avail to interrogate any stone face outside the chateau as to interrogate that face of his. The nephew looked at him, in vain, in passing on to the door.
domestication: 问他跟问城堡外的石头雕像一样白费劲。侄子看了他一眼，白搭，然后就朝门口走去。
foreignization: 向他询问就如同向城堡外的任何一块石头脸询问一样无效。侄子经过时徒劳地看了看他，然后走向门去。

messages_domestication.content:

source: Mr. Lorry glanced at the work in his hand, and observed that it was a shoe of the old size and shape. He took up another that was lying by him, and asked what it was.
domestication: 洛里先生看了看手里的活计，发现是一只老式的老号鞋。他又拿起旁边的一只，问这是什么。
foreignization: 洛里先生瞥了一眼手里的活计，注意到那是一只旧尺寸和形状的鞋。他拿起旁边躺着的另一只，问这是什么。

messages_foreignization.content:

Mr. Lorry glanced at the work in his hand, and observed that it was a shoe of the old size and shape. He took up another that was lying by him, and asked what it was.
洛里先生瞥了一眼手里的活计，注意到那是一只旧尺寸和形状的鞋。他拿起旁边躺着的另一只，问这是什么。

source: At length the jackal had got together a compact repast for the lion, and proceeded to offer it to him. The lion took it with care and caution, made his selections from it, and his remarks upon it, and the jackal assisted both. When the repast was fully discussed, the lion put his hands in his waistband again, and lay down to meditate. The jackal then invigorated himself with a bumper for his throttle, and a fresh application to his head, and applied himself to the collection of a second meal; this was administered to the lion in the same manner, and was not disposed of until the clocks struck three in the morning.
domestication: 狐狸终于为狮子准备了一顿丰盛的晚餐，然后恭恭敬敬地呈了上去。狮子小心翼翼地接过，仔细挑选，边吃边评头论足，狐狸也跟着一起吃喝。吃完这顿大餐后，狮子把手又插回腰带里，躺下开始冥思苦想。狐狸则给自己倒了一杯壮胆的酒，又往头上抹了些提神的东西，然后就开始张罗第二顿饭。这顿饭也照例是献给狮子的，一直吃到半夜三更才结束。
foreignization: 最后，豺狼为狮子准备了一顿丰盛的餐食，并向他提供了这顿饭。狮子小心翼翼地接受了，从中挑选了一些食物，并对此发表了一些评论，而豺狼也一起参与了。当餐食充分讨论完毕后，狮子再次把手插进腰带里，躺下思考。接着，豺狼喝了一大口提神饮料，又在头上擦了些东西，开始着手收集第二顿饭；这顿饭同样以同样的方式提供给狮子，直到凌晨三点钟才处理完毕。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

At length the jackal had got together a compact repast for the lion, and proceeded to offer it to him. The lion took it with care and caution, made his selections from it, and his remarks upon it, and the jackal assisted both. When the repast was fully discussed, the lion put his hands in his waistband again, and lay down to meditate. The jackal then invigorated himself with a bumper for his throttle, and a fresh application to his head, and applied himself to the collection of a second meal; this was administered to the lion in the same manner, and was not disposed of until the clocks struck three in the morning.

source: He embraced her, solemnly commended her to Heaven, and humbly thanked Heaven for having bestowed her on him. By-and-bye, they went into the house.
domestication: 他紧紧拥抱了她，庄重地把她托付给上天，又谦卑地感谢上天把她赐给了自己。随后，两人便进了屋。
foreignization: 他拥抱了她，庄重地将她托付给上天，并谦卑地感谢上天将她赐予他。过了一会儿，他们走进了房子。

domestication: 他坐着，双手插在兜里，两腿伸得老长，眼神盯着炉火。

source: The burst with which the carriage started out of the village and up the rise beyond, was soon checked by the steepness of the hill. Gradually, it subsided to a foot pace, swinging and lumbering upward among the many sweet scents of a summer night. The postilions, with a thousand gossamer gnats circling about them in lieu of the Furies, quietly mended the points to the lashes of their whips; the valet walked by the horses; the courier was audible, trotting on ahead into the dull distance.
domestication: 马车一出村口，便猛地冲上前方的山坡，但很快就被陡峭的山路给压了下来。慢慢地，车速降到了步行的速度，摇摇晃晃地爬坡，四周弥漫着夏日夜晚的各种香气。车夫身旁飞舞着成千上万只轻盈的小虫，像是代替了复仇三女神一样，他们则静静地修理着鞭子上的细绳；随从在马旁步行；信使则在前方不远处，小跑着消失在昏暗中。
foreignization: 马车从村庄出发，向山丘上疾驰，但很快由于山坡的陡峭而减速。渐渐地，它慢到了步行的速度，在夏夜的多种芳香中摇晃着、笨拙地上升。邮差们周围有成千上万的细小飞虫盘旋，仿佛取代了复仇三女神，他们静静地修理着鞭子上的鞭梢；男仆走在马旁边；信使则在前方昏暗的距离中听得见的节奏中轻快地奔跑。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The burst with which the carriage started out of the village and up the rise beyond, was soon checked by the steepness of the hill. Gradually, it subsided to a foot pace, swinging and lumbering upward among the many sweet scents of a summer night. The postilions, with a thousand gossamer gnats circling about them in lieu of the Furies, quietly mended the points to the lashes of their whips; the valet walked by the horses; the courier was audible, trotting on ahead into the dull distance.
马车一出村口，便猛地冲上前方的山坡，但很快就被陡峭的山路给压了下来。慢慢地，车速降到了步行的速度，摇摇晃晃地爬坡，四周弥漫着夏日夜晚的各种香气。车夫身旁飞舞着成千上万只轻盈的小虫，像是代替了复仇三女神一样，他们则静静地修理着鞭子上的细绳；随从在马旁步行；信使则在前方不远处，小跑着消失在昏暗中。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The burst with which the carriage started out of the village and up the rise beyond, was soon checked by the steepness of the hill. Gradually, it subsided to a foot pace, swinging and lumbering upward among the many sweet scents of a summer night. The postilions, with a thousand gossamer gnats circling about them in lieu of the Furies, quietly mended the points to the lashes of their whips; the valet walked by the horses; the courier was audible, trotting on ahead into the dull distance.
马车从村庄出发，向山丘上疾驰，但很快由于山坡的陡峭而减速。渐渐地，它慢到了步行的速度，在夏夜的多种芳香中摇晃着、笨拙地上升。邮差们周围有成千上万的细小飞虫盘旋，仿佛取代了复仇三女神，他们静静地修理着鞭子上的鞭梢；男仆走在马旁边；信使则在前方昏暗的距离中听得见的节奏中轻快地奔跑。

source: “Dearest dear! Can you tell me, this last time, that you feel quite, quite sure, no new affections of mine, and no new duties of mine, will ever interpose between us? I know it well, but do you know it? In your own heart, do you feel quite certain?”
domestication: 最亲爱的！你能告诉我吗，最后一次，你是否真的、真的确定，我的新感情和新责任永远不会成为我们之间的障碍？我心里非常清楚，但你心里有没有数呢？在你自己的心里，你是不是也十分确定？
foreignization: 最亲爱的！你能告诉我，最后一次，你是否完全、完全确定，我的任何新感情和新责任都不会介入我们之间吗？我很清楚，但你知道吗？在你自己的心里，你是否完全确定？

source: “For gracious sake say something else besides ‘indeed,’ or you’ll fidget me to death,” said Miss Pross: whose character (dissociated from stature) was shortness.
domestication: “看在老天的份上，你能不能换个词儿说说，别老是‘的确’，你再这么磨叽我非得被你烦死不可。”普罗斯小姐说道，她的性格（撇开身材不谈）就是直爽。
foreignization: “看在老天的份上，别说‘确实’了，再说你就会把我烦死了，”普罗斯小姐说道；她的性格（不考虑身高的话）是急躁。

source: Accordingly, Mr. Stryver inaugurated the Long Vacation with a formal proposal to take Miss Manette to Vauxhall Gardens; that failing, to Ranelagh; that unaccountably failing too, it behoved him to present himself in Soho, and there declare his noble mind.
domestication: 于是，斯特莱弗先生开始了长假，先是正式邀请曼内特小姐去沃克斯豪尔花园；不成的话，就去拉内拉格；结果这两处都无功而返，他只好来到苏活区，向她表白自己的高尚情操。
foreignization: 于是，斯特莱弗先生以正式提出带马内特小姐去沃克斯豪尔花园开始长假；如果这一提议失败，就去拉纳勒格；如果这一不可理解地也失败了，他就必须在苏豪出现，并在那里宣示他的高尚心灵。

source: Now, from the days when it was always summer in Eden, to these days when it is mostly winter in fallen latitudes, the world of a man has invariably gone one way—Charles Darnay’s way—the way of the love of a woman.
domestication: 从伊甸园里四季如夏的时光，到如今堕落之地寒冬为主的岁月，男人的世界始终沿着一条路走——查尔斯·达内这条爱情之路。
foreignization: 现在，从伊甸园里总是夏天的日子，到堕落纬度大多为冬天的这些日子，男人的世界始终沿着一个方向前进——查尔斯·达内的方式——一个女人的爱的方式。

source: The leprosy of unreality disfigured every human creature in attendance upon Monseigneur. In the outermost room were half a dozen exceptional people who had had, for a few years, some vague misgiving in them that things in general were going rather wrong. As a promising way of setting them right, half of the half-dozen had become members of a fantastic sect of Convulsionists, and were even then considering within themselves whether they should foam, rage, roar, and turn cataleptic on the spot—thereby setting up a highly intelligible finger-post to the Future, for Monseigneur’s guidance. Besides these Dervishes, were other three who had rushed into another sect, which mended matters with a jargon about “the Centre of Truth:” holding that Man had got out of the Centre of Truth—which did not need much demonstration—but had not got out of the Circumference, and that he was to be kept from flying out of the Circumference, and was even to be shoved back into the Centre, by fasting and seeing of spirits. Among these, accordingly, much discoursing with spirits went on—and it did a world of good which never became manifest.
domestication: 不真实的麻风病扭曲了每一位侍奉老爷的人。最外间房里有六七个出类拔萃的人物，他们几年前就开始隐约觉得世道不太对劲。为了纠正这股歪风邪气，六个人中有三个加入了名叫“抽搐派”的荒诞教派，此刻还在盘算着是否应该当场发疯、咆哮、痉挛，以此向老爷指明通向未来的道路。除了这些“托钵僧”外，还有三个人则投身到了另一个教派，这个教派用一堆关于“真理中心”的胡言乱语来修补世道：认为人类已经偏离了真理中心——这一点不需要太多的证明——但还没偏离真理的边缘，因此需要通过禁食和见鬼魂来防止人们飞出边缘，甚至要把人们重新推回真理中心。于是，这些人成天和鬼魂对话——这种做法的好处从未显现过。
foreignization: 麻风般的虚幻扭曲了在座的每一位大人身边的人。在外室有六位杰出人物，他们有少数几年的时间里，内心深处有一种模糊的不安，觉得世事总体上正逐渐走向错误的方向。作为纠正这种状况的一种有希望的方法，这六人中的一半加入了荒诞的痉挛教派，甚至正在考虑是否应该当场吐沫、狂怒、咆哮，并陷入僵直状态——这将为未来树立一个极为明确的路标，供大人指引。除了这些托钵僧之外，还有另外三人，他们冲入了另一个教派，该教派用“真理中心”的行话来修补世事：认为人类已经偏离了真理中心——这一点无需过多证明——但尚未脱离圆周，需要通过禁食和见灵来防止人类飞出圆周，甚至要将其推回中心。因此，在这些人中间，与灵界的大量交流持续进行——它带来的益处从未显现。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The leprosy of unreality disfigured every human creature in attendance upon Monseigneur. In the outermost room were half a dozen exceptional people who had had, for a few years, some vague misgiving in them that things in general were going rather wrong. As a promising way of setting them right, half of the half-dozen had become members of a fantastic sect of Convulsionists, and were even then considering within themselves whether they should foam, rage, roar, and turn cataleptic on the spot—thereby setting up a highly intelligible finger-post to the Future, for Monseigneur’s guidance. Besides these Dervishes, were other three who had rushed into another sect, which mended matters with a jargon about “the Centre of Truth:” holding that Man had got out of the Centre of Truth—which did not need much demonstration—but had not got out of the Circumference, and that he was to be kept from flying out of the Circumference, and was even to be shoved back into the Centre, by fasting and seeing of spirits. Among these, accordingly, much discoursing with spirits went on—and it did a world of good which never became manifest.

source: Thus, the rustling of an Angel’s wings got blended with the other echoes, and they were not wholly of earth, but had in them that breath of Heaven. Sighs of the winds that blew over a little garden-tomb were mingled with them also, and both were audible to Lucie, in a hushed murmur—like the breathing of a summer sea asleep upon a sandy shore—as the little Lucie, comically studious at the task of the morning, or dressing a doll at her mother’s footstool, chattered in the tongues of the Two Cities that were blended in her life.
domestication: 于是，天使翅膀的轻响与其它回声交织在一起，这些声音并非完全来自人间，其中蕴含着天上的气息。微风吹过小小墓园的声音也混杂其中，这两种声音都传入了露西的耳中，如同夏日海边轻柔的呼吸，在沙滩上静静流淌。小露西或是在早晨认认真真地做功课，或是在妈妈脚边给洋娃娃换衣服，嘴里叽叽喳喳地说着两个城市的话，这两个城市在她的生活中交织在一起。
foreignization: 因此，天使翅膀的沙沙声与其它回声融为一体，这些声音并非完全属于尘世，而是带有天国的气息。吹过一个小花园墓地的风的叹息也与它们混在一起，两种声音都以低沉的低语传入露西的耳中——就像夏日大海在沙滩上沉睡时的呼吸——而小露西，滑稽地专注于早晨的任务，或在她母亲的脚凳上给洋娃娃穿衣，用融入她生活的两座城市的语言喋喋不休。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Thus, the rustling of an Angel’s wings got blended with the other echoes, and they were not wholly of earth, but had in them that breath of Heaven. Sighs of the winds that blew over a little garden-tomb were mingled with them also, and both were audible to Lucie, in a hushed murmur—like the breathing of a summer sea asleep upon a sandy shore—as the little Lucie, comically studious at the task of the morning, or dressing a doll at her mother’s footstool, chattered in the tongues of the Two Cities that were blended in her life.
于是，天使翅膀的轻响与其它回声交织在一起，这些声音并非完全来自人间，其中蕴含着天上的气息。微风吹过小小墓园的声音也混杂其中，这两种声音都传入了露西的耳中，如同夏日海边轻柔的呼吸，在沙滩上静静流淌。小露西或是在早晨认认真真地做功课，或是在妈妈脚边给洋娃娃换衣服，嘴里叽叽喳喳地说着两个城市的话，这两个城市在她的生活中交织在一起。

source: The people closed round, and looked at Monsieur the Marquis. There was nothing revealed by the many eyes that looked at him but watchfulness and eagerness; there was no visible menacing or anger. Neither did the people say anything; after the first cry, they had been silent, and they remained so. The voice of the submissive man who had spoken, was flat and tame in its extreme submission. Monsieur the Marquis ran his eyes over them all, as if they had been mere rats come out of their holes.
domestication: 人们围了上来，盯着马奎斯先生看。众人的眼神里，除了警惕和好奇，没有半点威胁或愤怒的迹象。人群也没有说话，自从一开始的喊叫后，就一直沉默着。那个开口说话的人声音低沉而温顺，卑微到了极点。马奎斯先生扫视着周围的人，仿佛他们只是些从洞里钻出来的小老鼠。
foreignization: 人们围了上来，看着马尔基斯先生。许多眼睛盯着他看，但除了警惕和热切之外，没有流露出任何威胁或愤怒。人们也没有说什么；在最初的呼喊之后，他们一直保持沉默，继续沉默着。那个卑微说话人的声音，在极度顺从中显得平淡而温驯。马尔基斯先生用眼睛扫视了所有人，仿佛他们只是从洞里钻出来的一些老鼠。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The people closed round, and looked at Monsieur the Marquis. There was nothing revealed by the many eyes that looked at him but watchfulness and eagerness; there was no visible menacing or anger. Neither did the people say anything; after the first cry, they had been silent, and they remained so. The voice of the submissive man who had spoken, was flat and tame in its extreme submission. Monsieur the Marquis ran his eyes over them all, as if they had been mere rats come out of their holes.
人们围了上来，盯着马奎斯先生看。众人的眼神里，除了警惕和好奇，没有半点威胁或愤怒的迹象。人群也没有说话，自从一开始的喊叫后，就一直沉默着。那个开口说话的人声音低沉而温顺，卑微到了极点。马奎斯先生扫视着周围的人，仿佛他们只是些从洞里钻出来的小老鼠。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The people closed round, and looked at Monsieur the Marquis. There was nothing revealed by the many eyes that looked at him but watchfulness and eagerness; there was no visible menacing or anger. Neither did the people say anything; after the first cry, they had been silent, and they remained so. The voice of the submissive man who had spoken, was flat and tame in its extreme submission. Monsieur the Marquis ran his eyes over them all, as if they had been mere rats come out of their holes.
人们围了上来，看着马尔基斯先生。许多眼睛盯着他看，但除了警惕和热切之外，没有流露出任何威胁或愤怒。人们也没有说什么；在最初的呼喊之后，他们一直保持沉默，继续沉默着。那个卑微说话人的声音，在极度顺从中显得平淡而温驯。马尔基斯先生用眼睛扫视了所有人，仿佛他们只是从洞里钻出来的一些老鼠。

source: “Our family; our honourable family, whose honour is of so much account to both of us, in such different ways. Even in my father’s time, we did a world of wrong, injuring every human creature who came between us and our pleasure, whatever it was. Why need I speak of my father’s time, when it is equally yours? Can I separate my father’s twin-brother, joint inheritor, and next successor, from himself?”
domestication: 我们家，我们这个有头有脸的家族，这份体面对我们俩来说都至关重要，只是我们看重的方式不同罢了。就是在我父亲那辈，咱们也没少干伤天害理的事，谁要是挡了我们的路，不管为了什么享乐，咱们就绝不会手软。何必单说父亲那会儿的事，那些事你我也一样清楚。我能把我父亲的同胞兄弟、共同继承人和接班人跟他分开吗？
foreignization: 我们的家族；我们可敬的家族，其荣誉对我们两人来说都如此重要，尽管方式不同。即使在我父亲的时代，我们做了许多坏事，伤害了每一个挡在我们享受之间的人，无论那是什么快乐。为什么我要提到我父亲的时代，当这同样也是你的时代？我能把我父亲的孪生兄弟、共同继承人和下一顺位继承人与他自己分开吗？

Can I separate my father’s twin-brother, joint inheritor, and next successor, from himself?”
我们家，我们这个有头有脸的家族，这份体面对我们俩来说都至关重要，只是我们看重的方式不同罢了。就是在我父亲那辈，咱们也没少干伤天害理的事，谁要是挡了我们的路，不管为了什么享乐，咱们就绝不会手软。何必单说父亲那会儿的事，那些事你我也一样清楚。我能把我父亲的同胞兄弟、共同继承人和接班人跟他分开吗？

source: Very orderly and methodical he looked, with a hand on each knee, and a loud watch ticking a sonorous sermon under his flapped waist-coat, as though it pitted its gravity and longevity against the levity and evanescence of the brisk fire. He had a good leg, and was a little vain of it, for his brown stockings fitted sleek and close, and were of a fine texture; his shoes and buckles, too, though plain, were trim. He wore an odd little sleek crisp flaxen wig, setting very close to his head: which wig, it is to be presumed, was made of hair, but which looked far more as though it were spun from filaments of silk or glass. His linen, though not of a fineness in accordance with his stockings, was as white as the tops of the waves that broke upon the neighbouring beach, or the specks of sail that glinted in the sunlight far at sea. A face habitually suppressed and quieted, was still lighted up under the quaint wig by a pair of moist bright eyes that it must have cost their owner, in years gone by, some pains to drill to the composed and reserved expression of Tellson’s Bank. He had a healthy colour in his cheeks, and his face, though lined, bore few traces of anxiety. But, perhaps the confidential bachelor clerks in Tellson’s Bank were principally occupied with the cares of other people; and perhaps second-hand cares, like second-hand clothes, come easily off and on.
domestication: 他显得十分井井有条，两手放在膝盖上，翻领马甲下，一只大怀表滴答作响，仿佛在对那活跃的火苗讲述一则庄重的长篇大论，似乎是在用自己的沉稳和长久来对抗火焰的轻浮与短暂。他的腿脚好得很，对此还有点儿沾沾自喜，因为他穿的棕色袜子贴身又光滑，质地极佳；鞋子和鞋扣虽然简单，但很整洁。头上戴了一顶小巧光亮的亚麻假发，紧贴着脑袋。这顶假发虽说是用头发做的，却看起来更像是用丝线或玻璃纤维织成的。他的衬衫虽然没有袜子那么讲究，但洁白如邻近海滩上波浪的泡沫，或是远处海面上在阳光中闪烁的帆点。一张平时总是克制而平静的脸，在那古怪的假发下，还有一双湿润明亮的眼睛，那一定是费了好一番功夫才训练出泰隆银行所需的那种镇定和内敛的表情。他的脸颊红润健康，尽管脸上有些皱纹，但焦虑的痕迹却不多。不过，也许泰隆银行里的那些单身职员们主要操心的是别人的事；二手的烦恼，就像二手衣服一样，穿脱起来都容易得多。
foreignization: 他看起来非常有条理和方法，每只手放在一个膝盖上，一件翻领的背心下，一只响亮的怀表在发出洪亮的嘀嗒声，仿佛以其庄重和长久对抗着活泼火焰的轻浮和短暂。他有一条好腿，并且对此有点自傲，因为他的棕色长袜贴身光滑，质地优良；他的鞋子和鞋扣虽然朴素，但很整洁。他戴着一顶奇特的小巧平滑的亚麻色假发，紧贴在头上：这顶假发，尽管可以假设是由头发制成的，但看起来更像是由丝绸或玻璃丝纺成的。他的亚麻衣物虽然不如长袜那样精细，但像附近海滩上波浪的顶端或远处阳光下闪烁的帆点一样白。一张平时被压抑和平静的脸，在那古怪的假发下，仍被一对湿润明亮的眼睛照亮，这些眼睛一定让它们的主人在过去花费了不少功夫才训练出与泰隆银行相称的镇定和矜持的表情。他的脸颊上有健康的肤色，尽管脸上有些皱纹，但却很少显露出忧虑。不过，也许泰隆银行里的那些机密单身职员主要忙于处理别人的事情；而二手的烦恼，就像二手的衣服一样，容易穿上也容易脱下。

source: “I thought he was rather a handsome fellow, and I thought I should have been much the same sort of fellow, if I had had any luck.”
domestication: 我觉得他挺帅的，心想我要是运气好点，大概也能像他那样。
foreignization: “我认为他相当英俊，而且我想如果我有好运的话，我也会是那样的人。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I thought he was rather a handsome fellow, and I thought I should have been much the same sort of fellow, if I had had any luck.”
我觉得他挺帅的，心想我要是运气好点，大概也能像他那样。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I thought he was rather a handsome fellow, and I thought I should have been much the same sort of fellow, if I had had any luck.”
“我认为他相当英俊，而且我想如果我有好运的话，我也会是那样的人。”

source: “You are a cursed emigrant,” cried a farrier, making at him in a furious manner through the press, hammer in hand; “and you are a cursed aristocrat!”
domestication: “你这该死的逃荒者！”一个铁匠挥舞着锤子，从人群中冲过来，怒气冲冲地吼道，“你这该死的贵族！”
foreignization: “你是一个该死的移民，”一个铁匠穿过人群向他冲去，手中挥舞着锤子，狂怒地喊道；“你是一个该死的贵族！”

source: “To me, women!” cried madame his wife. “What! We can kill as well as the men when the place is taken!” And to her, with a shrill thirsty cry, trooping women variously armed, but all armed alike in hunger and revenge.
domestication: “给我上，女人们！”夫人一声怒吼。“什么！攻下城后，我们女人也能杀人！”她话音未落，一群女人蜂拥而至，个个手持不同武器，但都怀着同样的饥饿和复仇之心。
foreignization: “向我这儿来，妇女们！”他的妻子喊道。“什么！我们也能像男人一样杀人，等这个地方被占领的时候！”于是，一群妇女，武器各异，但同样都怀着饥饿和复仇之心，响应她的尖锐而渴望的呼喊，向她聚集过来。

“向我这儿来，妇女们！”他的妻子喊道。“什么！我们也能像男人一样杀人，等这个地方被占领的时候！”于是，一群妇女，武器各异，但同样都怀着饥饿和复仇之心，响应她的尖锐而渴望的呼喊，向她聚集过来。

source: Mr. Solicitor-General then, following his leader’s lead, examined the patriot: John Barsad, gentleman, by name. The story of his pure soul was exactly what Mr. Attorney-General had described it to be—perhaps, if it had a fault, a little too exactly. Having released his noble bosom of its burden, he would have modestly withdrawn himself, but that the wigged gentleman with the papers before him, sitting not far from Mr. Lorry, begged to ask him a few questions. The wigged gentleman sitting opposite, still looking at the ceiling of the court.
domestication: 随后，总检察长的副手也照着老大的样子，审问起了这位爱国者：约翰·巴萨德，一位绅士。他的灵魂多么纯洁，故事讲得跟总检察长说的一模一样——要说有啥不好，就是太一模一样了。他本来已经倾诉完自己高尚的心声，想谦虚地退下，可是一位坐在洛里先生不远处、面前摆着文件的戴假发的先生却请求问他几个问题。对面坐着的另一位戴假发的先生，还在盯着法庭的天花板看。
foreignization: 然后，王室副检察官按照他的领导的指示，审问了爱国者：约翰·巴塞德先生，一位绅士。他的纯洁灵魂的故事正是总检察长所描述的那样——或许，如果说有什么缺点的话，那就是太过准确了。在卸下了他高贵胸膛的重负后，他本想谦逊地退下，但是一位坐在离劳瑞先生不远、面前放着文件的戴假发的绅士请求问他几个问题。对面坐着的另一位戴假发的绅士仍在看着法庭的天花板。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Mr. Solicitor-General then, following his leader’s lead, examined the patriot: John Barsad, gentleman, by name. The story of his pure soul was exactly what Mr. Attorney-General had described it to be—perhaps, if it had a fault, a little too exactly. Having released his noble bosom of its burden, he would have modestly withdrawn himself, but that the wigged gentleman with the papers before him, sitting not far from Mr. Lorry, begged to ask him a few questions. The wigged gentleman sitting opposite, still looking at the ceiling of the court.
随后，总检察长的副手也照着老大的样子，审问起了这位爱国者：约翰·巴萨德，一位绅士。他的灵魂多么纯洁，故事讲得跟总检察长说的一模一样——要说有啥不好，就是太一模一样了。他本来已经倾诉完自己高尚的心声，想谦虚地退下，可是一位坐在洛里先生不远处、面前摆着文件的戴假发的先生却请求问他几个问题。对面坐着的另一位戴假发的先生，还在盯着法庭的天花板看。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Mr. Solicitor-General then, following his leader’s lead, examined the patriot: John Barsad, gentleman, by name. The story of his pure soul was exactly what Mr. Attorney-General had described it to be—perhaps, if it had a fault, a little too exactly. Having released his noble bosom of its burden, he would have modestly withdrawn himself, but that the wigged gentleman with the papers before him, sitting not far from Mr. Lorry, begged to ask him a few questions. The wigged gentleman sitting opposite, still looking at the ceiling of the court.
然后，王室副检察官按照他的领导的指示，审问了爱国者：约翰·巴塞德先生，一位绅士。他的纯洁灵魂的故事正是总检察长所描述的那样——或许，如果说有什么缺点的话，那就是太过准确了。在卸下了他高贵胸膛的重负后，他本想谦逊地退下，但是一位坐在离劳瑞先生不远、面前放着文件的戴假发的绅士请求问他几个问题。对面坐着的另一位戴假发的绅士仍在看着法庭的天花板。

source: “Ah! most gracious Monsieur heretofore the Marquis, where is that emigrant? I cry in my sleep where is he? I demand of Heaven, will he not come to deliver me? No answer. Ah Monsieur heretofore the Marquis, I send my desolate cry across the sea, hoping it may perhaps reach your ears through the great bank of Tilson known at Paris!
domestication: 哎呀！好心的先生，以前的侯爵大人，那个流亡者去哪儿了？我在梦中哭喊，他在哪儿？我向天祈求，他难道不来救我吗？可是一点回音都没有。啊，先生，以前的侯爵大人，我这孤苦伶仃的呼喊声，越过重洋，但愿能通过巴黎的泰尔森银行传到您的耳中！
foreignization: 啊！最仁慈的先生，先前的侯爵，那个流亡者在哪里？我在睡梦中呼喊他在哪里？我向天堂祈求，他难道不会来解救我吗？没有回答。啊，先前的侯爵先生，我将我这孤独的呼喊越洋发送，希望它或许能通过在巴黎知名的蒂尔森大银行传到您的耳中！

foreignization: “不。这一直对她隐瞒着，我希望永远对她保密。这只有我自己和另一个可以信任的人知道。”

source: “I don’t know, Mr. Darnay; I told you it was a foolish fancy, but you asked for it. When I have yielded myself to it, I have been alone, and then I have imagined them the footsteps of the people who are to come into my life, and my father’s.”
domestication: 我不知道，达内先生；我跟您说过这是个傻念头，可您偏要问。每当我沉溺其中时，我就独自一人，然后就想象那是将要走进我和父亲生活的那些人的脚步声。
foreignization: “我不知道，达奈先生；我告诉过你这是一个愚蠢的幻想，但你却问起了它。当我屈从于这个幻想时，我总是独自一人，然后我就想象那是将要进入我和我父亲生活的那些人的脚步声。”

source: “It is of the utmost importance to me (you know, Citizen, even better than I, of how much importance), that I should be able to communicate to Mr. Lorry of Tellson’s Bank, an English gentleman who is now in Paris, the simple fact, without comment, that I have been thrown into the prison of La Force. Will you cause that to be done for me?”
domestication: 这事儿对我来说至关重要（你比我还清楚，公民，这有多重要），我得把一件简单的事情告诉泰勒森银行的劳瑞先生，他现在在巴黎。就是说，我已经被关进了拉弗斯监狱。您能帮我把这个消息传给他吗？
foreignization: “对我来说非常重要（你知道，公民，甚至比我更清楚这一点的重要性），我能够将这一简单的事实，不加评论地告知泰勒森银行的劳瑞先生，他现在在巴黎，即我已经被投入了拉弗斯监狱。您能否替我办到这件事？”

Will you cause that to be done for me?”

source: Yet, this Mr. Carton took in more of the details of the scene than he appeared to take in; for now, when Miss Manette’s head dropped upon her father’s breast, he was the first to see it, and to say audibly: “Officer! look to that young lady. Help the gentleman to take her out. Don’t you see she will fall!”
domestication: 可这位卡顿先生却比表面上看起来更留意现场的细节。这不，当曼内特小姐的头低垂到她父亲胸前时，他第一个发现了，大声喊道：“警官！快看看那位小姐，帮那位先生把她扶出去。没看见她要晕倒了吗！”
foreignization: 然而，这个卡顿先生比他表现出来的更加注意到了现场的细节；因为此刻，当马内特小姐的头倒在她父亲的胸前时，他是第一个看到并大声说道：“警官！看看那位年轻女士。帮那位先生把她扶出去。你没看见她要倒下了吗！”

source: He had suddenly started up, with his hand to his head. His manner and his look quite terrified them all.
domestication: 他突然一跃而起，手捂着头。这模样和神情把大家都吓坏了。
foreignization: 他突然站起来，手扶着头。他的举止和表情把他们都吓坏了。

source: Cramped in all kinds of dim cupboards and hutches at Tellson’s, the oldest of men carried on the business gravely. When they took a young man into Tellson’s London house, they hid him somewhere till he was old. They kept him in a dark place, like a cheese, until he had the full Tellson flavour and blue-mould upon him. Then only was he permitted to be seen, spectacularly poring over large books, and casting his breeches and gaiters into the general weight of the establishment.
domestication: 在泰勒森银行，各种昏暗的柜子和小房间里，最年长的老人们严肃地经营着这家最古老的银行。每当他们招进一个年轻人，就会把他藏在某个地方，直到他变老。他们像藏奶酪一样把他放在黑暗的地方，直到他完全有了泰勒森的味道，身上长满了岁月的“蓝霉”。只有到那时，他才能被允许展示出来，摆出一副认真研读大书的样子，穿着长裤和护腿，给整个银行增添分量。
foreignization: 在泰勒森的各种昏暗的柜子和小屋里，年纪最大的人们庄重地经营着业务。当他们把一个年轻人招进泰勒森的伦敦分行时，他们会把他藏在某个地方直到他变老。他们把他放在一个黑暗的地方，像奶酪一样，直到他有了完整的泰勒森风味和蓝色霉菌。只有到那时，才允许他被看到，壮观地翻阅大书，并把他穿着的马裤和绑腿投入到公司的总体分量中。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

When they took a young man into Tellson’s London house, they hid him somewhere till he was old. They kept him in a dark place, like a cheese, until he had the full Tellson flavour and blue-mould upon him. Then only was he permitted to be seen, spectacularly poring over large books, and casting his breeches and gaiters into the general weight of the establishment.

source: Who could that be with Mr. Lorry—the owner of the riding-coat upon the chair—who must not be seen? From whom newly arrived, did he come out, agitated and surprised, to take his favourite in his arms? To whom did he appear to repeat her faltering words, when, raising his voice and turning his head towards the door of the room from which he had issued, he said: “Removed to the Conciergerie, and summoned for to-morrow?”
domestication: 和洛里先生一起的那个人是谁呢？他穿着那件马甲，还不能让人看见。刚到的是谁，让他这么激动又惊讶，出来时还搂着他的宝贝儿？他对谁复述她断断续续的话，提高声音，转头朝着他出来的房间门口说：“转移到候审所了，还传唤明天？”
foreignization: 谁会是和洛里先生在一起的那个人——椅子上披风的主人——不能被看见的人？他刚从哪里来，带着激动和惊讶走出来，把他心爱的人抱在怀里？当他说：“转移到了看守所，并传唤到明天？”时，他是向谁重复她断断续续的话，同时提高声音并转头朝向他出来的房间的门？

source: “Indeed, sir? That was before my time here, sir. Before our people’s time here, sir. The George was in other hands at that time, sir.”
domestication: 真的吗，先生？那是在我来这儿之前的事了，先生。也是我们这些人来之前的事，先生。那时候乔治旅店是别人在经营，先生。
foreignization: “确实，先生？那是在我来这儿之前，先生。在我们的人来这儿之前，先生。那时乔治旅店是在别人手中，先生。”

source: The spy, well used to his business, did not change his unconscious attitude, but drained his little glass of cognac, took a sip of fresh water, and asked for another glass of cognac. Madame Defarge poured it out for him, took to her knitting again, and hummed a little song over it.
domestication: 这间谍干这行久了，老练得很，表面丝毫没露出破绽，一口喝光了小杯里的白兰地，又喝了口水，接着又要了一杯白兰地。德发日太太给他倒上，重新拿起针线活，还哼起了小曲儿。
foreignization: 这个间谍对自己的业务早已驾轻就熟，没有改变他的无意识态度，而是喝干了小杯白兰地，喝了口水，又要求再来一杯白兰地。德发日太太给他倒了酒，再次拿起她的针线活，边做边哼着小曲。

source: A tall man in a nightcap had caught up a bundle from among the feet of the horses, and had laid it on the basement of the fountain, and was down in the mud and wet, howling over it like a wild animal.

“呃，好！”德法日太太说着，扬起眉毛，一副冷静的生意人模样。“有必要把他登记下来。他们叫那男人什么名字？”

source: He had laid aside his coat and waistcoat; his shirt was open at the throat, as it used to be when he did that work; and even the old haggard, faded surface of face had come back to him. He worked hard—impatiently—as if in some sense of having been interrupted.
domestication: 他脱下了外套和背心，衬衫领口也敞开着，就像以前干活儿时那样；连那张憔悴苍老的脸也恢复了原样。他干得特别卖力，急不可耐的样子，好像觉得自己的工作被打断了一样。
foreignization: 他脱下了外套和背心；衬衫领口敞开，就像他干那活儿时的样子；甚至那张憔悴、苍老的脸色也回到了他身上。他工作得很卖力——不耐烦地——仿佛有一种被中断的感觉。

source: When the mail got successfully to Dover, in the course of the forenoon, the head drawer at the Royal George Hotel opened the coach-door as his custom was. He did it with some flourish of ceremony, for a mail journey from London in winter was an achievement to congratulate an adventurous traveller upon.
domestication: 邮件在上午顺利抵达多佛后，皇家乔治酒店的头号服务员像往常一样打开了马车门。他做这事时还带点仪式感，毕竟冬天从伦敦一路赶来，对这些敢于冒险的旅客来说，真是一件值得恭喜的事。

source: They fished with a spade, at first. Presently the honoured parent appeared to be adjusting some instrument like a great corkscrew. Whatever tools they worked with, they worked hard, until the awful striking of the church clock so terrified Young Jerry, that he made off, with his hair as stiff as his father’s.
domestication: 一开始他们用铲子钓鱼。不一会儿，老爹出现了，好像在摆弄个大螺旋钻一样的玩意儿。不管用什么工具，他们都干得很卖力，直到教堂的钟声一响，把小杰里吓得不轻，头发都竖得跟老爸一样硬了，他拔腿就跑。
foreignization: 他们最初是用铲子钓鱼的。不久，那位受人尊敬的家长似乎在调整一个像大瓶塞钻那样的仪器。无论他们使用什么工具，都干得很卖力，直到教堂的钟声可怕地敲响，吓得小杰里逃走了，他的头发和他的父亲一样硬邦邦的。

source: “I must do, to live, what others of my countrymen, even with nobility at their backs, may have to do some day—work.”
domestication: 为了活着，我必须去做那些事，即便是有贵族撑腰的同乡们，将来有一天也得去做的——劳动。
foreignization: “我必须去做，为了生存，那些我的同胞，即使有贵族支持，也许有一天也不得不去做的事情——工作。”

source: “Believe it, love! Indeed it is so. Consider how natural and how plain it is, my dear, that it should be so. You, devoted and young, cannot fully appreciate the anxiety I have felt that your life should not be wasted—”
domestication: 信我吧，亲爱的！真的就是这样。你想想，这多自然，多合情合理啊，宝贝。你年轻又一心一意，根本体会不到我有多担心你会虚度光阴——
foreignization: 相信吧，亲爱的！确实如此。想想看，我亲爱的，这是多么自然、多么简单的事。你这样忠诚而年轻，无法完全理解我对你的一生不要虚度的担忧——

source: Rendered in a manner desperate, by her state and by the beckoning of their conductor, he drew over his neck the arm that shook upon his shoulder, lifted her a little, and hurried her into the room. He sat her down just within the door, and held her, clinging to him.
domestication: 她当时的情形加上领路人的催促，他急得像热锅上的蚂蚁，一把搂过搭在他肩上的那只颤抖的手臂，将她稍稍抱起，匆匆忙忙地进了房间。一进门，他就让她坐了下来，紧紧地拥着她，生怕她再倒下。
foreignization: 以一种绝望的方式，由于她的状态和他们的向导的召唤，他把那条在他肩上颤抖的手臂搭到自己的脖子上，把她稍微抱起一些，匆匆地把她带进了房间。他把她放在门边，紧紧地抱着她。

source: What winds conveyed this hurry to the grizzled mender of roads, already at work on the hill-top beyond the village, with his day’s dinner (not much to carry) lying in a bundle that it was worth no crow’s while to peck at, on a heap of stones? Had the birds, carrying some grains of it to a distance, dropped one over him as they sow chance seeds? Whether or no, the mender of roads ran, on the sultry morning, as if for his life, down the hill, knee-high in dust, and never stopped till he got to the fountain.
domestication: 是什么风把这个急匆匆的消息吹到了村外山顶上修路的老头那儿？他已经在那儿干活了，午饭就带了一点，包在布里，放在一堆石头上，连乌鸦都不屑去啄。是不是有鸟儿叼着饭粒飞远了，不小心掉在他头上，就像播下了一颗偶然的种子？不管怎样，这修路的老头在炎热的早晨，仿佛拼命一般，从山上往山下跑，尘土没过了膝盖，直到跑到泉边才停下。
foreignization: 什么风把这股急促传给了那位已在村外山顶上修路的白发老人，他的午餐（带的东西不多）放在一堆石头上的一个包裹里，连乌鸦都不屑啄食？是不是鸟儿们把其中的一些谷粒带到远处时，有一粒掉在他身上，就像它们播撒偶然的种子一样？不管怎样，修路人在闷热的早晨，好像在拼命逃跑一样，从山上跑下来，尘土没到膝盖，直到跑到喷泉边才停下来。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

What winds conveyed this hurry to the grizzled mender of roads, already at work on the hill-top beyond the village, with his day’s dinner (not much to carry) lying in a bundle that it was worth no crow’s while to peck at, on a heap of stones? Had the birds, carrying some grains of it to a distance, dropped one over him as they sow chance seeds? Whether or no, the mender of roads ran, on the sultry morning, as if for his life, down the hill, knee-high in dust, and never stopped till he got to the fountain.

source: I have saved him.” It was not another of the dreams in which he had often come back; he was really here. And yet his wife trembled, and a vague but heavy fear was upon her.
domestication: “我救了他。”这回不是那些他常常回来的梦了，他是真的在这儿了。可她还是打起了哆嗦，心里头升起了股说不出的沉重感。
foreignization: “我已经救了他。”这并不是他常常回来的那种梦；他真的在这里。然而他的妻子颤抖着，一种模糊但沉重的恐惧笼罩着她。

source: Having thus given his parent God speed, young Jerry seated himself on the stool, entered on his reversionary interest in the straw his father had been chewing, and cogitated.
domestication: 年轻人杰里跟父亲道了声珍重，便一屁股坐在凳子上，接过了父亲嚼剩的稻草，开始琢磨起来。
foreignization: 这样向他的父亲道了平安之后，小杰里便坐在凳子上，继承了他父亲嚼过的稻草的利益，并开始沉思。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Having thus given his parent God speed, young Jerry seated himself on the stool, entered on his reversionary interest in the straw his father had been chewing, and cogitated.
年轻人杰里跟父亲道了声珍重，便一屁股坐在凳子上，接过了父亲嚼剩的稻草，开始琢磨起来。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Having thus given his parent God speed, young Jerry seated himself on the stool, entered on his reversionary interest in the straw his father had been chewing, and cogitated.
这样向他的父亲道了平安之后，小杰里便坐在凳子上，继承了他父亲嚼过的稻草的利益，并开始沉思。

source: “And has left me,” answered the nephew, “bound to a system that is frightful to me, responsible for it, but powerless in it; seeking to execute the last request of my dear mother’s lips, and obey the last look of my dear mother’s eyes, which implored me to have mercy and to redress; and tortured by seeking assistance and power in vain.”
domestication: “把我给害了，”外甥回答说，“绑在了一个让我害怕的系统上，要对它负责，却又无力改变；想完成母亲临终前最后的愿望，遵从她眼中最后的恳求，希望我行善举，纠正错误；可四处求助，却屡屡碰壁，求告无门，真是痛苦不堪。”
foreignization: “并且抛弃了我，”侄子回答道，“束缚在一个令我恐惧的体系中，对此负责，却无力改变；努力执行我亲爱的母亲临终时最后的请求，遵从她临终时最后的眼神，那眼神祈求我宽恕并纠正；却因徒劳地寻求帮助和支持而备受折磨。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“And has left me,” answered the nephew, “bound to a system that is frightful to me, responsible for it, but powerless in it; seeking to execute the last request of my dear mother’s lips, and obey the last look of my dear mother’s eyes, which implored me to have mercy and to redress; and tortured by seeking assistance and power in vain.”
“把我给害了，”外甥回答说，“绑在了一个让我害怕的系统上，要对它负责，却又无力改变；想完成母亲临终前最后的愿望，遵从她眼中最后的恳求，希望我行善举，纠正错误；可四处求助，却屡屡碰壁，求告无门，真是痛苦不堪。”

“And has left me,” answered the nephew, “bound to a system that is frightful to me, responsible for it, but powerless in it; seeking to execute the last request of my dear mother’s lips, and obey the last look of my dear mother’s eyes, which implored me to have mercy and to redress; and tortured by seeking assistance and power in vain.”
“并且抛弃了我，”侄子回答道，“束缚在一个令我恐惧的体系中，对此负责，却无力改变；努力执行我亲爱的母亲临终时最后的请求，遵从她临终时最后的眼神，那眼神祈求我宽恕并纠正；却因徒劳地寻求帮助和支持而备受折磨。”

source: That he had no recollection whatever of his having been brought from his prison to that house, was apparent to them. They heard him mutter, “One Hundred and Five, North Tower;” and when he looked about him, it evidently was for the strong fortress-walls which had long encompassed him. On their reaching the courtyard he instinctively altered his tread, as being in expectation of a drawbridge; and when there was no drawbridge, and he saw the carriage waiting in the open street, he dropped his daughter’s hand and clasped his head again.
domestication: 他完全不记得自己是从牢里被带到这所房子的，这一点很明显。他们听见他嘟囔着：“北塔一百零五号；”当他环顾四周时，显然在寻找那长期以来围绕着他的坚固堡垒的墙壁。一进到院子里，他本能地改变了脚步，好像在等着吊桥落下；可是没有吊桥，只见马车停在大路上，他便松开女儿的手，又抱住了自己的头。
foreignization: 他完全不记得自己是从监狱被带到这座房子来的，这一点对他们来说是显而易见的。他们听到他喃喃自语：“一百零五，北塔；”当他环顾四周时，显然在寻找长期以来包围着他的坚固堡垒墙。当他们到达庭院时，他本能地改变了步伐，似乎在期待一座吊桥；而当没有吊桥，他看到马车停在开阔的街道上时，他松开了女儿的手，再次抱住了自己的头。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

That he had no recollection whatever of his having been brought from his prison to that house, was apparent to them. They heard him mutter, “One Hundred and Five, North Tower;” and when he looked about him, it evidently was for the strong fortress-walls which had long encompassed him. On their reaching the courtyard he instinctively altered his tread, as being in expectation of a drawbridge; and when there was no drawbridge, and he saw the carriage waiting in the open street, he dropped his daughter’s hand and clasped his head again.
他完全不记得自己是从牢里被带到这所房子的，这一点很明显。他们听见他嘟囔着：“北塔一百零五号；”当他环顾四周时，显然在寻找那长期以来围绕着他的坚固堡垒的墙壁。一进到院子里，他本能地改变了脚步，好像在等着吊桥落下；可是没有吊桥，只见马车停在大路上，他便松开女儿的手，又抱住了自己的头。

That he had no recollection whatever of his having been brought from his prison to that house, was apparent to them. They heard him mutter, “One Hundred and Five, North Tower;” and when he looked about him, it evidently was for the strong fortress-walls which had long encompassed him. On their reaching the courtyard he instinctively altered his tread, as being in expectation of a drawbridge; and when there was no drawbridge, and he saw the carriage waiting in the open street, he dropped his daughter’s hand and clasped his head again.
他完全不记得自己是从监狱被带到这座房子来的，这一点对他们来说是显而易见的。他们听到他喃喃自语：“一百零五，北塔；”当他环顾四周时，显然在寻找长期以来包围着他的坚固堡垒墙。当他们到达庭院时，他本能地改变了步伐，似乎在期待一座吊桥；而当没有吊桥，他看到马车停在开阔的街道上时，他松开了女儿的手，再次抱住了自己的头。

source: In the hope of his recovery, and of resort to this third course being thereby rendered practicable, Mr. Lorry resolved to watch him attentively, with as little appearance as possible of doing so. He therefore made arrangements to absent himself from Tellson’s for the first time in his life, and took his post by the window in the same room.
domestication: 为了他能早日康复，也好让这第三条路变得可行，洛里先生决定密切关注他，但又尽量不让他察觉。于是，洛里先生有生以来第一次请了假，没去台尔森银行上班，而是守在同一个房间的窗边。
foreignization: 为了希望他能恢复健康，从而使采取这第三种方法成为可能，洛里先生决定密切观察他，同时尽可能不显出他在这样做。因此，他第一次安排自己离开泰勒森银行，并在同一个房间的窗户旁站好了位置。

source: Headlong, mad, and dangerous footsteps to force their way into anybody’s life, footsteps not easily made clean again if once stained red, the footsteps raging in Saint Antoine afar off, as the little circle sat in the dark London window.
domestication: 狂奔乱闯的脚步，危险又疯狂，硬是要闯进别人的生活中，一旦染红了就再也洗不干净。这脚步声在远处的圣安托万怒吼着，而此时小圈子里的人正坐在伦敦的暗窗下。
foreignization: 莽撞、疯狂且危险的脚步声，强行闯入任何人的生活，一旦染红便不易洗净的脚步声，在圣安托万远处怒吼，而小圈子的人则坐在伦敦黑暗的窗前。

source: “Now, I tell you where it is!” said Mr. Cruncher to his wife, on entering. “If, as a honest tradesman, my wenturs goes wrong to-night, I shall make sure that you’ve been praying again me, and I shall work you for it just the same as if I seen you do it.”
domestication: “现在，我告诉你它在哪儿！”克伦彻先生一进门就对他妻子说。“要是我这老实生意人今晚的冒险不顺，我准会认为你又在背后祈祷害我，我可不会饶了你，就算我没抓到你现行也一样。”
foreignization: “现在，我告诉你它在哪里！”克伦彻先生进门后对妻子说，“如果，作为一个诚实的商人，我今晚的冒险出了问题，我会确信你又在为我祈祷了，我会因此而让你付出代价，就像我亲眼看到你这样做了一样。”

“Now, I tell you where it is!” said Mr. Cruncher to his wife, on entering. “If, as a honest tradesman, my wenturs goes wrong to-night, I shall make sure that you’ve been praying again me, and I shall work you for it just the same as if I seen you do it.”

source: She laid her head upon her father’s breast, as she had laid his poor head on her own breast, long, long ago. He was happy in the return he had made her, he was recompensed for his suffering, he was proud of his strength. “You must not be weak, my darling,” he remonstrated; “don’t tremble so. I have saved him.”
domestication: 她把头靠在父亲的胸前，就像很久很久以前她曾把他的头靠在自己的胸前一样。他为自己能回报她而感到幸福，觉得自己的苦难得到了补偿，为自己现在的力量感到骄傲。“你不能这么软弱，我的宝贝，”他劝说着，“别抖得这么厉害。我已经救了他。”
foreignization: 她把头放在父亲的胸前，就像很久很久以前她把他的可怜的头放在自己的胸前一样。他对自己的回报感到幸福，对自己的痛苦得到了补偿，为自己的力量感到自豪。“你不能软弱，我的亲爱的，”他劝说道；“不要这么颤抖。我已经救了他。”

source: Next noontide saw the admirable woman in her usual place in the wine-shop, knitting away assiduously. A rose lay beside her, and if she now and then glanced at the flower, it was with no infraction of her usual preoccupied air. There were a few customers, drinking or not drinking, standing or seated, sprinkled about. The day was very hot, and heaps of flies, who were extending their inquisitive and adventurous perquisitions into all the glutinous little glasses near madame, fell dead at the bottom. Their decease made no impression on the other flies out promenading, who looked at them in the coolest manner (as if they themselves were elephants, or something as far removed), until they met the same fate. Curious to consider how heedless flies are!—perhaps they thought as much at Court that sunny summer day.
domestication: 第二天中午，那位可敬的女士又坐在酒馆的老位置上，勤快地织着毛衣。旁边放着一朵玫瑰，偶尔她会看看这花，但她的神情依旧心不在焉。有几个顾客，有的在喝酒，有的不喝，站着的，坐着的，零星散落着。天气非常热，成群的苍蝇在酒馆里四处乱窜，好奇又大胆地探进女士周围一个个粘稠的小杯子里，最终死在了杯底。其他在外游荡的苍蝇对这些死去的同伴毫不在意，冷眼旁观，好像自己是大象，或者离这一切很远似的，直到它们也难逃同样的命运。真是奇怪，苍蝇怎么这么不在乎！——也许那天阳光明媚的夏日，宫廷里的人也是这么想的。
foreignization: 下一个中午，那位可敬的女士像往常一样坐在酒馆里，勤勉地织着毛衣。一朵玫瑰放在她旁边，如果她偶尔瞥一眼那朵花，也丝毫没有破坏她惯有的心不在焉的神情。有几个顾客，或喝酒或不喝酒，或站着或坐着，零星散落各处。那天非常热，成群的苍蝇正将其好奇和冒险的探查延伸到madame附近的每一个粘糊糊的小酒杯中，纷纷毙命于杯底。它们的死亡并没有给其他在外游荡的苍蝇留下印象，后者以最冷静的态度看着它们（仿佛自己是大象，或者离它们远得不能再远的东西），直到遭遇同样的命运。真有趣去思考苍蝇是多么的漫不经心！——也许那个阳光明媚的夏日，在宫廷里也有类似的想法。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Next noontide saw the admirable woman in her usual place in the wine-shop, knitting away assiduously. A rose lay beside her, and if she now and then glanced at the flower, it was with no infraction of her usual preoccupied air. There were a few customers, drinking or not drinking, standing or seated, sprinkled about. The day was very hot, and heaps of flies, who were extending their inquisitive and adventurous perquisitions into all the glutinous little glasses near madame, fell dead at the bottom. Their decease made no impression on the other flies out promenading, who looked at them in the coolest manner (as if they themselves were elephants, or something as far removed), until they met the same fate. Curious to consider how heedless flies are!—perhaps they thought as much at Court that sunny summer day.

source: A singular circumstance then arose in the case. The object in hand being to show that the prisoner went down, with some fellow-plotter untracked, in the Dover mail on that Friday night in November five years ago, and got out of the mail in the night, as a blind, at a place where he did not remain, but from which he travelled back some dozen miles or more, to a garrison and dockyard, and there collected information; a witness was called to identify him as having been at the precise time required, in the coffee-room of an hotel in that garrison-and-dockyard town, waiting for another person. The prisoner’s counsel was cross-examining this witness with no result, except that he had never seen the prisoner on any other occasion, when the wigged gentleman who had all this time been looking at the ceiling of the court, wrote a word or two on a little piece of paper, screwed it up, and tossed it to him. Opening this piece of paper in the next pause, the counsel looked with great attention and curiosity at the prisoner.
domestication: 这案子中出现了一个特别的情况。要证明的是，五年前的十一月一个星期五晚上，被告和一个未被追踪到的同谋一起乘坐了开往多佛的邮车，并在夜里在一个他并未停留的地方下车，以作掩护，然后从那里返回了大约十几英里，到了一个有驻军和船厂的地方，在那儿收集信息。为此，传唤了一名证人来指认，被告就在那个特定的时间，在那个驻军和船厂镇的一家酒店咖啡室里，等着另一个人。辩护律师盘问这个证人，结果除了证人从未在其他场合见过被告外，没有得到任何有用的信息。这时，那位一直抬头看着法庭天花板的戴假发的先生，写了几个字在一张小纸条上，揉成一团扔给了律师。律师在下一个停顿时打开了这张纸条，然后非常专注和好奇地盯着被告看。
foreignization: 然后在这个案件中出现了一个特殊的情况。当前的目标是证明被告在五年前十一月的那个星期五晚上，与某个未被追踪到的同谋一起乘坐多佛邮车，并在夜间在一个他并未停留的地方下车，作为幌子，而从那里他返回了大约十二英里或更远，到达了一个军营和造船厂，并在那里收集了情报；为此传唤了一位证人来确认他在所需的确切时间出现在那个军营和造船厂城镇的一家酒店的咖啡厅里，等待另一个人。被告的律师正在盘问这个证人，但没有取得任何结果，除了证人从未在其他场合见过被告外，这时那位一直注视着法庭天花板的戴假发的先生在一张小纸上写了几个字，卷起来扔给了他。律师在下一个停顿的时候打开了这张纸条，非常专注和好奇地看向被告。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

A singular circumstance then arose in the case. The object in hand being to show that the prisoner went down, with some fellow-plotter untracked, in the Dover mail on that Friday night in November five years ago, and got out of the mail in the night, as a blind, at a place where he did not remain, but from which he travelled back some dozen miles or more, to a garrison and dockyard, and there collected information; a witness was called to identify him as having been at the precise time required, in the coffee-room of an hotel in that garrison-and-dockyard town, waiting for another person. The prisoner’s counsel was cross-examining this witness with no result, except that he had never seen the prisoner on any other occasion, when the wigged gentleman who had all this time been looking at the ceiling of the court, wrote a word or two on a little piece of paper, screwed it up, and tossed it to him. Opening this piece of paper in the next pause, the counsel looked with great attention and curiosity at the prisoner.

source: “My brave wife,” returned Defarge, standing before her with his head a little bent, and his hands clasped at his back, like a docile and attentive pupil before his catechist, “I do not question all this. But it has lasted a long time, and it is possible—you know well, my wife, it is possible—that it may not come, during our lives.”
domestication: “我勇敢的老婆啊，”德法热站在她面前，头微微低着，双手背在身后，像个听话的学生在老师面前一样，“我可不是说这些不对。不过，这事儿拖得太久了，我们俩心里都清楚，这事儿也许在咱们有生之年都见不着了。”
foreignization: “我的勇敢的妻子，”德法日站在她面前，头微微低着，双手背在身后，像一个顺从且专注的学生站在他的教义问答老师面前，“我并不质疑这一切。但它已经持续了很长时间，而且有可能——你知道的，我的妻子，这是可能的——它可能在我们有生之年不会到来。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“My brave wife,” returned Defarge, standing before her with his head a little bent, and his hands clasped at his back, like a docile and attentive pupil before his catechist, “I do not question all this. But it has lasted a long time, and it is possible—you know well, my wife, it is possible—that it may not come, during our lives.”
“我勇敢的老婆啊，”德法热站在她面前，头微微低着，双手背在身后，像个听话的学生在老师面前一样，“我可不是说这些不对。不过，这事儿拖得太久了，我们俩心里都清楚，这事儿也许在咱们有生之年都见不着了。”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“My brave wife,” returned Defarge, standing before her with his head a little bent, and his hands clasped at his back, like a docile and attentive pupil before his catechist, “I do not question all this. But it has lasted a long time, and it is possible—you know well, my wife, it is possible—that it may not come, during our lives.”
“我的勇敢的妻子，”德法日站在她面前，头微微低着，双手背在身后，像一个顺从且专注的学生站在他的教义问答老师面前，“我并不质疑这一切。但它已经持续了很长时间，而且有可能——你知道的，我的妻子，这是可能的——它可能在我们有生之年不会到来。”

source: The lion then composed himself on his back on a sofa on one side of the drinking-table, while the jackal sat at his own paper-bestrewn table proper, on the other side of it, with the bottles and glasses ready to his hand. Both resorted to the drinking-table without stint, but each in a different way; the lion for the most part reclining with his hands in his waistband, looking at the fire, or occasionally flirting with some lighter document; the jackal, with knitted brows and intent face, so deep in his task, that his eyes did not even follow the hand he stretched out for his glass—which often groped about, for a minute or more, before it found the glass for his lips. Two or three times, the matter in hand became so knotty, that the jackal found it imperative on him to get up, and steep his towels anew. From these pilgrimages to the jug and basin, he returned with such eccentricities of damp headgear as no words can describe; which were made the more ludicrous by his anxious gravity.
domestication: 狮子随后在酒桌旁的沙发上躺了下来，而豺狼则坐在酒桌另一边，自己那堆满文件的桌子前，瓶瓶罐罐和杯子都触手可及。俩人都没少喝酒，但喝法不同：狮子大多时候懒洋洋地躺着，手插在腰间，时而望望火炉，偶尔翻翻轻薄的文件；豺狼则眉头紧锁，神情专注，埋头苦干，连伸手拿酒杯时眼睛都不抬一下，他的手常常摸索一会儿才能找到杯子送到嘴边。有好几次，手头的工作复杂得让豺狼不得不站起来，重新浸湿毛巾。他从水壶和脸盆前回来时，头上那湿漉漉的样子简直无法形容，再配上他那焦虑的表情，显得格外滑稽。
foreignization: 狮子然后在饮酒桌一侧的沙发上仰卧下来，而豺狼则坐在饮酒桌另一侧他自己的满是纸张的桌子旁，瓶瓶罐罐和玻璃杯随手可及。两人都不限量地光顾饮酒桌，但方式各不相同；狮子大部分时间都斜躺着，双手插在腰带里，看着火炉，或偶尔摆弄一些较轻的文件；而豺狼则眉头紧锁，全神贯注，沉浸在自己的任务中，以至于他的眼睛甚至没有跟随他伸出去拿酒杯的手——那只手常常摸索了一两分钟才找到送到嘴边的酒杯。有两三次，手头的事情变得如此棘手，以至于豺狼不得不站起来，重新浸湿他的毛巾。从这些前往水壶和脸盆的朝圣之旅归来时，他头上戴着的湿漉漉的头饰呈现出无法用言语描述的怪异模样；这些怪异的模样因他那焦虑的严肃表情而显得更加滑稽。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The lion then composed himself on his back on a sofa on one side of the drinking-table, while the jackal sat at his own paper-bestrewn table proper, on the other side of it, with the bottles and glasses ready to his hand. Both resorted to the drinking-table without stint, but each in a different way; the lion for the most part reclining with his hands in his waistband, looking at the fire, or occasionally flirting with some lighter document; the jackal, with knitted brows and intent face, so deep in his task, that his eyes did not even follow the hand he stretched out for his glass—which often groped about, for a minute or more, before it found the glass for his lips. Two or three times, the matter in hand became so knotty, that the jackal found it imperative on him to get up, and steep his towels anew. From these pilgrimages to the jug and basin, he returned with such eccentricities of damp headgear as no words can describe; which were made the more ludicrous by his anxious gravity.

source: “The occupation resumed under the influence of this passing affliction so happily recovered from,” said Mr. Lorry, clearing his throat, “we will call—Blacksmith’s work, Blacksmith’s work. We will say, to put a case and for the sake of illustration, that he had been used, in his bad time, to work at a little forge. We will say that he was unexpectedly found at his forge again. Is it not a pity that he should keep it by him?”
domestication: 洛里先生清了清嗓子，说：“这阵子他受了点小病的折磨，现在既然已经康复了，咱们就说——打铁的活儿，就是打铁的活儿。为了举例说明，就假设他在那段不好的日子里，常常在一座小铁匠铺里干活。现在他又意外地回到了铁匠铺。他身边留着这些工具，不是挺可惜的吗？”
foreignization: “由于这场刚刚幸运地恢复过来的暂时性疾病的影响，这职业又恢复了，”洛里先生清了清嗓子说，“我们就叫它——铁匠的工作，铁匠的工作。我们假设一个例子，为了说明，就说他在他不好的时候，曾经习惯在一个小铁匠铺工作。我们假设他又意外地被发现正在他的铁匠铺工作。难道不遗憾他身边还保留着这个铁匠铺吗？”

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Is it not a pity that he should keep it by him?”

source: “Will you wake me, before departing? I have walked two nights without resting. Let me finish my pipe, and I shall sleep like a child. Will you wake me?”
domestication: 你走之前会叫醒我吗？我已经连着两个晚上没合眼了。让我抽完这袋烟，我就能睡得跟个孩子似的。到时候你可得叫我啊。
foreignization: 你会在离开前把我叫醒吗？我已经连续走了两个晚上没有休息。让我抽完这根烟斗，我就会像孩子一样睡去。你会叫醒我吗？

source: “Well then! Pledge me to the pretty witness,” said Stryver, holding up his glass. “Are you turned in a pleasant direction?”
domestication: 好嘞！斯垂弗举着酒杯说：“我以这位漂亮的小证人起誓。你是不是心情不错啊？”
foreignization: “好吧！为了这位美丽的证人干杯，”斯特莱弗举起了他的酒杯。“你的心情是否愉快？”

Translate the following text to Chinese.

“好吧！为了这位美丽的证人干杯，”斯特莱弗举起了他的酒杯。“你的心情是否愉快？”

source: He was a man of about sixty, handsomely dressed, haughty in manner, and with a face like a fine mask. A face of a transparent paleness; every feature in it clearly defined; one set expression on it. The nose, beautifully formed otherwise, was very slightly pinched at the top of each nostril. In those two compressions, or dints, the only little change that the face ever showed, resided. They persisted in changing colour sometimes, and they would be occasionally dilated and contracted by something like a faint pulsation; then, they gave a look of treachery, and cruelty, to the whole countenance. Examined with attention, its capacity of helping such a look was to be found in the line of the mouth, and the lines of the orbits of the eyes, being much too horizontal and thin; still, in the effect of the face made, it was a handsome face, and a remarkable one.
domestication: 这人约莫六十岁，衣着华丽，举止傲慢，面容俊美得像一具精致的面具。脸色苍白得透明，五官分明，表情固定。鼻子长得很好看，只是鼻孔上端微微凹陷。整张脸唯一的变化就在这两个小小的凹陷上。有时它们会变色，偶尔还会微微抽动，就像脉搏般跳动；这时，整张脸就显得阴险狠毒。仔细观察，这张脸之所以能显出这样的神情，是因为嘴巴和眼睛周围的线条太直、太细了。但即便如此，这张脸仍称得上英俊非凡，令人难忘。
foreignization: 他大约六十岁，衣着华丽，态度傲慢，脸庞如同精美的面具。一张苍白得透明的脸；每个特征都清晰可见；脸上带着一种固定的表情。鼻子本身美丽地成形，但在每个鼻孔的顶端都非常轻微地内收。这两处压缩或凹陷是这张脸唯一会变化的地方。它们有时会改变颜色，并偶尔因类似微弱脉动的东西而扩张和收缩；这时，它们给整张脸带来了一种背叛和残忍的神情。仔细观察，这种表情的效果可以从嘴线和眼眶线条过于水平和细薄上找到原因；尽管如此，这张脸依然是一张英俊的脸，一张引人注目的脸。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

He was a man of about sixty, handsomely dressed, haughty in manner, and with a face like a fine mask. A face of a transparent paleness; every feature in it clearly defined; one set expression on it. The nose, beautifully formed otherwise, was very slightly pinched at the top of each nostril. In those two compressions, or dints, the only little change that the face ever showed, resided. They persisted in changing colour sometimes, and they would be occasionally dilated and contracted by something like a faint pulsation; then, they gave a look of treachery, and cruelty, to the whole countenance. Examined with attention, its capacity of helping such a look was to be found in the line of the mouth, and the lines of the orbits of the eyes, being much too horizontal and thin; still, in the effect of the face made, it was a handsome face, and a remarkable one.
这人约莫六十岁，衣着华丽，举止傲慢，面容俊美得像一具精致的面具。脸色苍白得透明，五官分明，表情固定。鼻子长得很好看，只是鼻孔上端微微凹陷。整张脸唯一的变化就在这两个小小的凹陷上。有时它们会变色，偶尔还会微微抽动，就像脉搏般跳动；这时，整张脸就显得阴险狠毒。仔细观察，这张脸之所以能显出这样的神情，是因为嘴巴和眼睛周围的线条太直、太细了。但即便如此，这张脸仍称得上英俊非凡，令人难忘。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

He was a man of about sixty, handsomely dressed, haughty in manner, and with a face like a fine mask. A face of a transparent paleness; every feature in it clearly defined; one set expression on it. The nose, beautifully formed otherwise, was very slightly pinched at the top of each nostril. In those two compressions, or dints, the only little change that the face ever showed, resided. They persisted in changing colour sometimes, and they would be occasionally dilated and contracted by something like a faint pulsation; then, they gave a look of treachery, and cruelty, to the whole countenance. Examined with attention, its capacity of helping such a look was to be found in the line of the mouth, and the lines of the orbits of the eyes, being much too horizontal and thin; still, in the effect of the face made, it was a handsome face, and a remarkable one.

他大约六十岁，衣着华丽，态度傲慢，脸庞如同精美的面具。一张苍白得透明的脸；每个特征都清晰可见；脸上带着一种固定的表情。鼻子本身美丽地成形，但在每个鼻孔的顶端都非常轻微地内收。这两处压缩或凹陷是这张脸唯一会变化的地方。它们有时会改变颜色，并偶尔因类似微弱脉动的东西而扩张和收缩；这时，它们给整张脸带来了一种背叛和残忍的神情。仔细观察，这种表情的效果可以从嘴线和眼眶线条过于水平和细薄上找到原因；尽管如此，这张脸依然是一张英俊的脸，一张引人注目的脸。

source: The wood-sawyer, who was a little man with a redundancy of gesture (he had once been a mender of roads), cast a glance at the prison, pointed at the prison, and putting his ten fingers before his face to represent bars, peeped through them jocosely.
domestication: 那伐木的小个子男人（以前是修路的）朝监狱瞥了一眼，指了指监狱，然后把十根手指放到眼前比作栏杆，嬉皮笑脸地从指缝里向外瞧。
foreignization: 那个伐木工人是个小个子，手势丰富（他曾经是个修路工），他朝监狱看了一眼，指着监狱，然后把手的十个手指放在脸前，模仿栏杆的样子，嬉皮笑脸地透过手指缝隙往里看。

source: Miss Manette had taken some refreshment on the road, and required none then, and was extremely anxious to see the gentleman from Tellson’s immediately, if it suited his pleasure and convenience.
domestication: 曼内特小姐在赶路时已经吃过些东西，当时并不需要再吃，她非常希望能立刻见到从泰勒森银行来的先生，只要对方方便的话。
foreignization: 曼内特小姐在路上已经吃了一些点心，当时并不需要再吃，而且非常急切地希望如果对方方便的话，能立即见到从特尔森银行来的先生。

source: Saint Antoine had been, that morning, a vast dusky mass of scarecrows heaving to and fro, with frequent gleams of light above the billowy heads, where steel blades and bayonets shone in the sun. A tremendous roar arose from the throat of Saint Antoine, and a forest of naked arms struggled in the air like shrivelled branches of trees in a winter wind: all the fingers convulsively clutching at every weapon or semblance of a weapon that was thrown up from the depths below, no matter how far off.
domestication: 那天早上，圣安托万就像一大片灰蒙蒙的稻草人，在人群中起伏不定，头顶上波浪般的脑袋时不时闪现出光芒，那是阳光下刀光剑影的闪烁。圣安托万发出震天的吼声，无数赤裸的手臂在空中挥舞，像冬天里的枯枝，拼命抓向从人群深处抛出的每一件武器或半成品武器，不管多远都伸手去抢。
foreignization: 圣安托万那天早晨是一大群暗沉沉的稻草人在波浪般的人头中起伏不定，头顶上方不时闪烁着刀片和刺刀在阳光下的光芒。从圣安托万的喉咙里发出了一阵巨大的咆哮声，空荡荡的手臂像冬天里的枯树枝一样在空中挣扎：所有的手指都在痉挛地抓取从下面深处扔上来的一切武器或类似武器的东西，无论多远。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Saint Antoine had been, that morning, a vast dusky mass of scarecrows heaving to and fro, with frequent gleams of light above the billowy heads, where steel blades and bayonets shone in the sun. A tremendous roar arose from the throat of Saint Antoine, and a forest of naked arms struggled in the air like shrivelled branches of trees in a winter wind: all the fingers convulsively clutching at every weapon or semblance of a weapon that was thrown up from the depths below, no matter how far off.

source: Her golden hair, which she wore in long curls, had been hurriedly pushed aside, and fell down over her neck. Advancing his hand by little and little, he took it up and looked at it. In the midst of the action he went astray, and, with another deep sigh, fell to work at his shoemaking.

source: “Yes, sir. We have oftentimes the honour to entertain your gentlemen in their travelling backwards and forwards betwixt London and Paris, sir. A vast deal of travelling, sir, in Tellson and Company’s House.”

source: The necessity of being angry in a suppressed tone had put Mr. Stryver’s blood-vessels into a dangerous state when it was his turn to be angry; Mr. Lorry’s veins, methodical as their courses could usually be, were in no better state now it was his turn.
domestication: 斯特莱弗先生平时习惯压抑着怒气，这让他一发火血管就危险得很；现在轮到老洛里发火了，他那平时井井有条的血管也绷得紧紧的，好不到哪儿去。
foreignization: 被压抑的愤怒的必要性使得斯特莱弗先生的血管在轮到他生气时处于危险状态；洛里先生的血管，尽管其运行通常很有规律，现在轮到他生气时也处于同样不好的状态。

source: When a name was called, its owner stepped apart into a spot reserved for those who were announced as being thus fatally recorded. Charles Evrémonde, called Darnay, had reason to know the usage; he had seen hundreds pass away so.
domestication: 点到名字的人就 stepping out 走到一个指定的地方，那是给那些被宣判了死刑的人准备的。查尔斯·埃弗蒙德，也就是达内，对这种规矩再熟悉不过了；他见过几百号人这么一步步走向末路。
foreignization: 当一个名字被叫到时，它的主人就会走到一个为那些被宣布如此致命记录的人预留的位置。查尔斯·埃弗蒙德，被称为达内，有理由知道这一惯例；他见过数百人这样离去。

source: “I have lived with the darling—or the darling has lived with me, and paid me for it; which she certainly should never have done, you may take your affidavit, if I could have afforded to keep either myself or her for nothing—since she was ten years old. And it’s really very hard,” said Miss Pross.
domestication: 我跟这小宝贝一起生活，或者说是她在我这儿住，还给我钱；这钱她本来是不应该给的，这点你可以发誓作证，如果我有能力养活自己或她，我是不会让她掏钱的——从她十岁起就是这样。这真让人觉得不公平，普罗斯小姐说道。
foreignization: “我与亲爱的同住——或者说，亲爱的与我同住，并且为此付了钱；这她当然不该做的，你可以发誓，如果我能负担得起自己或她的生活费用的话——从她十岁起就是如此。这真的非常不公平，”普罗斯小姐说。

source: A quainter corner than the corner where the Doctor lived, was not to be found in London. There was no way through it, and the front windows of the Doctor’s lodgings commanded a pleasant little vista of street that had a congenial air of retirement on it. There were few buildings then, north of the Oxford-road, and forest-trees flourished, and wild flowers grew, and the hawthorn blossomed, in the now vanished fields. As a consequence, country airs circulated in Soho with vigorous freedom, instead of languishing into the parish like stray paupers without a settlement; and there was many a good south wall, not far off, on which the peaches ripened in their season.
domestication: 在伦敦，找不到比医生住的角落更古朴的地方了。这里没有穿堂路，医生住所的前窗正对着一条小街，街景宜人，透着一股子清静的味道。当时牛津路以北的建筑还不多，林木葱郁，野花烂漫，山楂花也绽放在这如今消失的田野上。因此，索霍区里时常有清新的乡间空气自由自在地流动，而不是像无家可归的穷人一样，在教区内奄奄一息；附近也有不少向阳的南墙，到了季节，上面的桃子便成熟了。
foreignization: 比医生住的角落更古色古香的地方，在伦敦是找不到的。那里没有穿堂路，而医生住所的前窗则俯瞰着一条带有宜人隐居气息的小街美景。当时牛津路以北建筑物稀少，森林里的树木茂盛，野花盛开，山楂花在如今已消失的田野上绽放。因此，索霍区的乡村空气自由地流通，而不是像无籍可依的流浪者那样在教区内萎靡不振；附近还有许多不错的南墙，每到季节，桃子就在这些墙上成熟。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

A quainter corner than the corner where the Doctor lived, was not to be found in London. There was no way through it, and the front windows of the Doctor’s lodgings commanded a pleasant little vista of street that had a congenial air of retirement on it. There were few buildings then, north of the Oxford-road, and forest-trees flourished, and wild flowers grew, and the hawthorn blossomed, in the now vanished fields. As a consequence, country airs circulated in Soho with vigorous freedom, instead of languishing into the parish like stray paupers without a settlement; and there was many a good south wall, not far off, on which the peaches ripened in their season.
在伦敦，找不到比医生住的角落更古朴的地方了。这里没有穿堂路，医生住所的前窗正对着一条小街，街景宜人，透着一股子清静的味道。当时牛津路以北的建筑还不多，林木葱郁，野花烂漫，山楂花也绽放在这如今消失的田野上。因此，索霍区里时常有清新的乡间空气自由自在地流动，而不是像无家可归的穷人一样，在教区内奄奄一息；附近也有不少向阳的南墙，到了季节，上面的桃子便成熟了。

source: Defarge glanced darkly at him for answer, and walked on in a steady and set silence. The deeper he sank into this silence, the fainter hope there was—or so Darnay thought—of his softening in any slight degree. He, therefore, made haste to say:
domestication: 德法日阴沉沉地瞥了他一眼，然后大步向前走去，一声不吭。他越是沉默，丹奈心里就越觉得希望渺茫，对方恐怕连一点软化的可能都没有。于是，丹奈赶紧说道：
foreignization: 德法日阴沉地看了他一眼作为回答，然后稳步地、固执地沉默着往前走。他越是陷入这种沉默，希望他就此稍微缓和一些的机会就越渺茫——至少达奈是这么认为的。因此，他急忙说道：

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Defarge glanced darkly at him for answer, and walked on in a steady and set silence. The deeper he sank into this silence, the fainter hope there was—or so Darnay thought—of his softening in any slight degree. He, therefore, made haste to say:
德法日阴沉沉地瞥了他一眼，然后大步向前走去，一声不吭。他越是沉默，丹奈心里就越觉得希望渺茫，对方恐怕连一点软化的可能都没有。于是，丹奈赶紧说道：

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Defarge glanced darkly at him for answer, and walked on in a steady and set silence. The deeper he sank into this silence, the fainter hope there was—or so Darnay thought—of his softening in any slight degree. He, therefore, made haste to say:
德法日阴沉地看了他一眼作为回答，然后稳步地、固执地沉默着往前走。他越是陷入这种沉默，希望他就此稍微缓和一些的机会就越渺茫——至少达奈是这么认为的。因此，他急忙说道：

source: “When you were talking to Lucie,” Mr. Lorry repeated. “Yes. I wonder you are not ashamed to mention the name of Lucie! Wishing you were going to France at this time of day!”
domestication: “你跟露西说话的时候，”洛里先生重复道。“对啊，提露西的名字你不觉得羞愧吗！这会儿巴不得你去法国呢！”
foreignization: “当你和露西说话的时候，”洛里先生重复道。“是的。我真奇怪你竟不为提到露西的名字而感到羞愧！希望你现在就动身去法国！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“When you were talking to Lucie,” Mr. Lorry repeated. “Yes. I wonder you are not ashamed to mention the name of Lucie! Wishing you were going to France at this time of day!”
“你跟露西说话的时候，”洛里先生重复道。“对啊，提露西的名字你不觉得羞愧吗！这会儿巴不得你去法国呢！”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“When you were talking to Lucie,” Mr. Lorry repeated. “Yes. I wonder you are not ashamed to mention the name of Lucie! Wishing you were going to France at this time of day!”
“当你和露西说话的时候，”洛里先生重复道。“是的。我真奇怪你竟不为提到露西的名字而感到羞愧！希望你现在就动身去法国！”

source: “La Force! Lucie, my child, if ever you were brave and serviceable in your life—and you were always both—you will compose yourself now, to do exactly as I bid you; for more depends upon it than you can think, or I can say. There is no help for you in any action on your part to-night; you cannot possibly stir out. I say this, because what I must bid you to do for Charles’s sake, is the hardest thing to do of all. You must instantly be obedient, still, and quiet. You must let me put you in a room at the back here. You must leave your father and me alone for two minutes, and as there are Life and Death in the world you must not delay.”
domestication: “使出你的勇气来！露西，我的孩子，你一生中若是曾有过勇敢和助人的时候——而你一直都是这样的——那么你现在就要镇定下来，照我吩咐的去做；这关系重大，远超你所能想象或我能言说的。今晚你做什么都帮不上忙，绝对不能出门。我这么说是因为我要你为了查尔斯的缘故做一件最难的事。你必须立刻听话，安静，不动声色。我会把你安置在后面的一个房间里。你得让你父亲和我单独待两分钟，而且这关乎生死，千万不能耽误。”
foreignization: “力量！露西，我的孩子，如果你在生活中曾有过勇敢和乐于助人——而你一直都很勇敢和乐于助人——那么现在你必须镇定下来，完全按照我说的去做；因为这比你能想到或我能说出的都要重要。今晚你的任何行动都无法帮助你；你不可能出去。我这样说，是因为为了查尔斯的缘故，我必须让你做的是一件最难的事。你必须立刻听话、安静、不发出声音。你必须让我把你安排到这里的后屋。你必须离开你父亲和我两分钟，而且在这个世界上有生就有死，你绝对不能耽搁。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Lucie, my child, if ever you were brave and serviceable in your life—and you were always both—you will compose yourself now, to do exactly as I bid you; for more depends upon it than you can think, or I can say. There is no help for you in any action on your part to-night; you cannot possibly stir out. I say this, because what I must bid you to do for Charles’s sake, is the hardest thing to do of all. You must instantly be obedient, still, and quiet. You must let me put you in a room at the back here. You must leave your father and me alone for two minutes, and as there are Life and Death in the world you must not delay.”
“使出你的勇气来！露西，我的孩子，你一生中若是曾有过勇敢和助人的时候——而你一直都是这样的——那么你现在就要镇定下来，照我吩咐的去做；这关系重大，远超你所能想象或我能言说的。今晚你做什么都帮不上忙，绝对不能出门。我这么说是因为我要你为了查尔斯的缘故做一件最难的事。你必须立刻听话，安静，不动声色。我会把你安置在后面的一个房间里。你得让你父亲和我单独待两分钟，而且这关乎生死，千万不能耽误。”

“力量！露西，我的孩子，如果你在生活中曾有过勇敢和乐于助人——而你一直都很勇敢和乐于助人——那么现在你必须镇定下来，完全按照我说的去做；因为这比你能想到或我能说出的都要重要。今晚你的任何行动都无法帮助你；你不可能出去。我这样说，是因为为了查尔斯的缘故，我必须让你做的是一件最难的事。你必须立刻听话、安静、不发出声音。你必须让我把你安排到这里的后屋。你必须离开你父亲和我两分钟，而且在这个世界上有生就有死，你绝对不能耽搁。”

source: After hailing the morn with this second salutation, he threw a boot at the woman as a third. It was a very muddy boot, and may introduce the odd circumstance connected with Mr. Cruncher’s domestic economy, that, whereas he often came home after banking hours with clean boots, he often got up next morning to find the same boots covered with clay.
domestication: 这第二声问候刚完，他又朝女人扔了个靴子，算是第三下。这靴子可脏得很，泥巴糊了一层。说起这事，倒有个奇怪的现象：克伦彻先生常在银行下班后回家时靴子还干净得很，可第二天早上一觉醒来，那些靴子却满是泥巴。
foreignization: 在用这第二次问候迎接早晨之后，他朝那个女人扔了一只靴子作为第三次。那是一只非常泥泞的靴子，这可能引出与克伦彻先生的家庭经济有关的一个奇怪的情况，即他常常在银行下班后回家时穿着干净的靴子，而第二天早上起来时却发现同一双靴子上覆盖着泥土。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

After hailing the morn with this second salutation, he threw a boot at the woman as a third. It was a very muddy boot, and may introduce the odd circumstance connected with Mr. Cruncher’s domestic economy, that, whereas he often came home after banking hours with clean boots, he often got up next morning to find the same boots covered with clay.
这第二声问候刚完，他又朝女人扔了个靴子，算是第三下。这靴子可脏得很，泥巴糊了一层。说起这事，倒有个奇怪的现象：克伦彻先生常在银行下班后回家时靴子还干净得很，可第二天早上一觉醒来，那些靴子却满是泥巴。

After hailing the morn with this second salutation, he threw a boot at the woman as a third. It was a very muddy boot, and may introduce the odd circumstance connected with Mr. Cruncher’s domestic economy, that, whereas he often came home after banking hours with clean boots, he often got up next morning to find the same boots covered with clay.
在用这第二次问候迎接早晨之后，他朝那个女人扔了一只靴子作为第三次。那是一只非常泥泞的靴子，这可能引出与克伦彻先生的家庭经济有关的一个奇怪的情况，即他常常在银行下班后回家时穿着干净的靴子，而第二天早上起来时却发现同一双靴子上覆盖着泥土。

source: Its abiding place was in all things fitted to it. A narrow winding street, full of offence and stench, with other narrow winding streets diverging, all peopled by rags and nightcaps, and all smelling of rags and nightcaps, and all visible things with a brooding look upon them that looked ill. In the hunted air of the people there was yet some wild-beast thought of the possibility of turning at bay. Depressed and slinking though they were, eyes of fire were not wanting among them; nor compressed lips, white with what they suppressed; nor foreheads knitted into the likeness of the gallows-rope they mused about enduring, or inflicting. The trade signs (and they were almost as many as the shops) were, all, grim illustrations of Want. The butcher and the porkman painted up, only the leanest scrags of meat; the baker, the coarsest of meagre loaves. The people rudely pictured as drinking in the wine-shops, croaked over their scanty measures of thin wine and beer, and were gloweringly confidential together. Nothing was represented in a flourishing condition, save tools and weapons; but, the cutler’s knives and axes were sharp and bright, the smith’s hammers were heavy, and the gunmaker’s stock was murderous. The crippling stones of the pavement, with their many little reservoirs of mud and water, had no footways, but broke off abruptly at the doors. The kennel, to make amends, ran down the middle of the street—when it ran at all: which was only after heavy rains, and then it ran, by many eccentric fits, into the houses. Across the streets, at wide intervals, one clumsy lamp was slung by a rope and pulley; at night, when the lamplighter had let these down, and lighted, and hoisted them again, a feeble grove of dim wicks swung in a sickly manner overhead, as if they were at sea. Indeed they were at sea, and the ship and crew were in peril of tempest.
domestication: 它的栖身之所，无处不与它相宜。一条狭窄蜿蜒的街道，满是污秽和恶臭，旁边还有几条类似的街道岔开，到处都是衣衫褴褛、头戴夜帽的人，空气中也弥漫着破衣烂衫和夜帽的味道，所有可见的东西都带着一种阴郁的神情，显得不祥。尽管人们显得惊慌失措，但还存有一丝野兽般的念头，似乎随时可能反抗。他们虽然沮丧而猥琐，但并不缺少眼中燃烧着火焰的人；也不乏紧闭双唇，因压抑而发白的面孔；额头上因思考承受或施加绞刑的绳索而皱成一团。招牌（几乎每个店铺都有）都是贫穷的恐怖写照。屠夫和猪肉贩子只展示最瘦的肉；面包师卖的是最粗糙、最贫瘠的面包。酒馆里粗略描绘的酒客，对着稀薄的酒和啤酒咕哝，彼此间低声密谋，眼神阴沉。一切都不景气，只有工具和武器例外；刀匠的刀斧锋利明亮，铁匠的锤子沉重有力，枪匠的库存杀气腾腾。街道上的石板路满是泥水的小坑，没有人行道，到门口就突然中断。为了补偿，下水道沿着街道中间流淌——如果流淌的话：这只有在大雨之后才会发生，而且总是以古怪的方式流入房子里。每隔一段距离，街道上方悬着一盏笨重的灯，晚上，当点灯人把这些灯放下、点亮、再挂起来后，微弱的灯光像在海上摇曳，仿佛它们真的在海上。事实上，它们确实在海上，而这艘船及其船员正面临着暴风雨的威胁。
foreignization: 它的栖身之所完全适合它。一条狭窄蜿蜒的街道，充满恶臭和污秽，还有其他狭窄蜿蜒的街道分岔出去，全部住满了破衣烂衫和夜帽的人，而且到处都散发着破衣烂衫和夜帽的气味，所有可见的事物都带着一种阴沉的表情，看起来很不吉利。尽管人们显得胆战心惊，但其中还是有些野兽般的念头在考虑着是否要背水一战。虽然他们显得沮丧而猥琐，但在他们当中并不缺少炽热的眼睛；也不缺少因压抑而发白的紧闭的嘴唇；也不缺少因思考忍受或施加绞索而皱起的额头，这些绞索正是他们所沉思的对象。那些店铺招牌（几乎和店铺一样多）都是贫困的阴森写照。屠夫和猪肉商只画出了最瘦的肉块；面包师则展示着最粗糙的贫瘠面包。被粗略描绘成在酒馆里饮酒的人们，对着他们稀少的劣质葡萄酒和啤酒低声抱怨，并且相互之间怀着恶意地窃窃私语。除了工具和武器之外，没有什么东西被描绘得欣欣向荣；但是，刀匠的刀和斧头既锋利又明亮，铁匠的锤子沉重，枪匠的库存具有杀伤力。人行道上的破损石块，带着众多的小泥水池，没有行人道，而是突然在门边中断。作为补偿，排水沟沿着街道中间流淌——当它流淌的时候：这只有在大雨之后才会发生，然后它以许多奇怪的方式流入房屋。在街道上方，每隔很远的距离就悬挂着一盏笨重的灯，由绳索和滑轮吊起；到了晚上，当点灯人把这些灯放下来，点燃后再次吊上去时，一些微弱的昏暗灯芯像在海上一样摇摇晃晃地悬挂在头顶。确实，它们是在海上，船和船员正面临风暴的危险。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Its abiding place was in all things fitted to it. A narrow winding street, full of offence and stench, with other narrow winding streets diverging, all peopled by rags and nightcaps, and all smelling of rags and nightcaps, and all visible things with a brooding look upon them that looked ill. In the hunted air of the people there was yet some wild-beast thought of the possibility of turning at bay. Depressed and slinking though they were, eyes of fire were not wanting among them; nor compressed lips, white with what they suppressed; nor foreheads knitted into the likeness of the gallows-rope they mused about enduring, or inflicting. The trade signs (and they were almost as many as the shops) were, all, grim illustrations of Want. The butcher and the porkman painted up, only the leanest scrags of meat; the baker, the coarsest of meagre loaves. The people rudely pictured as drinking in the wine-shops, croaked over their scanty measures of thin wine and beer, and were gloweringly confidential together. Nothing was represented in a flourishing condition, save tools and weapons; but, the cutler’s knives and axes were sharp and bright, the smith’s hammers were heavy, and the gunmaker’s stock was murderous. The crippling stones of the pavement, with their many little reservoirs of mud and water, had no footways, but broke off abruptly at the doors. The kennel, to make amends, ran down the middle of the street—when it ran at all: which was only after heavy rains, and then it ran, by many eccentric fits, into the houses. Across the streets, at wide intervals, one clumsy lamp was slung by a rope and pulley; at night, when the lamplighter had let these down, and lighted, and hoisted them again, a feeble grove of dim wicks swung in a sickly manner overhead, as if they were at sea. Indeed they were at sea, and the ship and crew were in peril of tempest.

source: “But I would hold a pretty wager, sir, that a House like Tellson and Company was flourishing, a matter of fifty, not to speak of fifteen years ago?”
domestication: 不过，我敢打赌，像泰尔森公司这样的大买卖，在五十年前，不提十五年前，肯定是红火得很呢。
foreignization: “但是，先生，我敢打个相当的赌，像泰尔森公司这样的公司，在五十年前，更不用说十五年前，是繁荣的？”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“But I would hold a pretty wager, sir, that a House like Tellson and Company was flourishing, a matter of fifty, not to speak of fifteen years ago?”
不过，我敢打赌，像泰尔森公司这样的大买卖，在五十年前，不提十五年前，肯定是红火得很呢。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“But I would hold a pretty wager, sir, that a House like Tellson and Company was flourishing, a matter of fifty, not to speak of fifteen years ago?”
“但是，先生，我敢打个相当的赌，像泰尔森公司这样的公司，在五十年前，更不用说十五年前，是繁荣的？”

source: So resistless was the force of the ocean bearing him on, that even to draw his breath or turn his head was as impracticable as if he had been struggling in the surf at the South Sea, until he was landed in the outer courtyard of the Bastille. There, against an angle of a wall, he made a struggle to look about him. Jacques Three was nearly at his side; Madame Defarge, still heading some of her women, was visible in the inner distance, and her knife was in her hand. Everywhere was tumult, exultation, deafening and maniacal bewilderment, astounding noise, yet furious dumb-show.
domestication: 海浪推着他前行，那股力量如此强大，让他连呼吸或转头都像在南洋的海浪中挣扎一样不可能，直到他被带到了巴士底狱的外院。他在一面墙角处，努力挣扎着环顾四周。雅克三世几乎就在他身旁；德法日太太还领着她的几个女人，在内院里隐约可见，她手里还握着刀。到处都是喧嚣、狂喜，混乱得让人发懵，嘈杂声震耳欲聋，却也像是疯狂的哑剧。
foreignization: 海洋的力量如此不可抗拒地推动着他前进，以至于即使抽口气或转个头都和他在南太平洋的海浪中挣扎一样不可能，直到他被带到巴士底狱的外院。在那里，他靠着墙角努力挣扎着环顾四周。三号雅克几乎就在他身旁；德法热太太仍在带领一些她的妇女，出现在内侧远处，而她的刀子握在手中。到处都是喧嚣、狂喜、震耳欲聋且狂乱的混乱，惊人的噪音，但又是一种狂怒的哑剧。

There, against an angle of a wall, he made a struggle to look about him. Jacques Three was nearly at his side; Madame Defarge, still heading some of her women, was visible in the inner distance, and her knife was in her hand. Everywhere was tumult, exultation, deafening and maniacal bewilderment, astounding noise, yet furious dumb-show.

source: As he said the words he looked down, with an admiring pity, on the flowing golden hair; as if he pictured to himself that it might have been already tinged with grey.
domestication: 他一边说，一边低头看着那如瀑的金发，眼神里满是怜惜和赞赏，仿佛在想象这头秀发或许已染上了斑白。
foreignization: 他说着这话时低头看着那流动的金色头发，带着赞赏的同情，仿佛他在想象中已经看到了它被染上了灰白。

source: “Am I not?” Mr. Lorry opened his hands, and extended them outwards with an argumentative smile.
domestication: “我这不是嘛。”洛里先生摊开双手，向外一伸，脸上带着几分争辩的微笑。
foreignization: “我不是吗？”洛里先生张开双手，向外伸展，带着争论的微笑。
